abstract: With the rapid evolution of computing and communication technologies, lightweight web development frameworks have been widely and maturely applied in the development of small-scale software systems and the construction of lightweight internet services due to their characteristics of light weight, easy deployment and high development efficiency.For undergraduate students majoring in Software Engineering, mastering a lightweight web framework suitable for their learning stage is a prerequisite for successfully completing curriculum design, curriculum project development, as well as initial scientific research practice and innovative exploration. However, it is undeniable that undergraduate beginners often encounter problems such as vague selection criteria, low adaptability of learning curves and poor matching with hardware configurations when choosing frameworks. Accordingly, this paper selects three typical lightweight Python Web frameworks—Flask, FastAPI and Bottle—as research objects, and conducts a systematic and hierarchical comparative analysis from four dimensions: learning difficulty, development efficiency, resource consumption and application scenarios. More importantly, based on the learning foundation, hardware environment and project requirements of undergraduate beginners, this paper objectively and reasonably summarizes the technical characteristics, applicable scenarios and performance boundaries of the three frameworks, and puts forward targeted framework selection suggestions. The results of this paper not only help undergraduate beginners in Software Engineering and related majors quickly get started with Web development, but also enable them to efficiently and reliably build small-scale Web applications on personal computers, and lay a solid foundation for their future learning of heavyweight frameworks and engagement in industrial-grade development.

title: Locomotion Design and Experimental Validation of a 10-Ton-Class Firefighting Hexapod Robot
authors: Taiping Wu, Junyao Gao, Leilei Xie
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: Legged locomotion at the multi-ton scale remains largely unexplored due to challenges in actuation, structural robustness, stability, and power consumption. This paper presents the design and experimental walking implementation of a 10-ton-class firefighting hexapod robot on physical hardware. A hydraulically actuated hexapod with parallelogram leg mechanisms is developed, and a statically stable walking gait is designed based on kinematic constraints and phase coordination. Reinforcement learning is employed in a lightweight manner to modulate high-level gait parameters without introducing complex dynamic control. Simulation and physical experiments demonstrate stable and repeatable flat-ground walking, providing evidence that legged locomotion at the multi-ton scale is feasible.

title: Navigating the Future: AI in Shipping, Its Challenges and Legal Considerations
authors: Reetika Bansal, Trisha Gosain, Tanuja, Pratibha Verma, Gurpreet Saini, Trupti Maruti Muntode
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In this changing world, the shipping industry has been a critical component in trade and commerce. It has faced a lot of challenges, such as security, safety, system integration, and shipping costs. The introduction of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has played a major role in combating these challenges. This digital revolutionization offers various benefits such as reduction in port time, better coordination and lower environmental impact. However, these advancements also have significant impacts on the shipping industry, including cybersecurity risks such as navigation and phishing attacks, high implementation costs, and a shortage of professionals. If the system is integrated with AI, who will be responsible in case any malfunction occurs, and what kind of training will the system undergo to conquer the situation? To get the answer to various questions as mentioned, the paper discusses challenges of AI in the shipping industry and what solutions does the industry provide to contend with the mentioned challenges. Additionally, the paper talks about the legal framework, especially the Maritime Law, emphasizing its role in the maritime sector. With the current regulations posed by the International Maritime Organization (IMO), the study highlights case studies and statistical data to provide a more comprehensive view of the proposed challenges.

title: Development of Attribute-Based Cryptographic Mechanism for Secure Medical Data Retention in Cloud Systems
authors: P. Karthik, D. Latha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The rapid adoption of cloud computing in healthcare has highlighted the need for secure mechanisms to protect sensitive medical data. This paper proposes an Attribute-Based Encryption (ABE) approach for securing medical data in cloud systems, ensuring data confidentiality and access control. ABE enables encryption based on user attributes, such as role or medical specialty, ensuring that only authorized personnel can decrypt the data. In a cloud environment, medical data can be stored securely, with access granted based on predefined policies linked to user attributes. This method minimizes the risk of unauthorized access while enabling flexible sharing of data among healthcare providers. The tool used in this approach is an advanced cryptographic library that implements ABE techniques, ensuring efficient encryption and decryption processes. The proposed system addresses the challenges of secure data retention and access control in cloudbased medical data management, offering a robust solution to privacy concerns in healthcare.

title: AI-Driven Cyber-Physical Systems for Obstacle Avoidance in Robotic Caregivers: A Comprehensive Framework for Enhanced Human-Robot Interaction
authors: Movin Naurd, Harsh Tyagi, Lakshit Kanwal, Raj Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: A cyber-physical system (CPS) is an integration of computation, networking, and physical processes, where embedded computers monitor and control physical systems via feedback loops. The integration of artificial intelligence with (CPS) in robotic caregivers presents unprecedented opportunities for autonomous healthcare assistance. This paper proposes a novel AI-driven CPS framework specifically designed for obstacle avoidance in robotic caregivers, addressing critical gaps in existing navigation systems through a comprehensive six-layer architecture. The presented approach combines multi-sensor fusion using Extended Kalman Filtering (EKF), hybrid AI decision making algorithms, and robust human-robot interaction (HRI) capabilities to create a complete solution for safe and efficient caregiver robotics. The modular design enables easy deployment across various healthcare environments while maintaining safety-first principles through hardware watchdogs and recovery controllers. Key contributions include a hybrid AI-CPS architecture, comprehensive multi-sensor fusion framework, integrated HRI system, and extensive experimental validation demonstrating practical feasibility for real world healthcare applications.

title: A Gated Multi-Modal Fusion Framework Via Mixture of Experts for Environment-Aware UAV Detection
authors: Yumou Wang, Jianxiao Zou, Ru An, Lei Jia
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: The rapid evolution of the low-altitude economy necessitates robust perception systems capable of operating in complex, open-world environments. While the detection of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) has seen significant progress, maintaining performance in complex low-altitude environments remains non-trivial, particularly when single-modality systems are subjected to environmental stressors like extreme lighting variations or elevated background noise. This paper explores an environment-aware multi-modal detection framework based on the Mixture of Experts (MoE) approach, referred to as AVLEnvMoE. By integrating audio, visual, and LiDAR modalities, this framework seeks to capitalize on the complementary strengths of heterogeneous sensors. Central to the proposed architecture is an Environmental Gating Network, which adaptively assigns fusion weights to the expert branches of audio, vision, and LiDAR by utilizing real-time physical metadata, including image brightness, contrast, and acoustic noise levels. This mechanism allows the system to prioritize more reliable modalities when the visual channel is compromised. Evaluation results on the MMAUD dataset suggest that, compared to traditional fusion methods, the proposed gated multi-modal scheme provides enhanced robustness and positioning accuracy across challenging environmental conditions.

title: Operational Scenario Generation for Microgrids using Generative Adversarial Networks and Improved Mean-Shift Clustering
authors: Zhiwei Wan, Kai Yuan, Chongbo Sun, Yudi Ding, Ming Nie
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The planning and operation scheduling of microgrids involve the modeling and analysis of a large number of source and load scenarios, which has a significant impact on the calculation efficiency and reliability of the planning and scheduling model. This paper proposes a typical scenarios generation method for microgrids, where the generative adversarial networks algorithm is utilized to generate a large number of PV output and load demand scenarios, and the performance of generated scenarios is evaluated using autocorrelation coefficient and cumulative probability distribution. The mass scenarios and the associated weight are obtained by an improved mean shift clustering algorithm, considering the total amount and temporal characteristics of the data. A simulation analysis is conducted based on actual data from the northwest region of China, and the results show that the proposed method can effectively simplify original scenarios while balancing the computational complexity and accuracy of the operation scenarios.

title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence Model in Human Resources for Service Sector Organizations
authors: Ravinder Kaur, Rosy Singh, P. William
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper examines how artificial intelligence (AI) can be used in human resources (HR) and provides a case study of how it was applied in an Indian organization. AIpowered solutions are becoming more popular as a means of streamlining HR procedures, increasing productivity, and improving decision-making. This article outlines the advantages and difficulties of incorporating AI into HR tasks and offers details on how AI is being used in a real-world Indian company environment. This article also contrasts the use of AI in human resources using conventional and modern methodologies. The abstract offers a case study that was carried out in an Indian business to examine how AI was successfully incorporated into HR procedures. Along with the advantages of modern approaches like technology-driven recruiting and data-driven performance analysis, challenges in recruitment (talent scarcity, competition, diversity), as well as performance analysis (subjectivity, real-time feedback, data complexity), are described. The importance of AI in a number of HR domains is underlined, including hiring, employee engagement, performance management, and training. For successful AI application, the abstract highlights the significance of reliable data and strong data governance. In the article's conclusion, examples of Indian businesses employing AI for HR tasks are provided. According to the research study, AI is important for many different human resource department functions. Companies can manage duties like recruitment, hiring procedures, data analysis, data collecting, workload reduction, and improvement of general workplace efficiency by integrating robotics for research and development.

title: Federated Edge-AI and Digital Twin-Enabled IoT Framework for Real-Time Landslide Prediction and Multi-Modal Early Warning Systems in Arid and Mountainous Regions
authors: Thirupathi Regula, Warda Al-Hoqani, S Gouri Kiran Kumar, Sunitha Cherian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Landslides represent a major environmental hazard in arid and mountainous regions, often triggered by sudden rainfall, seismic activity, or soil instability. Traditional centralized monitoring systems are limited by latency, bandwidth, and data privacy concerns, especially in remote terrains with poor connectivity. This research proposes a Federated Edge-AI and Digital Twin–enabled IoT framework that integrates multi-source environmental sensing, edge intelligence, and federated learning to enable real-time landslide prediction and multi-modal early warning dissemination. The proposed framework includes a combination of geotechnical and climatic sensors such as MEMS-based accelerometers, soil dielectric probes, and rainfall detectors connected via LoRaWAN and 6G URLLC links. These sensors are connected by LoRaWAN and 6G URLLC links. Each edge node does inference based on TinyML and takes part in privacy-preserving federated learning to work together to make the model more accurate without sharing raw data. The cloud layer has a digital twin of the monitored terrain that is constantly updated through multi-source data fusion (sensor streams, satellite SAR imagery, and meteorological inputs) to simulate ground movement and predict areas that are likely to slide. The framework includes deep reinforcement learning, which leads to prioritizing alerts, making sure they are as accurate as possible by minimizing false positives while ensuring high responsiveness. The system sends alerts through a variety of channels, such as mobile notifications, tactile wearables, and public alerts that are only available in certain areas. This keeps people more aware of what's going on around them, helps them make better predictions, and supports long-term disaster resilience strategies that are in line with UN SDG 11 and SDG 13. Experimental validation and simulations show that it performs better, has less latency, and works well in a variety of terrain conditions.

title: Deep Learning Framework for Overcrowding Detection and Stampede Mitigation
authors: Vijay K, Dhanush Ishwar D, Akash SV, Shivesh M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In Countries like India, where large public gatherings frequently take place, such as religious festivals, political rallies, Celebrations, Concerts, and sports events, this leads to an overcrowded situation. Regrettably, these gatherings might lead to stampedes, which may cause severe injuries and fatalities. This project addresses a solution to overcome this. We have proposed AI-powered software as a remedy. This software takes input as CCTV camera footage to predict the density of the crowd in particular zones and analyze the crowd behavioral patterns in real time, and sends an alert in the dashboard to take preventive actions before a disaster could occur. This system uses YOLO- v11 for faster and accurate detection of people on the occasion. This is paired with Optical Flow (Lucas-Kanade Method) to analyze the movement patterns and identify any noticeable surges in the crowd using Seaborn/Matplotlib. Heatmaps are used to visualize the density of people in a particular zone with distinguishable colors, such as red for high density and blue/green for low density. This makes it trouble- free for dashboard operators to understand the crowd situation and take immediate action in case of a possible risk due to a stampede.

title: Integration of AI and IoT in Transforming Customer Experience for Digital Marketing
authors: Vikas Nain, Sandeep Kumar, Veenu Gupta, Annu Dahiya, Shafat Khan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the rapidly evolving digital marketing ecosystem, enhancing customer experience has become pivotal for competitive differentiation. This study investigates the transformative potential of integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) in reshaping customer interactions across digital platforms. Leveraging a mixed-methods research design, we analyze pre- and post-integration performance metrics across 30 organizations in various sectors. Key variables—average order value, cart abandonment rates, customised recommendations, customer satisfaction—were statistically tested using T-tests and ANOVA using Tableau and Power BI. Thanks to IoT-powered real-time data collection and AI-powered customization acting together to inspire predictive engagement and operational success, the findings reveal significant increases in every metric that was investigated. Even more supports the empirical research visual data proving a clear association between more IoT adoption and improved retention and satisfaction outcomes.

title: Real-Time Agricultural Environment Monitoring with ESP32 and Cloud Security
authors: Shaik Anas, Kolluri Sahithi, Nakka Siva Rami Reddy, Pellakuru Venkatesh, V. Niveatha Saro
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainability and efficiency in agriculture has prompted the use of Internet of Things for monitoring in real time of farms. Work revealed here is a real-time agricultural environment monitoring system which is developed in ESP32 microcontroller with various environmental sensors to measure parameter like temperature, humidity, soil moisture, and light. System will allow cloud-based monitoring by wireless transmission of sensed data, which will help the farmer in scheduling irrigation time, managing resources, protecting crop safety, etc. Apart from monitoring environmental conditions in the field, the system will emphasize securely transmitting and storing the data in the cloud. Due to the sensitive nature of farm data, IoT-enabled agricultural systems are vulnerable to cyber-attacks, unauthorized access, and data tampering. The key innovation lies in the incorporation of lightweight AES-based encryption and cloud-level access control within a cost-effective IoT architecture suitable for small-scale and remote farms. The integrated solution includes efficient sensing, reliable wireless communication and robust cloud security mechanisms to facilitate precision farming. The recommended approach improves the agricultural productivity and makes data security a basic requirement for any trustworthy IoT-based smart agriculture applications.

title: IoT Integrated Dual-Side Water Pumping System For Sustainable Agriculture
authors: E.B. Priyanka, S. Thangavel, P. Sivamathivanan, S Dharshana, P Jothika, P Kavyadharashini D, K P Vasanth Kumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The Dual-side water pumping water system is designed to achieve continuous and uninterrupted water flow for agricultural and industrial purposes. The system is powered by the scotch yoke mechanism to provide the pumping action. The system performance was very crucial to measure with the precise controller model. Implementing the IoT to monitor and control water flow rate, pressure level, vibration level, and pump status. The IoT-enabled PLC controller processes the data and push to the cloud data warehouse to ensure variation in the pressure level and flow. The system communication protocols like MQTT and HTTP to continuous data transfer to predict the variation for maintenance activities. In this system, water pumps will be operated bidirectionally to suck the high-velocity fluid with high pressure to achieve the continuous water flow. The microcontroller performs the decision-making algorithm to control the pump’s actuation and flow rate. The system proposed automatic system increases the system efficiency by $25 \%$ as compared to the initial phase to support the water delivery to ensure energy-efficient and seamless water flow.

title: Intelligent Big Data Analytics Framework for Scalable and Real-Time Data Processing in Distributed Systems
authors: Anshu Vashisth, Gagandeep Kaur, Hassandeep Singh, Ravi Shanker
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The sheer increase in the amount of data produced by distributed sources (IoT devices, cloud platforms, real-time applications) has led to a high demand for scalable and efficient big data analytics frameworks. Conventional data processing systems are limited with respect to their ability to process large volumes and high velocity and heterogeneous streams of data, especially in real-time. So as to mitigate such challenges, this paper will suggest an Intelligent Big Data Analytics Framework for scalable and real-time data processing in Distributed Systems that combines distributed computing with machine learning based analytics to effectively process and make decisions relating to data. The suggested framework comprises such main elements as data acquisition, distributed processing, an intelligent analytics engine, and a real-time decision module. The system uses parallel computing and adaptive learning to enhance scalability, minimize latency, and improve efficiency in processing data. Experimental testing shows that the suggested framework has a processing efficiency of 94.6, a latency decrease of 38, a scalability enhancement of 41, and an accuracy of 93.2 compared to the standard big data processing systems. The framework also uses resources most effectively and enables real-time analytics in making dynamic decisions. These findings demonstrate that the intelligent big data analytics model proposed has a strong, scalable, and efficient solution to real-time data processing in a distributed environment.

title: LAM-MD: A Large Action Model Integrated Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Framework for NOMA Networks
authors: Yuhong He, Wenjing Xiao, Miaojiang Chen, Anfeng Liu, Min Chen, Ahmed Farouk, Houbing Herbert Song, Athanasios V. Vasilakos
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: DualRateECGNet: Multi-Rate Latent State Estimation for Contactless ECG Reconstruction from mmWave Radar
authors: Meiyue Diao, Hengrong Cui
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) require continuous cardiac monitoring for early screening, risk assessment, and long-term management. Conventional contact-based electrocardiogram (ECG), however, is not ideal for prolonged use. Adhesive electrodes may cause discomfort, skin irritation, and restricted mobility. Millimeter-wave (mmWave) frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radar provides a promising non-contact alternative by sensing subtle chest vibrations. Nevertheless, reconstructing ECG from radar remains challenging due to electromechanical delay (EMD), motion interference, and the spectral bias of neural networks. This paper proposes DualRateECGNet, a multi-rate latent state estimation framework for radar-to-ECG reconstruction. The model combines a low-rate latent state estimation branch with a high-rate residual refinement branch. The data-driven Direct State Estimator is used to learn a compact latent representation associated with cardiac dynamics and thoracic motion, while the Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) refines high-frequency waveform details to better preserve diagnostic transients such as the QRS complex. Experiments on the MMECG dataset under a 5-fold cross-validation protocol show that DualRateECGNet achieves a median Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.9905 and a median root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.02658 mV, indicating strong reconstruction fidelity within this evaluation setting.

title: A Novel Approach to Active Directory Management using AI-Driven Identity and Access Management for Enhanced Enterprise Security
authors: Kamalakar Gunupati, Manish Kumar, Pell Reddy Rajender Reddy, Ramesh Julakanti, Ram Reddy Jonnalagadda, Kiran Kumar Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The advanced development in the digital landscape manages the identity and access in the enterprise efficiently and securely as significant challenge. The present study involves novel approach to active directory management using AI driven identity and access management system. The integration of intelligent automation and machine learning algorithms the proposed study involves the framework to enhance the detection ofabnormal behaviour and Ransomware attacks for deprovisioning and provisioning of streamline access and to ensure enforcement of dynamic policy across the enterprise units. The findings of the study are to mitigate the threats by the hackers, to improve compliance at regulatory standard and to reduce the administrative overhead. The study involves performance evaluation at various metrics to demonstrate the capability of the framework for significant strengthening of the security in the enterprise to maintain operational agility. The potential of artificial intelligence is analysed in the study to revolutionise Active Directory Management to offer adaptive and scalable security model for the model enterprises.

title: Performance Evaluation of Floating Solar Power Projects in India Using Multi-Criteria Decisionmaking Techniques
authors: Vasupalli Manoj, Guntu Nooka Raju, Akasapu Kanaka Varshini, Balajee Maram, Gadi Bheema Sankararao, Gudiya Kamal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: In India, floating solar photovoltaics (FSPV) are becoming more popular as a result of a scarcity of available land, improved energy efficiency, and other features, including decreased water evaporation losses. However, due to many different technical, financial, and ecological characteristics that go into evaluating these projects, these evaluations are complicated and difficult to make decisions on. Through this article, we provide a systematic evaluation of the performance of floating solar power projects in India by utilizing MultiCriteria Decision-Making (MCDM) techniques. An integrated framework that combines the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP), Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution (TOPSIS), and VIKOR will be used to accomplish this goal. AHP will be applied to sdevelop the relative weightings for the eight evaluation criteria while TOPSIS will be used to rank the ten floating solar project alternatives. The implementation of these methods will occur in MATLAB. The results indicate that regardless of the MCDM method applied, there was consistency among the rankings produced by each technique with respect to the floating solar power projects evaluated in terms of their overall performance; thus, the proposed methodology offers an effective decision-making tool for policymakers, planners, and investors interested in developing sustainable solar energy projects.

title: Dynamic Output Feedback RMPC for NCSs under Round-Robin Protocol subject to Hybrid Attacks
authors: Hongbin Cai, Fuchen Wang, Caiyun Sui, Ning Cui, Xiaoran Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper is concerned with the dynamic output feedback robust model predictive control problem for a class of networked control systems (NCSs) with round-robin (RR) protocol and subject to hybrid attacks. To begin with, two mutually independent Bernoulli random variables are used to characterize the DoS and deception attacks in the sensor-to-controller (S-C) channel. To avoid data collision, the RR protocol is employed to orchestrate the data transmission order in the controller-to-actuator (C-A) channel. Furthermore, the inequality analysis technique and slack matrices are subsequently used to resolve the complex non-convex optimization problem. Sufficient conditions are provided to guarantee the recursive feasibility of the proposed algorithm and the mean square (MS) security of the system. At last, a high-purity distillation column example demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Machine Learning for Air Quality Monitoring with Low-Cost Sensors: Evaluating Calibration Approaches Through Relative Expanded Uncertainty
authors: Evangelos Bagkis, Theodosios Kassandros, Kostas Karatzas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The current paper introduces machine learning (ML) as a method that supports field calibration of low-cost air quality sensors and their networks. The ML methodology initially focuses on individual/ad-hoc sensor node calibration, but it is then developed towards a global calibration approach, where one calibration function is trained to serve the needs of calibration of all nodes belonging to an AQ monitoring network. To achieve more meaningful results in terms of operational requirements, the evaluation of this calibration is based on statistical metrics such as the root mean squared error (RMSE) and coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), but also on the relative expanded uncertainty (REU). The latter is defined in the European Directive 2008/50/EC and is incorporated into the ISO standards CEN/TS 17660-1 (2021) and CEN/TS 17660-2 (2024), and it offers a more standardized and interpretable metric for uncertainty evaluation. REU quantifies the uncertainty between LCS and reference measurements based on co-located and simultaneous observations. The ML-oriented approach achieves REU values for PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</inf> below 50% across all tested configurations and below 25% specifically when sufficient training data are available (≥60% train-test split for this experimental setting) and pollutant concentrations exceed 30 μg/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. Critically, global calibration demonstrates superior performance in data-sparse scenarios, a common constraint in operational sensor network deployments. Our results highlight the importance of adopting REU as a unified performance metric and demonstrate the benefits of global calibration for scalable, transferable AQ sensor network deployment.

title: SDAF: An Anti-Forgetting Federated Learning Framework for Anomaly Traffic Detection
authors: Xiaotong Qu, Panpan Geng, Xiaodong Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: With the advancement of informatization, network infrastructures in critical domains face escalating cyber threats, making anomaly detection pivotal for security. Deep learning-based anomaly traffic detection suffers from privacy leakage and data silos due to reliance on centralized datasets, limiting deployment in distributed environments. Federated learning (FL) offers a privacy-preserving alternative but is plagued by client data heterogeneity, unstable convergence, and catastrophic forgetting. To address these issues, this study proposes SDAF, a privacy-preserving FL-based anomaly detection framework. It integrates causal learning into an Enforced Causal Inference Variational Autoencoder (ECI-VAE) for causality-based feature modeling, enhancing interpretability and robustness. A local self-distillation module TGS mitigates knowledge forgetting via soft-label knowledge transfer. Experiments on NSL-KDD and UNSW-NB15 datasets show SDAF outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving 3.9% and 5.8% average accuracy improvements under IID and Non-IID settings, respectively. This validates its effectiveness and potential for real-world distributed deployments like smart manufacturing and IoT.

title: Exploring University Students Behavioral Intentions Toward Generative AI
authors: Marcella Angelica, Anderes Gui, Vina Georgiana, Idris Gautama So, Riki Riki, Priscilia Calista Kowirya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Generative AI like Wilde can revolutionize the way students approach and absorb academic content. This study seeks to investigate the variables affecting students’ intentions and behavior in adoption of Generative AI in higher education based on the extended UTAUT2 model. The data was obtained through online survey with 390 student participants from several universities in Indonesia. Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) using SmartPLS software is used to perform analysis and synthesis of examination data. The findings indicate that performance expectancy, habit and price value are key determinants of usage in intention, while behavioral intention, habit and trust impact usage. In contrast, hedonic motivation, social influence, privacy, and trust have no effect on intention to use. These results suggest that students care more about the utility and use habit through the use of AI technology rather than the entertainment value or social influence. This study offers implications for educational institutions in developing better strategies on the implementation of AI technology, as well as encouraging policies that incorporate issues of ethics, trust and sustainability on use of technology in learning.

title: Predicting Social Media Addiction Among University Students Via Stacking Ensemble Machine Learning Models: A Comparative Study
authors: Jayapriya R, Jeyachidra J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The accelerated use of social media by university students has added to the worriment about addiction behavioral patterns. Subjectivity and inconsistency of self-report survey data make it difficult to identify high-risk individuals at an early stage. Conventional forms of supervised learning make the assumption that there are trustworthy ground-truth labels and as a result fail to work well when trained on noisy psychometric answers. To solve this problem, this paper suggests a Confidence Guided Adaptive Supervised Learning (CGASL) model, which overcomes label uncertainty by a teacher student model. One of the main models computes scores on the prediction of confidence to filter problematic samples adaptively before the final training. The model boosts generalization and decreases overfitting to conflicting responses in that the high-confidence observations only are retained. Tests on a sample of 1,598 college students show that CGASL reaches 94.06% accuracy and AUC-ROC of 0.97, which is a major improvement compared to baseline models (75.62% accuracy). Sensitivity analysis establishes that a certain threshold (0.75) exists between the elimination of noise and the retention of data. The results show the efficiency of confidence-aware filtering in ethically and scalably deployable in academic contexts social media addiction screening.

title: Fusing Instance-Based and Density-Based Learning for Enhanced Accident Prediction
authors: Lanke Lakshmi Prasanna Kumar, Jayalakshmi Machiraju, I.V. Shashikala, T. Aditya Sai Srinivas, K. Dora Babu, Dhanunjay Sirigiri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Predicting the severity of traffic accidents is important in the development of intelligent transport systems that facilitate emergency response systems and the allocation of resources. The high dimensionality of the accident data, its complex nature and class imbalance means that applying a singular machine learning algorithm produces insufficient results. To this end, we develop and propose a novel hybrid ensemble framework for the K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN) and Density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN) technologies. The proposed methodology is built on a solid foundation of preprocessing steps that include label encoding, standardization, and addressing the class imbalance. KNN is a powerful instance-based classifier for the local pattern density and feature proximity, while DBSCAN helps in the recognition of structures and outlier elasticities in a particular data density, thereby assisting in the distribution of the data from a different perspective. The main novelty of this paper lies in the weighted ensemble mechanism that integrates KNN classification probabilities with DBSCAN's cluster-density-based confidence score. We provide a detailed analysis and comparisons of the hybrid model with standalone KNN, DBSCAN and advanced benchmarks such as Random Forest, XGBoost and KNN-OPTICS hybrid models. Using a public dataset of large-scale traffic accidents, the KNN-DBSCAN ensemble is shown to achieve state-of-the-art predictive accuracy of 92.4 % and F 1 -score of 0.91. This represents a statistically significant improvement over the baseline models. Using feature importance, the study also explains the major contributors to the severity of the accidents. This study provides a more sophisticated, explainable and reproducible accident analytics model that has direct real-world applications in improving the practices of road safety management and the development of data-based strategies and policy frameworks.

title: A High-Risk Prediction Method for Coal Mine Fires Incorporating Path-Coupled Features
authors: Wenjing Yu, Yong Qin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To address the dispersed nature of risk information and the insufficient representation of chain-like combinational relationships in coal mine fire accident investigation reports, a CatBoost-based high-risk prediction method incorporating structured risk information and path-coupled features was developed in this study. Risk prediction and the analysis of key influencing factors associated with coal mine fires were conducted through structured risk information encoding, core path screening, model comparison, threshold determination, and SHAP-based interpretation. The results indicate that, after the introduction of path-coupled features, CatBoost exhibited superior overall predictive performance and higher precision for the high-risk class, while enhancing the identification of chain-like combinational relationships underlying high-risk accidents. The findings further reveal that high-risk accidents are jointly shaped by multiple categories of factors. These results can provide support for high-risk screening, warning-threshold setting, and the prioritization of governance measures.

title: Understanding Consumer Trade-Offs in the Adoption of IoT Services: Privacy Concerns vs. Perceived Benefits in the Indian Context
authors: Abhimanyu Ahluwalia, Shikha Kapoor, Lovleen Gupta, Vikas Garg
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: As Internet of Things (IoT) technologies increasingly integrate into daily life, this study examines how Indian consumers evaluate the trade-offs between the perceived benefits of IoT services—such as utility, convenience, and enhanced experiences—and their concerns about personal data privacy. The research identifies how key innovation-related attributes (perceived advantage, ease of integration, and visibility of use) influence adoption intentions. Additionally, it explores the role of Privacy Self-Efficacy (PSE) in shaping individual confidence in managing privacy risks, thereby moderating the relationship between perceived value and willingness to adopt IoT-enabled services. This study proposes a conceptual framework informed by Diffusion of Innovation (DOI) theory and Social Exchange Theory (SET) to examine Indian consumers’ adoption of IoT services. A cross-sectional survey was conducted among 700 Indian respondents who use or are aware of IoT-enabled products and services. To analyze the direct and moderating effects of key constructs, including Privacy Self-Efficacy, Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) was utilized.

title: Adaptive Resource Management and Security in Fog-Based IoT Healthcare Systems
authors: Deepak Kumar Tiwari, Smita Dash, Yogendra Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The applications of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) like remote patient monitoring and emergency response require ultra-low latency and high reliability and a strong level of data security, which cannot be ensured in federated cloud architectures. Fog computing is an operation that solves these shortcomings by moving computation much nearer to medical sensors and gateways, but current solutions mostly consider resource management and security to be independent design issues and do not provide an integrated adaptive control. The paper suggests a security conscious adaptive fog architecture which optimizes Quality of Service (QoS), resource utilization, and data protection at the same time by applying a closed-loop control architecture comprised of priority-aware task scheduling, dynamic load balancing, adaptive authentication and encryption, intrusion detection, and federated learning on a per-mog level. The system is formulated as a multi-objective optimization problem targeting end-to-end latency, energy consumption, service-level agreement compliance, and security risk, and is evaluated under realistic healthcare workloads and cyberattack scenarios against state-of-the-art scheduling, security, and privacy-preserving baselines. The results demonstrate that joint adaptation significantly reduces latency and SLA violations while enhancing resilience to attacks with moderate computational and communication overhead compared to static and single-objective approaches. By unifying adaptive resource orchestration, security enforcement, and privacy-preserving analytics within a single fog-based IoMT architecture, this study provides a resilient and scalable foundation for next-generation real-time healthcare systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence for A Greener Planet: Tackling Climate Change with Technology
authors: Anjali Makan, Bindu Jindal, Madhu Bala, Janvi Verma, Neeshu Rani, Anshul Pahwa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: An immediate worldwide issue affecting ecosystems, human health, economics, and biodiversity is climate change. Artificial intelligence (AI) is becoming a great ally as environmental problems expand in helping to create a civilisation more sustainable and climate-resistant. This study investigates the potential of artificial intelligence in reducing climate change by way of applications in energy optimisation, environmental monitoring, smart agriculture, emission tracking, and policy creation. Analysing adoption rates, emission reduction metrics, real artificial intelligence initiatives, and global perspective data reveals how artificial intelligence is changing environmental governance. The study also found sectoral variations in artificial intelligence effectiveness using ANOVA, therefore directing information on where AI investments would have the most impact on the surroundings. Results show substantial emissions reductions, improved system efficiency, and public trust in AI-driven solutions even if issues like digital inequality, ethical dilemmas, and energy use still exist. In essence, the research supports the ethical and inclusive use of artificial intelligence as a basic tool for accomplishing the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the international climate objectives.

title: Distillation-Driven Graph Recommendation by Unifying Local and Global Signals
authors: Hongjia Chen, Qunfeng Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Graph-based recommender systems can effectively exploit user - item interaction structures. However, under sparse feedback, many graph contrastive approaches fail to leverage reliable global cues, which may lead to unstable optimization and suboptimal generalization. In this paper, we develop an enhanced graph contrastive framework that integrates knowledge distillation with an exponential moving average (EMA) teacher. The EMA teacher produces temporally smoothed targets, and the student model learns from the teacher through distillation, resulting in more stable training dynamics and improved robustness to noise. Extensive experiments on multiple large-scale benchmarks demonstrate consistent gains over strong baselines, achieving notable improvements in ranking performance as well as training efficiency. These results suggest a practical way to strengthen global structural guidance in graph recommendation.

title: Real-Time Fire Detection Using a Computer Vision-Based YOLOv11 Model on Raspberry Pi 5
authors: Kenny Hoang, David Mung, Pallav Kumar Bera
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: An optimized Raspberry Pi 5 deployment of a real-time fire and smoke detection system was developed by using the YOLO object detection framework. A custom dataset with three classes (fire, smoke, none) was used to train the model for 200 epochs. The system achieved a precision of 94.41 %, recall of 89.46 %, F1-Score of 91.87 %, mAP@50 of 93.73%, and mAP@50-95 of 85.44%. Results show that smoke detection outperforms fire detection, while most misclassifications occurred between fire and background. Despite these challenges, the proposed system demonstrates reliable performance and feasibility for low-power, edge-based fire monitoring applications. The system also demonstrates the potential of integrating lightweight deep learning models with embedded edge devices for continuous environmental monitoring. The optimized implementation on Raspberry Pi 5 enables low-cost deployment in smart buildings, industrial areas, and public safety applications while maintaining stable detection accuracy and efficient real-time processing performance.

title: Traffic Congestion Management Techniques
authors: Parambrata Sanyal, Gopal Kumar Gupta, Ajit Kumar Singh, Manvandra Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Different cities around the globe are becoming victims of the persistent issue of traffic congestion. Traffic congestion is a major headache for commuters and cities. The exponential and bullish rise in the number of traffic is followed by rapid urbanization, which is posing a serious threat to the traffic system. This transit scenario is now evident enough that it is uncontrollable for the traditional traffic management techniques. In this scenario, a traffic management technique with optimized traffic flow and congestion measures can greatly reduce the overall waste of the nation’s economy, resources, and pollution. Since the previous two decades, the intelligent transport system (ITS) has turned out to be the only solution to use transport networks in an efficient and coordinated manner. To address this widespread issue, this paper underscores the different efficient traffic management techniques that the researchers have proposed to date aiming for the intelligent transport system. The authors have reviewed the different methodologies for different techniques in this paper.

title: Improved ECC Algorithm Based on Dynamic Base Point and Dynamic Private Key and Its Application in Remote Capacity Verification Communication in Substations
authors: Yunchen Xu, Zonghe Ding, Ling Chen, Liming Chang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the advancement of smart grid construction, remote battery capacity verification for DC systems in substations has become a critical focus in digital transformation for operation and maintenance. However, multi-system interactions face security risks such as communication delays, data leaks, and device authentication vulnerabilities. This study proposes an improved ECC algorithm with public keyless transmission, dynamic base points, and dynamic private keys. Both parties derive these parameters via real-time synchronization, eliminating risks of public key interception, forgery, and static key accumulation. Security evaluation using the Dolev-Yao threat model demonstrates that the algorithm effectively resists various attacks through formal and informal analyses. Experimental results show that the proposed improved ECC scheme achieves only 60.1%,33.0%, and 17.2% of the computational, communication, and storage overheads compared to traditional ECC schemes, respectively, while maintaining 44.9%,44.8%, and 62.5% of the computational, communication, and storage overheads of conventional RSA public key encryption algorithms. The exponential increase in cryptographic complexity further highlights the scheme's high efficiency and robust security in edge computing scenarios.

title: Fault-Tolerant Trajectory Tracking for Quadrotor UAVs via Sliding Mode Control
authors: Haowen Sun, Xichao Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper proposes a fault-tolerant control scheme based on Sliding Mode Control for quadrotor UAVs subject to actuator efficiency loss during flight. First, a mathematical model of the quadrotor considering nonlinear dynamics is established. Subsequently, a dual-loop control architecture is employed: the outer position loop calculates the desired attitude angles and total thrust based on the reference trajectory , while the inner attitude and altitude loops are designed using SMC algorithms to enhance robustness against parameter uncertainties and external disturbances via error switching functions. Furthermore, a fault estimation module is developed to evaluate the health status of actuators by monitoring the deviation between actual thrust and control commands in real-time. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed control scheme effectively suppresses oscillations and achieves precise tracking of complex reference trajectories even under partial actuator failure, exhibiting superior disturbance rejection and fault-tolerant robustness.

title: Exploring the Human–AI Interface in Recruitment: A Qualitative Study of Chatbots in HRM
authors: Rashmi Kumari, Sutapa Mondal, Ritesh Kumar Upadhyay, Sushanta Mahanty, Subhash Adhikari, Kunal Sarkar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly embedded in human resource management (HRM), with recruitment emerging as one of the earliest domains of adoption. Among AI-enabled tools, chatbots are widely deployed to automate high-volume and transactional recruitment activities such as candidate screening, scheduling, and communication. However, empirical evidence on their practical utility, limitations, and contextual fit in emerging and linguistically diverse markets remains limited. This study explores the human–AI interface in recruitment by examining how AI-powered chatbots are perceived, implemented, and constrained in Indian organizational contexts. Using a qualitative research design, semi-structured interviews were conducted with senior HR professionals possessing extensive experience in talent acquisition, HR analytics, and AI-enabled recruitment. Reflexive thematic analysis reveals that chatbots deliver substantial efficiency gains and improved candidate responsiveness when integrated with applicant tracking systems, particularly for entry-level and high-volume hiring. At the same time, significant limitations persist due to natural language processing constraints, contextual ambiguity, and challenges in assessing soft skills, leadership potential, and cultural fit. Findings further highlight clear task boundaries, with chatbots functioning effectively as process-support and triage tools, while human judgment remains indispensable for evaluative and strategic hiring decisions. The study contributes practitioner-centered insights to the AI-in-HRM literature by demonstrating that chatbot adoption in recruitment is best understood as augmentative rather than substitutive. It concludes by emphasizing the need for hybrid human–AI recruitment models, robust governance mechanisms, and context-aware chatbot design to ensure responsible and effective deployment in emerging markets.

title: PROMISE-Net: A Progressive Contextual Multi-Scale Integrated Network for Accurate Lung Nodule Segmentation and Classification
authors: V. Kavithamani, Jayashivani M, Asraf Ali A, Sowndharya. L, Suruthika M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Accurate lung cancer prediction demands precise nodule segmentation and reliable malignancy classification. It is very critical to analyze when nodules show low contrast, irregular shapes and small sizes. The proposed PROMISE-Net is a unified deep learning framework that incorporates progressive multi-scale segmentation with maskguided contextual classification. The segmentation module, Contextual Residual Attention UNet (CR-AUNet) integrates the modules of Contextual Pyramid Attention and Progressive Boundary Refinement for enhancing structural detail and boundary accuracy. The classification module such as MaskConditioned Contrastive Transformer (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{M C}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>-Transformer) is proposed to create mask-guided ROI features together with global contextual embeddings. The Cross-Task Feature Bank(CTFB) enforces consistent representation learning through supervised contrastive loss. The proposed PROMISENet was tested on a lung CT dataset with benign, malignant and normal cases, achieving a Dice score of 0.95, segmentation accuracy of 97.8 %, and a classification accuracy of 98.6 %. These results showed that this model outperformed than existing baseline models in a lung cancer prediction.

title: Enhancing Collaborative Robotics through Large Language Model Integration: A Vision-Guided Approach Using ROS MCP Server
authors: Vaasantha Sri Surya K, Bhavya Singh, Kanchamreddy Suresh, Vishnu Sekhar R, Seenu Nagarajan, T. Sudalai Muthu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The combination of large language models (LLMs) and collaborative robots is a paradigm shift in human-robot interaction because it allows easy control of large robots using natural language, and increased environmental perception. This paper proposes a single architecture that combines a large language model and a collaborative robot via a Model Context Protocol (MCP)-based system with depth-sensing vision used to manipulate objects. The system also has a spatial alignment mechanism that is used to align the perception module with the robotic workspace. Based on the introduction of Robot Operating System (ROS) as the middleware layer, smooth communication can be achieved between the command interpreter based on the LLM and the robot controller. Experimental validation proves the fact that the system can correctly read complex natural language commands and perform the related manipulation actions with high precision. It uses the architecture to provide real-time perception of the environment, localize objects, and run executable tasks adaptively, with safety constraints that collaborative robotics necessarily carry. Findings show that the cognitive reasoning functionality of LLMs has been successfully intertwined with the physical performance of tasks, with an average interpretation accuracy of command instructions of 94.3 percent and a record success rate of 91.7 percent of task performance. The work contributes to the current state of the assistive robotics through the offer of a scalable model towards the implementation of intelligent and language-driven robotic systems in the real-life setting.

title: The Rise of Mobile-First Financial Ecosystems through Telecom and Fintech Integration
authors: Giri Hallur, Prasanna Kulkarni, P Ashok
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The convergence of telecommunications and financial technology is leading a wave of innovation catalysed by state-of-the-art infrastructures like 5G, blockchain and artificial intelligence. The ultra-fast low-latency capabilities of 5G allow real-time monetary exchange as well as analytics and blockchain provides unalterable, transparent peer-to-peer settlement mechanisms and smart contracts. Concurrently, AI provides anticipatory models for fraud detection and tailored services to promote nascent applications in mobile banking, Internet-of-Things-enabled payments as well as the decentralized finance platforms. Digital payment services are designed to ensure a frictionless means of money exchange between corporations, individuals and financial organisations and protect data integrity and confidentiality using rigorous encryption and authentication protocols throughout the life-cycle of transaction interactions. Modern payment architectures combine mobile apps, QR codes, and near-field communication to allow users to engage in transactions from any of their devices, such as PCs, tablets, and smartphones. The use of credit and debit instruments, mobile wallets, and peer to peer transfers in these ecosystems further emphasize the benefits of transactional fluidity and therefore increases the efficacy of the entire flow of money in the contemporary digital economy, driving the need for financial inclusivity. The increase in digital payments and mobile governance in India has significantly increased the impact on financial inclusion, government effectiveness as well as civic participation. The country is well on its way to achieve a cashless society and has been expanding its public service delivery by the development of innovations such as Unified Payments Interface (UPI) and Smartphone-based applications. These efforts are building simultaneously on digital empowerment and socioeconomical meaning. This scholarly work raises questions about the areas of digital payment services and governance and explain how the maturation of telecommunications technologies has simplified and also redefined these crucial fields.

title: Design and Implementation of a Dual-Input Boost Converter with a State-Space MPPT Controller
authors: Harman Preet Singh Sandhu, Jie Sheng, Thillainathan Logenthiran
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: This paper presents the design, testing, and implementation of a dual-input boost converter with a state-space Maximum Power Point Tracking controller. The system accepts input from solar panels as the primary source and USB as a secondary source. Key features of our system include automated input source selection, current and voltage monitoring, battery charging, PWM-based implementation of a Maximum Power Point Tracking controller, and a custom-designed boost converter. The system was simulated using PLECS, with state-space modeling performed in MATLAB. PCB board was designed to manage all components and reduce interference. Experimental results demonstrated a stable 5V output under varying charging conditions.

title: Machine Learning–Based Access Quality Prediction and Trigger-Based Mitigation in Wi-Fi Networks
authors: Mingjun Lu, Shoudong Lu, Zhiwei Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Wi-Fi access quality can fluctuate under dynamic traffic and interference. This paper studies access quality prediction and trigger-based mitigation in a trace-driven setting using AWID3 Evil Twin Wi-Fi traces. Packet records are aggregated into 10-second windows, where throughput is modeled as payload-byte rate and packet loss is approximated by retry statistics. After stability and payload-aware filtering, we compare RF, XGBoost, and SVR for next-window throughput prediction; RF achieves the best accuracy. Using predicted throughput as an early-warning signal, prediction-driven triggering reduces throughput shortfall and improves lower-tail throughput compared with a reactive baseline with one-window control delay. These results indicate that prediction-enabled triggering can mitigate unfavorable windows under this trace setting.

title: A Hierarchical Virtual Machine Scheduling Algorithm for Multi-Zone Heterogeneous Environments
authors: Xiao-ming Hu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Large-scale cloud deployments increasingly span multiple geographic zones whose hardware capabilities and pricing structures diverge substantially, rendering uniform scheduling policies inadequate. Existing VM placement mechanisms address either cost or load balance in isolation, leaving a gap for joint multi-objective optimization. We propose MZHSA, a two-stage hierarchical scheduler that separates zone-level admission from host-level placement. At the upper stage, candidate zones are ranked by a weighted composite of normalized price, current utilization, and residual headroom; the lower stage resolves host selection by jointly minimizing post-placement fragmentation and occupancy imbalance. Simulation across 1000-request workload traces shows that MZHSA cuts average provisioning cost by 21.5% over round-robin baselines while simultaneously reducing load-variance by 39% and sustaining sub-millisecond scheduling latency at scale.

title: Skin Lesion Classification for Melanoma Detection Using Pretrained CNN Architectures
authors: Ch Amarnatha Sarma, P. Sree Lakshmi, S. Dinakar Jose, L. R. Sujithra, M. K. Prathiba, M. V. Subramanyam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The prognosis for melanoma patients greatly depends on how quickly the disease is detected. Automated diagnosis from dermoscopic images however continues to be a computer vision challenge due to the complex multi-class intra and inter-class variations. In this work, we present a framework for the classification of dermoscopic skin lesion images for melanoma using different convolutional network (CNN) architectures. Our method combines lesion-specific preprocessing and feature enhancement, and multiple pretrained CNNs backs for transfer learning. Our approach is empirically tested on the ISIC 2018 dermoscopic image datasets, and we propose an ensemble-based fine-tuning strategy to classifications that are superior to existing state-of-the-art methods. Our model has an accuracy of 96.84 %, a sensitivity of 97.92 %, a specificity of 95.73 %, and an AUC of 0.984. Furthermore, our model is simple to implement and demonstrates non-oscillatory convergence, low number of missed detections, and generalized to novel/non-previously used datasets. Our experiments proved that a combination of pretrained CNN and our task-specific optimizations developed the most reliable melanoma detection. Our approach is rapid, simple, and has the potential to be integrated into real-world automated skin cancer detection systems.

title: Electromechanical Coupling Modeling of Piezoelectric-Electromagnetic Vibration Energy Harvesters Under Transmission Line Micro-Wind Vibration
authors: Shikai Zeng, Sheng Ye, Zhenyu Zi, Yeming Lai, Hongxiao Yang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To meet the self-powered demand of online monitoring equipment on transmission lines under micro-wind vibration conditions, an electromechanical coupling model of a piezoelectric–electromagnetic vibration energy harvester (PEVEH) is established. A mechanical vibration model combined with a mechanical-electrical equivalent circuit model is adopted to describe the vibration dynamics of the system. Based on this model, the electromagnetic conversion model and the piezoelectric conversion model are derived respectively. The electromagnetic part is analyzed in terms of induced voltage, load current, and output electrical power, while the piezoelectric part is described using an equivalent electromechanical circuit model to characterize the relationship between strain, voltage, and output power. The damping characteristics, frequency response, and power output mechanism of the coupled system are theoretically analyzed. Furthermore, the output power of four different configurations of piezoelectric-electromagnetic cantilever beams covering an energy harvesting bandwidth of 10 Hz to 50 Hz were verified through finite element analysis. The results demonstrated potential application prospects of the PEVEH in transmission line monitoring systems for recharging low-power wireless sensors.

title: 3A Novel EnvAdaptive-CFAR Radar Target Detection Method in Extreme Environments
authors: Yaxuan Zhao, Hao Wu, Xuan Zhu, Xun Ni, Shiao Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: To address the issue of low target detection accuracy of the conventional constant false alarm rate (CFAR) target detection algorithm in the extreme clutter environment, this paper proposes a novel environment-adaptive CFAR algorithm in extreme environment. The environment is evaluated across four dimensions to derive an environment-adaptive false alarm rate threshold, which is input the CFAR algorithm to improve detection accuracy in different environments. Then, the density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN) is used to optimize the position of the target center. Experimental results show that the accuracy and F1 score of EnvAdaptive-CFAR are 10.5% and 8.5% higher than that of the conventional CFAR algorithm, respectively. The proposed method enhances robust target detection in extreme environments and thus serves as a critical supplement for developing highly reliable radar systems.

title: Adasa: Cross-Domain Deepfake Detection Using Multi-Stream Multimodal Fusion
authors: Hessa Almaazmi, Abdulrahman Alblooshi, Manar Alkhatib
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: The rapid advancement of generative models, including GANs and diffusion-based architectures, has significantly increased the realism and accessibility of synthetic imagery, posing serious challenges to media authenticity, digital forensics, and cybersecurity. Existing deepfake detection approaches, particularly those relying on single-modality representations, often exhibit limited generalization, with performance degrading substantially when evaluated on unseen generative models. In this paper, we propose Adasa, a reliability-oriented deepfake detection framework that integrates spatial, spectral, temporal, and forensic metadata representations through a progressive multi-stream architecture. The proposed method introduces a novel 39-channel frequency representation, capturing complementary spectral artifacts via FFT magnitude and phase, DCT-II coefficients, frequency-band decomposition, and wavelet transforms. In addition, we formulate a multi-scale temporal modeling strategy using a bidirectional CNN–LSTM architecture, enabling the detection of cross-scale inconsistencies in static images, a largely unexplored direction in deepfake forensics. To ensure transparent performance attribution and systematic validation, we further design a four-phase progressive evaluation methodology, isolating the contribution of each architectural component. Extensive experiments conducted on large-scale datasets exceeding 500,000 images demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves 99.65% accuracy and 0.9998 ROC-AUC, while maintaining 99.26% fake recall, a critical metric in security-sensitive applications. More importantly, Adasa exhibits strong cross-domain generalization, achieving 99.43% accuracy on Kaggle-140k and improving performance on the challenging CIPLab dataset by 18.3 percentage points without fine-tuning. To support real-world deployment, we introduce a dual-model strategy, comprising a lightweight CNN–LSTM variant for edge environments and a high-capacity fusion model for forensic analysis. Overall, this work advances the state of the art in deepfake detection by demonstrating that multi-stream multimodal fusion, combined with progressive validation, provides a robust and scalable solution for reliable cross-domain detection, bridging the gap between laboratory performance and real-world applicability.

title: Initial Development of Fault Tolerant Agricultural Robotic System using ROS2
authors: Balasubramaniy Chandrasekaran, Vu Thien Nhi Do
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: This paper presents the initial development of an autonomous robotic system built on ROS 2, beginning with early prototyping on a Raspberry Pi and later transitioning to NVIDIA Jetson Orin for GPU-accelerated performance. We outline the motivation for adopting ROS 2, describe the preliminary system architecture, and present our planned migration toward Isaac ROS–based perception pipelines on Jetson hardware to enable real-time, AI-driven robotic behavior. This is research in progress.

title: Nyaya Panchayats in India: A Study of Their Role, Effectiveness, and Future Prospects
authors: Nirmal Kumawat, Nidhi Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Nyaya Panchayats represent India’s experiment with localized justice delivery, functioning as community-based forums under the Panchayati Raj system. Designed to provide accessible, affordable, and culturally sensitive justice, these bodies primarily address minor civil and criminal disputes in rural areas, thereby reducing pressure on the formal judiciary. This paper critically examines the historical trajectory, institutional framework, and functional dynamics of Nyaya Panchayats while situating them within the broader context of alternative dispute resolution (ADR). It highlights their advantages in promoting grassroots democracy and quick justice delivery but also exposes persistent challenges, including caste and gender bias, limited legal expertise, political interference, and weak enforcement mechanisms. The study further compares Nyaya Panchayats with Lok Adalats and Gram Nyayalayas, showing both overlaps and divergences. Drawing on constitutional provisions, legislative initiatives, and policy debates, the paper suggests reforms such as legal training for members, digital integration, and stronger oversight systems to enhance their credibility. It argues that while Nyaya Panchayats embody the promise of participatory and decentralized justice, meaningful reforms are required to align them with principles of fairness, inclusivity, and rule of law in contemporary India.

title: A Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization Approach for SVM Based Islanding Detection in Microgrids
authors: Ramswarup Kumar, U K Sinha, Jitendra Kumar, Pintu Kumar, Thakur Pranav Kumar Gautam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: In this study, a Support Vector Machine (SVM) enhanced through Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization is employed for intelligent islanding detection in microgrids. The method is validated on the IEEE 13-bus microgrid system using real-time measurements of voltage, frequency, and power under various load and disturbance scenarios. Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization automatically selects optimal SVM parameters (kernel, C, gamma, and degree), significantly improving classification accuracy compared to conventional manual tuning. The optimized SVM achieves fast detection and clear separation between islanding and non-islanding events, making it suitable for reliable real-time operation. Additionally, the accuracy of training and testing for the microgrid islanding detection problem is evaluated between the Bayesian-optimized SVM and a decision tree-based method.

title: Gen AI - Enhanced IIOT System for Automated Carbon Monitoring and Hazard Prediction
authors: R. Prabha, Karishma M, Kevin Nashmith P, Lithish K, Nandeeswaran C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Industrial carbon emissions are a critical contributor to environmental degradation and public health hazards, demanding the development of intelligent monitoring and control strategies. This work presents an AI-enabled carbon capture and monitoring system that integrates embedded IoT technology with advanced machine learning for real-time industrial emission management. The system is built around the ESP32 microcontroller and employs key environmental sensors—MQ135 for air quality, DHT11 for temperature and humidity, and a flame sensor for safety detection. Sensor data is continuously collected and transmitted to the ThingSpeak Cloud, enabling seamless mobile-based remote monitoring. A Python-based machine learning model processes the data to detect, classify, and predict pollution levels with high accuracy. When emissions exceed predefined thresholds, the system issues early alerts, facilitating timely preventive and corrective actions. By combining edge computing with cloud-based analytics, the system provides a scalable, cost-effective, and proactive solution for sustainable carbon management and enhanced industrial safety. This integration demonstrates the potential of AI-driven IoT frameworks in supporting data-driven environmental decision-making and advancing smart industrial practices.

title: A Unified Big Data Analytics Framework using AutoML and Deep Learning for Real-time Business Intelligence
authors: Varsha Mittal, Samariddin Makhmudov, Kunduz Bakdurdiyeva, Shakhista Allamova, Momogul Ismailova, Sindor Sapaev
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: This paper presents an artefact-centric analytics framework that reconciles predictive utility, low-latency inference, and auditable traceability for real-time business intelligence (BI). Modern BI systems increasingly require low-latency, auditable predictive analytics but suffer from gaps between offline model development and production serving—caused by feature-parity breaks, schema drift, tail latency, and weak KPI → exemplar traceability. Our design couples a parity-preserving feature-fabric (materialised Delta views) with a constrained AutoML with multi-fidelity search and a compilation/distillation pipeline producing registry-tracked ONNX/TVM artefacts. A gated serving policy and a distilled fast-path reconcile ensemble-quality decisions with median/tail-latency budgets, while provenance capture and schema/version governance enable KPI → exemplar traceability and auditable rollbacks. Evaluation on a production-like BI workload demonstrates that the AutoML-selected ensemble achieves F1 = 0.768, while the distilled fast-path recovers F1=0.754 and meets median latency targets by limiting ensemble invocations to ⩽10%. Ablation studies show multi-fidelity evaluation reduces search cost with modest utility loss, and drift-injection experiments show automated warm-start retraining restores KPIs within a single retraining cycle (∼3–4 hours) when thresholds are exceeded. Contributions include an artifact-centric pipeline enforcing offline/online parity and traceability; a constrained AutoML plus compilation workflow that meets deployment budgets; and an operational governance stack validating automated recovery across diverse BI domains.

title: LoRa-based IoT System for Remote Phytosanitary Monitoring in Agriculture
authors: José Acerbis, Javier González, Joel Gak, Matías Miguez, Álvaro Pardo
abstract: This work presents a proof-of-concept system designed for remote phytosanitary monitoring in rural agricultural environments using LoRa technology. The aim is to detect if the plant has been infected by a pest. In this work, the more traditional approach of transmitting the whole image to a central server for analysis was implemented and compared to including local image processing using edge computing and micro AI techniques at node level. A prototype based on an ESP32-CAM and Heltec WiFi LoRa 32 (V3) was implemented, leveraging both compression and fragmentation strategies to overcome LoRa’s inherent limitations in bandwidth and data rate. The proposed system demonstrates significant reductions in energy consumption and transmission time while maintaining detection reliability when using on the edge computing, highlighting the potential of integrated edge intelligence for sustainable agriculture.

title: Design and Simulation of Automated Dual Rotor Mechanism on Solar Powered Sugarcane Harvesting
authors: Sachin S Raj, Rajendra A Kapgate, Harshal Darekar, Krishna Darade, Nikita Dongare, Sarthak Narode
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Sugarcane being the vast cultivated cash crop of India’s agricultural economy has undoubtedly one of the most difficult harvesting processes. The existing mechanisms to harvest sugarcane are costly and are not affordable by rural based farmers. In this project a novel design mechanism is suggested that would serve the purpose of harvesting with least manpower, being cost effective and serving the purpose of cutting, stripping, sorting and loading of the harvested sugarcane. The machine consists of a dual stage rotary conveyor belt mechanism built on a sturdy box-frame consisting of a dual wire-cut mechanism setup. One on the top to chop the shoot of the crop and other at the bottom for the root removal. The DC motors for powering the wire cut mechanism are run by a 9 v 9-amp battery powered through a monocrystalline solar panel while the conveyor belts run with the power sourced from the tractor. The bottom belt is responsible for the sugarcane stem and the top rack conveyor roller transports the chopped shoot leaves to discard.
