title: Handwritten Morse Code Recognition Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Jad Abbass, Karthikraj Meda Nagaraja, Sujay Grama Suresh Kumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Even after many decades, Morse code has not disappeared from practical use. Still, recognising it in handwritten form is tricky, as each person’s writing style, spacing, and stroke patterns can differ greatly. The use of deep learning techniques for this purpose has been largely underexplored. In this paper, we evaluate five convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures—standard CNN, VGG16, ResNet50, MobileNet, and MobileNetV2—to address this challenge. These architectures were chosen to represent a range of model complexities and design philosophies—from lightweight mobile networks to deep residual and classical convolutional models. Three datasets were employed: (i) a manually curated dataset collected from 17 individuals with diverse handwriting styles and varying familiarity with Morse code, (ii) an external dataset sourced from the literature, and (iii) a combined dataset comprising approximately 38,976 images with extensive data augmentation. While the standard CNN was trained from scratch, transfer learning was applied to the four established models. ResNet50 consistently achieved the highest accuracies $(95.9 \%, 92.4 \%$, and 93.0%) across the three datasets, while the standard CNN also produced competitive results $(86.3 \%, 91.6 \%$, and $89.8 \%)$, outperforming the remaining models. These highly accurate models could be particularly valuable in educational contexts, where manually correcting learners’ guesses can be time-consuming.

title: Unveiling Factors Influencing University Students’ Intention to Adopt AI-Based Teaching Assistance
authors: Idris Gautama So, Razib Chandra Chanda, Miao Ling Zhang, Anderes Gui, Syaiful Bachri, Priscilia Calista Kowirya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is reshaping higher education with its personalized teaching help fit to each student’s way of thinking. However, bringing AI into universities is still fairly limited, especially in developing nations. This study set out to discover the key factors that influence whether university students in these regions are willing to use AI-powered teaching assistance. This study conducted a quantitative survey, gathering data from 402 university students through targeted sampling. The information was analyzed using a statistical method of PLS-SEM. The Key Findings are: System Quality Matters, But Indirectly: We found that the quality of the AI system itself has a strong positive effect on both student satisfaction and the perception of service quality. However, surprisingly, it doesn't directly influence the student's overall attitude toward the AI. Service Quality is Vital: The quality of the service provided by the AI (how helpful and reliable it is) positively impacts both how satisfied students are and their overall attitude. Student satisfaction is a major predictor of both their attitude and their ultimate intention to use the AI tools. Attitude Predicts Action: A student's positive attitude strongly forecasts their behavioral intention— meaning if they like it, they plan to use it. The study suggests that effectively using of AI has significant potential for developing countries. It can be a crucial tool for solving educational challenges and dramatically improving student learning outcomes in higher education.

title: Smart Cloud and IoT Architectures for AI-Powered Personalization and Scientific Data Systems
authors: Dilip Mehendra Sharma, Manish Ravindra Sharath, Sarath Vankamardhi Nirmala Varadhi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) provides a smooth connection between individuals and items, and its integration with the cloud enhances everyday life. Forecasting in the clinical arena can assist in transforming an adaptive clinical method into an innovative one, since modern artificial intelligence (AI) technologies pervade the market. Deep learning, as a subarea of AI, has the revolutionary ability to reliably analyze enormous amounts of data at high rates, extract smart conclusions, and solve complex problems quickly. Reliable and fast medical data management for monitoring cardiac patients is critical to provide preventative treatment and prompt therapy for those in need. With the increased use of digital medical archives, developing forecasting algorithms with higher accuracy is critical for leveraging recurrent neural network (RNN) types of DL that may handle consecutive time-dependent statistics. The suggested system collects data from IoT gadgets, and digital medical statistics regarding medical conditions are saved in the cloud and used for statistical analysis. The smart cloud and IoT framework for monitoring the medical data system of heart patients developed using Bi-LSTM (bidirectional long short-term memory) exhibits an accuracy of 98.9%, which is significantly higher compared to current smart cardiovascular control systems.

title: How Low Can Medical Llms Go? an Empirical Study of Low-Bit Quantization
authors: Tanha Tasfia, Abu Kaisar Mohammad Masum, Emilien Meyer, Fabio Cumbo, Sercan Aygun
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently shown very promising results on medical tasks such as clinical question answering, report summarization, and decision support. However, their high computational cost makes them difficult to use on resource-constrained devices. Quantization reduces model size and enables lightweight deployment, yet the impact of low-bit precision on medical language understanding remains underexplored. In this work, we present a systematic empirical study of low-bit quantization for medical LLMs. We evaluate multiple 2-, 3-, and 4-bit quantization techniques across several medical natural language processing (NLP) benchmarks and report their results on task accuracy, model size, and inference efficiency. Our study focuses on evaluating different quantization methods on medical data rather than device-specific deployment; the findings show that medical LLMs can be compressed without significant performance degradation, making them strong candidates for future edge and mobile healthcare applications.

title: Blind Separation Algorithm for Convolutive Frequency-Hopping Signals Based on Dynamic Step-size IVA
authors: Le Sun, Juncheng Liu, Yu Guo, Yabo Wang, Songhu Ge, Lin Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: To address the challenges of man-made interference and multipath propagation faced by ultra-short wave frequency-hopping communication systems in complex electromagnetic environments, this paper presents a Dynamic Step-size Independent Vector Analysis algorithm (DS-IVA), based on the dynamic regulation of the learning step-size using the time-frequency matrix of source signals. By incorporating the concept of the second-order Newton method into the optimization process, the algorithm adaptively adjusts the iterative step-size, thereby significantly accelerating the convergence speed while ensuring algorithm stability. Simulation results demonstrate that the separation performance of the presented algorithm for convolutive frequency-hopping signals is significantly superior to that of the traditional Blind Source Separation (BSS) algorithm. Compared with the traditional IVA algorithm, the presented method improves the output average Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) by 1.5 dB and the average correlation coefficient by 7.1%, while reducing the average computation time by 94.74%.

title: Classic and Quantum Task-Based Intelligent Runtime for QIRs Running on Multiple QPUs
authors: Narasinga Rao Miniskar, Elaine Wong, Vicente Leyton-Ortega, Jeffrey S. Vetter, Travis S. Humble
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: High-performance computing systems are rapidly evolving into heterogeneous platforms that fuse quantum accelerators with traditional classical processing units (CPUs) and graphical processing units (GPUs). This convergence calls for runtimes capable of managing both classical and quantum workloads in a unified manner. We introduce an intelligent, task-based runtime that marries the Intelligent RuntIme System (IRIS) asynchronous scheduler with a quantum programming stack through the Quantum Intermediate Representation Execution Engine (QIR-EE). Our design allows programs written in the quantum intermediate representation (QIR) to be dispatched concurrently to a variety of back-ends, including multiple quantum simulators and nascent quantum processors, enabling genuine hybrid execution on a single node. To illustrate its practicality, we partition a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{4}$</tex>-qubit and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 0}$</tex>-qubit circuit into three sub-circuits using quantum circuit cutting via the QCut library. Each sub-circuit is simulated independently by the QIR-EE driver within IRIS, after which a classical post-processing step merges the simulation results to recover the outcome of the original full-circuit computation. This case study demonstrates how finer task granularity can enable the parallel execution and lower the simulation burden per quantum task while preserving overall accuracy, highlighting the feasibility of our hybrid approach.

title: The Convergence of AIML, Humanoid Robotics, and the Metaverse: A Framework for Next-Generation Consumer Engagement
authors: Pradeep Kumar Mishra, Mritunjay Kr. Ranjan, Ambrish Singh, Ronak Modi, Brahmmanand Sharma, Ankita Patil
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The digital transformation is evolving gradually, where artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), humanoid robots, and Metaverse are becoming equal partners to revolutionize marketing tactics and create a futuristic consumer experience. AI and ML are the technologies that throw marketers off the trail of the consumers, and still, these technologies give the marketers the clearest foresight of consumer behavior thus making customer experiences immediate and responsive. Machine learning improves the efficiencies of decisions, analyze large volumes of data and optimize advertisement placements, while AI-driven chatbots and virtual assistants are revolutionizing customer care by providing quick and tailored solutions. Humanoids, because of their human-like experience, are an entirely new dimension of personalization for brands to get better emotional engagement with consumers. Besides, with the help of Metaverse, the consumer can visit various brand spaces and interact with the selected products and brands in new real ways; for instance, through virtual shops or brand events, which, on the other hand, are presenting marketers with new ways of reaching out to the customer besides the physical world. This paper addresses these technologies and the impact of their interaction on consumers and illustrates how businesses can utilize these technologies for more effective personalized marketing and data assimilation. The amalgamation of AI and ML with humanoid robotics and the Metaverse not only changes the marketing features but also radically changes the conventional marketing strategies and paradigms, thus the consumer is still the one who connects with the brand.

title: Three-Vector MPCC-LADRC Cascade Control Strategy and Performance Analysis for Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors
authors: Ye Gu, Chao Dong, Song Cai
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) are widely used in industrial servos, new energy vehicles and other fields due to high efficiency, high power density and fast dynamic response, and their control performance directly determines the stability and reliability of the drive system. To solve the problems of slow dynamic response, large torque ripple in the current loop and insufficient disturbance rejection ability of the speed loop in traditional PMSM control schemes, especially under load disturbances and parameter perturbations, this paper proposes a dual-loop cooperative control strategy combining three-vector model predictive current control (three-vector MPCC) and linear active disturbance rejection control (LADRC). The three-vector MPCC is applied in the current loop to reduce current harmonics and torque ripple while improving current tracking and dynamic response, and the LADRC adopted in the speed loop can estimate and compensate internal and external disturbances in real time via a linear extended state observer, resulting in strong disturbance rejection and robustness without requiring an accurate motor model.

title: Model of a Linear NEMS Comb Drive Accelerator for MAC Operations
authors: Owen O'Connor, Tarek Elfouly
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: With the growing need for energy-efficient accelerators in edge computing systems, conventional transistor-based designs are no longer an attractive solution due to their high energy consumption. Two alternative technologies that can address this limitation are micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS) and the closely related nano-electromechanical systems (NEMS). This paper introduces a mathematical model showing how MEMS and NEMS comb drives could perform multiply and accumulate (MAC) operations, a crucial first step in demonstrating their viability for co-processors in embedded computing.

title: Optimization of Material Allocation in a Dedicated Storage System Using a Linear Programming Model
authors: Ahmed Raecky Baihaqy, Nurhadi Siswanto, Tazkia Mahda Ummil Husna, Mutyara Shafa Tsany
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Warehouse is one of the most vital components in overall supply chain activities. Warehouse operation costs are contributed from four main activities: receiving, holding, picking, and shipping. Recognizing that order picking accounts for up to 50% of warehouse operational costs, the objective of this research is to minimize material handling and transportation expenses. The proposed solution employs a linear programming (LP) model, adapted for a system operating under a dedicated storage policy. The model's key parameters integrate the distance between storage locations, and the input/output (I/O) point with the measured frequency of material flow. This optimization approach, aided by LINGO software, resulted in a 69% reduction in total cost compared to the existing system, indicating a substantial technical improvement in warehouse material allocation efficiency.

title: Federated Learning and Differential Privacy Preserve for Precise Disease Prediction
authors: Koushik Sundar, Ravikumar S, Babu R, Eugene Berna I, Vijay K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: A new phase in biological and clinical research has come with machine learning and pattern-recognition-based systems, which have shown complex interaction networks between clinical parameters in demanding human diseases. Throughout this task it has been made clear that AI algorithms are now responsible for critical predictions in relation to disease susceptibility given some values on different factors such as gene expressions. But the requirement for training at a scale needs to be reconciled with medical organizations’ concerns about data privacy. It is inspired by federated learning, a scheme designed to protect data confidentiality in which neural networks are trained locally at various locations and the knowledge is pooled together to produce a global model. We incorporate the federated learning framework with differential privacy in our novel diabetes prediction scenario. The process includes shaping the data to acquire an artificial federated learning environment and applying the training algorithm on server and client sides. The TensorFlow-Privacy library also incorporates differential privacy, a technique for protecting sensitive data by selectively adding noise This study employs traditional performance metrics to assess the power of prediction from our model as a balancing act between accuracy and privacy. These comparisons show FL's advantage in maintaining privacy, sharing data, scalability, system, and communication efficiency, and regulatory compliance in comparison with traditional ML methods. Federated learning shows the potential to revolutionize disease prediction by enabling the medical community (doctors, academics, and data scientists) to concentrate on privacy-preserving predictive models for clinical decision-support systems despite interpretability issues and communications delays. The extent to which AI research now includes discussion of federated learning’s potential to transform the moral privacy-protecting engaging AI ecosystems becomes more evident.

title: Trajectory Optimization of AUV for Oil Spill Monitoring Based on ConvLSTM Environmental Prediction*
authors: Hanhui Fu, Jicheng Ding, Yi Duan, Chuanqi Li
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) are core equipment for marine environmental monitoring. Efficient and safe navigation in complex dynamic marine environments is key for oil spill emergency response. Traditional AUV trajectory optimization based on static environmental models cannot adapt to spatiotemporal variability of marine multi-physical fields, causing path failures due to eddies and density fronts. We propose a 3D trajectory optimization method integrating ConvLSTM-based spatiotemporal prediction. A ConvLSTM-3D network predicts key physical fields (density, flow) for early perception of severe conditions. Predicted fields are quantified into communication, safety, and timeliness cost functions, then integrated into an improved Genetic Algorithm for optimal path planning. Simulation results for oil spill monitoring show the proposed algorithm significantly improves path safety and mission efficiency, reducing comprehensive path cost by 32.6% compared to static methods.

title: Spatio-Temporal Analysis of Traffic Accidents in Bahrain using Geographic Information System
authors: Uneb Gazder
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The objective of this paper is to design and develop a Geodatabase and analyze spatial and temporal fatal traffic accidents in the Kingdom of Bahrain utilizing Geographic Information Systems (GIS) as a tool. The data was collected from the General Traffic Department of the Ministry of Interior from the years 2012 to 2018. The analysis involved spatio-temporal analysis and mapping of the fatal traffic accidents. The analysis identified both persistent high-risk locations as well as the temporal shift in the hotspot locations towards the northern part of the country, demonstrating the advantage of spatio-temporal analysis over the traditional static methods. A primary example of recurrent high-risk location was node 286 at Dry Dock Highway and Hidd Avenue, and Shaikh Jaber A. AlSubah Highway. These results provide evidence-based insights for targeted interventions and guide the future direction of research towards the understanding of underlying causal factors behind these spatial dynamics.

title: Design of a Low-Noise Wideband Preamplifier Based on Multiple Discrete Current Sources
authors: Kun Guan, Xulei Han, Qidi Sun, Meitao Zhai, Limin Zhang, Xiaofeng Yi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: In borehole transient electromagnetic (BTEM) detection, the received signals are extremely weak and broadband, making them highly susceptible to intrinsic circuit noise and low-frequency interference. To address these challenges, a low-noise wideband preamplifier based on a multi-discrete current source scheme is proposed. The preamplifier employs a four-stage cascaded architecture, including a junction field-effect transistor (JFET) differential input stage with independent current source biasing to suppress device parameter dispersion, a second-order high-pass filter for low-frequency interference rejection, a low-noise operational amplifier stage for signal amplification, and a second-order low-pass filter for bandwidth limitation and high-frequency noise suppression. Theoretical analysis and parameter optimization are conducted for each stage. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves an input-referred noise voltage below 2.00 nV/√Hz over a bandwidth of 150 Hz to 65 kHz, with a passband gain of approximately 60 dB. The proposed preamplifier satisfies the requirements of borehole TEM systems and offers an effective approach to enhancing detection depth and resolution.

title: A Hybrid Monkey Spider Optimization Approach for Economic Load Dispatch with Renewable Energy
authors: Pitchala Vijaya Kumar, Aruna Jeyanthy P, Shilaja C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Conventional methods struggle with a nonlinearity, a non-convexity, and a complexity in the renewable energy sources. Hence, in this paper, we propose a novel hybrid optimization method for solving the ELD problems in the power systems that use both the wind and solar energy as well as other renewable sources. This strategy named Hybrid Monkey Spider Optimization (HMSO) tends to combine the the Monkey Algorithm (MA) and Spider Monkey Optimization (SMO). The hybridization makes it easier to refine things locally and search for things globally. We test the suggested HMSO algorithm no IEEE 30-bus and 118-bus systems. The results show that the proposed HMSO lowers the generation costs, speed up convergence, and a better deal with the renewable energy sources.

title: From Isolation to Integration: Road Infrastructure Development and Economic Growth of Eastern and North-Eastern States of India
authors: Sumit Oberoi, Pooja Kansra, Gagan Kukreja, Sugandh Arora
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Road infrastructure is a major component of economic development particularly in the less served areas where the lack of connectivity and governmental presence are found. This paper focuses on the relationship of improvement of road infrastructure with economic development in Eastern and North-Eastern India. This study using the panel data from year 2001 to 2022 investigates the bidirectional and unidirectional relationship between road infrastructure and the GDP in the Granger causality relationship. The data indicates notable differences across various regions. In the East, there is a reciprocal relationship between road infrastructure and economic activity, while in the North-East, it is economic growth that propels infrastructure development. This underscores the need for increased financial resources and customised strategies. The findings suggest that improvements to national road infrastructure yield long-term advantages, despite any short-term effects. Analyses suggest that initiatives like the Bharatmala Pariyojna contribute to diminishing regional disparities and promote sustainable development. It promotes tailored policies for specific regions, continuous investments, and fair distribution of resources to improve connectivity and foster economic growth across the diverse areas of India. The findings will assist decision makers, legislators or interested parties in using infrastructure for equitable development.

title: AI-Powered Circular Economy: Towards More Sustainable and Efficient Industrial Operations
authors: Ankit Garg, Sarjue Pandita, Parth Balyan, DR.R. Srikala, Dr. Ravi Prakash, Dr. Neha Verma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This research examines the use of circular economy strategies—namely closed-loop systems and economic decoupling—within major industrial sectors such as refining, renewable energy, and electric vehicles. It uses a mixed-methods methodological approach, combining qualitative case studies with quantitative modeling through the Brazilian Land-Use Model for Energy Scenarios (BLUES) and the ARIMA forecast method, to evaluate the effect of circular practices on resource productivity and environmental sustainability. Additionally, artificial intelligence models based on neural networks were used to optimize process efficiency, distinguishing them from purely statistical predictive approaches. The outlook is encouraging: waste generation will fall by 20 to 25%, and recycling performance may increase from 50% to 83% within the next decade. The validity of these forecasts was verified, with measurement of errors such as MAPE and RMSE showing that the deviation between predictions and occurrence was below 5%. These findings illustrate the ways in which circular economy methods can assist us in consuming fewer new materials, reducing carbon emissions, and illustrate the need for technology and laws to continue to evolve. Companies can gain valuable insights that assist them in growing sustainably today and ensure that resources are responsibly utilized for generations to come by integrating these approaches.

title: AI-Powered Smart Defect Detection System for Printed Circuit Boards
authors: Vijay K, Sharukesh M, Shrinithi S, Sivabalan T
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In electronics manufacturing companies PCB defect detection is needed to see the quality of products whether it is good. basically in which this process is done by people and machine in which this takes so much time and also it is expensive. So overcome this problem ,we have implemented Smart Defect Detection System that can detect the PCB issue by using AI. In which we have used two models YOLOv11, MobileNet. YOLOv11 model is used for detecting the surface area problem like missing components, misalignment parts, solder bridges. MobileNet Model is basically used for checking the internal circuit patterns, in which small and hidden defects can be detected. These two models are combined with OpenCV and python to make a low cost and efficient inspection tool. In which it includes Graphical User Interface(GUI) so that non technical user can also able to upload the PCB images and can see the defected issues in the screen. This helps mainly for small electronic manufacturing companies where we can reduce their cost. In increases accuracy, reducing human errors and in which it makes the inspection process faster and reliable. the experiment result shows it is a good balance between performance, cost, flexibility, making it suitable for real time application.

title: Optimizing End-to-End Communication Latency in UAV-Satellite Integrated Networks
authors: Anamika Kumari, Mannat Thakur, Akash Singh, Chandra Mohan, Prince Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The integration of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) with satellite communication systems has been identified as an effective solution in providing communication services in a reliable manner in environments that are difficult to access. However, the high end-to-end latency is a major problem in the integration of satellite communication systems with UAVs due to the high satellite propagation delay. In this research, a novel latency-aware communication framework is proposed with the main purpose of optimizing the end-to-end delay in the integration of UAVs and satellite communication systems. In this paper, the proposed communication framework is presented, and the results obtained in the simulation of the proposed communication framework using network simulation tools are presented. In the simulation, the proposed communication framework was evaluated using different UAV density and mobility conditions. In the simulation results, the proposed communication framework was observed to have reduced the average end-to-end delay from 420 ms to 285 ms, the packet delivery ratio was improved from 86.2% to 94.6%, and the throughput was improved from 1.8 Mbps to 2.6 Mbps.

title: A Data Driven Approach to Analyze Crime Data of Los Angeles
authors: Abdallah Al Naboulsi, Mohamed Abdallah Nour, Saadat Mehmood Alhashmi
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: Crime analysis and prediction research is essential in order to improve safety in the digital era. This study utilizes Los Angeles crime data to investigate how victim age, gender, and ethnicity vary across certain types of violent crime. This paper adopts a comparative quantitative approach to violent crime types, and it does so by applying Binary Logistic Regression Models. The analysis models victim characteristics as the outcomes and crime types as the predictors. First, the results show that victim ages differ meaningfully across crime types. Second, the findings reveal variation in gender across offenses. Third, the analysis showed clear variation in ethnicities across different offenses. Finally, these results highlight the significance of treating violent crime types as their own separate categories since treating the offenses as one uniform category may hide meaningful patterns in the demographic. This paper should meaningfully contribute by coordinating police resources and efforts with certain victimization patterns and strengthening victim support services for victim groups that are more likely to be implicated in particular offense types.

title: A Programmable CRISPR-Phage Strategy to Disable Antibiotic Resistance and Biofilm Formation in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
authors: Mariam Khalid, Marah Altabbal
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: Antibiotic resistance has become an impending global crisis in modern medicine, and Pseudomonas aeruginosa is recognized by the World Health Organization (WHO) as a critical priority pathogen. In its published list of antibiotic-resistant bacteria requiring urgent development of new antibiotics, P. aeruginosa ranks among the highest due to its prevalence, the difficulty of treating it in clinical settings, and its prominent role in nosocomial infections, notably ventilator-associated pneumonia, bloodstream infections, and chronic lung disease in cystic fibrosis patients. This bacterium uses overlapping layers of defense that include β-lactamase production, efflux pumps, changes in porins, and quorum-sensing systems such as lasI/lasR that regulate biofilm formation, which can make the bacteria up to a thousand times more tolerant of antibiotics than planktonic cells. Current therapeutic options from β-lactam/inhibitor combinations and phage cocktails to quorum-sensing inhibitors are gradually losing efficacy, as each addresses only one aspect of resistance without fully eliminating resistance determinants or preventing biofilm maturation. Engineered bacteriophages with dual functions, bacterial lysis and CRISPR-based disruption of targets such as ampC and lasI, could therefore provide a comprehensive approach to counter both antibiotic resistance and biofilm development, paving the way for more effective treatments against multidrug-resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Accordingly, the objective of this study is to evaluate whether bacteriophage-mediated delivery of a dual CRISPR–Cas9 system targeting ampC and lasI can simultaneously enhance β-lactam antibiotic susceptibility and reduce biofilm formation in Pseudomonas aeruginosa.

title: An enhanced Lightweight Key Synchronization scheme for Secure IoT Communication in smart cities
authors: A. AbhdulKalam, M.Arul Pugazhendhi, Ananth Kumar Tamilarasan, Saikat Gochhait, Zafrul Allam
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: As the Internet of Things (IoT) continues to evolve rapidly, establishing secure communication between devices becomes essential, not just to guard sensitive data, but to uphold user privacy and prevent any form of unauthorized access. With IoT touching everything from homes to industries, the need for strong yet efficient protection has never been more urgent. Home automation to industrial automation requires cryptographic algorithms to be robust and lightweight enough to run effectively on low-end devices. Traditional encryption techniques typically incur significant computational overhead, making them unsuitable for low-power Internet of Things devices designed for these constrained environments. In this paper, an innovative lightweight real-time key synchronization and encryption method is proposed. The envisaged lightweight security framework is demonstrated through an exemplary smart door lock application scenario, which can be considered a typical case representing secure communication across constrained IoT devices. The proposed system employs elliptic curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) to generate temporary shared keys, thus ensuring forward secrecy and eavesdropping immunity. ChaCha20-Poly1305, a high-speed, low-memory, and low-latency authenticated encryption algorithm, is employed to secure data transfer after setting up the keys. A socket-based client-server protocol is implemented in Python to allow real-time key exchange and secure transmission of command.

title: DCS-Based Coal Feed Control for Variable-Load Power Units
authors: Wenlong Ma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Variable-load operation of coal-fired power units is strongly affected by fluctuations in coal quality, which make accurate coal feed control difficult under conventional design-based fuel scheduling. To address this issue, this paper proposes a DCS-based coal feed control method for variable-load power units. The method estimates the calorific value of the as-fired coal online from unit load, coal feed rate, and the load-dependent coal consumption relation. A representative target heating value is then obtained through time-window-based signal processing and steady-state discrimination. To preserve closed-loop stability, the target value is not directly imposed on the control loop but gradually assigned to the control heating value under a pressure-deviation-constrained updating logic. Based on the updated heating value, the target coal feed rate is calculated in real time for a new load demand. The method provides a practical feedforward compensation mechanism for coordinated control systems and improves the rapidity, accuracy, and stability of coal feed control under variable-load and variable-coal-quality conditions.

title: Study on the Detection and Tracking of Broken Pavement Using YOLO Based on Retinex Image Processing
authors: Che-Ping Hung, Hong-Yu Lin, Kuo-Yang Tu, Wei-Xiang Huang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: In this paper, the detection and tracking of broken pavement based on Retinex image processing are designed and implemented. In traditional methods, there are some problems in the detection systems of broken pavement, such as the detection influenced by environmental lighting changes, and unstable identification when the system gets closed to broken target. In aspect of detection, Multi Scale Retinex (MSR) with color restoration is engaged to enhance training images. This method improves the problem of training model influenced easily by lighting and shadow changes. In addition, YOLO and Deep SORT are combined to boost the identification stability because of approaching broken targets.In this paper, the experiment scheme is divided into three parts. In the first part, images of broken pavement taken at different speeds are used to train a YOLO model, and the most suitable model for target identification is selected. In the second part, Retinex image processing is applied to enhance the training data set’s image which is obscure due to severe light and shadow’s changes and re-training the model. In addition, the video of lower motorcycle speed used to demonstrate the identification influenced by speed is also included. In the third part, the detected broken pavement is tracked through Deep SORT to promote the identification stability and to result in different Track ID

title: Intelligent Human Detection for Efficient Disaster Response
authors: Ravindra Ghugare, Sayali Datir, Madiha Diwan, Sanika Kadam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Search and rescue operations are essential for saving lives in the aftermath of natural and man-made disasters. This project presents the Intelligent Rescue Navigator, an innovative system aimed at improving the detection of individuals trapped in disaster-affected areas. By modernizing traditional rescue methods into real-time operations, this system harnesses advanced technologies, including artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). The Intelligent Rescue Navigator utilizes AI algorithms alongside high-resolution cameras and thermal sensors mounted on UAVs to locate individuals in challenging environments. Our methodology incorporates a diverse array of datasets to train deep learning models capable of recognizing human shapes and movements, even when individuals are concealed by debris or in low-light conditions. The UAVs are designed for autonomous navigation, enabling efficient coverage of both extensive and intricate areas. Furthermore, the system includes a comprehensive communication interface that relays real-time information and GPS coordinates of detected individuals to ground rescue teams, thereby improving response times and resource allocation efficiency.

title: STAMICS: Splat, Track and Map with Integrated Consistency and Semantics for Dense RGB-D SLAM
authors: Weiyun Yi, Yongxu Wang, Xu Cao, Zhaoxin Fan
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is a critical task in the field of robotics, enabling systems to autonomously navigate and understand complex environments. Current SLAM methods predominantly rely on geometric cues for localization and mapping, but they often struggle to ensure semantic consistency, particularly in dynamic or densely populated scenes. To address this limitation, we propose a novel method called STAMICS, which integrates semantic information with 3D Gaussian representations to enhance the accuracy of localization and mapping. STAMICS consists of three key components: a 3D Gaussian-based scene representation that combines semantic information with 3D Gaussian splatting to ensure that semantic information is preserved and correctly aligned with scene geometry, enabling high-fidelity reconstruction; a graph-based clustering technique that strengthens temporal semantic consistency, ensuring consistent semantic information for objects across different time frames; and an open-vocabulary system that allows the classification of previously unseen objects, improving the system's adaptability to diverse and complex real-world environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate that STAMICS improves camera pose estimation and map quality, performing competitively compared to existing methods while reducing reconstruction errors. The code will be made publicly available.

title: Benchmarking the Benchmarks for Quantum Code Generation with LLMs
authors: Yunhe Feng, Xiaokun Yang, Kewei Sha, Junhua Ding
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as quantum coding copilots, assisting researchers by generating complex programs for frameworks such as Qiskit, PennyLane, and OpenQASM. However, accurately evaluating the practical utility of these models remains a significant challenge. Existing benchmarks are highly fragmented, ranging from syntax-centric unit tests to simulator- and hardware-aware metrics, making results difficult to compare across different languages and hardware targets. To address this fragmentation, this paper systematically reviews recent evaluation suites and post-training methodologies for quantum code generation. We distill current evaluation criteria into four practical dimensions: syntax and API validity, semantic and physical correctness, resource and hardware feasibility, and execution-based feedback. Building upon these dimensions, we present a unified taxonomy that maps current benchmarks to emerging model adaptation techniques, specifically retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), fine-tuning, multi-agent workflows, and reinforcement learning (RL). Our analysis highlights a clear shift: moving away from prompt-only baselines and toward agentic workflows driven by quantum-verifiable rewards. This shift directly targets the semantic gap between superficially compilable code and physically executable quantum programs. In addition, we identify key research opportunities-such as standardized semantic metrics and integration with quantum error correction-aimed at fostering a more reproducible, verifiable, and hardware-grounded ecosystem for automated quantum software development.

title: Welding Robot Selection in Automotive Seat Frame Manufacturing: Pythagorean Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process
authors: Nitidetch Koohathongsumrit, Wasana Chankham
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: The selection of an appropriate welding robot influences production quality and operational efficiency in automotive seat frame manufacturing. This study applies the Pythagorean fuzzy analytic hierarchy process (PF-AHP) to the welding robot selection problem. The method starts with pairwise comparisons of six criteria: horizontal reach, vertical reach, price suitability, precision performance, number of poles, and load handling capability. Decision-maker evaluations expressed through linguistic terms are converted into Pythagorean fuzzy numbers, which support the formation of the difference, interval multiplicative, determinacy, and pre-normalization weight matrices to calculate the criteria weights. The same computational procedure applies to six welding robot alternatives under each criterion. The final ranking results from the aggregation of the criteria weights and the weights of the alternatives. In the case study, implementation of the selected welding robot based on the PF-AHP results led to a substantial improvement in production quality, with welding-related defects reduced by up to 75%. The results provide the most appropriate welding robot that best aligns with the operational priorities of the case study, with its performance standing out across the criteria that hold the greatest influence. The proposed approach provides decision makers in industry with a structured basis for selecting welding equipment, and it also creates opportunities for further work that may involve changing production environments, the inclusion of additional evaluation factors, or the application of other multi-criteria decision methodologies.

title: Innovation in Educational Media: Development of Augmented Reality-Based Multimedia Flashcards for Introducing Yogyakarta's Local Culture
authors: Rizal Justian Setiawan, Khakam Ma’ruf, Siti Hardila, Fatimatuzzahro’, Akhmad Mukhibun, Darmono
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustaining Heritage: The Future of Conservation: Embracing Technology and Innovation for Sustainable Heritage (ICSH)
abstract: Globalization poses a significant threat to the preservation of Yogyakarta’s cultural heritage, particularly among digital-native youth who tend to favor global culture over traditional practices. Conventional learning methods, such as lectures, textbooks, and static visual media, have proven insufficient in engaging students effectively. This study develops an Augmented Reality (AR) based multimedia flashcard to address this gap, employing the ADDIE model (Analysis, Design, Development, Implementation, Evaluation) with a sample of 30 eighth-grade students from three junior high schools in Yogyakarta. The AR flashcards integrate 3D cultural objects, audio narration, quizzes, and immersive mobile interactions to enhance the learning experience. Results from pre-test and post-test assessments indicate a significant improvement in cultural understanding, with an average increase of 22.9 points. The greatest gains were observed in knowledge of the Keraton architecture, with an increase of 24.5 points, and comprehension of classical dance, with an increase of 25.2 points. User satisfaction, measured using the System Usability Scale (SUS), achieved an average score of 86.2, which is categorized as excellent usability. These findings confirm the effectiveness of AR flashcards in promoting cultural education and heritage preservation through technology, providing practical implications for educational practice and theoretical insights for strategies in digital cultural preservation.

title: Design and Optimization of Rocker Arm of Diesel Engines by Using Finite Element Method
authors: Choy Hau Yan, Shua Jun Jian, Lee Yoon Ket
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: A rocker arm is a reciprocating lever used as an internal combustion engine tool to transfer pushrod motion to the valve stem. This study investigates the failure of rocker arms in internal combustion engines and proposes seven modification ideas using Finite Element Analysis (FEA) simulation. The rocker arm model is designed using SOLIDWORKS and the material used is S-Glass Fiber. Static structural and modal analysis are performed on the modified designs to determine the best modification idea. The study explores material selection for rocker arms using four different materials - S-Glass Fiber, Aluminum Alloy 6061, Carbon Steel, and High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE). The results suggest that the best modification for static structural analysis is the model M6. S-Glass Fiber is recommended as the preferred material for rocker arms due to its high stiffness and low total deformation with higher natural frequency.

title: Depression Detection Using Social Media and Multimodal Data: Toward Explainable and Fair Deep Learning Advances
authors: Harikrishna Pydi, P. Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Depression is one of the most significant global health issues with millions of people across the globe being affected. Social media offers rich multimodal signals-linguistic, visual, audio, and behavioral-that have made ML and DL-based approaches possible for early detection. The review integrates findings from 32 papers, providing a comprehensive overview of the field. The review covers extensively used datasets (RSDD, SMHD, eRisk, DAIC-WOZ), methodologies (SVM, Random Forest, CNN, RNN, BERT, Transformers), fusion techniques, explainability, fairness, and ethical issues related to privacy. The review identifies various research gaps, suggests various future directions, and defines concrete goals for the development of efficient, fair, and explainable systems for the detection of depression. The findings have the purpose of bridging the computational progress with the field of clinical practice, with the guarantee of the efficient and ethical application of AI-based mental health solutions.

title: Lightweight and Explainable Phishing URL Detection Using Feature Selection and XGBoost
authors: Nandan Srinivasarao Vaddempudi, Dinesh Sajja, Chilukuri Phanidar, Sagar Basavaraju
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: More than 70% of the initial attacks are phishing sites that attack millions of users using fake links. Conventional detection systems, e.g., rule-based and blacklist-based systems, do not keep up with quickly changing phishing patterns, whereas deep learning models, though correct, are plagued by high computational ability and low interpretability. This paper presents the lightweight and interpretable phishing URL detection framework, which combines Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) to select the features, and Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) to deal with the imbalance between classes, as well as SHapley Additive explanations (SHAP) to understand the models. The lexical features that are proposed in the XGBoostbased framework include URL length, Digit count, Dot count, Has HTTPS among others to achieve real time detection without any reference to the content of a webpage or to external API. The model was trained using the PhiUSIIL Phishing URL Dataset (235,795 URLs) and when the model was optimized to four features, it provided 85.4% accuracy, which is close to the full-feature model (85.6%) but reduced the computation time by nearly a fourfold. Following the usage of SMOTE, the recall rose to 78% and F1-score rose to 0.558, which proved the best phishing detection using unbalanced data. The balance between accuracy, interpretability, and efficiency is high in the proposed framework.

title: A Robust Incremental Deadbeat Predictive Current Control for SPMSM With Double-Weighted Compensation
authors: Halzat Yitniyaz, Guomao Li, Jihong Zhu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper addresses the steady-state error and limited robustness of deadbeat current predictive control (DPCC) for permanent magnet synchronous motors under parameter mismatches. Incremental DPCC (IDPCC) is analyzed, showing effective suppression of resistance and flux mismatch-induced deviations but sensitivity to inductance mismatch, leading to oscillations. A dual-weighted robust incremental predictive control (RIDPCC) is proposed, enhancing stability and parameter robustness while maintaining fast dynamic response. Simulations demonstrate that RIDPCC achieves superior current tracking and effectively suppresses oscillations under various parameter mismatches, outperforming conventional DPCC and IDPCC.

title: Evaluating the Environmental and Occupational Impact of Work-From-Home Practices: A Sustainable Shift in Post-Pandemic Workforce Dynamic
authors: M. Asmath Haseena, P. Shymala
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Taking a holistic approach While this research attempts to cover the multifaceted environmental and occupational health impacts associated with work-from-home (WFH) practices in the post-pandemic workforce; it is conducted by using comprehensive lifecycles of assessment and occupational health monitoring tools. Using activity-based 573rganizat and greenhouse gas accounting schemes found that remote workers can potentially lessen their carbon footprint as much as 54% than onsite workers and hybrid options showed its carbon footprint decreased by anywhere from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1}-\mathbf{2 9 \%}$</tex> in the case where a work period contained two to four days of remote work PNAS. However, environmental advantages depend on sustainable configuration of lifestyles, the impacts of information and communication technology are still negligible at the moment when non-commute travel and home energy use are becoming significant factors GreenlyPNAS. This research incorporates carbon footprint measuring resources with virtual ergonomic evaluation resources and comprehensive well-being monitoring systems to provide evidence-based recommendations on sustainable adjustments to the rapport of hybrid workforce crew including optimizing and directive strategies in local energy policy to focus association sustainable commute 573rganizational573, facility downsizing, renewable energy usage and ahead occupational health support systems.

title: Vibration of Soft, Twisted Beams for Underactuated Quadrupedal Locomotion_supp1-3686693.mp4
authors: Daniel Aukes
abstract: Underactuated compliant robotic systems offer a promising approach to mitigating actuation and control challenges by harnessing predesigned, embodied dynamic behaviors. This article presents Flix-Walker, a novel, untethered, centimeter-scale quadrupedal robot inspired by compliant underactuated mechanisms. Flix-Walker employs flexible, helix-shaped beams as legs, which are actuated by vibrations from just two motors to achieve three distinct mobility modes. We analyze the actuation parameters required to generate various locomotion modes through both simulation and prototype experiments. The effects of system and environmental variations on locomotion performance are examined, and we propose a generic metric for selecting control parameters that produce robust and functional motions. Experiments validate the effectiveness and robustness of these actuation parameters within a closed-loop control framework, demonstrating reliable trajectory-tracking and self-navigation capabilities.

title: Deadline-Driven Deterministic Wireless Communication for Time-Sensitive Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Mikael Gidlund, Tao Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: Time-triggered industrial cyber–physical control requires decode-by communication: information arriving after a fixed deadline is control-invalid and must be discarded. Conventional feedback-based wireless reliability mechanisms (e.g., HARQ) introduce random completion times, which complicates worst-case analysis and certification in time-sensitive industrial networks. This paper presents a deadline-driven coded reliability framework that enables a 5G/6G wireless hop to operate as a deterministic decode-by service with a bounded latency contract. In each control cycle, the payload is encoded into a fixed number <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math></inline-formula> of transmissions scheduled within a reserved window that ends at a strict cut-off <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t^*$</tex-math></inline-formula>. To provision the minimal radio-time reservation for a target violation probability <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\epsilon$</tex-math></inline-formula>, we formulate an exact dynamic program under a Gilbert–Elliott loss model that maps channel evolution to cycle-level failure events. Based on this analysis, the proposed Minimal-Overhead Deterministic Scheduler (MODS) computes the smallest feasible transmission budget <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n^*$</tex-math></inline-formula> satisfying the latency–reliability contract. Trace-driven evaluation using industrial 5G/6G measurements shows that the framework achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-5}$</tex-math></inline-formula> violation probability while reducing deterministic airtime by up to 45% compared to replication-based baselines. By enforcing a fixed completion boundary, the approach eliminates stochastic latency tails by construction and enables certifiable wireless integration for TSN/DetNet systems. The results further show that decode-by performance is governed by burst-correlated losses rather than average error rates, and that efficient provisioning requires dimensioning transmission budgets against the burst-loss envelope. The proposed framework exposes an explicit latency–reliability trade-off through <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(n^*,t^*)$</tex-math></inline-formula>, enabling predictable resource allocation and scalable admission of time-sensitive flows. These properties provide a practical path toward integrating wireless links into deterministic industrial networks without introducing timing uncertainty.

title: Convolutional Estimation of Reactive Betweenness and Dependency from DC Load Flow Data
authors: Mohammad Shahraeini
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: This paper introduces a lightweight U-Net that estimates the full reactive dependency matrix D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Q</inf> from the DC dependency matrix D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dc</inf> with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> =0.985, RMSE = 0.0198, and MAE = 0.0097 on masked entries of the IEEE 14-bus system. Using a direct input skip-addition and standard MSE loss, the CNN accurately captures reactive flow mediation patterns from computationally inexpensive DC load flow inputs. Post-processing thresholding eliminates small negative artifacts, ensuring physical consistency. Results on 50 unseen scenarios confirm near-perfect reconstruction of D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Q</inf>, enabling fast reactive power flow analysis without full AC load flow. The approach offers a scalable, interpretable, and real-time AI-driven solution for modern power system analytics.

title: Integrating AI in Food Engineering Education: Raising Student Competencies in Market Research and Consumer-Driven Product Development
authors: Blanca Isabel Maldonado-Guevara, Juan José Carracedo-Navarro, Lucio López-Cavazos
abstract: New product development is vital for the competitiveness and survival of the food industry. Consequently, future Food Engineers must master Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to manage the high-dimensionality data required to understand evolving consumer needs. This study analyzed seventh-semester students who utilized ATLAS.ti software to investigate product categories through social media, scientific databases, and field research. By leveraging AI-driven sentiment analysis and pattern recognition, students processed complex information more efficiently than the control group. The results indicate that AI integration enables students to organize diverse data sources effectively and establish deeper analytical relationships in less time. Ultimately, this approach fostered clearer, more creative contextual interpretations, allowing students to design food product proposals with superior commercial viability and closer alignment with market demands.

title: Upgrading LSTM-Attention to a Transformer Decoder for Image Captioning with Object and Visual Features
authors: Nima Sharifinia, Hasti Marjaneh, Mohammad Javad Parseh, Pejman Rasti
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Accurately generating natural language descriptions of visual scenes remains a core challenge in vision–language understanding. Existing image captioning models often entangle improvements in architecture, feature extraction, and large-scale pretraining, making it difficult to isolate the contribution of each component. In this work, we present a controlled architectural study that replaces a recurrent LSTM-attention decoder with a Transformer-based decoder while preserving the same visual feature inputs. The proposed framework fuses global context features from Xception and object-level embeddings from YOLO, ordered by detection confidence to emphasize salient visual entities. This design enhances long-range dependency modeling and cross-modal attention without introducing additional semantic or relational supervision. Trained solely on the MS-COCO dataset, the model achieves substantial improvements over a strong baseline—raising CIDEr by more than 100%—and produces captions that are both syntactically fluent and semantically grounded. These findings provide a reproducible and efficient foundation for future transformer-based captioning systems.

title: Student Perceptions of Usefulness and Ease of Use of a Gamified Learning Platform in STEM Courses
authors: Guillermo M. Chans, César Merino-Soto, Ana Paula Valle-Arce, Alec Martinez-Gallardo, Angeles Dominguez, Genaro Zavala
abstract: This study explores the implementation of a webbased gamification platform, Fred!, designed to enhance student engagement and motivation in STEM higher education. Grounded in Self-Determination Theory, the platform integrates short-term, medium-term, and long-term achievements, token-based rewards, and peer collaboration. A total of 270 undergraduate students from chemistry courses participated in the study, divided into experimental (gamified) and control (traditional) groups. Two constructs-perceived usefulness and perceived ease of use-were evaluated through adapted Likert-scale items. Results from confirmatory factor analysis supported the two-factor model with high reliability across both groups. A significant difference was found in perceived ease of use, favoring the experimental group. While no global differences emerged for perceived usefulness, local analysis revealed variation at lower score percentiles. Findings suggest that gamification can be implemented effectively and perceived as easy to use when supported by a dedicated platform. Implications for broader applications and future research directions are discussed.

title: Correlation between particles and induced voids at the bonding interface
authors: Vasco Matias, Hans Billington, Soheila Bagherilimaei, Serena Iacovo, Soon Aik Chew
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Contamination particles induce voids at the bonding interfaces in wafer-to-wafer (W2W) and die-to-wafer (D2W) hybrid bonding (HB). Such bonding voids can be detrimental to the electrical properties of the devices being fabricated.In this study we have deposited W-particles with e-beam assisted deposition on the surface of SiCN-coated wafers, proceeded to bonding the wafers with a blanket counterpart and inspected the resulting bonding voids with a Scanning Acoustic Microscope.We could obtain a correlation between the sizes of the deposited particles and the respective induced voids – an amplification factor of 50x. Such correlation allows determining the sensitivity of bonding voids to the dimensions of particles at the bonding interface.

title: Low-Precision POSIT Arithmetic for Spiking Neural Networks with Kahan Summation
authors: Shayan Naghizadeh, Reza Adinepour, Morteza Saheb Zamani
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) are highly suitable for energy-efficient neuromorphic computing due to their event-driven nature. Unlike Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), where POSIT arithmetic has been used for storage saving and require decoding and encoding during each operation, SNNs process information only when events occur, minimizing the overhead. This makes POSIT arithmetic particularly advantageous for SNNs, as it avoids unnecessary operations. However, at low bit-widths such as 6 bits, accumulation errors can arise, affecting accuracy. To address this problem, we propose integrating Kahan's compensated summation algorithm into the synaptic accumulation process of neurons in SNNs, which restores loss precision and enhances the stability of low-precision SNNs. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed 6-bit POSIT format achieves 93.2% accuracy—close to 8-bit POSIT ( 95.1% ) and 32-bit floating point ( 97.6% )—while reducing LUT usage by about 27% and FF usage by about 55% compared to the 32-bit floating-point implementation, highlighting its efficiency for low-power, resource-constrained neuromorphic hardware and demonstrating its potential for next-generation neuromorphic accelerators.

title: A novel approach for controlling robotic eye movement using U-Net based model
authors: Reza Javanmard Alitappeh, Saeed Abbasi, Mohammad Reza Jafari, Mahyar Eslamian
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: This paper proposes a simplified vision-based approach to the control of robotic eye systems using deep learning. Instead of complex physical modeling and nonlinear control equations, desired eye movements are encoded as 2D images, and a U-Net encoder predicts corresponding motor control signals. We present a model that has a CNN encoder, multilayer perceptron, and smoothing module that generates six motor signals for a 6-DoF cable-driven robotic eye. Trained on 199 trajectories in simulation, a system based on the proposed approach achieved a validation loss of 0.0075 (MSE) and rendered smooth, biologically plausible movements at reduced computational cost. While there is still some residual noise, our results indicate that trajectory images can be an effective representation of motion dynamics and an efficient alternative to physics-based models. The work described here points out the potential of image-driven control to simplify robotic eye design and lays the foundation for future real-time implementations on physical hardware.

title: Intelligent Model for Identifying Defective Aircraft Parts
authors: Zohre Fasihfar, Hamidreza Rokhsati, Hossein Lotfi, Peyman Vafadoost Sabzevar
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: The aviation industry heavily relies on the safety and reliability of aircraft parts. Detecting and addressing defective parts promptly is crucial to ensure the overall safety of aircraft operations. In this paper, we propose an AI-enhanced Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) model for the identification of defective aircraft parts. Our objectives include developing a comprehensive dataset of aircraft parts with labeled defect samples, training the ELM model using this dataset, integrating real-time monitoring for proactive defect detection, and creating a user-friendly interface for aviation professionals to interact with the system. Our methodology involves collecting a diverse dataset of aircraft parts with annotated defect labels, preprocessing the data, training the ELM model, incorporating sensor data for real-time monitoring, and developing an intuitive interface for users to visualize and analyze identified defects. The model achieves an impressive accuracy rate of 94.27% in accurately detecting and classifying defects in aircraft components. The high accuracy achieved by our model demonstrates its effectiveness in identifying defective aircraft parts and contributes to enhanced aviation safety and maintenance efficiency. Ongoing evaluation and validation of the model's performance are essential to ensure its continued accuracy and reliability in real-world scenarios. The successful implementation of our AI-enhanced ELM model has the potential to revolutionize the identification of defective aircraft parts, leading to safer and more reliable aviation operations.

title: Measuring Changes in Sustainability Knowledge: A Case Study of a Civil Engineering Course
authors: Khaled Shaaban
abstract: Introductory transportation courses are commonly offered in any civil engineering program to educate students about the various aspects of transportation systems. It is important to assess whether these courses effectively deliver knowledge and promote sustainable transportation literacy among students. The purpose of this work is to evaluate the effectiveness of an introductory transportation course in developing sustainable transportation literacy among college students. The study assesses the baseline transportation literacy of students at the beginning of the semester, evaluates the impact of the course on students' knowledge of transportation systems, and identifies any areas of improvement. The pre-course survey indicated a baseline of limited awareness across environmental, social, economic, and technological aspects of transportation sustainability. However, the post-course survey shows a different picture. The students showed an increase in their awareness of all areas. The observed changes affirm the effectiveness of the course in increasing students' knowledge of sustainable transportation.

title: Research on Integrated AC/DC Shore Power Supply based on DC Bus
authors: Mengtao Zheng, Yifei Yang, Minjia Tan, Zhenfei Wu, Jianpeng Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To address the need for coexisting AC and DC power supply for various ships in port shore power, this paper proposes an AC-DC integrated shore power system based on a DC bus, ensuring stable DC bus voltage and high-quality AC voltage and current output. A dual closed-loop control strategy for voltage and current based on a synchronous rotating coordinate system has been designed to achieve stable voltage of the DC bus and high-quality voltage and current output on the AC side. Simulation verifies its steady-state and dynamic performance under load changes. Experiments on a low-voltage prototype test AC and DC outputs, showing good power quality and stability, meeting diverse ships' direct and AC shore power demands.

title: When Systolic Arrays Meet AI Engines: Architectural Constraints on AMD Versal ACAP
authors: Jason Kimko, Jason Cong
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: The AMD Versal ACAP provides a powerful heterogeneous architecture for accelerating kernels like general matrix multiplication (GEMM). Because the AI Engine (AIE) array mimics the spatial regularity of a systolic array (SA), mapping SA dataflows onto the fabric is highly intuitive. However, maximizing performance requires overcoming specific coordination challenges between data movement and compute cores. In this work, we demonstrate that the gap between theoretical peak and realized performance is the result of a fundamental "Systolic Tax" imposed by the AIE architecture. We provide a systematic study of 2D systolic mapping to quantify the architectural cost incurred when traditional wavefront data forwarding consumes execution cycles otherwise available for processing. This engine-level overhead leads to a simulated 8.2% compute cycle loss. To address this constraint, we implement a multicast-based SA (MCSA) design that leverages hardware multicasting for efficient operand delivery. This design is supported by a Mixed-Integer Non-Linear Programming model used for architectural optimization. Our design achieves a programmable logic frequency that analytically saturates the logic-engine communication bandwidth. While MCSA delivers a 1.28× throughput improvement over prior state-of-the-art systolic mappings, performance is ultimately limited by synchronization overhead, establishing a ceiling for systolic designs on these platforms and revealing the lack of parallel AIE-initiated data movement as a critical architectural bottleneck.

title: Interpretable Stacking Ensemble for Photovoltaic Power Forecasting under Extreme Low Irradiance
authors: Hanyang Guo, Weiru Wang, Xiaojuan Han
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Precise forecasting of photovoltaic (PV) power under extreme low-irradiance weather is essential for grid stability yet remains a formidable challenge due to complex nonlinearities. This paper proposes an interpretable Stacking ensemble framework that synergistically integrates prediction and explanation. The model employs a two-stage architecture, where six heterogeneous base learners are strategically combined via a meta-learner trained with cross-validation, effectively enhancing robustness and accuracy under diverse extreme conditions. Beyond achieving superior performance (average R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>>0.95, RMSE reduction>9.5% versus the best standalone model), the framework incorporates SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) to quantitatively interpret predictions. SHAP analysis reveals scenario-specific driving features—such as diffuse irradiance dominance during heavy rainfall—and deciphers nonlinear feature-power interactions. The proposed approach not only delivers a highly accurate forecasting tool but also provides actionable physical insights, offering a reliable and transparent decision-support solution for PV integration under increasingly volatile climates.

title: A Smart On-Site Verification System for 220-kV Voltage Transformers With Multi-Source Data Fusion and Credibility Assessment
authors: Kan Zhang, Yufu Zhao, Ke Zheng, Minzhe Tian, Xiaojie Liu, Yuanyuan Gu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: On-site verification of 220-kV voltage transformers is critical to the accuracy of electric energy metering, relay protection, and operational monitoring. However, existing field practices still rely heavily on independent testing instruments and manual data handling, which often leads to fragmented data chains, insufficient support from single-error indicators, and weak result traceability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a smart on-site verification system covering the entire inspection workflow. The system integrates equipment parameters, measurement data, process status information, and historical records into a unified multi-source data organization framework. On this basis, a method set is established, including unified complex error representation, repeated-measurement quality modeling, weighted fusion estimation, stability and operational variation analysis, historical consistency evaluation, comprehensive state scoring, anomaly screening, and result credibility assessment. The proposed system enables integrated execution of data acquisition, analytical evaluation, and report generation. A representative case study on a 220-kV voltage transformer shows that the proposed method can effectively suppress the influence of abnormal measurement rounds on the final conclusion, improve interpretability under boundary operating conditions, and enhance the traceability of report outputs. The proposed approach provides practical support for the digital, standardized, and intelligent upgrading of on-site voltage transformer verification.

title: Compliant Magnetic Sensor Arrays Enable Real-Time Force Myogram Pattern Recognition for Dexterous Hand Control by Amputees
authors: Wen-Yu Cheng, Erik D. Engeberg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Control of powered prosthetic hands could benefit from improved techniques to infer the amputees’ desired grasp intentions. Force myography (FMG) has recently emerged as a potential alternative to electromyography, which is traditionally used in clinical practice. Thus, we introduce innovative compliant magnetic FMG sensor arrays for forearm muscle pattern recognition that enables ten subjects, including three upper limb amputees, to have real-time control of a dexterous artificial hand. Sensor arrays with 18 or 24 compliant magnetic FMG sensor modules are fabricated, demonstrating the customizability of our 3D scanning—3D printing process to create individualized wearable sensor arrays for varying limb differences. On average, all 10 subjects control 16.20 ± 3.68 classes with mean accuracy of 93.64% ± 2.90% in real-time control experiments. Additionally, principal component analysis (PCA) is used to both select the most impactful sensors and improve classification accuracy in subsequent offline analyses. Seven out of the ten subjects achieve their highest accuracy with a reduced number of compliant magnetic sensors, highlighting the value of the PCA-informed sensor selection approach. Because the proposed compliant magnetic sensor arrays are highly practical and have a signal to noise ratio (SNR) of 34.51 dB ± 1.49 dB, we are providing our open-source dataset of FMG signals from 3 amputees and 7 non-amputees as a resource for the research community. These novel compliant magnetic FMG sensor arrays have the potential to advance the state of the art for prosthetic hand control and could be used broadly in the fields of tactile sensing, haptics, medical robotics, and rehabilitation.

title: An RGB-Event Hierarchical Fusion Enhancement Network for Object Detection
authors: Kaiyi Wang, Xinyu Hou, Siying Liu, Chen Cheng, Lei Deng, Hao Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Object detection in frame-based vision systems often faces the problem of significant performance degradation under challenging conditions due to the inherent sensing limitations of conventional cameras. Event cameras, with their advantages of high temporal resolution and sparse data output, offer a promising solution for addressing the performance degradation problem. However, due to the asynchronous and sparse nature of event data, event camera–based detection methods generally exhibit lower accuracy than their image-based counterparts. Therefore, effectively fusing data from both event and frame-based cameras represents the main challenge. To this end, this study introduces a hierarchical fusion enhancement network (FENET) designed to achieve more robust object detection through complementary multimodal fusion. First, to address the problem of underutilization of event timescales in conventional dense representations, this study designs an innovative, learnable multi-timescale event preprocessing module. This module generates temporal aggregation frames (TAFs) by leveraging event data from a spectrum of intervals, ranging from standard RGB exposure times down to finer timescales. This approach ensures the comprehensive capture and clear delineation of the target's structure, thereby enhancing the integrity of the visual information.
