Furthermore, a two-branch adaptive enhanced cross-modal aggregation module (ECAM), which guides the adaptive fusion of features from both modalities by first self-enhancing the features of each, is employed. The main goal of the ECAM is to achieve efficient feature aggregation by combining global context with local spatial information, subsequently using the aggregated features to steer the adaptive fusion process. Finally, by recognizing the scarcity of large-scale, high-quality annotated datasets for event-based object detection, the DSEC-D dataset comprising 60 sequences, with a total of 70,739 images and over 250,000 labels, is manually constructed. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed FENET can successfully overcome the perceptual limitations of conventional cameras. Notably, the proposed FENET surpasses current RGB-event fusion schemes, achieving an 8.6% improvement in the mAP50 metric and a 3.7% improvement in the mAP50:95 metric over the FRN baseline. We have released the code and the dataset at https://github.com/wkydawang/FENET.

title: Message Passing Based Bayesian Detection for Distributed Dual-Channel Passive MIMO Radar
authors: Bin Li, Jun Li, Qinghua Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper investigates Bayesian target detection for distributed dual-channel passive multi-input multi-output (MIMO) radar to systematically exploit prior statistical knowledge of unknown parameters. First, a closed-form Bayesian generalized likelihood ratio test (B-GLRT) detector is derived by marginalizing random channel coefficients while estimating the unknown transmitted signals via maximum likelihood estimation, yielding a computationally efficient detector. Second, to further incorporate the discrete constellation structure of the transmitted symbols, a message passing based maximum-a-posteriori (MP-MAP) ratio test detector is proposed. It is efficiently realized via an iterative MP algorithm to jointly infer the channel coefficients, transformed signals and symbols, without exhaustive searching over all possible symbol sequences. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed detectors consistently outperform existing adaptive detectors across a wide range of signal-to-noise ratios.

title: Transmission Cross Section of Electrically Large Dielectric Interface
authors: Chong Li, Tieying Feng, Yongjiu Zhao, Lei Xing, Qian Xu, Yi Huang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The transmission cross section (TCS) of an electrically large dielectric interface is evaluated. By computing the plane wave reflection coefficients from both sides of the aperture, the corresponding TCSs are obtained using numerical integral. It is interesting to find that the average TCS can be derived analytically, and the ratio of the average TCSs from two sides of the interface equals the ratio of the dielectric permittivities. Both integral representations and analytical expressions of the average TCS are derived, and full-wave simulations are conducted in dielectric-filled reverberation chambers (RCs) for validation.

title: Optical Simulation & Image Quality for Automotive Semantic Segmentation
authors: Daniel Jakab, Joel Herrera Vázquez, Mahendar Kumbham, Brian Deegan, Tim Brophy, Reenu Mohandas, Anthony Scanlan, Enda Ward, Fiachra Collins, Ciarán Eising
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Cameras are often deployed in automated driving systems for multiple perception tasks, including object recognition. In previous work, a novel optical simulation framework, SOLAS, was proposed to simulate wide-angle and fisheye cameras on automotive data for 2D object detection. The main focus of this work is to assess semantic segmentation networks in relation to camera quality, mainly used for collision avoidance, lane marking detection, and enhanced road navigation. Characterising semantic segmentation performance across the full range of camera production tolerances is essential, given that image quality may vary across units. This study further develops the novel optical simulation framework, SOLAS, where a Pythonic ray-tracing library, KrakenOS, is adapted to simulate wide-angle surround-view optical systems across a range of optical defocus, directly in automotive scenes. Optical defocus is achieved through sensor displacement. In this paper, we propose extending SOLAS with ARDÁN, a pipeline for measuring image quality in automotive scenes. Furthermore, we demonstrate that our framework, utilising a diverse set of cameras and dense grids of Point Spread Functions (PSFs), is scalable in semantic segmentation, achieving consistent results across various datasets supported by statistical metrics. The primary metric used for performance evaluation is mean Intersection over Union (mIoU). Based on our results, the Segformer network with 24.73M parameters was the most robust across wide-angle automotive datasets, namely, nuScenes and Virtual KITTI 2.0. In nuScenes, image sharpness (cycles/pixel) dropped by 20.5% from a nominal focus, with a small Segformer performance drop from 55.21% mIoU by 0.37%. In Virtual KITTI 2.0, image sharpness dropped by 44.66% from a nominal focus, with a Segformer performance drop by 37.42%. However, for fisheye, the PIDNet model with 28.76M parameters was the most robust model across the defocus range. In Woodscape, image sharpness dropped by 53.45% from a nominal focus, with a PIDNet performance drop by 8.55%. In Parallel Domain Woodscape, image sharpness dropped by 34.46% from a nominal focus, with a PIDNet performance drop by 7.28%.

title: Manipulation of Deformable Objects with Soft Grippers Based on Adaptive Fuzzy Control and a Self-Supervised Feature Descriptor
authors: Yuexuan Xu, Ning Han, Jinrui Li, Tong Yang, Yun-Hui Liu, Ning Sun, David Navarro-Alarcon
abstract: Although visual servoing control is widely used in practical applications, its application to deformable object manipulation (DOM) remains underexplored. This is due to the significant diversity of material properties and geometric configurations, the high uncertainties of deformation characteristics, and the high-dimensional configuration spaces. To this end, a new data-driven visual servoing control method is proposed for three-dimensional DOM. Specifically, a self-supervised keypoint detection network is developed without manual annotation while preserving spatial geometric information, addressing limitations of traditional dimensionality reduction methods. The network employs weighted feature aggregation modules to effectively balance the fusion of diverse local information while preserving features essential for keypoint localization. Furthermore, a geodesic distance loss function ensures the semantic consistency of detected keypoints across deformation processes. Based on the detected keypoints, an adaptive fuzzy-based Jacobian estimator is developed to establish the online mapping relationship between keypoint velocities and robot joint velocities. To optimize transient performance, a prescribed performance control method is designed to ensure that keypoint errors converge within predefined spatial funnel functions. Additionally, a pneumatic soft gripper based on a rigid origami-inspired mechanism is designed to prevent damage to deformable objects and provide compliant interaction. Finally, detailed stability analysis and a series of experimental results are provided to verify the feasibility of the proposed method.

title: PIVOT: A Framework for High-Fidelity Facade PV Layouts via Generative Seeding and Differentiable Optimization
authors: Dongxu Zhuang, Ruyu Liu, Xiufeng Liu, Per Sieverts Nielsen, Tangao Hu, Jianhua Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: The automation of Building-Integrated Photovoltaic (BIPV) design is essential for the digital transformation of urban renewable energy, yet current methods struggle to bridge the gap between semantic facade assessment and engineering-grade installation plans. Existing AI-based approaches typically yield raw segmentation masks that lack the geometric precision and physical validity required for real-world deployment. This paper presents PIVOT, a novel end-to-end framework for the automated synthesis of high-fidelity, collision-free PV layouts from single 2D facade images. We formulate the layout generation as a two-stage neuro-symbolic and differentiable optimization process. First, an LLM-based programmatic seeding stage uses structured facade information and elementary spatial-relation cues to generate initial layout hypotheses. Second, a differentiable layout optimization engine refines these proposals by treating panel placement as a continuous constrained optimization problem, effectively resolving spatial overlaps while maximizing geometric regularity. Validated on a diverse dataset of 80 building facades, experimental results demonstrate that PIVOT significantly outperforms heuristic (MaxRects) and evolutionary (MOGA) baselines. Our framework reduces the layout collision rate from 9.7% to 0.7% and improves geometric regularity by over 10%, while requiring only approximately 80 seconds of processing time per building. This work advances BIPV design automation by converting semantically parsed facade information into geometrically constrained, constructibility-oriented PV layouts for downstream performance evaluation.

title: Control-Integrated Pipeline for Diseasome Analysis: A Case Study on Hypertension Comorbidities in U.S. Veterans_supp7-3698948.pdf
authors: A. MAGNONI

title: Beyond Artifacts: Enhancing Morphing Attack Detection with Beauty Latent Features
authors: Rasmus Stilling, Juan E. Tapia, Christoph Busch
abstract: This work investigates the relationship between facial attractiveness and morphed face images to improve morphing attack detection in biometric systems. We propose a Differential Morphing Attack Detection (D-MAD) method that integrates beauty latent features derived from Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) trained on facial attractiveness with those from CNN models trained on facial recognition datasets. By incorporating beauty features alongside facial recognition features, our approach aims to boost the accuracy of morph detection. Experiments conducted on synthetic and non-synthetic datasets of morphed images demonstrate the potential of utilizing beauty latent features to enhance D-MAD performance, particularly when dealing with synthetic images. Our results suggest that incorporating beauty information can significantly improve the detection capabilities of D-MAD methods, especially when synthetic images are involved in either training or testing.

title:
High-Performance Mid-Infrared Photodetector Based on WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /WTe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /WS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    P-Type-Semimetal-N-Type Heterojunction
authors: Preeti Yadav, Anuj K. Sharma, Vandana Devi Wangkheirakpam
abstract: A high- performance PD comprising p-type WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>-WTe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> semi-metal/n-type WS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (PSN) configuration is reported by sandwiching 2D semi-metal between two semiconductor layers. In this heterojunction configuration, the top and bottom semiconductor layers generate an internal built-in electric field oriented vertically within the T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>-WTe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, which serves as an absorption layer for low energy photons leading to reduced dark current and enhanced collection efficiency. Finite difference time domain (FDTD) is used for simulation of proposed PD design. Notably, the simulated device can respond to the wide spectrum of 8-12 μm with the optimal wavelength around 9.68763 μm. In particular, the device demonstrated remarkable photovoltaic performance, characterized by a maximum responsivity of 4.56 A/W, a detectivity of 2.108 ×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</sup> Jones, and a low dark current of∼10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−17</sup> A at 9.687 μm. Further, the transient analysis led to rise and fall times of 5.29 μs and 4.48 μs, respectively, indicating a fast-responding PD design. Furthermore, the 3dB bandwidth of the proposed PSN design is calculated to be 66.16 kHz. This work presents a major step towards Weyl semimetal-based PDs on the silicon platform for MIR applications.

title: Polarization-Stable Conformal FSS for ISM-Band (5.8 GHz) EMI Shielding in Conformal IoMT Devices
authors: Md Kutub Uddin, Touhidul Alam, Mohamad A. Alawad, Abdulmajeed M. Alenezi, Mohamed S. Soliman, Mohammad Tariqul Islam
abstract: The growth of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has intensified electromagnetic-interference (EMI) concerns in the 5.8 GHz ISM band. Frequency-selective surfaces (FSS) offer a practical alternative to rigid enclosures for wearable and conformal medical devices, providing lightweight, integrable shielding. This article presents a polarization-insensitive, conformal FSS designed for broadband EMI shielding in 5.8 GHz ISM-band for IoMT applications. The proposed structure employs a complementary patch configuration printed on opposite sides of a thin dielectric substrate to achieve a broadband band-stop response. The top-layer resonator defines the lower stopband with S21 < −10 dB and a compact unit-cell size of 0.17λ₀, where λ₀ corresponds to the resonance frequency. The FSS achieves a <−10 dB stop band from 4.0 GHz to 7.7 GHz, centered at 5.8 GHz with the bandwidth of 63.793% that fully covered the ISM spectrum. Owing to its fourfold symmetric geometry, the design demonstrates polarization independence and maintains angular stability up to 75°. To validate the real time performance, A fabricated 6×6 array prototype is experimentally evaluated under both planar and conformal conditions, showing strong agreement with simulation results. The proposed lightweight and flexible FSS provides an effective solution for broadband EMI suppression, enabling reliable electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) and signal integrity in compact and wearable biomedical IoT systems.

title: DeGMix: Efficient Multi-Task Dense Prediction with Deformable and Gating Mixer
authors: Yangyang Xu, Yibo Yang, Bernard Ghanem, Lefei Zhang, Bo Du, Jun Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Convolution neural networks (CNNs) and Transformers offer complementary strengths for multi-task dense prediction: CNNs are effective at modeling local spatial patterns, while Transformers capture long-range dependencies. However, existing methods often rely primarily on one paradigm or do not fully exploit their complementarity for task-aware representation learning. In this work, we propose DeGMix (Deformable and Gating Mixer), a unified encoder–decoder framework for multi-task dense prediction that combines deformable convolution and attention-based task interaction. DeGMix consists of a deformable mixer encoder for task-aware spatial feature extraction and a task-aware gating Transformer decoder for cross-task interaction and task-specific feature selection. In this way, the proposed framework jointly models local deformable cues, global dependencies, and task-aware feature adaptation within a single architecture. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets, including NYUD-v2, PASCAL-Context, and Cityscapes, show that DeGMix consistently outperforms competitive CNN-based and Transformer-based multi-task methods across multiple dense prediction tasks. For example, with a Swin-L backbone, DeGMix achieves 57.55 mIoU on semantic segmentation on NYUD-v2, surpassing the previous best result by +5.2 mIoU. In parameterefficient fine-tuning on PASCAL-Context, DeGMix obtains a +1.96 Δm gain with 2.2M trainable parameters, approaching MTL-full fine-tuning (+2.23 Δm) while updating 13.7× fewer parameters. Our code and models are publicly available at https://github.com/yangyangxu0/DeGMix.

title: Thermo-Mechanical Frequency Stability and Anisotropic Modeling of Carbon Fiber-Reinforced PETG Accelerometers for Additive Manufacturing
authors: Xuan Luc Le, ThuHang Bui, Tuan Vu Quoc
abstract: This research presents a rigorous investigation into the structural optimisation and material-driven performance enhancement of 3D-printed inertial transducers, specifically focusing on the transition from monolithic thermoplastics to carbon fibre-reinforced composites (PETG-CF). Conventional Polyethylene Terephthalate Glycol (PETG) has been demonstrated functional viability under ambient conditions; however, its susceptibility to temperature-induced mechanical fluctuations and viscoelastic degradation remains a barrier to precision applications. The present study employs integrated modal, harmonic, and thermo-mechanical Finite Element Method (FEM) analysis to delineate the relationship between matrix reinforcement and operational stability across an extended thermal gradient from -40 °C to 100 °C. The analytical model yielded resonant frequencies at ambient temperature of 62.38 Hz for PETG and 102.62 Hz for PETG-CF, demonstrating a high correlation with FEM simulation results of 62.94 Hz and 103.71 Hz, respectively. The simulation results indicate that standard PETG-based transducers exhibit high thermal sensitivity, with the fundamental natural frequency decreasing from 67.1 Hz at −40 °C to 47.0 Hz at 75 °C, representing a linear frequency drift of approximately 0.32 Hz/°C. Conversely, the incorporation of carbon reinforcement (PETG-CF) significantly enhances mechanical resonance performance, achieving a fundamental natural frequency of 103.71 Hz at 25 °C, corresponding to a 65% increase over the baseline (62.945 Hz). The PETG-CF variant demonstrates superior thermal robustness, evidenced by a reduced average frequency shift of approximately 0.26 Hz/°C. The experimental validation of these findings was conducted through the utilisation of free-decay vibration measurements, which substantiated the observations. The resonant frequencies observed were found to be approximately 71 Hz for PETG and 106 Hz for PETG-CF. Static calibration yielded a sensitivity of approximately 108 mV/g. The results obtained establish a practical operational temperature limit between -40 °C and 75 °C, thus providing a fundamental framework for developing high-performance, low-cost micro-sensors through advanced Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM) techniques.

title: Physically-Based Polarization Reflection Separation for Laser Stripe Extraction on Highly Reflective Metal Surfaces_supp2-3699730.mp4
authors: Yiting Yu
abstract: Polarization-based laser vision imaging offers high accuracy, non-contact sensing, and strong robustness to interference, making it promising for weld seam localization and intelligent welding. However, for planar fillet-weld workpieces with highly reflective metallic surfaces, reliable laser stripe segmentation and precise extraction of centerlines and feature points remain challenging. This is because inter-reflection interference and camera saturation severely degrade measurement accuracy. In this paper, we propose a novel physically-based polarization reflection separation method using polarization features and spatial structural information for robust laser stripe extraction. The physical principles and polarization properties of direct- and inter-reflections from metallic surfaces are investigated through theoretical modeling and simulation. By combining the Stokes parameter S2 with a saturation region segmentation criterion, the inter-reflection regions of the laser stripe are automatically determined. An adaptive mask dilation approach is then introduced to suppress saturation-induced interference without complex parameter tuning. Finally, the centerlines and feature points are accurately extracted using the gray-gravity method with least-squares fitting. Experiments demonstrate superior segmentation and extraction performance under strong reflective interference, achieving an average feature-point extraction error of 2.36 pixels, a repeatability error within ±1.16 pixels, and an average computational time of 0.2531 s. Moreover, comprehensive evaluations across varying groove angles, laser rotation angles, exposure times, surface conditions and dynamic robotic platforms further validate the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed method. This study highlights the potential of polarization imaging for addressing optical interference challenges in industrial welding scenarios, providing a solid foundation for intelligent and automated welding.

title:
Adaptive Cross Section Optimization Method for Oversized Circular TE
                    <sub>01</sub>
                    -Mode Waveguide Bends
authors: Zhipeng Tu, Xiaoyi Liao, Ran Zhang, Guo Liu, Wei Jiang, Jianxun Wang, Yong Luo, Zewei Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article proposes a synthesis method for highly efficient, broadband, and compact circular TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub>-mode waveguide bends based on adaptive optimization of the waveguide cross section. To enable convenient parametric adjustment of the waveguide cross section while meeting the structural continuity and smoothness requirements for practical high-power applications, a construction method for waveguide cross sections based on Gaussian radial basis function (GRBF) superposition is introduced. For the sake of achieving an optimized cross section for efficient TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub> mode transmission, an improved particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm incorporating shape-family clustering, cross-family weak competition, and geometry-weighted parameter scaling is proposed. Based on the proposed method, a Ka-band 90° high-power TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub> mode waveguide bend was designed, fabricated, and tested. Simulation results show that the bend achieves a transmission efficiency above 95% and a reflection coefficient below −35dB across the 18.5–40.3 GHz frequency range. Near-field measurements further confirm an output mode purity exceeding 98%. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in realizing high-efficiency, high power-capacity, broadband, and compact circular TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub> mode bending, offering a new technical reference for the development of high-performance high-power microwave devices.

title: Multilevel Converter based PV-system with Low-voltage Global Maximum Power Point Tracking during Partial Shading
authors: Subhojit Das, Suman Maiti, Naba Kumar Peyada
abstract: Conventional photovoltaic (PV) converters typically employ a two-stage architecture comprising a DC-DC converter for maximum power point tracking (MPPT) followed by a central inverter for grid or load-side regulation. This arrangement encounters challenges in locating the Global Maximum Power Point (GMPP) at reduced voltages under partial shading conditions. This paper proposes a single-stage power conversion topology featuring a multilevel voltage output, which provides three key benefits: (i) reduced overall losses and enhanced performance ratio, (ii) elimination of AC-side passive filters, and (iii) capability to operate at low-voltage maximum power point. The proposed multilevel converter integrates PV panels directly with each submodule. Emphasis is given on overmodulation in submodules caused by reduction of the DC-bus voltage during partial shading. An auxiliary submodule, supplied via a tertiary winding of the power transformer, is introduced to mitigate low-voltage GMPP-related issues. Various control strategies, like AC-side control to match the grid requirements, active and reactive power regulation of individual submodules, overmodulation control, and PFC control to address GMPP operation have been developed for satisfactory operation during partial shading scenario. The losses of the converter are computed in PLECS incorporating different non-idealities of the switching devices and circuit elements to increase accuracy of calculation. The proposed converter is compared with the contemporary topologies encompassing component count and efficiency to highlight the effectiveness of this configuration. The operation of the converter including control methodologies are validated using MATLAB/Simulink simulations. A laboratory prototype of this converter is built using a DSP-based digital control platform to experimentally validate the closed-loop operation. Both simulation and experimental results show the effectiveness of the use of auxiliary submodule to effectively mitigate low-voltage GMPP-related issue during partial shading without allowing the submodules to enter into overmodulation zone.

title: RIS-Assisted Atomic MIMO Receiver
authors: Qihao Peng, Jiuyu Liu, Qu Luo, Yi Ma, Pei Xiao, Maged Elkashlan, George K. Karagiannidis
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel and low-complexity atomic multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) receiver architecture assisted by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). By introducing RIS and utilizing pulse amplitude modulation (PAM), the phase of the transmitted signal is effectively aligned with that of the local oscillator (LO), mitigating phase ambiguity and reducing the complexity of the receiver. To tackle the resulting non-convex optimization problem, we reformulate it into a tractable form by minimizing the Frobenius norm of an equivalent matrix, which is solved using an Adam-based gradient descent algorithm. Building upon the optimized equivalent signals, we develop a low-complexity signal detection algorithm and evaluate its performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed RIS-assisted atomic MIMO receiver significantly enhances detection performance compared to the conventional atomic MIMO receivers. More importantly, these results underscore the proposed architecture's potential for scalable and high-performance atomic MIMO communication systems.

title: Active Neutral Point Clamped Configured Topologies with Equal and Unequal Step Voltages: Topological Derivation and Performance Assessment
authors: Md Reyaz Hussan, Marif Daula Siddique, Md Samiullah, Md Shafiullah, Ali T. Al-Awami, Mehdi Seyedmahmoudian, Saad Mekhilef, Alex Stojcevski
abstract: Over several decades, multilevel inverters have continued to play a vital role in power conversion because of their numerous advantages and one such advantage has been the improved power quality. Although it is commonly assumed that if number of levels are higher, the THD will reduce, however, in most of the cases, the step voltage is not considered in this statement. In this paper, this convention has been checked through nine different multilevel inverter topologies with different number of levels. First, a four-level topology is discussed, followed by the five-level, six-level, and seven-level topologies with a voltage gain of 1.5. With a slight modification to the 4 L topology, the voltage gain is increased to 2.5. With this voltage gain, 5 different topologies have been developed for six-level, eight-level, two topologies with nine-levels, and 11-levels. All the developed topologies shows different step voltages. A power quality assessment of output voltage and current has been carried out for different filter sizes as well as carrier frequencies for all nine topologies. The power loss analysis using two different modulation techniques has been carried out for all nine topologies. A detailed comparative study of all nine topologies has been carried out regarding component count, power loss, and harmonics. Simulation and experimental results have been provided for different topologies at the same operating conditions for a better decree of the topologies.

title: Efficient Attention-Mamba Trajectory Prediction for Intelligent Vehicles Based on Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Hui Li, Qinghua Lin, Xiao Li, Xiugong Qin, Xiangzhen Kong, Jun Yan
abstract: Existing trajectory prediction models often fail to balance computational efficiency with the ability to capture complex interactions, typically hindered by the quadratic complexity of attention mechanisms and the limitations of supervised learning on imperfect data. To address these challenges, we propose EAT-SL, an Efficient Attention-Mamba Trajectory Prediction framework based on Self-Supervised Learning. First, we design a novel masking strategy comprising Interaction-Aware Masking and Lane Restructure Masking for the pre-training phase. This strategy encourages interaction-sensitive representations among vehicles, as well as between vehicles and map elements, via self-supervised reconstruction. Secondly, we propose the Inherit Mamba Net and Hamba Scene Encoder, which synergize the linear complexity of State Space Models with attention mechanisms to efficiently capture both global semantics and local dependencies. Finally, to mitigate the accumulated errors inherent in long-horizon trajectory prediction, we design a Recursive Memory Decoder to capture spatiotemporal interactions and maintain trajectory continuity during the fine-tuning phase. Experimental results on the Argoverse 2 dataset demonstrate that the proposed EAT-SL achieves a superior balance between accuracy and efficiency. Compared to the state-of-the-art baseline QCNet, EAT-SL reduces MinADE1 by 2.4% and MinFDE1 by 4.9%, while requiring only 42% of the parameters and maintaining a high inference throughput of 4.67 GFLOPs.

title: Single-Stage Instance Segmentation Survey: A “1+2+3” Technical Framework
authors: Tao Zhou, Defang Chang, Huiling Lu, Wenwen Chai, Ke Song, Feiyu Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Single-stage instance segmentation is a prominent research direction in computer vision. However, in existing single-stage instance segmentation methods, there is a lack of summary on the 6 key questions: “How to select the learning paradigm?”, “How to extract features?”, “How to fuse features?”, “How to classify objects?”, “How to generate object masks?”, and “How to localize objects?”. These questions restrict the development and application to some degree. To address these questions, this paper proposes a “1+2+3” technical framework for summarizing single-stage instance segmentation. “1” represents one learning paradigm. Four mainstream learning paradigms and selection strategies are systematically summarized. “2” represents two local structures. The three characteristics of feature extraction are summarized, and the two feature fusion strategies are summarized. “3” represents three aspects of instance prediction. The object classification, object mask generation methods and object localization are discussed. Furthermore, the paper discusses new technologies such as instance segmentation based on promptable foundation models and instance segmentation based on vision-language models, as well as their typical applications in medical, video, and remote sensing image segmentation. The “1+2+3” technology framework proposed in the paper provides a problem-oriented technical map for instance segmentation, promoting the further development of single-stage instance segmentation.

title: LegoBot: Modular, Compact, Device-Agnostic Robot for Endovascular Catheter and Guidewire Manipulation in Stroke Thrombectomy
authors: Weibo Gao, Nitin Srinivasan, Dongming Piao, Qianhe Fan, Miki Guo, Rui Li, Peter Patalano, Hao Su
abstract: State-of-the-art endovascular robots are often limited by coupled translation–rotation actuation, narrow compatibility with off-the-shelf devices, and fixed architectures that are difficult to reconfigure for multidevice procedures. These limitations are especially restrictive in neurovascular intervention, where long working lengths, tortuous anatomy, and coordinated manipulation of multiple catheters and guidewires are frequently required. In this work, we present LegoBot, a modular, compact, device-agnostic robot for neurovascular catheter and guidewire manipulation. First, we design a decoupled two-degree-of-freedom (2-DOF) actuation mechanism together with an instrument-agnostic adjustable clamping mechanism, enabling independent or simultaneous instrument translation and rotation while accommodating off-the-shelf devices with diameters ranging from 0.33 to 3 mm. Second, we present the mechatronic design of a modular catheter robot with interchangeable adapters for different instruments, which support module-level device exchange. We also design a module coupler that connects adjacent robotic modules for multi-instrument actuation and improves rotational torque transmission through long, compliant instruments. Benchtop characterization of the proposed 2-DOF mechanism showed peak insertion force up to 5.3 N and rotational torque up to 8.4 mN<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot$</tex-math></inline-formula>m, exceeding reported clinical requirements. Phantom studies demonstrated a two-module (4-DOF) configuration for diagnostic angiography and a three-module (6-DOF) configuration for aspiration thrombectomy, including successful clot aspiration in a neurovascular phantom.

title: Performance Analysis on Hybrid NOMA under Varying Channel Conditions
authors: Chenzhan Xu, Yanshi Sun, Ximing Xie, Fang Fang
abstract: By combining pure non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and conventional orthogonal multiple access (OMA) techniques, hybrid NOMA (H-NOMA) can achieve significantly high energy efficiency. However, existing work on H-NOMA assumes that the channel gains of a user in different channel resource blocks remain constant, which might be unrealistic in many practical systems. To address this limitation, this letter provides a new analytical characterization of the H-NOMA framework under statistically independent channel realizations. Rigorous analysis reveals that under independent fading, the probability that H-NOMA achieves a higher instantaneous data rate than conventional OMA while consuming less transmit energy approaches one in the high SNR regime, which indicates the superiority of H-NOMA in energy efficiency. Numerical results are provided to validate the developed analysis and also demonstrate the superior performance of H-NOMA.

title: A Blind Signal Detection Method Based on Constrained Dual-Cluster K-means for FSO Communications with Atmospheric Turbulence
authors: Zhenning Yi, Hao Zhou, Zhiyong Xu, Jianhua Li, Jingyuan Wang, Jiyong Zhao, Yang Su, Cheng Li, Han Zhou, Ying Du
abstract: Existing blind detection schemes for free-space optical (FSO) communications often suffer from bit error rate (BER) floors. These floors fundamentally arise from the misclassification of extreme signal blocks like all-0 and all-1 blocks. This letter proposes a robust blind signal detection method based on constrained dual-cluster K-means. The proposed method incorporates the physical properties of on-off keying modulation. It fixes the cluster center for the “0” symbol and iteratively optimizes only the “1” symbol center. Furthermore, the method integrates a temporal center retention mechanism to explicitly handle all-0 blocks. This combined strategy effectively prevents the center drift issue inherent in conventional unsupervised learning. Consequently, the proposed scheme completely eliminates the extreme-block-induced BER floor without requiring pilot overhead or prior channel statistics. Simulation results using measured intensity data confirm the superiority of this approach. The method outperforms existing blind detection schemes and achieves robust performance approaching ideal maximum likelihood (ML) detection. It also maintains a linear computational complexity suitable for real-time processing.

title: A Closed-Form Phase Design for RIS-Aided UCA-OAM Systems
authors: Zhongpeng Wang, Hongyang Han
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) phase optimization is essential for enhancing the capacity of RIS-aided uniform circular array (UCA) based orbital angular momentum (OAM) systems. In this letter, we propose a closed-form phase design that aligns the propagation paths of all OAM modes to maximize the total received power. The proposed scheme computes the RIS reflection coefficients without iteration, offering a low-complexity alternative to conventional iterative algorithms. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves near-optimal sum-rate performance across a wide range of RIS sizes and transmit powers, while iterative algorithms (e.g., DSM) provide marginal gains only under extreme high-SNR conditions. The proposed design also serves as an efficient initialization for more sophisticated optimizers.

title: A Location-Sensitive Gaussian Attention Transformer Method for Hyperspectral Target Detection
authors: Ying Pang, Qiang Qu, Mingxing Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral target detection (HTD) remains a challenging task due to high spectral dimensionality, complex background interference, and the difficulty of jointly modeling local spectral–spatial structures and long-range contextual dependencies. Existing Transformer-based methods have demonstrated strong global modeling capability, but their effectiveness is often limited by the lack of explicit and continuous spatial priors. To address this limitation, we propose a novel Location-sensitive Gaussian Attention (LGA) framework for hyperspectral target detection. The core of the proposed method is a Gaussian-based attention mechanism that embeds a continuous, data-driven spatial prior directly into Transformer self-attention, fundamentally differing from conventional discrete or handcrafted positional encoding strategies. By integrating Gaussian-weighted spatial priors with global self-attention, the proposed approach adaptively emphasizes target-relevant regions while suppressing cluttered backgrounds, leading to more accurate target localization. In addition, spectral–spatial feature extraction is incorporated to preserve local spectral continuity and spatial coherence. Extensive experiments conducted on seven widely used hyperspectral benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed LGA method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art classical and deep learning–based detectors in terms of detection accuracy and robustness. The results confirm that the proposed Gaussian-enhanced attention mechanism provides an effective and generalizable solution for hyperspectral target detection in complex remote sensing scenes.

title: BIFFNet: Bi-Branch Interaction and Frequency-Domain Feature-Enhanced Network for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Chenyang Wang, Jing Wang, Runzhou Li, Lu Wang, Dong Ren, Li Liu, Hao Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In recent years, remote sensing image change detection has attracted increasing attention and achieved remarkable performance gains. However, it remains challenging to effectively disentangle background and object features in multi-temporal imagery, which makes the results susceptible to pseudo-changes under variations in illumination, season, and sensor characteristics. Meanwhile, most existing methods rely on simple concatenation or subtraction for feature fusion, making it difficult to simultaneously ensure semantic consistency and detail fidelity, and consequently leading to missed detections of small objects and imprecise boundary delineation. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Bi-branch Interaction and Frequency-domain Feature-enhanced Network (BIFFNet) for change detection. Specifically, we construct a Temporal Feature Interaction and difference-enhancement Module (TFIM), which jointly models difference-enhanced features and original feature fidelity to mitigate the information loss and domain shift caused by naive differencing. Furthermore, we design a Frequency-domain Decomposition and Multi-branch Modeling module (FDMM), which explicitly decomposes features into high- and low-frequency components: the high-frequency branch emphasizes edge and texture details, while the low-frequency branch models global illumination and background structures, thereby improving boundary accuracy and robustness in complex scenes. Experimental results on multiple remote sensing change detection datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed network in suppressing pseudo-changes and enhancing boundary delineation. The code will be made available on <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/WCYGORETZKA/BIFFNet</uri>.

title: Enhancing Privacy-Preserving Software Development from a Developers' Perspective: A Survey
authors: Tharaka Wijesundara, Shakya R.D.N., Matthew Warren, Nalin Arachchilage
abstract: None

title: Bidding Strategies for Multi-Type Charging Operators in Peak-Shaving Markets
authors: Shiyu Wang, Qinyi Xue, Qingyu Wu, Linjun Shi, Yang Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Electric vehicles are a significant controllable load resource, offering potential for grid regulation. EV types exhibit both regularity and uncertainty, so this study proposes separate data structure models for public and private charging stations, which are then aggregated. During day-ahead bidding for charging operators in peak-shaving ancillary services across various station types, a two-tier bidding-allocation model is proposed. This allows operators to participate in electricity ancillary services markets while also building these markets through flexible resource dispatch. Real-world simulation results show that the two-tier bidding-allocation strategy lowers net operating costs for charging operators and improves power mobilisation at public transport charging stations.

title: Research on Space Power Supply Technology Based on GaN Device
authors: Baolei Dong, Zirui Zhu, Xiao Wang, Xu Huang, Lidong Shi, Tao Liu, Suliang Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: As the core energy guarantee of spacecraft, space power supply system puts forward strict requirements for power density, efficiency, extreme environmental adaptability and long-term reliability of devices. As the core representative of the third generation wide band gap semiconductor materials, gallium nitride (GaN) has shown revolutionary application potential in the field of space power supply with its high breakdown electric field, high electron mobility, low switching loss and excellent radiation resistance and high temperature resistance. In this paper, the core characteristics of GaN devices and their adaptability to space power demand are systematically summarized, and its application status in typical space power subsystems such as solar array power conversion, energy storage system management, electric propulsion power supply and load point power supply is expounded in detail. The key challenges such as radiation effect, extreme temperature stability, long-term reliability and packaging compatibility faced by GaN devices in space power supply applications are deeply analyzed, and the corresponding solutions are summarized from the aspects of device design, process optimization and system integration. Finally, combined with the development trend of space technology, the application prospect of GaN devices in super-large satellite constellation power network, deep space exploration power system and space solar power station is prospected. This paper aims to provide reference for the research and development and engineering application of GaN devices in the field of space power supply, and promote the development of space power supply system in the direction of high efficiency, miniaturization and long life.

title: An MLP-Based Multi-Frequency Enhancement Long-term Prediction Model for Multivariate Load Forecasting
authors: Huaiyu Xing, Bin Jia, Wenlong Ding, Jinlong Wu, Changlong Li, Jinqiu Song, Xiaobo Chi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of the power system and the growing global demand for electricity, it is increasingly important to obtain accurate and reliable forecasts of load, price, and key meteorological variables. However, existing methods focus on a single target variable—typically the electricity load— rather than simultaneously predicting multiple indicators such as load, price, and weather-related features. Such single-output forecasting is often inadequate for generation companies and other market participants when making informed operational and economic decisions. While some time series forecasting methods can predict multiple indicators simultaneously, they often lack effective mechanisms to adaptively extract global frequency features and model long-term dependencies. To address these issues, this paper proposes an MLP-based multi-frequency enhancement network called MFE-Net. It first introduces a Multi-level Discrete Wavelet Transform to capture intricate information in both the time and frequency domains. By treating each frequency component as an independent channel, it utilizes an adaptive frequency fusion block to further integrate frequency components via learnable weight adjustment and fuses outputs to enhance forecasting accuracy. Experimental results show that the proposed model outperforms state-of-the-art models in different time periods and sampling frequencies, achieving the best results in forecasts with a maximum lead time of up to 30 days.

title: DiffRouter: A Differentiable Routing Framework for UltraScale FPGAs
authors: Xiaohan Gao, Zhili Xiong, Yusu Wang, David Z. Pan
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: Routing has become a major bottleneck in large-scale FPGA design flows, where traditional routers rely on sequential net-by-net processing and rip-up-reroute heuristics. This inherent sequentialism limits parallelism, leads to suboptimal utilization of routing resources, and significantly increases runtime on modern FPGAs. This paper introduces DiffRouter, the first differentiable, gradient-based FPGA routing framework with GPU acceleration. By lifting routing into a continuous optimization space, DiffRouter enables highly concurrent routing and improved global optimality before projecting solutions back onto the discrete fabric. The framework consists of three key stages: (1) Resource-pruned preprocessing, which extracts compact routing variables per net to reduce memory footprint and problem complexity; (2) Differentiable global routing via gradient descent, which formulates routing as a Lagrangian-relaxed optimization that minimizes wirelength while iteratively enforcing connectivity and congestion constraints as relaxed terms; and (3) Postprocessing, which maps the continuous wire distributions to a valid UltraScale routing solution and completes solutions by detailed routing. Experiments on the FPGA 2024 Routing Contest benchmarks show that DiffRouter outperforms state-of-the-art parallel routers, achieving over 5× speedup over RWRoute and 2× over Vivado, and running 18% faster than the state-of-the-art academic FPGA router Potter while delivering competitive critical path wirelength.

title: A Transfer Learning Modeling Framework Fusing Domain Knowledge and Its Application in District Heating Network
authors: Liang Zhang, Hui Zhang, Bo Sun, Jing Chen, Xiangyu Zhou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: District heating networks (DHNs) represent a critical infrastructure for decarbonizing the heating sector. However, as DHN systems grow in scale and complexity, the computational burden associated with numerical simulations increases significantly. To meet both user demand and energy efficiency objectives under emission reduction policies, efficient and accurate simulation modeling approaches are essential for the optimal operation and scheduling of DHNs. This paper proposes a transfer learning-based modeling framework integrated with domain knowledge to enable high-fidelity network modeling. First, the partial differential equation governing the dynamic temperature evolution of fluid in heating pipelines is incorporated into a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) as embedded domain knowledge. Subsequently, high-resolution data generated by Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulations are employed to construct a pre-trained model for an individual heating pipe. Finally, this pre-trained model is fine-tuned according to the specific initial and boundary conditions of various pipelines within the district heating network, enabling accurate knowledge transfer through integration with sparse measurement data. Experimental results show that the proposed framework agrees well with reference numerical solutions in the literature and exhibits superior accuracy compared to conventional neural network–based modeling approaches.

title: Research on Electric Field Distribution of Composite Insulators under Different Extreme Weather Conditions
authors: Shicong Sun, Xuchen Lu, Yi Lin, Guanhua Li, Quanying Liu, Jingwei Han, Zhenchi Zhang, Bingbing Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Extreme weather associated with the northeast cold vortex tends to alter the surface conditions of composite insulators, thereby distorting their electric field distribution and threatening the safe and stable operation of transmission lines. Taking 500 kV high-voltage composite insulators as the research object, this paper establishes multi-physics coupling simulation models under three typical extreme weather conditions using COMSOL Multiphysics. The influence laws of different weather conditions on the electric field intensity in various directions and the surface electric field distortion of insulators are systematically analyzed. Key regional electric field parameters are extracted through simulation to clarify the influence degree of different extreme weather events, and the correlation between electric field distortion characteristics and operation risks is analyzed. The results show that short-duration heavy precipitation and rainstorm with strong wind exert the most significant distortion effect on the electric field distribution of insulators. The uniformity of electric field distribution is relatively poor under hail weather, and the edges of insulator sheds are always the high-incidence areas of electric field concentration. The research results provide theoretical support and data reference for the insulation design, risk assessment of composite insulators and safety protection of transmission lines under extreme weather, and possess important engineering value for improving the adaptability of high-voltage transmission lines to extreme weather.

title: A Computationally Efficient Hierarchical MPC with Adaptive Horizon for Real-Time Electro-Thermal Control of IGBTs
authors: Yi Liu, Hongtao Niu, Yunhui Mei
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: High-fidelity electro-thermal models are essential for accurately predicting and controlling the junction temperature of Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistors (IGBTs). However, integrating these complex models into a Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework imposes a prohibitive computational burden, rendering real-time implementation infeasible. To resolve this conflict between model fidelity and computational tractability, this paper proposes a Hierarchical Adaptive-Horizon MPC (HAH-MPC) framework. The architecture decouples the control problem into a two-level structure: a slow-timescale outer loop for thermal management, which utilizes a high-fidelity coupled model, and a fast-timescale inner loop for current tracking. The key feature is an adaptive-horizon mechanism within the outer loop that dynamically adjusts the prediction length based on system operating conditions, effectively balancing control performance with computational load. The proposed controller is implemented and experimentally validated on a 15-kW test bench. Experimental results demonstrate that the HAH-MPC effectively limits junction temperature overshoot during severe load transients, a significant improvement over conventional methods, while maintaining its worst-case execution time safely within the 1-ms control period. This work presents a viable framework for deploying advanced, model-based thermal control strategies in practical power electronic applications.

title: Multi-objective Optimization Scheduling for Integrated Energy Systems Considering Exergy Efficiency and Stepped Carbon Trading
authors: Jinlong Wu, Xing Dong, Wenlong Ding, Huaiyu Xing, Xiaobo Chi, Changlong Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Traditional optimization strategies for integrated energy systems (IES) predominantly focus on the balance of energy quantity while overlooking energy quality matching, often resulting in significant exergy destruction due to high-grade energy degradation. Furthermore, exergy efficiency is typically treated as an evaluation metric rather than an active optimization objective. To address these issues, an IES multi-objective optimization scheduling model is proposed, which incorporates exergy efficiency as a key objective and introduces a stepped carbon trading mechanism to achieve low-carbon, cost-effective, and high-quality system operation. The ε-constraint method is adopted to generate the Pareto front, and a Pareto front curvature analysis (PFCA) method is proposed to determine the optimal compromise solution that balances economic performance and exergy efficiency. Case studies validate the comprehensive superiority of the proposed method in achieving high-quality, low-carbon, and economic operation of the IES studied.

title: Degradation-Aware Stochastic Sizing of Second-Life Battery Energy Storage System for Microgrids
authors: Yifang Ren, Qiuwei Wu, Guocheng Song, Tianyi Zhao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The optimal sizing of second-life battery energy storage systems (SLBESS) in microgrids is influenced not only by operating cost, but also by battery degradation, renewable and load uncertainty, and distribution-network constraints. To address this issue, this paper proposes a degradation-aware stochastic sizing model for SLBESS in a radial microgrid. In the proposed formulation, battery degradation is explicitly modeled by a state-of-charge-weighted throughput method and embedded into the sizing problem through a degradation cost term. On this basis, a two-stage stochastic optimization framework is established to coordinate SLBESS sizing and microgrid operation scenario-based uncertainty. The model jointly considers electricity purchasing cost, degradation cost, and demand charge, while network constraints are represented by a linearized power flow formulation. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, a modified five-bus radial microgrid is adopted as the test system. The results show that the proposed method can reduce the annual electricity bill and suppress peak grid import while satisfying the voltage and branch-flow constraints of the microgrid. After accounting for the annualized storage cost, the system still maintains a positive net annual benefit. Overall, the proposed model provides an effective approach for SLBESS sizing in microgrids while explicitly considering battery degradation, uncertainty, and network constraints.

title: Intention to Continue Using Information Technology in Accounting Information Systems–The Mediating Role of Information Technology Mindfulness
authors: Thuan Luong Duc, Huong Truong Thi Thu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Information and Network Technologies (ICINT)
abstract: The study applies the Expectation Confirmation Model (ECM) to investigate the impact of key factors on the intention to continue using the Information Technology (IT) of accountants and users of Accounting Information System (AIS) at public educational institutions in Vietnam. The research employs a quantitative method, surveying 171 participants. The Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) technique, utilizing SmartPLS 4.1 software, is employed to analyze the data. The research results clarify the relationships in the proposed model, where all hypotheses are accepted. Notably, IT mindfulness has the strongest influence on the intention to continue using IT. The research results propose theoretical implications and practical management implications to enhance perceived usefulness and IT mindfulness in maintaining the use of IT at public educational institutions in Vietnam.

title: Workshop: Fostering Quality Participation and Critical Thinking in Hybrid Classrooms with the Whytson App
authors: Mexitli Sandoval-Reyes, Maria Ballesteros, Ricardo Ganem
abstract: This interactive online workshop introduces “Whytson” a novel mobile application designed to gamify and enhance student participation in educational settings. Traditional methods of eliciting student questions often fail to engage the entire class, limiting the development of critical thinking and social intelligence. Whytson addresses this by providing a structured platform for students to request turns to speak, with instructors providing real-time, qualitative feedback on their contributions. Originally developed and tested in face-to-face bachelor's degree courses, this workshop will adapt the Whytson methodology for online and hybrid environments. Participants (primarily educators and educational innovators) will experience the app from a student's and instructor's perspective, engage in collaborative discussion on its pedagogical foundations, and brainstorm implementation strategies for their own contexts. The session is grounded in constructivist learning theories, neuroscience of learning, and gamification principles. Furthermore, the workshop will serve as a live research session to validate our instruments (measuring empathy, critical thinking, technology adoption, and engagement) with an expert audience. Attendees will leave with a practical understanding of how technology can be used to foster a culture of respectful, empathetic, and high-quality classroom dialogue.

title: Cu Via Resistance Failure Induced by CMP in Advanced Interconnects
authors: Wong Kian Hui, Leong Voon Kiong, Tubati Sricharan, Peck Yan Zheng
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Elevated electrical via resistance in copper (Cu) top vias is attributed to insufficient CU removal during Platen 2 (P2) Chemical Mechanical Planarization (CMP), which is responsible for soft Cu landing and stops at Ta barrier by endpoint detection. Failure analysis based on Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) identified barrier metal cracking, Cu voids, and Cu migration from top via (TV) into the top metal (TM). Simulations confirm that reduced polishing from P2 process results in residual Cu remaining and subsequently induced a longer higher frictional force during barrier polishing on Platen 3 (P3), causing microstructural damage at the Cu/barrier interface. These defects, exacerbated by subsequent high-temperature processes, compromise via integrity and further increase resistance. The study highlights the need for optimized CMP endpoint control and uniform Cu removal to ensure interconnect reliability in advanced semiconductor fabrication.

title: A Study on the Impact of Extraction Electrode Axes on Implant Dose in Applied Materials E500 Medium Current Implanter
authors: Yik Han Kwee, King Suen Lee, Ching Chee Chu
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This study describes how the extraction electrode axes can have an adverse impact on the implant dose in the Applied Materials (AMAT) E500 medium current implanter. The extraction electrode, through its beam steering capability, influences the ion beam trajectory, which in turn affects the ion beam’s parallelism — a critical factor for accurate dose calculations during implantation in this implanter. Experiments were conducted using B⁺ implants at 200 keV under conditions of proper and improper extraction electrode axes tuning, with beam parallelism quantified using delta deflector voltage (DDV). DDV was found to be significantly higher under the improper axes tuning condition which indicated a greater degree of non-parallelism. Despite meeting the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) specifications for beam parallelism at 100 kV, the wafers implanted under improper tuning with B⁺ implants at 200 keV exhibited up to 5% overdose, through sheet resistivity measurements and dynamic secondary ion mass spectrometry (DSIMS) analysis. These findings highlight the importance of monitoring extraction electrode axes positions and DDV to ensure implant accuracy and minimize dose deviations. The findings also suggest that the OEM specifications for beam parallelism may be insufficient for higher energy implants and additional beam parallelism verification at larger energy ranges should be performed to ensure accurate ion implantation in the AMAT E500 medium current implanter.

title: Wavelet-Domain Masked Image Modeling for Color-Consistent HDR Video Reconstruction
authors: Yang Zhang, Zhangkai Ni, Wenhan Yang, Hanli Wang
abstract: High Dynamic Range (HDR) video reconstruction aims to recover fine brightness, color, and details from Low Dynamic Range (LDR) videos. However, existing methods often suffer from color inaccuracies and temporal inconsistencies. To address these challenges, we propose WMNet, a novel HDR video reconstruction network that leverages Wavelet domain Masked Image Modeling (W-MIM). WMNet adopts a two-phase training strategy: In Phase I, W-MIM performs self-reconstruction pre-training by selectively masking color and detail information in the wavelet domain, enabling the network to develop robust color restoration capabilities. A curriculum learning scheme further refines the reconstruction process. Phase II fine-tunes the model using the pre-trained weights to improve the final reconstruction quality. To improve temporal consistency, we introduce the Temporal Mixture of Experts (T-MoE) module and the Dynamic Memory Module (DMM). T-MoE adaptively fuses adjacent frames to reduce flickering artifacts, while DMM captures long-range dependencies, ensuring smooth motion and preservation of fine details. Additionally, since existing HDR video datasets lack scene-based segmentation, we reorganize HDRTV4K into HDRTV4K-Scene, establishing a new benchmark for HDR video reconstruction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that WMNet achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple evaluation metrics, significantly improving color fidelity, temporal coherence, and perceptual quality.

title: ROBBO: An Efficient Method for Pareto Front Estimation With Guaranteed Accuracy
authors: Roberto Boffadossi, Marco Leonesio, Lorenzo Fagiano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: A new method to estimate the Pareto front (PF) in bi-objective optimization problems (BOPs) is presented. Assuming a continuous PF, the approach, named ROBBO (RObust and Balanced BOP), needs to sample at most a finite, precomputed number of PF points. Upon termination, it guarantees that the worst case approximation error lies within a desired tolerance range, predefined by the decision maker (DM), for each of the two objective functions. Theoretical results are derived about the worst case number of PF samples required to guarantee the desired accuracy, both in general and for specific sampling methods from the literature. A comparative analysis, both theoretical and numerical, demonstrates the superiority of the proposed method with respect to popular ones. The approach is finally showcased in a constrained path-following problem for a 2-axis positioning system and in a steady-state optimization problem for a continuous-flow stirred tank reactor (CSTR). An open demo implementation of ROBBO is made available online.

title: Stray Current Mitigation With Reverse Running Rail Current Control Strategy for Virtual-Return-Based DC Traction Power Supply System
authors: Miao Wang, Jingyue Lu, Wenzhong Ma, Hailiang Xu, Pingjuan Ge, Xiaofeng Yang, Trillion Q. Zheng
abstract: The virtual-return-based DC traction power supply system (VRTPS) has been studied to solve the stray current and rail potential issues in urban rail transit. By utilizing the power electronic devices (PEDs) and return line, VRTPS constructs the zero-resistance virtual return path (VRP) by diverting traction current from the running rail into this newly established path. However, the mitigation performance of existing VRTPS control strategies is still suboptimal, leading to incomplete suppression of stray current and rail potential within the train-occupied section. Thus, the reverse running rail current control strategy of VRTPS is proposed to further reduce the stray current and rail potential. Firstly, the topology and modeling of VRTPS are investigated to analyze the inherent limitations of the ideal zero-resistance VRP. Secondly, the detailed analysis of the reverse running rail current control for VRTPS is presented, which optimizes the running rail current distribution with the variable-resistance VRP. Thirdly, the comparative researches on both the stray current and rail potential distribution are conducted between the conventional system and VRTPS with different control strategies. Finally, the correctness and effectiveness of VRTPS with proposed strategy on the stray current and rail potential mitigation are validated through simulation and experimental results.

title: A Data-Driven Algorithm for Indoor/Outdoor Detection Based on 3GPP MDT measures
authors: Giuliano Muratore, Davide Micheli, Roberto Verdone
abstract: Using cellular networks outdoors generally leads to terminals experiencing elevated power levels and increased interference from neighboring cells. These conditions are significantly different from those encountered by the same terminals when they are indoors, where absorption caused by building walls reduces the available serving cell received power and mitigates interference from other cells. Conditions where mobile radio services are predominantly used by terminals located outdoors or, conversely, predominantly used within buildings, typically mix depending on the city area, the specific time, or peculiar circumstances. Therefore, the ability to track the evolution of the dominant indoor or outdoor context within a network over time is crucial for automatically adjusting, optimizing, and enhancing radiomobile service quality. Although numerous methods exist for estimating a device's indoor or outdoor status, based on specific positioning strategies, our approach stands apart due to its real-time applicability to a large-scale set of smartphones across any current 4G/5G 3GPP radiomobile network worldwide—something not achieved by other solutions in the literature. The proposed model is validated using an extensive dataset sourced from a live 5G network access.

title: Enhancing Secondary Use of Health Data in Finland – Environments, Costs, and Potential EHDS Implementation Challenges
authors: Hannu Vilpponen, Antti Piirainen, Maari Parkkinen, Tommi Mikkonen
publish: Proceedings of the 41st ACM/SIGAPP Symposium on Applied Computing
abstract: None

title: Fixed-Time Synchronization of Delayed Chaotic Memristive Neural Networks Modeling in Terms of Voltage-Charge-Time
authors: Yanting Huang, Jiening Wu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper addresses fixed-time interlayer synchronization for delayed chaotic duplex memristive neural networks (MNNs) . First, a continuous MNNs model is established using a charge-controlled HP memristor operating in the voltage-charge-time domain (x, q, t), with such memristors are treated as time-continuous and dynamically varying parameters. Subsequently, novel fixed-time (FXT) controllers are developed based on Lyapunov functions and inequality techniques, ensuring complete synchronization of voltage and charge states between the two layers of MNN within a fixed settling time, independent of initial conditions. The correctness and effectiveness of the proposed method are verified by numerical simulation.

title: MERIT: Multimodal Enhanced Retrieval through Instruction-level Transformer-based Chunking in Product Manuals
authors: Trinh The Minh, Van Duc Cuong, Le Hong Ha Anh, Nguyen Van Son, Huynh Cong Phap, Nguyen Thi Hanh
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: Product manuals are critical in helping users understand and operate consumer products. Yet, their increasing length and reliance on complex graphical elements often frustrate information retrieval. Traditional Product Manual Question Answering (PMQA) systems primarily focus on text and overlook visual content such as icons and diagrams, resulting in incomplete or ambiguous answers. This gap highlights the need for a multimodal approach that integrates textual and visual cues to ensure accurate and contextually grounded support. In this paper, we introduce MERIT, a novel multimodal QA framework that advances beyond page-level and text-only baselines. MERIT segments manuals into fine-grained, semantically coherent instruction chunks and encodes them using a Mixture-of-Experts Q-Former encoder, which effectively fuses textual summaries with graphical features. Trained under a contrastive learning objective, MERIT constructs a unified embedding space optimized for dense retrieval. A vision-language model then processes chunks and their associated graphics to generate accurate, fluent, and visually grounded answers. Extensive experiments on the PM209 dataset demonstrate that MERIT consistently improves retrieval precision and answer quality over state-of-the-art baselines, underscoring the importance of fine-grained multimodal reasoning for practical e-commerce applications.

title: Trajectory Tracking Control for Inspection Robots via Tube-Based Linear Model Predictive Control
authors: Haojie Cheng, Wei Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Conventional Linear Model Predictive Control (LMPC) strategies often suffer from trajectory divergence under persistent external disturbances, potentially leading to violations of safety constraints. To address this challenge in the context of unmanned inspection robots, this paper proposes a robust Tube-Based Linear Model Predictive Control (Tube-LMPC) framework. By incorporating uncertainty tubes into the LMPC formulation, the proposed method constrains the system states within a time-varying tubular region surrounding the nominal trajectory. This mechanism guarantees that the actual tracking trajectory remains bounded within the tube despite the presence of disturbances and model uncertainties. Simulation results demonstrate that the Tube-LMPC controller significantly reduces trajectory deviations and enhances tracking stability compared to traditional approaches. Furthermore, the method ensures that the robot operates strictly within safe boundaries, validating its effectiveness for substation inspection scenarios where high safety standards and strong disturbance rejection capabilities are paramount.

title: Momentum-Based Cryptocurrency Trading Strategy : Data Synthesis and Machine Learning for Predictive Signal Classification
authors: Maryam AlBahri, Chinthaka Dinesh
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: This study proposes a momentum-based machine learning framework for weekly Bitcoin trading signal classification, whose novelty lies in the unification of economically inspired feature design, temporally aware validation, and interpretable modeling to achieve high predictive accuracy without relying on resource-intensive deep learning architectures, using a multi-source dataset that integrates technical indicators, on-chain metrics, economic variables, and event-driven sentiment features. The task is modeled as a three-class classification problem (Buy, Hold, Sell) under class imbalance and temporal dependencies. Data preprocessing involved manual feature construction and alignment from raw sources, guided by financial domain expertise and economic theory-driven selection. The dataset was partitioned chronologically, allocating $15 \%$ for validation and $15 \%$ for final testing to preserve temporal order and prevent data leakage. Multiple classifiers of varying algorithmic complexity were evaluated using walk-forward and rolling-window validation schemes designed to emulate real deployment conditions. Rolling-window validation consistently yielded superior temporal stability, demonstrating the effectiveness of recency-weighted learning in non-stationary financial environments. Ensemble learning represented in Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) with rolling window training achieved the highest performance, attaining $91.0 \%$ accuracy, an F1-score of 0.87, and a ROC-AUC of 0.98 on the test set. Limitations include class sparsity and imperfect threshold calibration, highlighting the need for future work in probabilistic decision tuning.

title: Two-Stage Variable-Decoupling Framework for Trajectory Optimization of Industrial Manipulators
authors: Gu Li, Jin Huang, Zichuan Wu, Qi Gao, Yong Jia, Gang Li
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper presents a two-stage trajectory optimization framework for point-to-point motions of industrial manipulators. The problem is formulated as a nonconvex optimization model with kinematic constraints, in which segment durations and waypoint offsets are treated as decision variables. In the first stage, TVAC-PSO performs a global joint search over both variable groups. In the second stage, the segment durations are fixed and the waypoint offsets are further refined by L-BFGS-B, thereby improving smoothness without altering the execution time obtained in the first stage. Experiments over three representative scenarios and 30 independent runs show that, relative to TVAC-PSO alone, the proposed method reduces the composite objective by 4.66%–4.77% and the average RMS jerk of the six joints by 20.61%–22.18%.

title: A Hybrid ML Framework Integrating CAI and EFRI Metrics for Smart Predictive Maintenance of Power Transformers
authors: T.S.Suganya, S. Rukmani Devi, A. Belin Jude, Kada Tulasi, Sameer Yadav, Preshni Shrivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Power transformers produce rich multimodal telemetry (SCADA, DGA, FRA/EFRI, thermal imaging) that can enable predictive maintenance, but practical adoption is hindered by delayed fault detection, fragile performance when sensors are missing or asynchronous, and poorly calibrated risk outputs. The study presents Synapse-PdM, a physics-aware, causal, Bayesian multimodal Transformer that fuses modality-specific encoders with a causal disentanglement module, a hierarchical cross-modal mixture-of-experts Transformer, and a Temporal Graph Neural Network to capture topology-driven propagation. Synapse-PdM issues Bayesian survival-based EFRI curves and conformalized RUL quantiles, trained under a composite loss combining censored survival likelihood, quantile RUL losses, physics-informed regularization, causal penalties, self-supervised pretraining, and a decision-aware cost term. On held-out time-split evaluations Synapse-PdM achieves AUC-ROC 0.995, PR-AUC 0.968, RUL-MAE 4.8 days, and detects 82% of failures at least seven days ahead; Brier score is 0.060 and expected annual maintenance cost under the test cost model reduces to ₹9.15M. These results translate to earlier, better-calibrated alerts, fewer false alarms, and substantive operational savings, supporting pilot deployment in utility environments. The model’s interpretability and uncertainty outputs enable actionable maintenance decisions and facilitate regulator acceptance and scalability.

title: An Ensemble Machine Learning Model for Thwarting Impact of Vampire Nodes in WSNs
authors: Pediredla Srilatha, Suneel Pappala, Sripada Rama Sree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: In Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs), the resource depletion attacks termed vampire attack influence the network factors such as energy consumption, bandwidth and link quality. This vampire attack performs route depletion which has the probability of draining the battery from the nodes that eventually resulted in poor network performance. In this paper, a Weight-Optimized Extreme Gradient Boosting-based Machine learning (WOEGBML) model is proposed for determining potential features from the network and explore it extensively for detecting and isolating vampire attacks. It specifically used an adaptive weight determination method which computed weights associated with the network factors considered for detection such that vampire attack is accurately isolated from the network. It further used k-fold validation strategy for handling the problem of overfitting and enhancing the degree of generalization. It is proposed with the capability of exploring multiple numbers of features that contributed towards vampire attack detection and quantify their impact during the launching process. It specifically handled the limitations of class imbalance during the use of datasets which are considered for experimental evaluation. The results of this machine learning model under different density of vampire attacks maximized packet delivery rate and throughput with minimized latency and energy utilization rate.

title: A Temperature Compensation Method for MSR Monitoring under In-Service Condition using EMAT
