title: Towards Next-Generation SLAM: A Survey on 3DGS-SLAM Focusing on Performance, Robustness, and Future Directions
authors: Li Wang, Ruixuan Gong, Yumo Han, Lei Yang, Lu Yang, Ying Li, Bin Xu, Huaping Liu, Rong Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Traditional Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) systems often face limitations including coarse rendering quality, insufficient recovery of scene details, and poor robustness in dynamic environments. 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS), with its efficient explicit representation and high-quality rendering capabilities, offers a new reconstruction paradigm for SLAM. This survey comprehensively reviews key technical approaches for integrating 3DGS with SLAM. We analyze performance optimization of representative methods across four critical dimensions: rendering quality, tracking accuracy, reconstruction speed, and memory consumption, delving into their design principles and breakthroughs. We further discuss the trade-offs among these dimensions and their implications for platform deployment. Furthermore, we examine methods for enhancing the robustness of 3DGS-SLAM in complex environments such as motion blur, dynamic environments, and edge platforms. Finally, we discuss challenges and future trends in this area. This survey aims to provide a technical reference for researchers and foster the development of next-generation SLAM systems characterized by high fidelity, efficiency, and robustness.

title: DyGIN: Modality-Unified Dynamic Graph Inception Network for Context-Aware Emotion Recognition
authors: Yingxue Gao, Huan Zhao, Zixing Zhang
abstract: Graph representation learning has attracted considerable attention for its ability to generate node representations by aggregating information from neighboring nodes. However, existing graph-based methods for sequential data merely focus on a static graph constructed on an entire unimodal sequence, largely ignoring their dynamic evolution. To overcome this limitation, we introduce a modality-unified Dynamic Graph Inception Network (DyGIN) that models dynamic evolutionary graphs for different modalities. DyGIN constructs dynamic graphs from temporal subsequences using a sliding window, and incorporates a Temporal Graph Evolution Gated Recurrent Unit (TGE-GRU) to update graph weights at each segment. Additionally, we propose a context-aware similarity matrix, which updates node representations based on the degree of neighboring nodes, replacing the traditional averaging method. Our model is optimized with a combination of graph structure loss, classification loss, and a learnable pooling function. We validate DyGIN on emotion recognition tasks across three public datasets—RML, IEMOCAP, and DEAP—where it outperforms state-of-the-art models by 1.6% on RML, 1.81% and 2.33% on IEMOCAP, and 2.78% and 6.81% on DEAP, based on accuracy and weighted F1 score. Our code is available at https://github.com/G22-web/DyGIN.

title: Multi-Static Integrated Sensing, Identification and Backscatter Communication for IoT Networks
authors: Songmin Li, Ying-Chang Liang
abstract: In this paper, a multi-static integrated sensing, identification and backscatter communication (ISIBC) system is proposed for Internet-of-Things (IoT) networks, where the backscatter devices (BDs) attached to the targets utilize ambient radio frequency (RF) signals for data transmission and multiple receivers are used for target localization. Each receiver estimates the directions of arrival (DoAs) of the incident signals from the RF source, the targets with BDs and the environmental scatterers, detects the activities of each BD, and demodulates the symbols transmitted by each active BD. With the DoA estimation and activity detection results, the receivers are required to identify the targets, i.e., associating the estimated DoAs with the corresponding targets attached with active BDs. These tasks, however, are challenging as the ambient RF signal is unknown. To tackle this issue, we propose a semi-blind generalized likelihood ratio test (GLRT) detector to estimate the activities of the BDs, which tests the homogeneity of the received signal samples over different BD symbol periods without requiring any information about the ambient RF signal and backscattering channels. A constant false alarm rate (CFAR) threshold is derived for the proposed GLRT detector. Further, we propose a novel semi-blind eigenvector-based identification method to identify the targets without using knowledge of the ambient RF signal. The eigenvector-based demodulator is proposed to demodulate the symbols transmitted by active BDs. Finally, the estimation and identification results from each receiver are utilized to jointly localize the active BDs. Extensive simulation results are presented to show the effectiveness of the proposed methods for the studied ISIBC system.

title: Meta-Learning-Enhanced Task Assignment and Resource Scheduling for UAV-Assisted WSNs in 6G-Enabled ITS
authors: Mesfin Leranso Betalo, Amr Mohamed, Amin Sharafian, Zongze Wu, Jianqiang Li, Xiaoshan Bai
abstract: The integration of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), wireless sensor networks (WSNs), and 6 G technologies is transforming the design of next-generation Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), enabling real-time, energy-efficient, and adaptive urban mobility services. This paper proposes a novel meta-learning-enhanced UAV-assisted WSN architecture, tailored for large-scale, dynamic ITS environments characterized by high vehicular mobility, dense sensor deployments, and stringent latency requirements. We introduce a new task assignment framework to address critical challenges such as coverage limitations at urban intersections, energy constraints of sensor nodes, and the need for rapid decision-making under complex traffic conditions. We aim to maximize energy-efficient data throughput (EEDT) and ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) in UAV-assisted WSNs. The framework jointly optimizes traffic sensor node selection, UAV trajectory planning, and communication resource allocation by formulating the problem as a Constrained Markov Decision Process (CMDP). To solve this, we develop the Meta-Learning Weighted Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MW-MAD3PG) algorithm, which embeds model-agnostic meta-learning (MAML) within a cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning (MADRL) setting. MW-MAD3PG enables UAV agents to rapidly adapt to dynamic traffic patterns, fluctuating road conditions, and evolving network states with minimal retraining, ensuring high energy efficiency and system scalability. Extensive simulations show that the proposed framework achieves up to a 25% improvement in UAV coordination efficiency, a 30% increase in data offloading capacity, and substantial enhancements in energy-aware operations compared to baseline methods such as MADDPG, Meta-SGD, and Meta-Q-Learning. These results validate our architecture and framework as robust, intelligent solutions for future 6G-enabled ITS, supporting resilient, real-time, and energy-optimized traffic monitoring and control.

title: Lightweight Steganalysis with Low Distortion for Countering Robust Image Steganography
authors: Qianqian Zhang, Yinhong Huang, Duo Ma, Xiangyang Luo, Yuanyuan Ma, Ning Ruan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Robust image steganography in social networks presents significant challenges for steganalysis. Existing methods exploit low payload visibility and resistance to evade conventional passive steganalysis, leading to high false-positive and missed detection rates, which undermines reliable verification and poses a significant security threat to cyberspace. Active steganalysis is designed to eliminate hidden information through controlled modifications. However, existing methods usually encounter challenges such as a decline in the visual quality of processed images, excessive training costs of the models, and dependence on the image size. All these have limited the practical application of active steganalysis. To address this issue, we propose a block-based multi-objective analysis framework, which integrates dual-block constraints with multi-objective loss optimization. This framework achieves adaptive image partitioning by dividing the image into non-overlapping 2×2 pixel blocks. The partitioning simultaneously satisfies distortion boundary constraints and luminance preservation requirements. A multi-objective loss function is proposed to eliminate redundant information effectively while maintaining image visual clarity. The weights of loss function are set based on evolutionary optimization. Furthermore, the performance of the proposed method is enhanced by a linear-time complexity depth-first search algorithm, which minimizes the PSB loss under block-level constraints. The optimization process incorporates perturbation-based refinement to facilitate global convergence. Extensive experiments conducted on the BOSSBase-1.01 and CelebA datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed framework in defending against robust image steganography prevalent in social networks. Evaluations across different payload settings indicate that the proposed method achieves an average PSNR improvement of approximately 34% over AO-Net across both BOSSBase-1.01 and CelebA datasets. Furthermore, the framework processes standard test images with a latency of 6.4ms, without requiring any preset image size.

title: Geometry-Dependent PCRLB for Target Tracking in Clutter With Radar Measurement Uncertainty
authors: Yifang Shi, Yu Zhang, Lingjiao Fu, Dongliang Peng, Qiang Lu, Haewoon Nam, Alfonso Farina
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: In realistic radar tracking scenarios, the target measurement uncertainty (TMU)—encompassing both detection probability and measurement error covariance—is strongly influenced by the target-to-radar (T2R) geometry. However, the existing posterior Cramér–Rao lower bounds (PCRLBs) largely overlook the fundamental impact of T2R geometry on the TMU and, consequently, on the mean square error (MSE) of state estimation, often leading to overly conservative bounds. To bridge this gap, this paper first develops a generalized model of target measurement error covariance for bistatic radar systems with moving transmitters and receivers, in which the impact of T2R geometry on the error covariance is explicitly characterized. Based upon this TMU formulation, we subsequently derive a geometry-dependent PCRLB (GD-PCRLB) that fully incorporates both measurement origin uncertainty and geometry-dependent TMU. In this derivation, both detection probability and measurement error covariance are treated as state-dependent parameters when differentiating the log-likelihood function with respect to the target state. Unlike existing PCRLBs that partially or completely ignore the geometry-dependent nature of TMU, the proposed GD-PCRLB captures substantial performance benefits by extracting additional Fisher information arising from geometry-dependent TMU. The resulted GD-PCRLB provides a significantly less conservative MSE lower bound compared to existing bounds that only partially or fully neglect the influence of T2R geometry on TMU. Numerical results demonstrate that the improvement offered by the GD-PCRLB becomes more pronounced as the level of TMU increases.

title: Quantum Enhanced Data-driven Learning for Defect Reduction in Etching Process
authors: Zusing Yang, Li-Wei Wang, Yi-Che Chen, Yi-Han Chao, Cheng-En Tsai, Ming-Tsung Wu, Hong-Ji Lee, Nan-Tzu Lian, Tahone Yang, Kuang-Chao Chen, Chih-Yuan Lu
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Defect reduction in plasma etching is a critical challenge in semiconductor manufacturing, traditionally addressed through empirical tuning of multiple process parameters with significant resource and time consumption. This study presents a quantum-enhanced data-driven learning framework that integrates quantum neural networks (QNN), quantum annealing (QA), and quantum evolution (QE) to optimize 25 etching parameters simultaneously for improved defect mitigation and profile control. The quantum circuits and annealing procedures were simulated using PennyLane’s variational quantum simulator backend. The proposed approach enables extrapolative prediction beyond historical domain knowledge, overcoming limitations of conventional machine learning methods such as artificial neural networks (ANNs). Experimental results demonstrate a substantial reduction in total defect density from 16.39 to 0.42 defects/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> while maintaining stable etching critical dimension (ECD) control. Notably, QE uncovers hidden sensitivities in parameters previously held constant, highlighting the potential of quantum-enhanced learning to both optimize processes and reveal latent manufacturing insights. This work underscores the promise of hybrid quantum-classical machine learning frameworks as the means for systematic process optimization in advanced semiconductor fabrication.

title:
CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    emission prediction based on the Type 2 Fuzzy Recurrent Neural Network (T2FRNN)
authors: Moammad Fiuzy, Saber Ghasemiyan Mazaher, Ali Mousavi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: The deterioration of atmospheric quality has become a pressing global environmental concern. The focus has always been on reducing or avoiding environmentally harmful gas emissions. This study introduces an original methodology for estimating levels of carbon dioxide emissions. A new modeling framework based on a Type-2 Fuzzy Recurrent Neural Network (T2FRNN) is proposed, using real-world data obtained from Delhi, one of the world’s most heavily polluted urban areas. We have also presented the new learning algorithm for this purpose and also proposed a method that has shown satisfactory prediction accuracy, with a noteworthy value of 10units for its prediction RMSE error, signifying its effective functioning.

title: An Interpretable and Efficient KNN-Based Approach for Object Segmentation
authors: Sina Saadati, Abdolah Sepahvand
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Computer vision and image segmentation play a critical role in diverse applications, including industry, urban infrastructure, and medical procedures. Deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are widely used for object segmentation; however, they demand high computational resources, leading to increased energy consumption. In this study, we propose a lightweight KNN-based algorithm for image segmentation that reduces computational cost. The method requires the user to select a few points around the target object, after which the algorithm automatically segments the object of interest with minimal computation. Since many objects can be distinguished by color, the proposed approach enables efficient image annotation and segmentation, producing labeled data that can be used to train deep CNNs. This method offers a practical solution for reducing computational overhead while supporting computer vision tasks.

title: Multi-Time-Scale Flexible Regulation Capability Evaluation and Bi-Level Optimal Configuration of Integrated Energy Systems Considering an Improved Sparrow Search Algorithm
authors: Yishu Kong, Guangqian Ding, Jinhan Zhao, Zhuoqun Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of insufficient flexibility and high penalty costs caused by severe source-load fluctuations in high-penetration renewable energy microgrids . This paper proposes a bi-level optimization configuration model that takes into account flexibility across multi-time-scales. The upper level employs improved sparrow search algorithm(ISSA) for global optimization of flexibility resource capacity, while the lower level relies on MILP to realize refined day-ahead and intra-day multi-time-scale scheduling. The study analyzed the trans-medium flexibility mechanism under multi-physics field coupling. Through simulation, it revealed the envelope accommodation capability of the low-inertia electrolyzer at noon for the overflow of photovoltaic power, as well as the economic arbitrage phenomenon where redundant electrical energy is transferred to P2G hydrogen production under the rigid constraints of the system at night. The results show that, compared with the traditional single- level planning, the ISSA-MILP bi-level framework proposed in this paper reduces the penalty for short-term flexibility deficiency and achieves high proportion of local consumption of new energy, and lowers the comprehensive annualized cost of the system over its full life cycle. This research provides quantitative theoretical support for the flexible autonomy and economic operation of future integrated energy systems(IES).

title: Research on Control Strategy for Seamless Transition Between Grid-Connected and Islanded Modes
authors: ZhiWei Wang, Xu Jun Ma, Xiao Wei Li, Kai Zhang, XuHuan Zhao, Cheng Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Seamless transitions between grid-connected and islanded modes in hybrid microgrids are critical for uninterruptible power and system stability. However, dynamic mode transitions frequently cause voltage flicker, transient current surges, and phase jumps. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a comprehensive seamless transition control strategy for T-type neutral-point-clamped (NPC) energy storage converters. For the grid-to-island transition, an improved dual-loop control method with load current feedforward is introduced. Pre-setting the integrator states ensures continuous controller output across the transition boundary, effectively suppressing DC-bus voltage fluctuations and transient current surges. Conversely, during the island-to-grid recovery, a real-time phase pre-synchronization loop adjusts the converter’s output angular frequency to track the grid phase. This guarantees smooth and continuous phase alignment before the dynamic reconnection. Underpinning these strategies, the control loops are modeled and parameterized using standard Type-I and Type-II system theories. Simulation results verify that the proposed approach successfully eliminates transient surges and achieves high-quality bidirectional seamless transitions.

title: Fast Partitioning Strategy for Three-Phase Unbalanced Distribution Networks Based on Electrical Coupling Strength
authors: Jiaxiao Wang, Yan Liu, Yanyan Liang, Can Liu, Yaya Xu, Yan Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasingly widespread application of distributed computing in distribution network analysis, efficient network partitioning methods have become a crucial foundation for supporting parallel computing and distributed state estimation. This paper proposes a partitioning strategy suitable for three-phase unbalanced distribution networks. Based on the inherent electrical characteristics of the network, the strategy constructs a partitioning model for distribution network scenarios without distributed generation. First, the electrical distance is defined using the node impedance matrix to quantify the coupling strength between nodes. On this basis, a community detection algorithm with modularity optimization is adopted for initial partitioning, resulting in multiple highly cohesive initial subareas. Subsequently, a merging strategy is proposed, which iteratively merges the adjacent subareas with the strongest electrical coupling and the smallest scale until a predefined number of partitions is achieved. This ensures tight electrical connections within each subarea while maintaining relatively balanced scales across subareas.

title: A Distribution Network Situation Awareness Method Based on Multi-Time-Scale Hybrid Measurements
authors: Yan Liu, Jiaxiao Wang, Yanyan Liang, Chengcheng Wu, Peng Xu, Yaya Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of active distribution networks, measurement data sources have become increasingly diverse, and different types of data are updated at inconsistent rates. Therefore, characterizing uncertainty and achieving reliable situation awareness under multi-time-scale hybrid measurements has become an urgent challenge. This paper first proposes a distribution-network situation awareness method based on multi-time-scale hybrid measurements. To fuse real-time data, historical data, and short-term forecast data, a sliding-window mechanism is used to temporally align them to a unified evaluation instant. Then, affine arithmetic is adopted to model multi-source uncertainties in a unified manner: correlation among data from different sources is captured via shared error terms, while random errors are described by the inherent noise of each data source, ultimately transforming uncertainty into interval-valued state estimates. Finally, a risk situation index is constructed based on the upper and lower bounds of the voltage intervals, and comparative experiments are conducted on the improved IEEE 13-bus and improved IEEE 33-bus test systems. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method improves state prediction accuracy under incomplete measurements and correlated errors, providing interpretable situation information and technical support for distribution-network operational risk assessment and active dispatch.

title: Research Progress on Flexible Interconnection Devices for Active Distribution Networks
authors: Ran Chen, Peng Zhang, Wei Bao, Chengzhi Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The continuous growth of distributed generation and loads is driving the evolution of distribution networks towards greater flexibility and intelligence. As a key enabler of this transformation, flexible interconnection devices can effectively enhance the controllability, reliability, and renewable energy hosting capacity of distribution networks. This paper firstly outlines the basic structures and operational principles of typical flexible interconnection devices. Subsequently, it systematically summarizes the current research status and challenges in key technologies within the planning and design dimension. Finally, the paper delves into the future application bottlenecks and development trends in this field, aiming to provide valuable references for in-depth research and the scaled deployment of flexible interconnected distribution networks.

title: An Intra-Area VSC-HVDC Back-to-Back Retrofit Scheme Based on Transfer Impedance
authors: Zuohong Li, Feng Li, Hua Pan, Haochun Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of power grid scale and the large-scale integration of renewable energy, short-circuit current levels in the China Southern Power Grid have increased markedly, posing severe challenges to the safe operation of densely meshed AC networks. Existing mitigation measures, including inter-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back links, are primarily designed for large-scale grid decoupling and exhibit limited effectiveness in suppressing excessive short-circuit currents within high-density intra-area partitions. To address this limitation, an intra-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back retrofit scheme is proposed in this paper. Transfer impedance is introduced as a key indicator to identify candidate AC transmission lines whose replacement can effectively reduce fault current contributions to critical nodes. On this basis, a comprehensive evaluation framework is established by considering short-circuit current variation, line loading characteristics, power flow magnitude, and voltage deviation, enabling systematic comparison among multiple retrofit schemes. A typical zonal power supply model is established using the BPA platform to represent the current grid topology of the China Southern Power Grid. Three-phase short-circuit simulations are carried out to evaluate system performance before and after the proposed retrofits. Simulation results show that the intra-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back integration significantly increases transfer impedance between key nodes and surrounding network areas, resulting in a pronounced reduction of short-circuit current levels. All critical nodes are maintained within acceptable safety margins while normal power exchange capability and operational flexibility are preserved. The results demonstrate that the proposed planning approach and evaluation framework provide an effective and practical solution for mitigating short-circuit currents in large-scale AC grids with dense internal coupling.

title: Aligning Engineering and Technology Management Education with AI-Driven Industry Expectations
authors: Ehud Menipaz, Natalia Bukhshtaber, Ben Bulmash
abstract: The paper examines how the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is reshaping work practices, elevating performance expectations, and redefining competencies in engineering and technology management. Drawing on interviews with industry professionals, it identifies a cognitive shift toward human-AI co-thinking as the mechanism driving these transformations. The study links empirical findings to a conceptual framing that explains why and how AI alters practices, performance benchmarks, and skill demands within hybrid socio-technical systems, and translates these insights into recommendations for curricula, pedagogy, and assessment. By exposing the widening gap between industry requirements and educational provision, it underscores the urgency of reform. The paper calls for systemic change to prepare graduates not only to function effectively in AI-augmented workplaces but also to lead innovation and socio-technical transformation in the AI era.

title: Alongamento passivo de alta intensidade no músculo antagonista gera alterações no comportamento das unidades motoras do músculo agonista durante contração isométrica? Um estudo preliminar
authors: André Brand Bezerra Coutinho, Hélio de Veiga Cabral, Liliam Fernandes de Oliveira
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Static stretching is commonly used to increase range of motion (ROM) and prepare the neuromuscular system for exercise. While prolonged stretching of agonist muscles may impair performance, the effects of stretching antagonist muscles on agonist behavior remain unclear. This preliminary study investigated whether high-intensity passive stretching of the triceps surae (antagonist) alters the motor unit behavior of the tibialis anterior (agonist) during isometric dorsiflexion. Five healthy male participants performed maximal voluntary contractions and submaximal trapezoidal torque tasks before and after a stretching protocol consisting of five 45-s passive stretches separated by 15-s rest. High-density surface electromyography (HDsEMG) was recorded from the tibialis anterior and decomposed to identify motor unit discharge times, allowing matched motor units to be tracked across conditions. Peak torque and ROM were compared pre- and post-stretching, and motor unit parameters were analyzed using linear mixed-effects models. Stretching significantly increased ROM (p = 0.048) but did not significantly change peak torque (p = 0.107). No significant differences were observed in mean discharge rate (p = 0.28) or discharge rate variability (p = 0.08), although variability showed a tendency to increase. These findings suggest that high-intensity antagonist stretching may induce subtle neural adjustments in the agonist muscle.

title: A Cross-Validation Framework for Robust ANN Modeling: Application to Electrical Discharge Machining of Inconel 718
authors: Mohammad Hossein Aminian, Arash Khosravi, Ahmad Azarnik
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Artificial Neural Networks are widely used for modeling nonlinear processes in experimental fields such as machining, where datasets are often small and noisy. However, many studies rely on weak baseline models, manual single train–test splits, and unclear hyperparameter tuning, which limit reproducibility and model reliability. This work introduces a systematic and reproducible pipeline for ANN training and evaluation under data-scarce conditions. The framework integrates k-fold cross-validation, structured hyperparameter tuning, and a balanced-score selection criterion that accounts for both predictive accuracy and generalization stability. The approach is applied to Electrical Discharge Machining of Inconel 718, comparing traditional backpropagation with two metaheuristic optimizers—Genetic Algorithm and Gray Wolf Optimization. Results show that while backpropagation achieves the lowest training error, metaheuristic training offers improved robustness and stability across folds. The proposed framework minimizes bias from arbitrary data splits and provides a transparent, fair basis for evaluating ANN-based models in small experimental datasets.

title: Triboelectric Charges induced from Detaping and the Influencing Factors
authors: Ke Kian Seng Joseph
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a study on the charges induced by de-taping process because of the electrostatic discharge (ESD) events caused by the separation of insulating tape from the wafer surface. The removal process induced the triboelectrification on the tape and wafer. High drain current with outliers was observed on a NMOS depletion transistor at the Process Control Monitoring (PCM) structure as a result of these charges. Several design of experiments were carried out to investigate the factors that influence these charges and the result are discussed and illustrated in this paper.

title: Intelligent Modeling for Deriving Human Lens Stiffness Based on Patients’ Demographic Features with Outcome in Phacoemulsification Surgery
authors: Sara Rahmati, Hadi Tabesh
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Background: Accurate preoperative assessment of human lens stiffness is a critical determinant in modern cataract surgery, particularly for optimizing ultrasound energy in phacoemulsification. Current clinical grading methods often rely on subjective judgment, which limits personalized surgical planning. Methods: We developed a predictive framework utilizing routine demographic and clinical features to estimate the gold-standard ex vivo elastic modulus (Young's modulus) of 50 cataractous human lenses, obtained via extracapsular cataract extraction (ECCE). A suite of supervised regression algorithms, including Support Vector Regression (SVR), was implemented and rigorously compared based on Mean Squared Error (MSE) and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> metrics. Results: SVR achieved the highest predictive performance on the test set (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.9409). Feature importance analysis consistently identified cataract grade (LOCS III), lens diameter, and age as the most influential factors driving stiffness prediction. Conclusion: This study establishes a successful intelligent proof-of-concept demonstrating the feasibility of correlating readily available clinical data with fundamental biomechanical properties using advanced machine learning. The models lay the necessary groundwork for developing non-invasive, AI-driven diagnostic tools to enhance surgical safety and efficiency.

title: Tyndall-Effect-Based Optical Monitoring of Micro-Plastic Pollution in Aquatic Environments
authors: Victor Ababii, Viorica Sudacevschi, Galina Marusic, Viorel Carbune
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: The paper presents the development and validation of an experimental system for monitoring microplastic pollution in aquatic environments, based on the Tyndall effect and artificial intelligence techniques. The proposed system uses a laser light source and a video acquisition module to detect light scattered by microplastic particles, with data processed in real time on the NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Developer Kit (SBC) platform. Mathematical models and discrete algorithms are developed for correlating the optical signal with the concentration of microplastics, and the performance of the method is improved by integrating artificial neural networks such as CNN and LSTM. The experimental results, presented in the form of images and quantitative graphs, demonstrate the system's ability to identify the presence of microplastics and dynamically monitor concentration variations. The proposed solution offers a fast, non-invasive, and scalable approach with high potential for continuous monitoring of the aquatic environment.

title: Study on Si-contamination in Developable BARC (DBARC) in High-K Metal Gate Process
authors: Wu Wenbin, Zhang Lei, Li Xiaojing, Fu Yong Feng
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a study on the effect of a Si-containing DBARC chemical during lithography step in HKMG process. Compared to normal good batch and unopen bottle of same type of DBARC chemical, an additional thin film was observed if the Si-containing DBARC material was coated together with photoresist. The formation of such Si–O film on top of wafer surface later caused related device failure. Cross-section TEM and EDX mapping techniques were used to characterize the film. Further experiment of characterization of this thin film with SIMS analysis indicates a potential application of in-line SIMS tool to monitor contamination from DBARC.

title: Maintaining Lot Schedule Adherence through Overlapping Q-times via Admission Control
authors: Thomas Hewlett, Srikanth Ramakrishnan, Himanshu Mogra, Shiladitya Chakravorty, Detlef Pabst
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Dispatching into a time-constrained sequence (Q-time or Time-link zone) poses a unique challenge for high volume manufacturing (HVM) factories, as schedule priority is, instead, often trumped by the protection of wafer quality. As fab processes mature, these constrained sequences may also continue to increase in complexity. This paper outlines a method by which both objectives of quality and schedule adherence can be achieved in an automated manner.

title: A Multi-Criterion Taxonomy of Cybersecurity Beneficiaries
authors: Svetlana Cojocaru, Alexandru-Andrei Ghita, Ludmila Peca, Marius Dumitrascu, Rostislav Călin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Cyber-attacks generate increasingly large global losses while financial resources for securing information systems remain limited. Existing reference frameworks (CIS Controls, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST CSF) lack fine-grained differentiation between beneficiary types, leading to inefficient resource allocation. This paper proposes a multi-criterion taxonomy consisting of ten organizational categories (O1–O10) and five population categories (P1–P5). The taxonomy extends the preliminary categorization matrix from [1], which was based solely on organizational size and occupational status, by introducing three additional classification criteria: sectoral risk profiles derived from attack frequency data, alignment with national legislative requirements (Republic of Moldova), and digital literacy assessment for population categories. Organizational classification employs EU SME definitions, while a four-level cybersecurity scale (Ordinary, Medium, Enhanced, High) correlates with CIS Controls implementation groups for practical application. Comparative analysis demonstrates advantages in sectoral granularity, population coverage, and applicability under resource constraints. The paper acknowledges the theoretical nature of the framework and presents a detailed three-stage validation protocol for future empirical testing, with limitations explicitly identified and primary contributions being conceptual and methodological.

title: Mathematical Models and System-Level Analysis of Modern Geolocation Technologies
authors: Lilia Rotaru, Irina Cojuhari, Maria Gutu, Artur Kazak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Accurate geolocation is essential for modern cyber-physical systems supporting applications such as autonomous navigation, intelligent transportation, mobile communications, and Internet of Things infrastructures. Contemporary solutions rely on complementary technologies, including Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), terrestrial wireless networks, and inertial sensors. This paper presents a theoretical system-level analysis of major geolocation technologies and their mathematical models, examining GNSS trilateration with clock bias, multi-constellation architectures, RSSI-based cellular and Wi-Fi localization, and inertial navigation. Estimation frameworks, including weighted least-squares and Kalman filtering for multi-sensor fusion, are also analyzed. A comparative evaluation of satellite, terrestrial, and inertial approaches highlights their operational characteristics, error sources, and accuracy regimes, emphasizing the importance of hybrid architectures for reliable positioning across diverse environments.

title: Innovative, effective and cost saving strategies for post implant ashing wet clean and post poly etch wet clean
authors: Janice Koh, Zhenjie Zhu, Sicong Chen, Tek Sing Lim, Christopher Lim
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents the organic residue removal performance using low-concentration O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> water in place of Sulfuric Peroxide Mixture (SPM) in post implant and poly etch wet clean. The replacement demonstrated better performance in Yield and Defect Density (YDD), Electrical Test (ET) and sort. With this replacement, we can save approximately 55 % of our chemical cost.

title: System Hang reduction on TEL Track Act8 Tools: A Case Study on Legacy Slave HDD Failure
authors: Shine Arkar Aung, Liu Houdong, Fazuli Sani
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Intermittent system hangs on TEL Track Act8 tools were causing unpredictable production interruptions despite repeated software reimaging, firmware alignment, and controller replacement. A structured root-cause investigation revealed that aging secondary ("slave") hard disk drives—originally intended only for local logging—were injecting intermittent timing disturbances and power-rail ripple during retry events. These effects degraded controller timing margins, triggering watchdog resets and system hangs. We combined field data, Self-Monitoring, Analysis, and Reporting Technology (SMART) attribute trend analysis, oscilloscope-based power integrity measurements, and reliability modeling using Mean Time Between Failure (MTBF) and Arrhenius acceleration to characterize the degradation mechanism. Removing the slave HDDs and migrating logging to network storage eliminated >80% of unexplained hangs across the fleet, restoring deterministic controller behavior and improving overall equipment availability. The findings highlight the reliability risks of legacy electromechanical storage within mature semiconductor tools and provide practical obsolescence-management strategies for High-Volume Manufacturing (HVM) environments.

title: An Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Precise Diagnosis of Lung Diseases Using Medical Imaging
authors: Nasibeh Termeh Mousavi, Azamossadat Nourbakhsh, Asadollah Shahbahrami
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Due to the rising burden of respiratory pathologies and the critical necessity for swift, precise diagnostics, especially amidst crises like the COVID-19 pandemic, this research introduces a hybrid deep learning architecture rooted in ensemble techniques for detecting lung disorders via CT and chest X-ray imagery. The methodology incorporates a bilateral filter to suppress noise without compromising edge integrity. Subsequently, Mask R-CNN is deployed to segment potential regions of interest (ROI), followed by feature extraction utilizing the VGG16 backbone. Classification is handled by an optimized ensemble of CNN classifiers with fine-tuned hyperparameters. Empirical outcomes reveal a marked enhancement, attaining 98% accuracy and sensitivity, thereby surpassing conventional baseline models in diagnosing COVID-19, pneumonia, and lung cancer. By merging sophisticated image processing with deep ensemble learning, this framework exhibits significant promise for clinical deployment.

title: Controller Tuning for First- to Fourth-Order Inertia Object Models with Astatic Behavior Using the Polynomial Method
authors: Bartolomeu Izvoreanu, Adrian Secrieru, Nadejda Palamarciuc, Ion Fiodorov, Dumitru Moraru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: This paper describes the procedure for tuning a controller for models of control objects with first- to fourth-order inertia and astatic behavior using the polynomial method, with imposed values of the damping ratio and settling time of the synthesized system. The numerator and denominator polynomials of the transfer function of the object model are factorized into factors with zeros located in the left-hand and right-hand parts of the complex plane.Based on the order of the object model and on the conditions for solving the system of algebraic equations, the physical realizability of the control algorithm, and the robustness of the system, the desired polynomial of the synthesized system of the corresponding degree is constructed. This polynomial contains two polynomials with unknown coefficients, and the degrees of the unknown polynomials as well as the degree of the desired polynomial are determined.From the specified damping ratio and settling time, the dominant poles of the synthesized system are determined, and on their basis the characteristic polynomial of the closed-loop system is constructed. If necessary, additional real poles located on the negative real semi-axis are added as far as possible from the dominant poles in order to ensure the physical realizability of the controller and to satisfy the required system performance.By equating the desired characteristic polynomial with the characteristic polynomial of the constructed system, and by matching the coefficients of the same powers of the variable s on both sides of the equality, a system of algebraic equations is obtained, from which the unknown coefficients and polynomials are determined.Based on the stable components of the object and the determined unknown polynomials, the transfer function of the controller is constructed. Examples of controller synthesis for first- to fourth-order object with astatic behavior using the proposed method are analyzed. The synthesized systems demonstrate high performance and good robustness.

title: Unload Clean Removal for Showerhead Pattern Defects Improvement
authors: Hong Kai Tan, Kenneth Chih Yuang Mah, Ker Peng Lau, Edward See, Chek Chou Chiam
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: In CVD systems, clean recipe settings are closely related to the three core tenets of manufacturing, which are cost, productivity, and quality. In the interest of throughput, a cleaning configuration termed as "Clean on Unload Loadlock" may be utilized to process multiple wafers consecutively before the cleaning process is triggered to maximize output, which is synchronized to the wafer loading and unloading cycle. In this paper, we show that this cleaning configuration introduces a critical process imbalance. Specifically, in twin-reactor CVD systems, one chamber becomes repeatedly exposed to cyclic cleaning erosion, due to final wafers of a lot processing only in one chamber, leading to the partner chamber being uncoated during the subsequent cleaning and subjected to chemical attack by fluorine radicals. Hence, the removal of such cleaning configuration is proposed to mitigate showerhead pattern defects, with an improvement from an average of 4.6 cases per year to zero cases since implementation to date, and gas cost savings.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Digital Credentialing: An Interoperable Hybrid Architecture for Academic Integrity and Verification
authors: Swarna Surekha, B Santhosh Kumar, P.K.Dhanalakshmi, R.Usha, N. Dhasarathan, R. Senthamil Selvan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Digital credentialing provided by blockchain technology offers portable proof and constant audit trails; however, utilizing public ledgers raises unique causes questions with cost, solitude, and the speed accompanying that revocations are processed. To attain a balance between immutability, solitude-maintaining discriminating disclosure, active annulment, and uniform affordability, this study assesses a pertinent composite architecture for instructional dependability and proof. A permissioned consortium ledger handles issuer dossier and credential annulment, while a permissionless coating anchors Merkle ancestries. The suggested design uses a nothing-information proof method, an established PLONK-style selective announcement to claim consumers' privacy. An inclusive evaluation of conduct, security, and interoperability is created likely by the whole end-to-end original, which is supported by a dataset of 100,000 synthetic attestations, containing 25,000 adversarial sketches. With a maximum throughput of 420 verifications/second, an average evidence length of 2.4 KB, and a cap of 42 bytes for exposed traits, the system achieves a misleading acceptance rate of 0.12%. The permissioned ledger's revocation transactions take 1.2 seconds, obtain interoperability of 98.7 allotment, and cost an average of 1.94 rupees to confirm. This cost-effective, secure, and solitude-continuing plan enables organizations to conduct lengthy credential proof accompanying little functional burden, internationally recognized honor, and strong privacy protection.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Voltage Control in Active Distribution Networks
authors: YongZe Ma, Hong Li
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The secure and economic operation of Active Distribution Networks (ADNs) is increasingly challenged by the high penetration of renewable energy and electric vehicles, which introduces significant source-load uncertainties leading to voltage violations and increased network losses. This paper proposes an intelligent voltage dispatching strategy based on a Sequential Deep Double Q-Network (SDDQN). The dispatching problem is formulated as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) within the Gym-ANM framework. The SDDQN algorithm decomposes the high-dimensional, continuous action space into an ordered sequence of one-dimensional sub-decisions, enabling the problem to be solved using only a value function and circumventing the convergence difficulties of Actor-Critic architectures. Simulation results on a modified IEEE 33-bus system demonstrate that the proposed SDDQN achieves a more stable training process with faster convergence compared to both DDPG and TD3 algorithms. The strategy ensures all nodal voltages remain within permissible limits while maintaining lower network losses throughout the dispatching period.

title: EAS-PQC: Analyzing Post Quantum Cryptography Algorithm Selection for IoT Device Classes
authors: Aisha Nasser Ahmed, Norziana Jamil, Nadia Badawi, Berini Aymen Dia Eddine, Yasir Amer Abbas
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: The migration of Internet of Things (IoT) systems to post-quantum cryptography (PQC) is essential to ensure long-term security against quantum enabled adversaries. However, the large computational, memory, and communication overheads of PQC algorithms pose challenges for resource-constrained IoT environments. In practice, IoT platforms span a wide range of capabilities, from battery-powered sensors to gateway devices, making a one-size-fits-all configuration impractical. This paper presents a new methodology for selecting suitable PQC schemes based on RFC 7228 device classes. We introduce the Efficiency-Adjusted Security for Post-Quantum Cryptography (EAS-PQC) metric that considers security strength, latency, energy consumption, memory footprint, communication overhead, and protocol reliability. Device-class-specific constraints and weighting profiles are incorporated to reflect the operational priorities of Class 0, Class 1, and Class 2 IoT devices. Our evaluation primarily uses performance benchmarks from the PQM4 framework, which are based on ARM Cortex-M4 microcontroller profiles, and performance results from several work on similar embedded platforms. Energy consumption is estimated using a constant active power model derived from Cortex-M4 power characteristics. We evaluate multiple PQC candidates, including ML-KEM and ML-DSA, and the results show a quantitative mapping between IoT device classes and PQC schemes. Specifically, Saber-LightSaber is identified as the most suitable option for Class 0 devices, while ML-DSA-87 combined with ML-KEM-768 achieves the highest overall scores for Class 1 and Class 2 devices. Our findings improve feasibility and efficiency by up to 30–40% compared to static baseline configurations. With these findings, we recommend EAS-PQC as a useful tool for the assessment of post-quantum security in industrial IoT systems.

title: Hybrid Frequency Regulation in Multi-Area Power Systems with Renewable Energy Sources, Electric Vehicles, and Cyber-Attack Mitigation: a MATLAB/Simulink Implementation Study
authors: Prakriti Kumar Srivastava, Pankaj Rai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Modern power systems are confronted with more than ever before in terms of frequency stability challenges as the utilization of renewable energy sources (RES) is beginning to rise in large volumes alongside low inertia nature. This paper will provide an extensive hybrid frequency control plan to multi-area interconnected power systems that will incorporate proportional-integralderivative (PID) controllers at the fractional-order, aggregators of electric vehicles (EV) to provide virtual inertia, and cyber-resilient architectures to avert denial-ofservice (DoS) and false data injection (FDI) attacks. Simulink was used to model and simulate the proposed system with realistic nonlinearities such as governor dead bands, generation rate limits, and communication weaknesses. The outcome of simulations shows that the hybrid FOPID controller (with EV aggregators) reduces the frequency overshoot by 67 %, and the settling time is 3.5 times shorter than that of the standard PID controllers. In the case of hybrid cyber-attacks, the Kalman filter-based detection and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> control switching preserve a nominal performance of 93 %. The experiment confirms the methodology with different system inertia (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H=5-8$</tex> seconds), levels of RES penetration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30-50 \%$</tex>), and attack strengths, and offers a practical idea of the next-generation implementation of smart grids.

title: Temperature Compensation in RF Reflectometry for Crop Quality Assessment
authors: Adeoluwa Oyinlola, Temitope Odedeyi
abstract: This study examines the impact of temperature on RF reflectometry (RF-R) measurements in four root crops: cassava, carrot, sweet potato, and potato. Using a dual-pin RF probe and a vector network analyzer, reflection coefficient, S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>, was measured across 401 frequency range of 30 kHz to 200MHz as samples cooled from 40 °C to 25 °C. A linear relationship between S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> and temperature was observed across all crops. Temperature correction factors (TCFs) were derived to normalise measurements, with the aim of improving the accuracy of RF-R-based quality characterisation in food and crop analysis, particularly under field conditions.

title: Trustworthy Big Data Pipelines: Fairness-Aware and Privacy-Preserving Knowledge Discovery Framework
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Myasar Mundher Adnan, Prasanta Parida, Manish Gupta, Ankam Sreejyothsna, Shashi Prakash Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: It can be indicated that the given paper deals with the design, implementation, and evaluation of reliable big-data pipelines that would also be fair and privacy conscious when knowledge is being discovered. Modern data pipelines are mediating an increasing set of high-stakes decisions across a variety of domains (healthcare, finance, public policy), but that are susceptible to privacy leakage, representational and procedural bias, and opaquitas that erode trust between the stakeholders. Our proposed conceptual framework (i) models the data pipeline as a process where each stage is handled in an accountable manner (data acquisition, cleaning, entity matching, model training, and deployment); (ii) incorporates interventions that are cautious of fairness (preprocessing, in-pipeline auditing, model selection, and post-processing); and (iii) incorporates provable privacy mechanisms (differential privacy, secure multi-party computation, and privacy-related generation of synthetic data) without impairing analytics utility. The framework also suggests the provision of instrumentation to support constant surveillance, provenance, and human-in-the-loop governance so as to enforce remedial measures and legal regulations. The paper formulates research problems and evaluation standards and an experimental roadmap to compare tradeoffs between privacy and fairness metrics and downstream utility by synthesizing recent contributions in the areas of privacy-preserving generative modelling, pipeline-sensitive fairness studies, and operational trustful AI practices. The work is based on the increasingly common demands regarding the pipeline-level implementation of fairness and the comprehensive consideration of privacyinterfairness dependencies in machine learning systems.

title: Enhancing Manifold Convexity in Deep MRI Image Clustering Using Adversarial Learning
authors: Sawsan Hilal, Nairouz Mrabah, Zyad Atef Allam, Riadh Ksantini
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Till today, no cure has been found for some diseases such as cancer and early detection remains the only reliable way to increase the survival chances for patients. Recently, machine learning has been used to aid in the diagnosis of large numbers of patients, especially using medical image data such as Magnetic Resonance Imaging images. However, due to the difficulty of obtaining large and labelled datasets, clustering techniques have been favored in this field of research with particular interest in deep clustering which has proven capable of understanding and accurately grouping highly complex data. One of the most recent developments in deep clustering came in the form of the dynamic auto-encoder (DynAE), which solves a historic problem that faced autoencoders (AEs) prior. However, it suffers from feature twist, a phenomenon which causes the cluster boundaries to flatten and become coarse and more complex which in turn hinders the clustering performance. This study proposes a new model (AdversFAE) using adversarial learning to combat feature drift in the auto-encoders by utilizing adversarial constrained interpolation in the clustering phase to escape feature twist. The proposed model was tested on six datasets from the MedMNIST v2 collection of medical datasets. The results showed that the proposed model outperformed the convolutional dynamic autoencoder (ConvDynAE) in terms of accuracy and F1_score. Interestingly, AdversFAE achieved an accuracy of 85.9%, 66.9%, and 53.5% on PneumoniaMNIST, BloodMNIST, and RetinaMNIST respectively compared to $62.1 \%, 46.3 \%$, and 31.5% achieved by ConvDynAE on the same datasets. The proposed changes proved effective in improving the convexity of cluster boundaries and hence the clustering performance.

title: Opacity-Informed UAV Navigation with 3D Gaussian Splatting and Model Predictive Control
authors: Jiahao Tu, Zhongrui Hu, Chenghao Yao, Jingyi Lu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated UAV navigation system that combines 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) for environment mapping, A* algorithm for global path planning, and Model Predictive Control (MPC) for trajectory tracking with obstacle avoidance. The 3DGS-generated Gaussian sphere map provides rich geometric information including opacity and scale parameters, which are utilized to construct a collision cost function embedded into the MPC optimization framework. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves significant improvements in both path planning and trajectory tracking performance. The A* algorithm with Gaussian opacity-aware cost function reduces the integrated occupancy probability by 26.7% compared to traditional grid-based methods. The MPC controller with collision cost achieves a position RMSE of 0.030m, representing a 66.3% improvement over the baseline MPC without collision cost. The integrated system operates at an effective control frequency of 75Hz with an average single-step computation time of 13.3ms, meeting real-time requirements for UAV navigation in GNSS-denied environments.

title: Research on Leak Source Localization in Complex Pipelines Based on Acoustic Emission Technology
authors: Gao Zhigang, Lu Ronghao, Zhong Weicheng, Yuan Xumin, Zhang Lanzhu, Li Ke
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Pipelines are widely employed in the petroleum, natural gas, and chemical industries due to their high efficiency and low cost. However, leakage frequently occurs at components such as valves and flanges in complex pipeline structures due to sealing failures or impact fractures, posing significant safety risks. This study conducts experimental research on leak source localization in complex pipelines using acoustic emission (AE) technology. Lead break tests are performed to analyze the propagation velocity and attenuation characteristics of AE signals. A multi-level analysis combining time-domain, frequency-domain, and wavelet transform methods reveals that the amplitude and frequency band distribution of leakage signals attenuate progressively during propagation. To enhance signal quality, a parameter optimization method based on vector weighted averaging is proposed and validated within the framework of variational mode decomposition (VMD). Effective signal modes are selected using sample entropy and correlation coefficients, followed by signal reconstruction through a wavelet thresholding denoising algorithm, resulting in a significant improvement in localization accuracy. Furthermore, the impact of signal attenuation on localization errors is investigated, and an optimized sensor placement scheme tailored for complex pipelines is proposed and experimentally verified.

title: Performance Evaluation of Non-Invasive Attention Classification Using Physiological and Ocular Metrics
authors: Akshit Munjal, Divneet Singh Kapoor, Kiran Jot Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The correct identification of cognitive attention states is critical for intelligent systems to perform optimally in applications like e-learning, driver monitoring, and humancomputer interaction. In this paper, the authors present a machine learning-based approach to classify three types of attention states: Sustained, Alternating, and Divided Attention. The classification is done by employing non-invasive physiological parameters and ocular responses. The data was collected using a Gazepoint eye tracker and a Galvanic Skin Response sensor. Relevant features like GSR values, fixations, and mean fixation duration were extracted. The dataset was normalized using the z-score method. The SMOTE technique was used to handle class imbalance. The performance of the proposed system was validated using four classifiers: Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and K-Nearest Neighbors. The experimental results showed that the KNN algorithm produced the best results with an accuracy of 96.13 %, followed by XGBoost with 96.00 %, and then the Random Forest algorithm with 95.86 %. The Logistic Regression algorithm produced the lowest accuracy of 49.04 %. The features were also ranked with the GSR values contributing the most to the classification at 70.5 %, followed by the fixation count at 16.0 %, and then the mean fixation duration at 13.5 %.

title: Multi-Agent Paradigms for Privacy Preservation in Federated Learning: A Review
authors: Rahul H.Bhole, Sachin R. Sakhare
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: In the recent years Federated Learning has emerged as a groundbreaking approach to train machine learning models using edge devices by ensuring that data is localized and central data aggregation is reduced. Despite the fact that this inherently provides a layer of privacy, it cannot be exempt from potential breaches or leaks during the training and aggregation phases. In this review, we examine how intelligent agents can augment privacy measures by integrating multi-agent systems with federated learning, emphasizing the innovative intersection between these two fields. In this paper, we examine the core functionalities of agents, from monitoring data exchanges to verifying node authenticity to proactively responding to anomalies, and provide an assessment of what is available in the state-of-the-art methodologies for using these agents. We highlight the challenges and potential future directions in harnessing multi-agent systems to enhance privacy in federated learning. In our opinion, our work illustrates the promise and complexity of this interdisciplinary crossroads and provides a foundation for future progress in developing robust, privacy-respecting federated learning infrastructure capable of meeting the needs of our students.

title: A Scalable Agentic Artificial Intelligence Architecture for Autonomous Decision-Making and Self-Optimization in Complex Cyberphysical Systems
authors: Satpalsing Devising Rajput, Kiran Veernapu, Minakshi Sati, Suresh Tiwari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Modern Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) must be autonomously adaptable to deal with growing complexity and uncertainty in the environment. In this paper, the author proposes a scalable agentic AI architecture that will allow CPS to sense, think, and act in autonomy. The framework combined both local edge intelligence and global coordination to solve this by balancing the distributed agents with the hierarchical reinforcement learning. By contrast with the traditional centralized models, our architecture employs the use of selfmonitoring and feedback loops in the optimization of performance in real time and without human intervention. Assessment of various CPS conditions reveals major gains in decision quality, latency, and resiliency, providing a solid base to the future of the industrial automation and intelligent infrastructure.

title: SMS Spam Detection Using Hybrid BERT + XGBoost Model
authors: K. Jeevitha, B. Jayasurya, N. Prakash, E. Loganathan, P. Anitha, R. Geetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: AIM: The aim of the study is to develop a SMS spam detection system using a Hybrid BERT + XGBoost model that can predict the spam messages from SMS communication. Materials and Methods: In this research, there are two groups. Group 1 refers to a baseline TF-IDF + Random Forest model while Group 2 refers to a proposed and advanced BERT + XGBoost Model algorithm. Each group consists of 200 samples, with the SMS providing more accurate and flexible spam classification. The total dataset is 10,962 samples. The power value is 97 % the significance threshold is 0.05, with a confidence interval of 99 %. Result: The BERT + XGBoost model is a faster prediction method and significantly higher accuracy than the TF-IDF. BERT + XGBoost algorithm attains a validation accuracy range from 98.68 % to 99.12 %, but the baseline TF-IDF + Random Forest model reaches an accuracy range from 96.65 % to 97.63 %. Statistical analysis indicates a significant improvement in performance, with the proposed model demonstrating higher accuracy compared to the baseline approach, and a significant improvement in the statistical analysis (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex> less than 0.001) in the detection of all sms spam categories. Precision, recall, and F1 scores are noticeably higher withBERT + XGBoost Model. Conclusion: In this work, it is observed that BERT + XGBoost achieves a greater improvement in spam detection compared to the Random Forest model methods. It provides an effective security for spam messages in text classification.

title: Synthetic Twins: Toward Sustainable, Tunable 3D Printed Models of Human Tissues
authors: Gianmarco Dolino, Damiano Coato, Alice Berardo, Emanuele Luigi Carniel, Paolo Gargiulo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The concept of the digital twin has already transformed engineering and medicine by enabling virtual replicas of organs and tissues for simulation, diagnostics, and treatment planning. Yet, many biomedical applications require physical counterparts that replicate not only the anatomy but also the mechanical behavior of living tissues. To address this gap, we introduce the Synthetic Twin: a sustainable, physical analog of human tissue, fabricated via advanced 3D printing with tunable microstructure that reproduce native mechanical properties. Unlike conventional phantoms, which depend exclusively on material composition, Synthetic Twins leverage microstructural engineering (e.g., fiber reinforcement, layering, porosity modulation) to replicate a wide spectrum of healthy and pathological conditions. As a proof-of-concept, we present the design and characterization of 3D printed fiber-reinforced cartilage phantoms, demonstrating how microstructural tuning enables anisotropic behavior that closely resembles native tissue. Using mechanical testing combined with Digital Image Correlation (DIC), we evaluated samples with single fiber orientations (superficial, middle, deep) as well as a novel multilayered construct mimicking the stratification of native cartilage. Results show that microstructural design governs stiffness and Poisson’s ratio in a depth-dependent manner, with multilayered constructs displaying the highest fidelity. This work demonstrates that Synthetic Twins can provide reproducible, customizable, and ethically sustainable alternatives to animal and cadaveric models, with applications in diagnostics, surgical training, and personalized healthcare.

title: Cyber Physical System Based Distributed Real Time Draining System for Smart Cities
authors: Avishek Golder, Simarpreet Kaur, Komal Sharma, Gaurav Kumar Bharti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: In this era of technology, almost everything is now automated. Cyber physical systems play an important role in making our lives smarter and contributes the development of a smart city. A smart drainage system significantly contributes in building a smart city. The residents are currently facing issues due to the accumulation of excess water in the drain and water logging caused by the inadequate flow of drain water. To date, none of the existing research has addressed the simultaneous monitoring, cleaning, and decontamination of underwater drains within manholes. In this paper, our aim to solve this issue by using cyber physical systems. Our proposed system will automate the process of drainage cleaning and solve the problem of manual drain cleaning process. It will decrease the problems faced by manual drainage cleaning such as disease, death of workers, time consuming process etc.

title: Incorporating Edge Computing in Wireless Sensor Networks for Efficient Data Processing
authors: Himanshu Rai Goyal, Yogendra Kumar, Zaid Alsalami, A. Kakoli Rao, Amit Srivastava, P. William
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper explores the integration of Edge Computing with Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) to address the challenges posed by traditional centralized data processing architectures. WSNs, pivotal in applications like environmental monitoring, healthcare, and smart cities, face issues of latency, bandwidth inefficiency, and scalability due to the vast data they generate. Edge Computing offers a paradigm shift by processing data near its source, thus enhancing responsiveness and operational efficiency. We propose a novel framework that employs localized computing at the edge to optimize network performance. Through simulations and real-world experiments, we demonstrate how this integration reduces latency, conserves bandwidth, improves security, and enhances scalability. Our findings indicate significant improvements in network management and efficiency, confirming the benefits of Edge Computing in transforming WSNs into more robust, efficient, and scalable systems.

title: Low-Power Wide-Area Networks (LPWAN) for IoT Applications: Engineering the Innovative Practices
authors: Narender Chinthamu, Krishna Kant Dixit, Mudit Mittal, Arun Pratap Srivastava, Akhilesh Kumar Khan, Rajeev Sobti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper explores the integration of Low-Power Wide-Area Network (LPWAN) technologies within the Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem. As IoT applications proliferate, the need for efficient, low-power communication solutions that can operate over extensive areas becomes critical. LPWAN technologies, characterized by their long range and low power consumption, are well-suited to meet these demands. This study examines various LPWAN technologies, including both cellular and non-cellular systems, and assesses their performance, design specifications, and suitability for different IoT applications. Through a comprehensive review and real-world use case analysis, this research aims to provide insights into the optimization of LPWAN technologies to enhance IoT connectivity. The paper also discusses potential challenges and future research directions in the deployment of LPWAN solutions, contributing to the broader understanding and development of IoT infrastructure.

title: Identification of vulnerable lines in power system considering the randomness of wind power
authors: Ning Wang, Lixia Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To deal with the uncertain challenges brought by the high proportion of wind power integration, this paper proposes a fragile line identification method considering the randomness of wind power. Firstly, based on the scene generation technology combining Latin hypercube, K-means clustering and Monte Carlo sampling, the random scene model of wind power is constructed; Furthermore, the network structure vulnerability, load impact vulnerability and branch breaking vulnerability are proposed, and the final vulnerability ranking is obtained by TOPSIS method. The simulation in IEEE 39 and IEEE 118 bus system shows that this method can effectively identify the vulnerable lines of the system in the random wind power scenario, and analyses the impact of wind power penetration on the vulnerability, which provides a theoretical basis for power grid security assessment.

title: TAC-DHER: Tactical-Consistency-Aware Confidence-Gated Dynamic Hindsight Experience Replay for Cooperative UAV Air Combat
authors: Wenxin Li, Yongxin Feng
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Dynamic-target cooperative unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) air combat poses substantial challenges to reinforcement learning due to continuously evolving tactical objectives, sparse rewards, and poor reuse of failed episodes. To tackle these issues, a tactical-consistency-aware confidence-gated dynamic hindsight experience replay method, termed TAC-DHER, is proposed. TAC-DHER replaces purely geometric goals with joint tactical goals that jointly encode mission mode, cooperative role, engagement geometry, resource status, and safety constraints. During failed-trajectory reconstruction, tactical soft matching is introduced to replace the rigid goal correspondence used in the original Dynamic Hindsight Experience Replay (DHER), thereby expanding the set of high-value failed segments that can be relabeled. During replay, confidence gating and adaptive scheduling are further incorporated to ensure reliable sample injection and stable training. Experiments in a 2 -vs- 2 heterogeneous cooperative UAV air combat environment show that TAC-DHER consistently outperforms the original DHER across multiple dynamic combat scenarios, including standard dynamic-target settings, constrained training configurations, riskconstrained cooperation, and extremely sparse-reward conditions. Ablation studies confirm the complementary contributions of tactical soft matching, confidence gating, and adaptive replay scheduling, while trajectory cases further demonstrate that TACDHER induces more stable cooperative suppression and targetelimination behaviors.

title: Architecture of an IoT–AI Digital Ecosystem for Air Quality Monitoring and Environmental Assessment
authors: Ali Abbasov, Elchin Aliyev, Ramin Rzayev, Asgar Almasov
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: This paper presents an Internet of Things (IoT)– Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based digital ecosystem for continuous air quality monitoring and environmental assessment in urban and ecologically sensitive regions of Azerbaijan. The system integrates distributed sensor networks, cloud storage, and intelligent analytics within a unified framework. Real-time and historical data are processed using machine learning models and fuzzy inference to estimate pollution levels, detect anomalies, and generate short-term forecasts. The platform incorporates automated data validation, adaptive model updating, and spatial– temporal analysis of pollutant dynamics. The approach was evaluated using multi-year datasets from urban monitoring stations and benchmarked against conventional statistical methods. Results demonstrate improved prediction accuracy and greater robustness under varying meteorological and emission conditions. The proposed architecture enables timely identification of high-risk pollution zones and supports evidence-based environmental management. Its modular and scalable design allows adaptation to regions with similar climatic and urban characteristics.

title: Propagation Characteristics and Parameters Optimization of Laser Ultrasonic Waves in C130 Post Insulators
authors: Bichuan Xu, Zhenhua Xu, Tao Lai, Yanglin Li, Fangji Liu, Hui Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Laser ultrasonic non-destructive testing (NDT) technology has the unique advantages of ultrasonic testing method and optical method, which improves the temporal and spatial resolution while realizing non-contact and non-destructive testing. In order to improve the excitation efficiency and defect detection accuracy of laser source ultrasonic non-destructive excitation, it is necessary to optimize the parameters of laser source to improve the energy conversion efficiency and reduce the testing cost. This paper mainly researched the propagation characteristics of laser ultrasonic waves under different conditions and simulated the process of laser ultrasonic action on C130 high-strength aluminum post insulators by using the finite element analysis software COMSOL Multiphysics 6.3. The parametric relationship between width of laser pulse 10-40ns, radius of beam 0.3-0.6mm, and resultant ultrasonic waves characteristics was quantitatively established. Simulation results revealed that a pulse width of 10ns combined with a beam radius of 0.3mm achieved optimal ultrasonic excitation efficiency. This result provided an important basis for the analysis of the internal ultrasonic waves propagation of C130 high-strength aluminum ceramic post insulators in the future.

title: A Unified AI-Based Approach for Performance Optimization and Threat Detection in Telecommunication Systems
authors: Prashant Roy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The rapid growth in telecommunication systems is resulting in increased complexity in these systems, thus creating problems in optimizing their performances and detecting intrusions in real-time. Conventional machine learning techniques and rule-based systems face difficulties in dealing with high-dimensional data and class imbalance problems. In this paper, an intelligent system called EATIO-Net (Ensemble Attention-Based Telecom Intrusion and Optimization Network) is presented for optimizing the performances of telecommunication systems and detecting intrusions simultaneously.
