By providing lifetime distributions and confidence intervals, the proposed framework offers a statistically rigorous and practical basis for reliability-oriented decision-making and condition-based management of XLPE cable insulation.

title: Fine-Grained Correction Method for Distributed Photovoltaic Hosting Capacity in Rural Distribution Networks
authors: Fengzhang Luo, Ranfeng Mu, Shengyuan Wang, Kuangyu Kong, Lukun Ge, Zhishuang Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of distributed photovoltaic (PV) installations in rural areas, traditional PV hosting capacity assessment methods based solely on electrical constraints fail to reflect actual interconnection conditions, resulting in deviations between the evaluation results and the practical level of engineering implementation. To address this issue, a refined correction method for the distributed PV hosting capacity of rural distribution networks is proposed. Based on the conventional hosting capacity optimization model constrained by voltage deviation, thermal limits, short-circuit current, and the "N-1" criterion, practical factors such as implementation conditions, user installation willingness, government subsidy policies, and the existing level of PV deployment are incorporated to construct a multidimensional constraint–based hosting capacity correction model, together with a corresponding computational procedure. A 10 kV feeder is used as a case study to analyze and compare the distribution of PV hosting capacity before and after correction. The results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively bridges the gap between idealized electrical assessments and practical engineering conditions, providing a more reliable basis for PV integration planning and management decisions in rural distribution networks.

title: Transformer Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Multi-Source Domain Generalization
authors: Bingsheng Li, Yuan Li, Jiaqi Peng, Shunqing Ma, Hui Pan, Shouping Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: As a core device in the power system, the fault diagnosis of transformers under unknown operating conditions is crucial for ensuring the stable operation of the power grid. Traditional diagnosis methods are susceptible to changes in operating conditions, leading to decreased accuracy. Graphene vibration sensors can accurately collect vibration characteristic data from transformer cores and windings, providing a high-quality data source for fault diagnosis. Therefore, a multi-source domain generalization intelligent diagnosis algorithm that integrates data-level and feature-level strategies is proposed for fault identification of transformer vibration signals. At the data level, an improved multi-source domain data augmentation strategy is designed to mix and generalize vibration data and labels under different operating conditions and construct augmented domains, enabling information exchange between domains and enhancing sample diversity. At the feature level, a supervised contrastive learning mechanism is introduced into a multi-source domain adversarial network. While extracting domain-invariant vibration features, the contrastive loss constrains the feature space to minimize the inter-class sample distance and maximize the inter-class sample distance, thereby mining more discriminative and generalizable vibration feature representations. Experimental results show that this intelligent algorithm can effectively address the distribution shift of vibration features in transformer cross-operating condition scenarios. Compared to traditional intelligent diagnosis methods such as CNN and DDC, the accuracy of fault diagnosis under unknown operating conditions of transformers is significantly improved.

title: Hybrid healthcare for heterogeneous patients: Impacts of telemedicine suitability and subsidy on chronic care
authors: Yunzhi Cao, Xiaoyan Zhu, Houmin Yan
abstract: Hybrid patient care is transforming healthcare practices. Powered by health information technology and by the prevalence of video-conferencing, this new mode of care brings together the best of telemedicine and in-person treatment. Consider a chronic care provider who gives both the two treatments to patients, and patients who are heterogeneous in geography to the provider. This study analyzes pricing policy for three different situations. First, consider the situation in which the telemedicine suitability coefficient, i.e., the proportion of treatments that can be done both in person and by telemedicine, can be well estimated by the specialists. Deterministic pricing optimization models are established to compare the performance of hybrid healthcare with traditional in-person care. Next, it studies the situation in which a two-sided telemedicine subsidy policy is offered by government. Game models among the patients, provider, and government are formulated to investigate the impact of the subsidy policy on hybrid healthcare. Finally, it addresses the situation in which the telemedicine suitability coefficient is highly uncertain due to lack of data and knowledge for estimation. A distributionally robust optimization model is formulated to derive a robust pricing policy that is expected to perform well even in the worst case. Using different models for the three situations, theoretical analysis and numerical experiments show that the introduction of telemedicine can increase the proportion of treated patients and the total patient utility for high travel cost and high telemedicine suitability. With robust pricing, both in-person and telemedicine prices are lower than under the deterministic policy, ensuring stable outcomes for treated patients and hospital profit in various cases. This study provides suggestions for the provider and the government concerning the implementation and promotion of hybrid chronic care.

title: Adaptive Frequency-Constrained Robust Unit Commitment Considering Adjustable Virtual Inertia and Multi-Stage Frequency Security Indices
authors: Zhengxi Yin, Xueyan He, Jiayan Lu, Yingjun Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: High penetration of wind power significantly reduces synchronous inertia and increases frequency stability risks in modern power systems. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an adaptive frequency-constrained robust unit commitment (AFCRUC) model integrating adjustable virtual inertia scheduling and multi-stage analytical frequency security constraints. An extended average system frequency (ASF) model is developed to derive closed-form RoCoF and frequency nadir expressions. Wind virtual inertia uncertainty is modeled using a bounded set, and a two-stage robust optimization framework is constructed. The nonlinear nadir constraint is reformulated into mixed-integer second-order cone programming (MISOCP). Case studies on a modified IEEE 118-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method reduces total operating cost while maintaining frequency security under severe contingencies.

title: Bidirectional Covertness and Security Enhancement in FD-ISAC Systems
authors: Jiajie Jian, Min Liu, Shu Fu, Yongming Huang
abstract: To mitigate diverse security threats in 6G full-duplex integrated sensing and communication (FD-ISAC) systems, this paper presents a bidirectional security enhancement framework. This framework simultaneously supports heterogeneous services involving uplink (UL) public and covert access, downlink (DL) secure transmission, and continuous radar sensing within a unified spectrum. Specifically, we exploit the intrinsic high-power radar and public user signals to mask UL covert transmissions while strategically embedding artificial noise into DL radar beams to degrade eavesdropping channels. We establish a joint beamforming and power optimization problem to maximize the aggregate covert and secrecy spectral efficiency. To address channel state information (CSI) uncertainty in the surveillance link, a worst-case constraint based on the S-procedure is derived to strictly guarantee covertness. The resulting non-convex problem is efficiently solved via successive convex approximation (SCA) and rank relaxation. Numerical simulations demonstrate the effective covert communication capability and robust security performance achieved by our proposed scheme.

title: Multi-Scale Symmetric Difference Attention-Based Transformer for Panchromatic and Multispectral Classification
authors: Yuantian Zhu, Hao Zhu, Zeyu Lei, Xiaotong Li, Huihui Dong, Biao Hou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Nowadays, effectively exploiting multispectral (MS) and panchromatic (PAN) imagery for their joint classification task has attracted wide attention. However, due to their heterogeneous resolutions and distinct modality characteristics, the precise alignment of different-scale semantic features and the compact fusion of modality-specific information remain challenging. To tackle these issues, we propose a multi-scale symmetric difference attention-based Transformer (MSAFormer). It comprises two key modules: texture-aware multi-scale feature generation (TMFG) module and symmetric difference feature divergence interaction (SDFD) module. By exploiting PAN’s high-resolution texture to bridge the inherent texture deficiency of MS at continuous resolutions, TMFG builds a multi-scale space with precise texture alignment. This module constructs a robust foundation for the comprehensive extraction of dual-source specific features during interaction. Subsequently, by applying the concept of symmetric difference between sets to the aligned dual-modality groups of different scales, SDFD generates compact and comprehensive multi-scale fusion representations in a non-decoupled manner. These lightweight fusion features integrate the enhanced modality-specific features and eliminate the overlapped redundancy of shared information, ultimately yielding superior classification performance. Several experimental results validate the effectiveness and robustness of the network. Codes are available at https://github.com/Xidian-AIGroup190726/MSAformer.

title: Planning 6-D Grasps for Two-Finger Grippers Considering Edge and Corner Contacts
authors: Zhengtao Hu, Ruishuang Liu, Mocong Qiu, Hao Chen, Liang Du, Takuya Kiyokawa, Weiwei Wan, Jianjun Yuan, Kensuke Harada
abstract: This article presents a novel model-based algorithm for planning antipodal grasps, especially considering the edge and corner contacts. Conventional model-based planners search for face contacts to build grasp synergies, which ensure superior grasp stability, but inherently restrict the geometric versatility of feasible grasp configurations. With soft contact, edges and corners can reshape fingerpads to form equivalent face contacts, enhancing the ability to resist gravity. This allows candidates with greater configuration variability, rather than being constrained solely by face normals. The developed planner detects regions of faces, edges, and corners from a computer-aided design (CAD) model, samples contacts from each region, detects paired contacts using contact detectors corresponding to contact types, and screens the pairs by stability and collision checking. It generates grasp candidates with qualified stability and flexible configurations, breaking the limits of planning for polyhedra, asymmetric objects, and objects with various sharp angles, etc. Experimental results quantitatively demonstrate that the proposed method has superior performance on grasp distributions and success rates using the selected objects from open datasets and off-the-shelf items.

title: Bringing Distributed Ordering to Heterogeneous Memory Consistency
authors: Yanpeng Yu, Nicolai Oswald, Anurag Khandelwal
abstract: CORD is a novel cache-coherence protocol that brings distributed-systems techniques to tightly coupled heterogeneous processors, unlocking efficient and programmer-friendly shared memory for emerging heterogeneous workloads such as AI/ML. Cache-coherence protocols have historically enforced consistency at the source processor, inflating application latency and interconnect traffic. Central to CORD’s efficiency is its reframing of heterogeneous coherence as a distributed-systems problem: drawing on logical-timestamp algorithms for event ordering, CORD assigns consistency-tailored timestamps to write-through stores and orders them at the cache directory — near each store’s completion point at the LLC. In our evaluation, CORD approaches message-passing efficiency while incurring a fraction of the programming complexity, with negligible storage, area, and power overheads. The result is a concrete demonstration that long-standing architectural challenges in shared-memory designs can be tackled through the lens of distributed systems.

title: Plenary: The Role of Engineering and Computer Technology as Artificial Intelligence Turns Into Reality: A Challenge for Professional Education
authors: Melany M. Ciampi, Claudio R Brito, Maria Feldgen, Osvaldo Clua, Agatha da Silva Ovando
abstract: This paper has the goal to present a detailed account of an initiative that has been carried out continuously over the past ten years, the EDUNINE2026 Conference. Educating leaders for continuous technological change requires rethinking engineering and computing in the age of real AI. Today's professionals must go beyond mastering technical tools; they need to understand how artificial intelligence systems are designed, deployed, evaluated, and governed in dynamic, realworld environments. This means integrating strong foundations in computer science and engineering with data literacy, ethical reasoning, systems thinking, and interdisciplinary collaboration. Future leaders must be prepared to adapt to rapid innovation, guide responsible AI adoption, and make strategic decisions that balance technical possibilities with societal impact. By fostering curiosity, resilience, and a commitment to lifelong learning, educational programs can empower engineers and computing professionals to lead confidently in an era defined by continuous technological transformation.

title: A Framework for Evaluating Metro Vehicle Layouts to Maximize Satisfied Passenger Demand: A Case Study of Genoa Metro
authors: Summair Anis, Davide Giglio, Nicola Sacco
abstract: Metro systems of a region play an important role in the provision of high-speed and seamless connectivity between two stops. They also play an important role in addressing the issues related to congestion and environmental concerns while operating at underground levels. However, they often face problems related to the overcrowding of carriages and platforms which affects their efficiency and the comfort they offer to commuters. To this effect, the vehicle carriage layout plays an important role in handling the passenger demand. In this framework, this paper focuses on identifying the best layout of vehicle carriages that should be adopted during different operational periods of the metro to better serve passenger demands with the best achievable resource utilization. In doing so, a mathematical model is formulated with the aim of maximizing the average capacity utilization of metros while analyzing the resulting impacts over satisfied passenger demand. The efficiency of the proposed model is evaluated using the case study for the metro network of Genoa, Italy. In this context, a number of different scenarios of metro vehicle carriage layouts are tested to identify the best layout during two selected time periods, namely peak hour and off-peak hour periods. Overall, the results provide insights to stakeholders for making informed decisions by analyzing the average occupancy of metros in relation to the satisfied passenger demand, under different tested scenarios.

title: Work in Progress: Enhancing Online Certification Through Quality Improvement to Support Workforce Skills in Engineering
authors: Jorge Ortega-Moody, Ghazal Barari, Kouroush Jenab, William Muldoon
abstract: University-industry certificates are increasingly designed to help employees move into new roles while continuing their work responsibilities. This work in progress describes the evaluation of two early courses in a fully online certificate codeveloped with an aerospace manufacturer. Electric Circuits uses mailed component kits and vendor-aligned digital multimeter training, while Industrial Robotics relies exclusively on offline simulation that mirrors teach-pendant programming when plant access is not possible. New course-specific pre and post surveys capture both confidence ratings and open-ended reflections. Early results suggest that industry-relevant topics and tools closely aligned with day-to-day responsibilities can drive meaningful improvement even in an online format. The analysis applies smallsample appropriate methods and highlights quality-improvement actions that will shape future offerings. Planned additions to the certificate include Thermodynamics, Computer-Aided Manufacturing, Sensors and Actuators, Safety, and Mechatronics, which will allow for certificate-level evaluation after Summer 2026.

title: Sparse Representation-based Plant Disease Detection using Leaf Image Processing
authors: Nooshin Derakhshan, Keyvan Asefpour Vakilian
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Existing image-based crop disease recognition approaches commonly determine multiple feature types from images of diseased plant leaves. Yet, a shared limitation remains: those discriminative features are commonly assumed to contribute uniformly to the classification decision. In this study, we present an apple leaf disease recognition pipeline composed of three sequential modules: segmenting diseased leaf images through K-means clustering, deriving shape and color features from the diseased region, and then performing classification of diseased leaf images via the sparse representation (SR) method. One notable strength of this pipeline is that conducting classification within the SR domain can notably decrease computational load while simultaneously boosting recognition accuracy. We benchmark this method against three baseline classifiers - support vector machines (SVM), artificial neural networks (ANN), and decision tree (DT) - using a leaf image dataset containing three apple leaf disease categories: black spot, frogeye leaf spot, and cedar apple rust. Experimental results indicate that the presented pipeline attains 93.9% overall accuracy, whereas the SVM, ANN, and DT achieve 76.8%, 83.7%, and 82.9%, respectively.

title: Complementing Traditional Mathematics Instruction with AI-Assisted Learning Activities
authors: Claudia Hernandez-Mena, Adriana E Martinez-Canton, Mariana Olivares Avalos
abstract: This paper presents an evidence-based instructional methodology for mathematics education that combines traditional instruction with AI-assisted problem solving using ChatGPT integrated with Wolfram Alpha. The approach was implemented in two first-year engineering topics, integral calculus and graphical analysis, to evaluate its effectiveness across different mathematical contexts. The instructional design included a traditional session and an AI-assisted session. Students' perceptions were measured through a Likert-scale survey applied to 49 participants, assessing perceived difficulty, attention, confidence, and ease of error correction. Statistical analysis showed higher perception scores for the AI-assisted activity, with significant improvement in the first implementation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.024,95 {\%}$</tex> confidence) and a positive trend in the second (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.053,90 {\%}$</tex> confidence). Additionally, 90% of students rated the AI feedback as helpful, and 84% reported increased confidence. Future work will focus on analyzing whether this perception translates into measurable learning gains. These results suggest that AI-assisted activities can effectively complement traditional instruction.

title: Developing Custom Arc Flash Protection Logic for Non-Standard BESS Substation Configurations
authors: Ivan Burdukov
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: The integration of large-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS) into medium-voltage distribution networks introduces complex protection challenges, particularly concerning arc flash protection (AFP) and fault current supervision. This paper investigates the vulnerabilities of implementing highly selective AFP in a non-standard 35 kV BESS topology equipped with a grounding transformer and a neutral grounding resistor. In such configurations, the specific routing of zero-sequence currents during single-line-to-ground faults fundamentally alters the network's fault signature. Consequently, conventional overcurrent supervision algorithms become unreliable, risking complete failure to trip during severe enclosed arc faults. To resolve this critical safety gap, the dynamic fault current distribution across multiple operational modes is analyzed using DIgSILENT PowerFactory. Based on this analysis, a custom, topology-adaptive protection logic is developed to ensure secure phase and ground fault supervision without introducing unnecessary complexity. The proposed logical scheme guarantees absolute selectivity and was successfully verified and commissioned in microprocessor-based intelligent electronic devices, demonstrating robust stability and operational reliability in a live industrial environment.

title: Reconfigurable Computing Challenge: FPGA-Based WebAssembly Stack Co-Processor
authors: Qiuping Wu, Mugeng Liu, Yixuan Zhang, Hongxiao Zhao, Yihan Fu, Gang Huang, Yun Ma, Bonan Yan
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: Large language models suffer from hallucinations when performing scientific computing, motivating the use of AI agents such as IronClaw that offload computation to specialized tools. IronClaw invokes tools implemented as WebAssembly (Wasm) plugins for security and extensibility, but the stack-based Wasm bytecode is mismatched with register-based processors (x86, ARM), causing runtime overhead. We propose PAWS, a native Wasm coprocessor that directly executes Wasm bytecode in hardware. PAWS features: (1) full support for all five Wasm instruction types; (2) dual digital stack circuits (operand stack and control stack) replacing register files to minimize memory access latency; (3) dedicated control logic for block-based branching; and (4) a sliding-window instruction fetch unit that decodes variable-length Wasm instructions. Evaluated on the PolyBench suite, PAWS achieves average execution latencies 28.6× lower than an Intel Xeon processor and 40.6× lower than an Nvidia Jetson TX2, making it highly suitable for IronClaw’s compute-intensive scientific applications. The design is available at https://github.com/Iris-WQP/PAWS_FPGA_softcore.

title: PathSteiner: Improving PathFinder with Quasi-Optimal Steiner-Tree Initialization
authors: Shashwat Shrivastava, Luka Kurešević, Alexandros Poupakis, Chirag Ravishankar, Dinesh Gaitonde, Stefan Nikolić, Mirjana Stojilović
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: FPGA routing has been studied for a few decades due to its critical role in determining both the quality of results and compilation time. Recent work has shown that constructing more resource-efficient routing trees can improve both runtime and wirelength. Yet, the proposed approach of constructing multiple routing trees per net via random sink orderings—within the PathFinder routing paradigm—and selecting the best candidate becomes increasingly impractical as net fanout grows. This limitation arises because the space of possible sink orderings grows super-exponentially, allowing only a small fraction of it to be explored in practice. Moreover, even exhaustive enumeration of all sink permutations for low-fanout nets does not necessarily yield an optimal routing tree. To address these limitations, we initialize routing with quasi-optimal rectilinear Steiner trees to better cover the solution space across net fanouts. Our results show that, because these trees are only weakly congestion-aware, the final routed wirelength gains are real but fall short of the wirelength advantage implied by the seeded trees. How much of the gain remains depends on how effectively PathFinder resolves congestion while staying close to the seed. We report an average 6% improvement in wirelength, along with an 8× reduction in runtime on the most congested benchmark, which otherwise takes more than half a day to route.

title: Parameter Optimization Design for Suppressing Second Harmonics in Star-Configured High-Voltage Transformer-less Battery Energy Storage System
authors: Yilin Liu, Xiqi Wu, Rui Li, Han Wang, Wenfei Liu, Xu Cai
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper presents a novel methodology for suppressing second harmonic current in the DC bus of power units within star-configured high-voltage transformer-less battery energy storage systems (BESS). The research extends beyond suppression techniques to explore optimized system design parameters that enhance harmonic current mitigation capabilities. The proposed approach introduces a third harmonic zero-sequence voltage injection method to effectively counteract the second harmonic current components flowing into the power unit's battery DC side. From a system optimization perspective, this methodology significantly improves battery current ripple performance while enhancing overall economic efficiency. Through comprehensive theoretical analysis and multi-objective optimization techniques, this study thoroughly investigates the suppression mechanisms affecting second harmonic current in power module DC sides. The optimization framework incorporates practical engineering constraints to determine optimal parameter configurations for high-voltage transformer-less BESS implementations. Unlike existing approaches that utilize third harmonic zero-sequence voltage for harmonic suppression, this research adopts a holistic system perspective that accounts for critical engineering factors including filter volume requirements and component oversizing costs. The resulting solution effectively minimizes module current ripple while maintaining cost-effectiveness, representing a significant advancement in transformer-less BESS design methodology.

title: Trajectory Tracking and Reinforcement Learning Based Obstacle Avoidance for a Cable-Driven Parallel Robot With Aerial-Ground Mobile Bases
authors: Ziyu Wan, Wenrui Xie, Hao Xiong, Long Li, Hantao Jiang, Lin Zhang, Yunjiang Lou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: A Cable-Driven Parallel Robot (CDPR) is a robot driven by a set of cables and has advantages such as large workspace and low inertia. Scholars have proposed to apply Aerial Mobile Bases (AMBs) and Ground Mobile Bases (GMBs) to a CDPR to achieve a large workspace and fully constrain the moving platform. However, trajectory tracking and obstacle avoidance for CDPR-AGMBs remain open and challenging problems due to their heterogeneous actuation and complex dynamics. This paper proposes a trajectory tracking planner based on optimization and a real-time obstacle avoidance planner based on reinforcement learning (RL) for CDPR-AGMBs. Both planners are developed for a CDPR-AGMB system consisting of four length-variable cables connected to four GMBs and one fixed-length cable connected to an AMB. A physical prototype of the CDPR-AGMB has been built to evaluate the proposed methods. The trajectory tracking planner is implemented according to the prototype’s physical parameters, while the obstacle avoidance planner is trained in simulation using MuJoCo and then deployed on the prototype for real-world validation.

title: BliChain: A Blockchain-Assisted and Lightweight Cross-Domain Authentication Scheme for 6G-Enabled VANET
authors: Jiaxin Li, Hong Zhong, Yun Shen, Lu Wei, Irina Bolodurina, Jie Cui
abstract: In the evolution of 6G-based Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks (VANETs) from closed single-domain environments to open cross-domain environments, several security challenges arise, including heterogeneous domain trust barriers, vehicle identity privacy leakage, and high authentication overhead in dynamic scenarios. Existing schemes based on centralized public key infrastructure (PKI) suffer from single point failure risks and have difficulty balancing low latency with conditional privacy preservation. Meanwhile, blockchain-assisted solutions are often constrained by on-chain storage expansion and high computational overhead. To address these issues, this paper proposes a blockchain-assisted anonymous authentication scheme for 6G-VANETs. The proposed scheme uses blockchain to construct distributed trust anchors and enable secure sharing of public parameters across domains. By generating lightweight authentication parameters based on Lagrange interpolation polynomials, the scheme supports direct mutual authentication and session key agreement between vehicles without requiring massive certificate-related on-chain transactions. In addition, a pseudo-identity mechanism is introduced to achieve conditional privacy preservation, thereby balancing vehicle identity privacy and traceability. Security analysis demonstrates that the proposed scheme satisfies the required security properties under the random oracle model. Performance evaluation shows that compared with existing solutions, the proposed scheme significantly reduces computational overhead by 22.31%, 11.02%, and 29.19%, respectively, and communication overhead by 51.2%, 20.08%, and 24.61%, respectively.

title: For Miniaturized Gas Chromatographs: An Acquisition Module with a High-Stability Constant-Current Source for Micro-Thermal Conductivity Detectors
authors: Shunlong Xu, Shaojie Ma, Wenbo Li, Zhirui Li, Fei Feng
abstract: Miniaturized gas chromatographs (GCs) are ideal for in situ detection due to their compact size and low power consumption. The MEMS-based micro-thermal conductivity detector (μTCD) is widely used in these systems. Its acquisition module typically includes an excitation source, protection circuitry, and differential acquisition circuitry. A constant current excitation reduces signal non-linearity more effectively than constant voltage excitation, while its high stability significantly suppresses output noise. This paper presents a voltage-controlled constant current source (VCCS) and analyzes the factors influencing its stability. Experimental results demonstrated that the VCCS outperformed commercial alternatives. At 50 mA, the current fluctuation remained within 3 μA over 12 hours—approximately one-tenth that of commercial products. The VCCS achieved a maximum accuracy of 0.0035% full scale (FS) and a relative standard deviation (RSD) of 0.0011%. This module provides a reliable solution for miniaturized GC design. Tests using light hydrocarbons (20–2000 ppm) showed a peak area RSD below 3.8% and excellent linearity with concentration. Comparative tests with a commercial GC module showed that, at an excitation current of 50 mA and a 10 ppm gas concentration, the proposed module provided better detection capabilities. It enabled clear observation of chromatographic peaks and achieved a limit of detection (LOD) of 6 ppm for propane.

title: Waveguide-Compatible Terahertz Direct Amplitude Modulator Based on a Schottky Barrier Diode Reconfigurable Square-Ring Unit
authors: Chunyang Bi, Zhichao Bi, Zhixiang Xu, Yu Ao, Sen Gong, Shixiong Liang, Ziqiang Yang, Yaxin Zhang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Direct amplitude modulation above 300GHz is challenged by strong device parasitics, high structural sensitivity, and limited separation between high- and low-transmission states. This letter presents a waveguide-compatible terahertz (THz) direct amplitude modulator using a Schottky barrier diode (SBD) reconfigurable square-ring unit. Two Schottky diodes are mounted across the gaps between metal patches and form a reconfigurable square-ring unit with the metal structure. With bias control, the unit changes between an open-ring, weak-loading state and a closed-ring, resonant-loading state. When the SBDs are cutoff, the unit weakly loads the incident THz field and maintains a high transmission. When the SBDs conduct, the metal patches and diodes form a closed square ring, which enhances the local resonance and suppresses the transmitted signal. Measurements show clear static transmission modulation above 300GHz and OOK-NRZ direct amplitude modulation up to 25Gb/s. The proposed structure provides a practical route for waveguide-compatible, THz direct amplitude modulation above 300GHz.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters

title: Smart Water Management System for Multi-Storey Residential Buildings
authors: Godlin Debby K. J, Eswar A, Jayasurya S, Naveen Kumar R, Suradhish R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This research work proposes a Smart Water Management System (SWMS) based on the use of Internet of Things (IoT) technology, embedded controllers, real, time sensors and cloud, based data sculping to automate and enhance water distribution in multi, storey residential buildings. The utilitarian of the scheme is intelligent pump automation, smart metering at individual flats, real, time leak detection, pressure monitoring, and cloud, based dashboards accessible through mobile and web applications. User alerts and consumption analytics foster transparency and facilitate responsible water usage. Simulation and experimental tests demonstrated that the proposes model has the potential to greatly increase water utilization efficiency, lower cost of operations, reduce water losses due to leakages and overflows, and simultaneously support sustainable urban water management.

title: Analysis of Multiple-Output Integrated Receivers for Wireless Power Transfer in Biomedical Implants
authors: Yifan Yao, Shilong Shen, Alice S. Lee, Hoi Lee, Jin Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) via inductive coupling is widely used to power different biomedical implants. Traditionally, a WPT receiver consists of a first-stage rectifier for AC-DC conversion and a second-stage single-inductor multipleoutput DC-DC converter that provides different supply rails to the analog, digital, and stimulation modules inside the implant. Recently, multiple-output-regulating (MOR) rectifiers have been reported to perform the same function as the conventional twostage architecture with a single power stage, significantly improving system efficiency and reducing receiver size. The paper reviews state-of-the-art MOR rectifiers and analyzes their pros and cons with respect to power efficiency and scalability. We then provide design suggestions to help researchers and engineers design a new high-performance MOR rectifier.

title: Architecting Ethical and Scalable Artificial Intelligence Systems: A Secure Deployment Model with Real-World Benchmarking
authors: Arvinder Singh, Mandeep Singh, Manpreet Kaur, Vikas Attri, Sakshi Chauhan, Rajinder Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The sheer rampant growth of artificial intelligence (AI) systems in the safety-critical, regulated, and large-scale areas of infrastructures has enhanced the demand of deployment models that are secure, ethical compliant, and scalable in terms of operation. The proposed research aims to develop a layered AI system architecture with verifiable privacy, federated orchestration, and explanation of decision-making as the first principles, as opposed to an afterthought. The framework consists of three modular parts, namely a privacy preserving computational substrate based on holomorphic encryption (HE) and differential privacy (DP), a distributed trust model that is enforced by a zero knowledge proofs (ZKP) and decentralized authentication protocols, and an ethical governance layer that includes policy-aware execution and explainable AI (XAI) reasoning. In contrast to the isolated sandbox setup, this architecture was tried in the realistic environment of a deployment using the representative of the domain data, such as OpenAQ sensor telemetry, MIMIC-III health logs, or smart energy grid datasets. The simulations were performed on 60-node Docker Swarm cluster using orchestrated adversarial attacks such as input reconstruction, consensus disruption, and metadata inference. Based on empirical evidence, the results indicate the latency stabilization at less than 140 ms, the cryptographic computation overhead at less than 22 percent and the privacy leakage reduction at more than 83 percent compared to centralized conventional baselines. These results confirm the correctness of implementing scalable AI solutions and not compromising ethical outcomes, regulatory guidance, and computational performance. This is the stepping block of future AI infrastructures that will have to be at scale, constrained, and in a similar spirit as the human-centric values.

title: A 12.5mW Deep Neural Network Accelerator for Continuous Seizure Forecasting in Edge Devices
authors: Bailey Heynsten, Muhammad Awais Bin Altaf
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Early prediction of epileptic seizures can significantly improve patient safety and enable proactive interventions. However, achieving accurate forecasting on wearable devices is challenging due to stringent power and area constraints. This paper presents a hardware-efficient Deep Neural Network (DNN) accelerator for continuous seizure prediction, capable of forecasting seizure risk up to 60 seconds (s) before onset. The proposed design leverages a hybrid feature set combining spectral ratios and temporal dynamics with patient-specific normalization to enhance sensitivity to pre-ictal transitions. A compact DNN architecture with quantized weights and a fixedexponent sigmoid activation, implemented via a 16-entry lookup table, achieves 31.4% area reduction and 15.4% lower energy per operation compared to IEEE-754 floating-point designs. FPGAbased validation using the CHB-MIT EEG database demonstrates prediction accuracies of 96.5%, 94.3%, 91.8%, and 89.7% for 10 s, 20 s, 30 s, and 60 s horizons, respectively, with an end-to-end latency of 0.8 s. Post-synthesis results in 180 nm CMOS report 12.5 mW power consumption, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.33 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex> area, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.33 \mu \mathbf{J}$</tex> per inference, making the accelerator well-suited for resource-constrained wearable applications. These results establish a practical pathway toward real-time, energy-efficient seizure forecasting at the edge.

title: Mindfulness Meditation in Workplace: Satisfaction and Harmonious Employee-Employer Relationships
authors: Aparna Vajpayee, Emery Bokey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This research investigates the transformative impact of mindfulness practices within organizational contexts, focusing on their influence on employee satisfaction, organizational culture, and overall well-being. Mindfulness, encompassing both individual and collective dimensions, is explored for its role in fostering adaptability and mental resilience in navigating life's challenges. The study employs a comprehensive research design, drawing on both qualitative and quantitative methods. Survey questionnaires are utilized to gather data from a diverse sample within organizational settings. The research design incorporates a multilevel approach, simultaneously exploring individual and collective mindfulness to provide a nuanced understanding of their effects. The measurements encompass a range of factors, including individual well-being, organizational culture, and employee satisfaction. The findings reveal the measurable and tangible impact of mindfulness practices. Individual and collective mindfulness are linked to positive outcomes at various levels, emphasizing their significance in shaping organizational dynamics. Clear connections are established between mindfulness and enhanced adaptability, mental resilience, and overall well-being. The study emphasizes the need for ongoing multilevel mindfulness research to comprehensively grasp the implications of these practices in organizational psychology and behavior.

title: Designing Adaptive User Interfaces for Elderly Users in Smart Homes: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Zainab Almahafdha, Shaikha Hasan, Wael Elmedany
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: As the elderly population increases, the adoption of smart home technologies to enhance elderly lives has gained significant attention. However, traditional user interfaces (UIs) in smart home environments do not fit with the needs of older users having poor vision and decreased cognition. This emphasizes the need for an adaptive user interface (AUI) that provides a solution for elderly problems by fitting the design to their needs and requirements. The aim of this study is to examine the current research trends and design principles for designing adaptive user interfaces specifically for elderly users in smart home environments. A systematic literature review (SLR) following PRISMA guidelines will be conducted to identify existing user interface design principles, and propose future design best practices for elderly users. The study synthesizes the findings from peer-reviewed articles which were published from 2014 to 2024. The results of this SLR identify key design considerations, challenges, and suggestions for enhancing elderly user experience and accessibility in smart homes.

title: A Detecting Method For Line-Spectrum Target Based on Time-Frequency Signal Presence Probability Sensing
authors: Lekai Chen, Xiang Pan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This study introduces a line-spectrum target recognition methodology utilizing time-frequency signal presence probability (TFSPP) sensing, aimed at estimating the bearings of weak targets in underwater settings characterized by significant interference. The proposed method utilizes the narrowband characteristics and long-term stability of line-spectrum signals to estimate the probability of signal presence at each frequency bin and time frame, employing these probabilities as weights for a weighted summation of the spatial power spectra across various beam directions. This amplifies the target's line-spectrum elements while attenuating noise and interference. Simulation results and maritime experimental data indicate that the TFSPP based conventional beamforming(TFSPP_CBF) surpasses traditional CBF, narrowband CBF, and variance-of-frequency weight based conventional beamforming(VFW_CBF) under low signal-to-noise ratio(SNR) and high-interference conditions. The suggested method efficiently emphasizes target bearings and mitigates interfering beams, thereby enhancing detection performance greatly.

title: Design and Launch of Solid Propellant Reusable Rocket
authors: Reel Mohamed Ahmed, Yousef Salman, Karan Anoop Whabi, Sharul Sham Dol, Mohammed Alavi, Ahmad Al Ramahi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: $A B S T R A C T$ -The purpose of this project is to design and build a solid propellant-powered reusable rocket using repurposed and easy to obtain materials, with a safe landing mechanism, emphasizing reusability, recyclable materials and cost effectiveness. Several factors are the success rate of the rocket, along with the efficiency of the propulsion system, which was a key driver in the success of this project. The available propulsion motor was an Estes B6-4 motor. Hence, the propulsion system influenced the design and choice of materials for the rocket. The rocket model was simulated using the OpenRocket simulator, a software which gave rapid estimates of the expected flight phases and characteristics allowing quick rectifications of the rocket design through testing for different configurations to achieve the optimum results. The simulation results closely matched results from the launch where an apogee altitude of 59 meters, a maximum velocity of $31.8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and a safe landing in the span of 16.6 s was achieved. These results marked the success of the rocket launch and imply the concept of reuseable model rocketry supported by easily adaptable softwares like OpenRocket

title: AI-Based Daily Heart Risk Guardian (DHRG) Using XGBoost Algorithm
authors: Aishweryam Jofin J, Elakkiya E
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: CVD is still the global leader of deaths and therefore, we need to detect those at risk of suffering a heart attack or other cardiovascular disease early, so that we can prevent them from developing into a serious health risk. The proposed DHRG system will take raw data from multiple sources, process this data into appropriate formats (i.e. standard units), then combine the processed data into a single database. Then the machine learning algorithm will use this single database to train the XGBoost model to predict a user's chance of having a heart problem. Finally, it will generate a risk level (low, medium, high) based on the user's daily activity levels (i.e., inactivity, active, very active, etc.) and give them personalized recommendations on what to do next (e.g., eat better, start exercising, etc.). Unlike current technologies, which rely almost exclusively on either ECG data or lifestyle data alone, the DHRG system integrates lifestyle, physiological, and environmental information into one area, thus providing health care providers and patients an integrated, detailed daily assessment of the risk of developing CVD and the ability to help prevent it.

title: Skin Lesion Classification for Skin Cancer Using MobileNetV2
authors: E. Loganathan, K.S. Chowmiya, N. Prakash, J.K. Kanimozhi, P. Anitha, R. Geetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This study focuses on building a dependable skin lesion classification model to support early skin cancer diagnosis. The work compares the performance of a standard CNN architecture the MobileNetV2 model when applied to dermoscopic images. Two independent groups were designed for the evaluation. Group 1 involved a CNN trained with traditional convolutional operations, whereas Group 2 used MobileNetV2 incorporating depthwise-separable filters and transfer learning to enhance feature extraction of lesion patterns. In the Kaggle dataset, 2,500 dermoscopic images were split for training, validation and testing. Group 1 achieved accuracy of 89.27% using MobileNetV2 outperforming Group 2 using CNN model that was inferior in performance with significant margin. When researchers measured the performances of the two techniques they found out that the difference was statistically significant. In other words, it was found that MobileNetV2 was more efficient in classifying skin lesion images. The MobileNetV2 architecture was found to be more efficient, reliable, and stable than the conventional CNN model for skin lesion classification. MobileNetV2 can be utilized in early skin cancer screening which can also be used for realtime dermatology-assisted systems.

title: Latency-Budgeted Terminal–Edge Collaborative Video Analytics with Adaptive Masking and Content-Aware Encoding
authors: Lixin Jin, Kangli Zhao, Penglin Dai, Xincao Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Real-time edge video analytics is critical for latency-sensitive applications but remains challenging under dynamic video content and fluctuating wireless bandwidth. Existing terminal–edge collaborative systems often rely on static offloading, fragile Region of Interest (RoI) extraction, or coarse-grained compression, leading to unstable latency or inefficient bandwidth usage. This paper proposes SCORE, an adaptive terminal–edge collaborative framework built on a batch-based processing model. SCORE integrates three tightly coupled modules: a Latency-Aware Keyframe Scheduler that dynamically selects keyframes under strict latency budgets, a Adaptive Foreground Masker that adaptively refines RoI extraction based on detection feedback, and a Joint Resolution–Quality Optimizer that jointly optimizes spatial resolution and encoding quality for RoI transmission. Experiments on a real hardware testbed with traffic surveillance datasets show that SCORE achieves up to 2.8× higher throughput than state-of-the-art methods while maintaining comparable detection accuracy and significantly reducing bandwidth consumption.

title: Enhancing Credit Scoring Accuracy Using Machine Learning and Alternative Data Sources in a Credit Card Company in Bahrain
authors: Mohamed Al-Shakhoori, Abdulrahman A. Shakh, Minwir Al-Shammari
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The purpose of this study is to evaluate the effects of introducing alternative internal variables into credit scoring models to improve forecast accuracy and interpretability. The ML models were assessed using cross validation and a held-out test set, with performance measured by the Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC-ROC), a performance metric for binary classification models that assesses a model’s ability to distinguish between positive and negative classes across all possible thresholds of AUC-ROC and related classification metrics. The findings show that models integrating alternative data consistently outperform those using only traditional variables. The Random Forest and CatBoost classifiers continued to be top performers, achieving AUC ROC scores of approximately 1 in the enhanced scenario. To improve interpretability and identify the leading causes of default risk, the study applied SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis. SHAP values are a method for explaining the output of any machine learning model. It employs a game-theoretic approach that measures each player’s contribution to the outcome. The overall findings highlight the importance of incorporating internal behavioral data to improve default prediction accuracy. This study contributes to the growing body of research in credit risk modeling, offering valuable insights and measurable evidence for credit organizations seeking to update and modernize their scoring methodologies and systems.

title: Data-Driven Insights in Consumer and Workforce Analytics Using AI and Natural Language Processing
authors: Ajay Narayan Shukla, Meghna Sharma, Raj Kumar, Ulugmurodova Nargiza, Richa Vijay, Harjeet Kaur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) have become major pillars in modern enterprise intelligence - transforming the way in which organizations analyze and act on large amounts of information. In this paper, data-driven methods to provide actionable insights through consumer behavior, workforce data by AI and Natural Language Processing models are reviewed. It explains how sentiment analysis, predictive modeling and machine learning enabled HR analytics plays an important role in improving HR decision making, engagement and productivity. The proposed framework addresses the structured and unstructured processing for strategic planning in the context of marketing and HR. A thorough review of the state-of-the-art available studies shows that real-time monitoring of the plume, feedback assimilation, and performance prediction are possible based on AI-based analytics. Recommendations for impacts suggest the development of more transparency and better operational efficiency and market competitiveness due to AI-NLP implementation. Future directions are multimodal forms of analytics that fuse text, speech and behavioral information to improve how a company understands and grammatical learning throughout omers and organizational ecosystems.

title: Fuzzy Controller Assisted DVR for Grid-Connected PV Nanogrid Under Severe Grid Faults
authors: Maheshwari Thippanolla, Sobhana Obulareddy, J. Viswanatha Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The increasing adoption of photovoltaic based nano grids necessities a critical need for fault management system to ensure uninterrupted supply to critical loads. This paper presents a Fuzzy assisted Dynamic Voltage Restorer (DVR) to mitigate power quality issues within the distribution system. The proposed PV nano grid has a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithm, a three-phase voltage source inverter with fuzzy controller assisted Dynamic Voltage Restorer (DVR). The MPPT controller sustains optimal photovoltaic output voltage under varying irradiance conditions during standard operational procedure. The analysis is carried out with significant three-phase faults and sensitive loads. As a series-connected FACTS device, the DVR regulates voltage by feeding a compensating voltage directly into the line. By adopting a novel FLC-assisted control strategy, the DVR achieves a faster response to system disturbances compared with conventional approaches. The proposed controller was modeled and validated using MATLAB/Simulink, and a comparative study was presented with and without DVR protection. The results indicate the FLC-DVR provides superior accuracy and performance over conventional PI controllers.

title: Web-Based Platform for Efficient User-Vendor Interaction and Marketplace Integration
authors: Vaibhav Dabhade, Kunal Ahire, Namrata Ghuse, Trupti M. Gunjal, Aishwarya S. Ghumare, Sakshi K. Pingle
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This Platform is a comprehensive web-based solution that is intended to facilitate the connection between users and vendors who provide a diverse range of services, including plumbing and electrical work. The platform resolves inefficiencies in the conventional service request process by allowing users to submit tasks and complaints, which vendors can view and approve in real-time. The system also includes a marketplace where users and vendors can list, purchase, sell, rent, donate, or exchange products. The platform provides a secure, scalable, and user-friendly experience by simplifying the handling of service requests through its comprehensive complaint management, task assignment, and product listing modules. The platform incorporates features such as user and vendor registration, complaint listing and management, product listing, and authentication mechanisms. Designed with Flask, SQL Alchemy, and SQLite, this system ensures data integrity, scalability, and usability.

title: AI-Powered Predictive Analytics Framework for Urban Heat Island (UHI) Mitigation
authors: Sana Alam, Manshi Chauhan, Ishaan Saxena, Akshaya Shukla, Lakshita Aggarwal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The UHI effect refers to cities being hotter than their rural counterparts, with a corresponding uptick in risk for health, energy, and food systems. Existing strategies reactively respond to events, while urban resilience situates predictive intervention on a data-driven assessment. This paper presents an AI-based framework that integrates multi-source datasets: satellite imagery, weather data, geospatial data, and socio-demographics, to create a predictive model. It considers the biological activities taking place around us, due to which the urban lands are more prone. The latest state-of-the-art shows the biological and chemical effects that become the primitive reasons for the same. Machine learning algorithms such as XGBoost, produces the best achievable accuracy for predicting land surface temperature. Causality can be explicitly touted using indicators from an Explainable AI module (SHAP), which quantifies the contribution of local drivers of UHI intensity using weights. The framework can also serve as a simulator where planners can model different interventions, such as green infrastructure, or reflectance, to assess impacts prior to implementing those interventions. They integrate prediction, explanation and simulation into a decision support system to create new and equitable racing routes for cities to improve resilience to climate change in terms of urban planners.

title: A CNN Framework for Accurate Fatty Liver Disease Prediction Using EfficientNetB3 and DenseNet121
authors: Karthi V, Manikandan K, Hariomprakaas M, Madhu Mitha M, Pradeep S, Saik Muhamed Aslam J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The primary goal of this work is to construct and apply an automated system leveraging deep learning methods to classify liver ultrasound images into Healthy and Hepatic Steatosis and to compare the performance of EfficientNetB3 and DenseNet121 transfer learning models. Materials and Methods: Data samples were allocated, where Group 1 utilized the existing single pretrained CNN approach and Group 2 utilized the proposed transfer learning model with Global Average Pooling. Result: The performance matrix at a 95% confidence level, and the proposed approach attained an accuracy of about 95–96%, whereas the existing approach attained an accuracy of about 90–91%. Conclusion: The proposed framework integrates EfficientNetB3 and DenseNet121 with a two-stage transfer learning strategy and statistical validation, achieving improved accuracy and reliability for early detection of hepatic steatosis.

title: Integrated Power Line Fault Detection Using Arduino With GSM
authors: Krosuri Lakshmi Revathi, Varagani Pavan Roshan Krishna, Thanniru Sudarshan, Rajulapati Dhanush
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Transmission Line Protection is a major problem in today’s power systems. In the modern era, more than 80 percent of the power transmission faults occur because of the transmission lines. In this Identifying the fault’s source location is very time consuming process and increased risk in the equipment damage. Sometimes due to natural phenomena the transmission line may be cut and these hanging electrical wires have caused multiple deaths. Identifying the fault location detection will reduce the time required for repairs also and minimize deaths. With the help of GSM Modem we can identify the fault location detection. The system also integrates fire detection sensor (IR sensor) and monitors the resistance variation to detect short circuits. A capacitor is added to measure the impedance, aiding in detection of the open-circuit faults. Transmission Line status displays on the LCD Board and if there is a fault an SMS alert is sent to predefined numbers through GSM modem, ensuring the notification. This is a low cost and efficient solution which provides better safety near power transmission systems.

title: Research on PCB Defect Detection Algorithm Based on YOLOv5
authors: Lu Gao, Kim Young Chul
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of electronic information technology, electronic products are evolving toward higher precision and smaller dimensions. Meanwhile, manufacturing quality directly determines product reliability and stability. As core components of electronic devices, printed circuit boards (PCBs) require rigorous quality inspections to ensure production integrity, yet PCB manufacturing processes are prone to various defects. To address challenges such as small target dimensions, diverse defect types, complex background interference, and difficult recognition of tiny defects in PCB defect detection, this study proposes an improved YOLOv5 algorithm. The proposed approach first incorporates a SENet network to autonomously learn feature channel importance weights, enhancing target detection accuracy for small and weak defects. It then integrates decoupled detection head architecture into the YOLOv5 network to eliminate task conflicts between classification and bounding box regression, while improving defect detection precision and accelerating network convergence. Furthermore, the original activation function is replaced with FReLU to enhance spatial feature capture capability. Experimental results demonstrate that the anchor-based algorithm achieves an accuracy rate (P) of 87.8%, representing a 1 percentage point improvement over the original YOLOv5 algorithm, and mAP@0.5 is increased by 3 percentage points, further validating the effectiveness of this optimization.

title: FarmSense Pro: A Data-Driven Approach to Crop Yield and Price Estimation
authors: Anupriya K, Satheesh Kumar D, Bala Murugan K, Bala Nithin RK, Gunal Christ A, Praveen A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The economic development and food security of every nation require farmers and policymakers to forecast future crop yields and market prices through systematic analysis of multiple data sets. The research project presents Farm Sense Pro as an advanced web-based platform which scientists can use to predict crop yields and market prices through the application of machine learning methods. The platform uses agricultural data and environmental data and market data to help farmers make better decisions about their crop production and sales activities. For example, when predicting crop production, several variables are considered, including soil type, amount and timing of rainfall, temperature and weather conditions, amount of fertilizer applied, irrigation methods, area of the country are farming, and time left until the crop is harvested. For predicting the selling price per crop, the platform will consider a demand index, supply index, seasonal variations in price, previous price at the market, temperature and amount of rain in a particular geographic area, and month. Recent machine learning algorithms, including Linear Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest and XGBoost are used in developing predictive models in order to improve the accuracy of predicting crop production and selling price. The platform was developed using Python and Stream lit to create an interactive, user-friendly web-based dashboard that allows users to enter data, generate predictions and visualize predictions through charts and analytics. Additionally, the platform contains support materials such as insights about the market and seasonality outlooks on prices to help users understand the seasonal price swings of their crops.

title: Adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) by Workers: Extending Theory of Planned Behaviour (TPB) with IT Knowledge and Price Value
authors: Ahmad Bilal Almagribi, Bambang Purnomosidi Dwi Putranto
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology has seen widespread adoption across various sectors, including by employees within numerous organizations. Despite its many benefits, accessing the full features of AI often involves a cost for these workers. Consequently, it is crucial to understand their behavioral responses to this technology. The purpose of this study was to evaluate the impact of the Theory of Planned Behaviour (TPB), IT Knowledge, and Price Value on the Behavioral Intention to Adopt AI. The study employed a quantitative method, collecting primary data from 169 Indonesian workers across various professions who were selected through purposive sampling. Subsequent analysis of the data was conducted using partial least squares-structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM) with the SmartPLS 4 software. The results indicated that both Attitude and Subjective Norms significantly influenced the Behavioral Intention to Adopt AI. The researchers recommend that organizations should embrace this technology but must also establish clear regulations and provide guidance to employees on its wise and ethical use. Future studies are encouraged to expand the sample size, explore alternative analytical techniques such as path analysis or multiple regression, and incorporate other potential moderating or mediating variables.

title: AI Based Engineering Framework Addressing Total Quality Management Based on Workplace Management
authors: K.S.Sivakumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the evolving landscape of organizational excellence, Total Quality Management (TQM) emphasizes systematic improvement and performance optimization. However, its effectiveness can be significantly enhanced by incorporating human-centric, values-based approaches. This study explores the integration of Bhagavad Gita’s spiritual teachings—such as Nishkama Karma (selfless action), Dharma (righteous duty), Samatvam (equanimity), and Seva (selfless service)—into the workplace, and their impact on TQM outcomes. Using a mixed-method approach involving employee surveys, organizational performance data, and ANOVA statistical analysis, the research finds that Gita-inspired workplace spirituality positively correlates with improvements in productivity, customer satisfaction, process efficiency, and employee retention. Additionally, it enhances ethical behavior, stress resilience, and emotional intelligence among employees. The findings demonstrate that spiritual engagement is not merely a personal attribute but a strategic asset that aligns inner transformation with external excellence. The study positions workplace spirituality as an essential dimension of sustainable quality management in the 21st century.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Dynamic State Abstractions
authors: Shripad Joshi, Bhavana Santosh Pansare, Anandakumar Haldorai, Donamol Joseph, Shaziya Islam, D. Vetrithangam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Generally, the performance of RL in generalized state space and the non-adaptive modes of representation in complex environments are poor. The current paper can offer a solution to the problem by coming up with a dynamic state abstraction scheme according to which the agent can revise its internal representations by the changing task structure and environmental feedback. Unlike traditional methods that rely on fixed or non-adaptive abstractions, the proposed method jointly optimizes a learnable policy network and encoder with smoothness regularization to maintain temporal coherence of abstract states. Long experiments in benchmark environments demonstrate that the dynamic abstraction approach significantly outperforms common baselines, such as DQN, Static Abstraction RL, and Latent Space PPO in terms of task accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(89.3 \%)$</tex>, generalization <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(83.1 \%)$</tex>, and sample efficiency (81.9%). The results of the experiment testify to the merits of adaptive abstractions for faster convergence and stability. The contribution shows the promise of dynamic representation learning towards developing more efficient and generalizable RL agents.

title: A Machine Learning Framework for Predictive Maintenance of Power Transformers Using CAI and EFRI Metrics
authors: C V P R Prasad, Shyam Kishore G, Parvathy K, N.Jyothsna, Senthilkumar Aandi, Anuradha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: A machine intelligence foundation for predictive perpetuation of capacity transformers utilizing CAI and EFRI versification is proposed to address postponed discovery and damaging unplanned outages in new grids. Motivated by assorted analyst like SCADA telemetry, Condition-Aware Indicators (CAI) calculations, Frequency Response Analysis (FRA)/EFRI spectral sweeps, and warm metaphors, the objective search out fuse multimodal signals to enhance early-collapse discovery, staying useful existence guess, and cost-knowledgeable decision-making. The Multimodal Cost-Aware Transformer for Predictive Maintenance (MCA-TransPdM) model joins windowed multimodal inputs and engages parallel encoders (Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM), Transformer) whose embeddings are melded into a meta-classifier; strength is enhanced by way of opposing preparation, denoising, modality disguising, and BRITS-style pretraining for uneven samples, while a conclusion-cost piece selects deployment thresholds. Evaluation on classical datasets manifests forceful performance: AUC ≈0.990, PR-AUC ≈0.952, RUL MAE ≈6.3 days, RMSE ≈ 8.1 days, and anticipated annual cost diminished to ≈ 13,396,200 INR at the optimum beginning versus ≈21,513,600 INR (SoTA-2024) and ≈ 43,707,800 INR (telemetry-only). Implementation analyses containing preprocessing, the earth's features-aware face, and approach people present at event are given to support reproducibility and integration accompanying existent advantage-management schemes, and manipulator-in-the-loop confirmation tests. These results indicate concrete functional harvests, fewer dishonest alarms, and former interferences, supporting arranged field pilots, routine recalibration, and connected to the internet incessant education to ensure arrangement eagerness across various sites

title: Evaluating the Impact of Work-Life Balance on Job Performance Among Women Police Personnel in Chennai City
authors: Nithya M, Kavitha M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Work-life balance (WLB) has emerged as a critical concern in the high-pressure occupations like policing, especially for women who often tends to face dual responsibilities at work and the home. In India, specifically in the urban settings like Chennai, where women police personnel tends to the encounter unique challenges in the balancing personal and the professional demands. There is a lack of an empirical data that examines if work-life balance tends to the affect the job performance of the women police officers in the metropolitan regions. This study aims to the address this gap by the assessment of the relationship between the WLB and the job performance among women in the Chennai police force. A mixed-methods approach was employed. Primary data were collected through the structured questionnaires (n=150) and the semi-structured interviews (n=20). The WLB was assessed using the Work-Life Balance Scale (WLB-S) and the job performance was measured using a modified version of the Police Job Performance Scale. Quantitative data were analyzed using SPSS, applying regression analysis, correlation, and the ANOVA. Sentiment analysis of qualitative data was performed using NVivo. The study helps to the find a moderate to the strong positive correlation (r = 0.68, p < 0.01) between the WLB and the job performance. Key WLB components such as flexible shifts, spousal support, and the access to the childcare services significantly influence job performance indicators like punctuality, task completion, and the stress management. Regression analysis shows that 52% of the variance in the job performance is explained by WLB-related factors.

title: FedAADC: Model Similarity-based Base Station Clustering for Federated Wireless Traffic Prediction
authors: Dongyang Li, Shuo Wang, Xinkun Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Wireless traffic prediction plays a central role in enabling efficient resource allocation and performance optimization in next-generation mobile networks by characterizing the spatio-temporal dynamics of future network demands. However, achieving high prediction accuracy while maintaining efficient distributed training remains a significant challenge in large-scale cellular networks, due to pronounced data heterogeneity and limited communication resources. To address this challenge, this paper proposes FedAADC, a model similarity–based clustered federated learning framework for wireless traffic prediction. First, this framework exploits intermediate-layer activation features extracted from local base-station models to characterize model similarity via activation-based kernel alignment. Based on the resulting similarity metrics, base stations are dynamically grouped, enabling delayed and adaptive clustering during the federated training process. Moreover, an attention mechanism is further incorporated to achieve effective intra- and inter-cluster model aggregation. Experimental results demonstrate that FedAADC significantly improves prediction accuracy, reducing the mean squared error (MSE) by up to 23.66% and the mean absolute error (MAE) by up to 11.36% compared with state-of-the-art baselines, while exhibiting high scalability for large-scale distributed base-station networks and meeting practical deployment requirements.

title: A Compact Population-Activity Detector for Event-Driven Gating in Neuromorphic Pipelines
authors: Gourab Datta, Sumaia Jahan Mishu, Sarah Safura Sharif, Yaser Mike Banad
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Event-driven systems benefit from suppressing unnecessary downstream computation when input activity is low. This paper presents a compact population-activity detector for event-driven gating, implemented as a purely combinational digital primitive (popcount + programmable threshold compare) in Cadence using a 90 nm process library. The circuit accepts five binary activity inputs and asserts an enable signal when the instantaneous population count exceeds a configurable threshold. We formulate gating as a trade-off between compute suppression and activation fidelity, and we provide an evaluation protocol based on duty-cycle, false activation/suppression rates, and activation-latency statistics under controlled activity/noise conditions. The proposed detector serves as a reusable low-overhead building block for gating expensive stages in neuromorphic and event-based processing pipelines.

title: Technological Analysis on Exploring Emotional Intelligence Through Cultural Lenses
authors: Emery Bokey, Aparna Vajpayee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The researches have shown vital importance of emotional intelligence in various hemisphere of life. The proposed research has an emphasis to compare the emotional intelligence levels of people from India and Africa as well as between athletes and non-athletes. It will be interesting to study the level of emotional intelligence in athlete and non-athlete among two cultural group to understand the impact of culture in shaping emotional intelligence. Study comprises 50-50 participant who are athlete and non-athletes from India and Africa for a comparative research approach. Individuals between the ages of 18 and 28 completed tests of emotional intelligence using established procedures. Two-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was used to analyses the data in order to determine how nationality and athletic status interacted with emotional intelligence scores. The results of the study have shown impact of culture in shaping the emotional intelligence. The cultural impact of Africa has more influence in shaping the emotional intelligence as compare to Indian athlete and non-athlete. Furthermore, across both nationalities, athletes scored higher on emotional intelligence tests than non-athletes. Nevertheless, there were no noteworthy interactions between athletic status and nationality.

title: A Big Data Architecture for Smart Bus System
authors: Mohd Azri Rosli, Nor Azuana Ramli, Nor Izzati Jaini
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: As urban transportation systems advance toward intelligent and data-driven operations, integrating big data technologies is vital for enhancing efficiency and decisionmaking. This paper proposes a comprehensive big data architecture for smart bus systems, focusing on the methodological aspects of data processing and analytics integration. The proposed framework covers the complete data pipeline, including data assessment, exploration, ingestion, storage using data lakes and data warehouses, preparation, computation, and analytics. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based model is incorporated within the analytics layer to support predictive insights for operational optimization. The study also evaluates cloud implementation in terms of cost and scalability, emphasizing the benefits of distributed and elastic computing for large-scale transportation data. A comparative analysis with existing architecture highlights the proposed framework’s modularity and adaptability. Although no empirical validation is presented, architecture provides a robust foundation for future implementation and evaluation in developing intelligent transportation systems.

title: Patchwork-Based Audio-Visual Watermarking Using ADPCM and Adaptive Embedding
authors: Chandani Sharma, Muhammad Khalid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: This research work proposes a novel approach to audio and visual watermarking leveraging adaptive techniques. These are inspired by current artificial intelligence frameworks with a comparative illustration of existing audio watermarking to a robust and transparent embedding strategy built upon Adaptive Differential Pulse Code Modulation (ADPCM). In the proposed method, watermark embedding is done using a dynamic audio patch, rather than fixed or low-frequency carrier that is generally used.
