The selected audio segment is divided into frames, and thereafter a pseudo-random key is used to determine the frame positions for watermark insertion. The watermark, is embedded by modifying the Least Significant Bit (LSB) of selected audio samples using post-ADPCM compression. This adaptive watermarking process ensures minimal perceptual distortion and high resilience to compression and tampering. Furthermore, the same methodology can be extended for image and video watermarking by treating image or video frames as analogous data blocks. The proposed system thus offers a unified framework for multi-modal watermarking applicable across audio, image, and video domains.

title: An Intelligent Ventilation Constant-Pressure Control Method Based on State Awareness and a Hybrid Optimization Algorithm
authors: Guang Yang, Zhongshun Su
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To address the nonlinear, time-varying, and disturbance-sensitive characteristics of intelligent ventilation constant-pressure systems under complex operating conditions, this paper proposes a hybrid optimization method, IPSO-BAS-SA, for fuzzy PID parameter tuning. First, the ventilation constant-pressure plant is equivalently linearized, and a unified second-order transfer-function model is established to provide a consistent mathematical basis for controller design and simulation comparison. Second, a fuzzy PID controller with the error and the rate of change of error as inputs is constructed, and a hybrid algorithm composed of an improved particle swarm optimization algorithm, chaotic-map initialization, beetle antennae search local refinement, and a simulated annealing acceptance mechanism is employed to optimize the key parameters. Finally, under a 50 Pa step input, a 100 Pa variable-condition step input, and pulse disturbance conditions, the proposed optimized fuzzy PID controller is compared with a conventional PID controller and a manually tuned fuzzy PID controller. In addition, ablation experiments are conducted to verify the effectiveness of each component in the proposed optimization framework. The results show that the proposed method outperforms the comparison methods in response speed, overshoot suppression, settling time, and disturbance recovery capability, demonstrating superior control performance and robustness.

title: Prakriti: Human Body-Type Identification to Recommend Lifestyle Using IoT and ML
authors: Mahesh Pawaskar, Suraj Vishwakarma, Shravan Thakur, Sameer Singh, Ritesh Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The skyrocketing prevalence of lifestyle-related disorders and chronic non-communicable diseases has seen preventive healthcare getting a major boost. Modern healthcare systems are effective, which is effective in the diagnosis and treatment of patients, but they usually do not have the mechanisms of continuous monitoring and personal wellness advice. An ancient Indian approach to medicine Ayurveda, which is more focused on prevention, is based on the study of individualized constitutions called Prakriti which is regulated by three types of Doshas: Vata, Pitta, and Kapha. This paper introduces a system of healthcare called Prakriti that incorporates the Internet of Things (IoT) technology with the use of the Machine Learning (ML) models to recognize the Ayurvedic type of the body of an individ and prescribe personal lifestyle changes. IoT sensors can be used to record real-time physiological data like heart rate, electro-cardiogram (ECG) body temperature, and oxygen sat- uration (SpO2) and process them using supervised learning algorithms such as Random Forest and Support Vector Machine (SVM). The system combines biometric patterns following the Ayurvedic principles to create individualized advice regarding diet, exercise, yoga, and daily practices. High prediction accuracy, low latency, and reliability have been proven via experimental assessment and it is true that the challenge to employ traditional medical knowlknowledge and the present computational intelligence is attainable. The suggested model facilitates wellness, early disease diagnosis, and preventive care.

title: IoT Data Analytics with Deep Neural Networks: Trends and Future Directions
authors: Jayamangala Sristi, Vinay Kumar Nassa, Isan Sahoo, M. K. Sharma, Maddikera Kalyan Chakravarthi, Mohit Tiwari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The study has shown how “deep neural networks” can enhance predictive maintenance according to the “Internet of Things (IoT)”. “The artificial neural networks (ANN) and the Long Short-term memory (LSTM) models are developed and tested analyzing data related to performance. These findings showed that, in spite of the LSTM being more efficient at identifying temporal relationships, the ANN is more precise and accurate overall, and appropriate for sequencing IoT data. These findings indicate that deep learning can streamline the work of the industry, minimize downtime, and significantly increase the ability to identify failures. Regardless of the favorable results, the existence of disproportional classes of failures hampered recall and model generalization. Therefore, deep neural networks become an effective tool for analytics of the IoT, providing a foundation to develop predictive maintenance systems that are more robust, reliable, and current.

title: Leveraging Generative AI to Advance Technology-Based Startup Incubation and Societal Progress
authors: Mohd Tabrej Alam
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The rapid development of Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen AI) based on foundation models (GPT-4, DALL E, and Claude) is capable of altering entrepreneurial ecosystems with evolving content creation, predictive analytics, and dynamic mentoring. Although the Gen AI application is rapidly expanding in all sectors, its practical absorption in the context of technology-orientated business incubators (TBIs) is an insufficiently developed area. In this study, this gap is covered by using empirical research to explore the impact of Gen AI on improving one in the quality of startup selection, improvement of innovation capability, and shortening of time-to-market in incubation settings. On the basis of the Technology–Organisation–Environment (TOE) framework and Entrepreneurial Ecosystem Theory, a quantitative research design is used by referencing data provided by the incubator managers and the founders of the emerging enterprises, which operate in various industries. With the partial least square structural equation modelling (PLS-SEM) and then robustness checks done using the two-stage least squares (2SLS) regression, the results support the substantial beneficial impacts of Gen AI adoption on startup performance outcomes. The most significant shift was AI-assisted mentoring, suggesting that large-scale skill development may be feasible. Findings provide both theoretical implications for the application of Gen AI capabilities in relation to stages of the incubation lifecycle and practical implications for incubators, entrepreneurs, and policymakers. This study establishes Gen AI as a business strength in terms of promoting inclusive, sustainable and innovation-based entrepreneurial ecosystems.

title: Blockchain for Secure Management of International Student Exchange Records: Opportunities, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: RajKumar, Sayam Mattoo, Vaishnavi Sharma, Krishika Thakur, Harshit Tomar, Archit Khanna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The blockchain technology is becoming a standout solution to the international student exchange records that must be managed safely. The Diploma forgery, cross-border verification, inefficiency and lack of interoperability and high administrative costs are some of the challenges associated with traditional centralized systems. In this review, the author evaluates the available literature on blockchain-based learning models, pointing to such technologies as Ethereum, Hyperledger Fabric, and models based on NFTs. The study is conducted in a systematic methodology, which starts with the analysis of the topic, literature review and the identification of past outputs, moving on to explore future opportunities and make a comparative analysis. The results indicate that blockchain increases credential management transparency, efficiency, and security, and tackles fraudulent behavior and increases trust in academic mobility. Nevertheless, there are still challenges like scalability, obstacles to adoption, and regulatory challenges. The future prospects are on hybrid blockchain frameworks, privacy verifying blockchain frameworks and the global standardisation of credential frameworks. It is summarized in the review that blockchain has a great potential to improve the management of academic records, and it is a potential avenue to a secure, decentralized, and internationally accepted model of international student exchanges.

title: Computational Engineering Based on the Intersection of Data Protection and Cybersecurity
authors: Vratika Singh, Bindu Jindal, Sujata Dahiya, Pranav Ranga, Anil Vats, Ankita Saini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: As our world becomes more digital and data-driven, the protection of personal and sensitive information has taken center stage. From healthcare records to financial transactions, organizations today are under increasing pressure to not only secure their systems but also comply with evolving data protection laws. This study explores the intersection of cybersecurity and data protection, showing how these two fields must work together to ensure both technical safety and legal accountability. Drawing on real-world data across industries like healthcare, finance, and education, the research analyzes how factors like compliance scores, cybersecurity investment, and breach response impact the scale and cost of cyber incidents. The results are clear: organizations with stronger compliance frameworks consistently face lower breach costs and recover more effectively. The study highlights the need for a unified approach—where legal awareness and technical defense are part of the same strategy—to build digital trust in an increasingly connected world.

title: Enhancing Healthcare Waste Management Through Deep Learning-Based Classification
authors: Gayathri G, Visaka P, Chitra P, Priya Thiagarajan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This project explores the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI), particularly Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL), to improve medical waste classification in healthcare settings. Classifying medical waste manually can pose significant health risks, result in inconsistent sorting, and heighten environmental damage due to human errors and inefficiencies. Automating this process ensures precise segregation, minimizes health hazards, enhances compliance with regulations, and fosters safer and more sustainable waste-management practices. In comparison, a test was conducted for three deep learning models: Vanilla Neural Network, EfficientNet-B0, and EfficientNet-B3, using a set of 2,769 images categorized into five types of medical waste. The aim is to classify two main types of waste: sharp waste, such as syringes and tweezers, and infectious waste, such as gloves, gauze, and masks. All images underwent preprocessing, including resizing and normalization, in addition to augmentation techniques. Among the models tested, EfficientNet-B3 performed well, with an accuracy of 98.44%. EfficientNet-B0 also performed well, with an accuracy of 97.92%. EfficientNet-B0 has high efficiency using minimal computational resources, and the Vanilla Neural Network reached 97%. Based on these results, a prototype system was developed for real-time classification of medical waste. This system improves the waste segregation efficiency, reduces contamination, and enhances the overall waste disposal process. Future improvements to this system include incorporating user feedback, expanding classification categories, and improving integration into healthcare settings. This project shows how AI-driven solutions can significantly contribute to safer and more efficient medical waste management, help to protect healthcare environments, and support workers in ensuring proper waste disposal.

title: Autonomous Agent-Based Intelligence for Continuous Learning and Adaptation in Cyberphysical Systems
authors: Vivek Sanjay Thorat, Sri Krishna Ravulapalli, Shambhu Shankar Rai, Nirmala Soren, Rajesh Kumar Tiwari, Abhishek M. Dhore
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) experience great operational complexity in the stochastic and non-stationary contexts when the control logic can be considered static. The current paper introduces an autonomous agent-based intelligence framework, which can be used in perpetually adaptive and decentralized organization of heterogeneous CPS systems. The given framework implements a multi-agent system (MAS) to bridge the gap between the cyber and physical layers with the help of built-in perception-action loops and planning modules. With the help of online reinforcement learning (RL) and predictive analytics, individual agents change control policies in real-time to alleviate disturbances in a system and changing operational constraints. A shared knowledge layer that is distributed is put in place to coordinate inter-agent coordination and policy refinement making the system-wide scalable and fault-tolerant. Experimental validation in a wide range of CPS situations proves that the suggested autonomous structure can be much more efficient and self-optimizing than centralized baseline frameworks, providing a stable channel of self-evolutionary industrial and robotic systems.

title: Study of Dynamic behaviour of the Linear Susceptibility on Pulse Propagation in Fiber Optics Communication
authors: Aayushi Mehta, A.A. Koser, Bhavana Kshirsagar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: This paper examines the impact of vibrant behaviour of linear susceptibility on the propagation of pulse in fiber optics communication. Our method to construct the adaptive response of the medium is mathematical, computational, and then we consider temporal variations in the linear susceptibility may influence the pulse modulation, group velocity dispersion (GVD) and pulse broadening. Some of the results are that even minor changes in time-dependence susceptibility affect the refractive index profile and broadening of the pulse.

title: Enhancing Employee Data Security with Blockchain Solutions
authors: Priyanka Rani, A. S. Kavitha, V. Vimala, Simranpreet Kaur, Shekhar Singh, Pankaj Kunekar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The secrecy of the information of the employees has become one of the most significant dilemmas of the contemporary organization, and the blockchain is one of the opportunities that are under consideration. The article cannot be called the abstract one as it is based on the recent research and practical examples to investigate the spectrum further through the contribution of blockchain to the appearance of data security. It has been demonstrated that accuracy and access control and transparency could be enhanced, with the help of a hybrid company, where certified blockchain is applied alongside the traditional off-chain databases. Even this system will be delivering the tradeoff between the raw nature of blockchain and the necessity to be regulated and an easy method of ensuring the security of HR data.

title: Location-Based Adaptive Grouping Algorithm for Federated Learning
authors: Jiachen Wang, Donghang Duan, Qi Zhou, Xu Zheng
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: In wireless federated learning networks, high communication overhead remains a critical bottleneck. While existing location-based clustering methods mitigate long-distance transmission, they typically employ fixed grouping strategies that overlook the heterogeneity of data distributions among local clients, leading to suboptimal training accuracy. To address this issue, we propose a Location-Based Adaptive Grouping Algorithm (ADBR) to jointly optimize communication energy and model accuracy. Specifically, we design a sliding window mechanism on clients to detect real-time performance fluctuations and trigger regrouping requests. On the server side, a batched re-grouping strategy is introduced to process requests in parallel, thereby reducing system coordination overhead. Theoretical analysis and experimental results demonstrate that ADBR significantly improves model accuracy and reduces communication costs while ensuring convergence and security.

title: Preserving What, Removing Where: Dual-Stream Beneficial Feature Pre-Debiasing for HOI Detection
authors: Fang Li, Lihua Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Human-object interaction detection is a crucial task in scene understanding, aiming to localize both people and objects and infer their interactive relationships in images. However, existing visual models often rely excessively on background information, leading to biased interaction predictions. To address this issue, this paper proposes a causal intervention framework, Dual-Stream Benefit Feature Pre-bias Removal (DSB-HOI), for the dual-stream HOI architecture, integrating a detector and a context mining module. This method requires no retraining and is plug-and-play, eliminating bias solely through feature adjustments during inference. In the factual scenario, the model makes predictions based on the visual features of people and objects, as well as semantic labels and background information. In the intervention scenario, we retain only the background information while setting the visual features and semantic labels of people and objects as unobservable. By comparing the two scenarios, we effectively isolate background interference, preserve the primary contribution of human and object visual features to the interaction, eliminate background bias, and simultaneously enhance the interpretability of the model’s decisions. Experimental results on the HICO-DET benchmark demonstrate the effectiveness of the DSB-HOI method, which substantially enhances human-object interaction detection performance and quantifies the dual-stream architecture’s dependence on background information.

title: Space Motion Modeling and Control System Optimization Design of Unmanned Underwater Vehicles Based on Comprehensive Simulation
authors: Jia-Lin Niu, Jia-Hao Fang, Meng Wen, Ya-Nan Niu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Based on the physical and motion characteristics of typical Unmanned Underwater Vehicles (UUVs), mathematical models for kinematics and dynamics were established. The spatial motion equations were then derived through mathematical methods, which effectively characterize the multidimensional motion parameters of the UUV. Subsequently, linearized models and their corresponding transfer functions for the pitch, yaw, and roll channels of the vehicle were obtained via mathematical approaches. PID control laws were designed for each channel, resulting in a stable PID control system for the UUV. Finally, simulations were conducted to verify the feasibility of the design method and the stability of the control system.

title: Blockchain Technology for Enhancing Transparency and Security in Financial Management Systems
authors: Bini Marin V, Santanu Roy, Sandeep Banerjee, Lokeshwari CK, Neeraj S Bakshi, Avinash Hanmant Ghadage
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Blockchain innovation is revolutionizing distributed networks by enhancing security and transparency. Blockchain has become an innovation that may be used in many fields, especially in financial services, over the past decade. It has been emphasized as an innovation that will make financial transactions easy, efficient, secure, and affordable. This work presents a blockchain system powered by cutting-edge artificial intelligence (AI) methods that will improve transparency and safety across the transaction system. The conceptual methodology used in this research depends on federated learning for Blockchain Inclusion, in which dispersed databases from various blockchain sites add to a worldwide AI system without exchanging unprocessed data specimens. Increasingly better safety standards for fraud and anomaly identification are developed with LSTM automatic encoders. This study suggests Smart Contractual Financial Control for AI algorithms in a distributed setting to achieve this goal. CNNs use the secure worldwide paradigm for examining streaming transactional statistics, generating prompt notifications, and taking appropriate action when fraud is identified. The system is considerably more dependable because of its accurate time. Based on the preliminary results, the suggested model is successful when regional frameworks are aggregated without data leaks, outliers are recognized effectively, algorithms are handled transparently, data confidentiality is maintained at an extremely advanced stage, and illicit transactions are easily identified.

title: Design and Development of 8 - Legged Robot Using Theo Jansen Mechanism
authors: K. Venkatesh, B Sudhakara Rao, Shaik Bazani, Durga Bhavani P, Jhansi Rani K, P.S.V. Ramana Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: In rough and rocky paths, wheels have been shown to be ineffective and legged robots using the Theo Jansen mechanism is a better solution to enhanced walking vehicles. This process allows the robots to overcome curbs, climb stairs and move through places where wheeled vehicles cannot reach. Theo Jansen mechanism is one of the most essential approaches since it does not need the microprocessor control or a sophisticated system of actuators. Rather, it uses pivot joints to connect the links to convert the rotational movement of a crank into a walking movement just like that of animals. The links proportions are also calculated to maximize the foot linearity during half a crank rotation to assure a smooth forward movement. In the other half-cycle, the foot is raised to an agreed height and brought back to the initial position and the process is repeated. Fitting two linkages to the crank one-half cycle in phase difference enables the vehicle frame to be kept parallel to the ground during motion. Hazardous material handling, mine field cleaning and the ability to secure the high-risk zones without risking the lives of personnel are big applications of this technology. Mechanical spider robots would be of great use to military, law enforcement, Explosive Ordnance Disposal (EOD) units and even to private security firms since they are good at stair climbing and going over obstacles which are difficult to the wheeled or tracked vehicles.

title: Apple Grading Based on Bagging Ensemble of Ordinal Evidential Neural Network
authors: Yiyang Wang, Wenxiao Guan, Shuhui Bi, Liyao Ma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Apple grading is essentially an ordinal classification problem, in which adjacent grades exhibit natural continuity and boundary uncertainty. To improve the efficiency and performance of automatic apple grading, this study adopts a Bagging ensemble method based on ordinal evidential neural network. Within an ordinal framework, this paper designs an ordinal evidential neural network based on Dempster–Shafer evidence theory to output mass functions, thereby effectively characterizing ordinal relationships and grading uncertainty. Meanwhile, a Bagging ensemble strategy is adopted to further improve the grading performance of the model. Experimental results on the Red Fuji apple dataset show that the proposed Bagging–ORENN model achieves an average accuracy of 89.19%.

title: Enhanced Smart Retail Decision Through Sentiment Analysis Using Machine Learning
authors: Yoganand Sharma, Ankita Dadhich, Minal Sharma, D. S. Rao, Reema Ajmera, Sangeeta Soni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Retail business is quick to digitalize with the adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), and big data technologies, which increase the amount of customer-generated data the number of online reviews, ratings, posts on social media, feedback replies exponentially. This huge unstructured textual information contains opinions, feelings, degree of satisfaction, and buying preferences of the customers and it is a valuable asset to be used in intelligent retail decision-making, but the problem is how to extract important information out of such data is also a complicated issue. Sentiment analysis also refers to opinion mining has been a lucrative remedy to the challenge as it works towards recognizing sentiment polarity, affects, and sentiments in customer-feedback text automatically. The paper is a review on the topic of sentiment analysis methods, and how they can help improve the process of smart retail decisions and includes a discussion of how the traditional machine learning models (Naive Bayes, Support Vector Machines, and Logistic Regression) have evolved over the years to more advanced deep learning methods (Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and transformer-based architectures like BERT and RoBERTa). It talks about data sources, preprocessing approaches, feature extraction approaches, evaluation approaches applied in retail sentiment analytics and mentions key uses of such analytics, including demand forecasting, product development, dynamic pricing, recommendation systems, brand monitoring, and customer retention. Other major issues such as the detection of sarcasm, multi-language data processing, detection of fake reviews, data imbalance, privacy issues, and the issue of realtime deployment are also discussed. Lastly, the paper identifies future research topics, which include multimodal sentiment analysis, explainable AI, emotion-aware analytics, and realtime intelligent retail systems, concluding that sentiment analysis based on ML is a core element in proactive, customercentric, and data-driven smart retail systems.

title: Quantum Computing-Powered Error Correction System for Secure Satellite Communication
authors: Sudhakiran Ponnuru, Siva Kumar. M, Sai Santhosh Ramadugu, M.S. Sivagama Sundari, Kalpana Umapathy, K. Rakesh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: In this research a new framework is proposed for the quantum computing based error correction of the secure satellite communication systems. Leveraging on quantum error correction codes (QECC) that are drawn with advanced machine learning architectures, we develop a system that is a hybrid (classical and quantum) sufficient and using IBM Qiskit and TensorFlow Quantum Libraries (TfQ). The proposed system is transformer based proudly practicing neural networks with prowded consideration activity mechanisms for decimating the syndrome size and formation of error bars for real-time pattern recognition for noisy quantum channels. We integrate in a distributed way the federated learning for satellite networks and provide reinforcement learning agents which take part in the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm for adaptive optimal error correction strategy choice depending on channel conditions. Research validation with PyTorch Lightning and Weights & Biases provide some experimental validations showing 34 % improvement on bit error rate (BER) and 28 % less decoding latency as compared to the conventional LDPC codes. The system has quantum-resilient security which is founded upon BB84 protocol implementation by neural network enhanced key distributing.

title: Research on Human-Following Control of a Smart Walker Based on IMU and Pressure Sensor Fusion
authors: Zirui Zhang, Shimin Wei, Ying Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: To address the high cost and environmental sensitivity of existing LiDAR-based walkers, this paper proposes a low-cost, lightweight <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<2 \text{kg})$</tex> human-following control scheme based on the fusion of an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) and Force Sensing Resistors (FSR). The system utilizes a distributed control architecture with Field Oriented Control (FOC) in-wheel motors. An adaptive gain Attitude and Heading Reference System (AHRS) algorithm is proposed to address IMU drift during dynamic walking, while a multi-source fusion strategy combines gait characteristics with handrail interaction forces to achieve compliant motion control. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves a gait cycle identification accuracy of 98% and a response latency of 24ms, with hardware costs only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 5 {\%} - 2 0 {\%}$</tex> of traditional solutions, demonstrating significant engineering value.

title: WayWaker: A Location-Based Alarm System for Travelers Using Transformer-Based Context-Aware Travel Recommendation
authors: Vallimmai C K, Sujitha P, Sherlin Jenefa I M, M B Anushlin Leena, M Robinson Joel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Analysts and specialists from diverse sectors, including customisation, data recovery, ubiquitous and adaptable computing, data mining, and management, have duly recognised the importance of contextualized data. While a detailed study was recently carried out in the domain of recommendation systems, most of the current approaches emphasize the recommendation of the best-suited products to consumers. Most of the additional contextual data such as time, space, climate, or the attractiveness of other places is usually ignored. To maximise the use of contextual information and avoid the mistakes of conventional sequential recommendation systems, we suggest a Transformerbased Context-Aware Travel Recommendation (TCATR) paradigm. Our model effectively captures the contextual fluctuations and long-range relationships in user trip behaviour by utilising a selfattention mechanism instead of recurrent structures. A ranking prediction module, a contextual feature embedding unit, and a sequential behaviour encoder are the three main parts of the model.

title: Secure Link Scheduling for UAV Swarms: From Perspective of Covert Communication
authors: Li Yang, Xufeng Zhan, Jia Yu, Jiguo Yu, Dongxiao Yu
abstract: The link scheduling is to determine which links should be scheduled at what times, each of which is a (sender, receiver) communication pair used to realize wireless transmission of a message. It is an appealing solution to fulfill the resource-constrained and latency requirements of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarms. However, most existing link scheduling algorithms primarily focus on the throughput or latency in wireless communication, neglecting the protection against the eavesdropping attack due to the openness of UAV swarms, thereby leading to unsafe message dissemination. To address this issue, we consider integrating the covert communication into the link scheduling by concealing the very existence of message transmission. We first present a successive interference cancellation-based link diversity partition (SIC-LDP) algorithm in the context of the physical interference model, to maximize the number of concurrently transmitting links in a single time round, also called the maximum link scheduling (MLS) issue. Then, using a friendly jammer, we further propose a covertness-aware SIC-LDP algorithm (named SIC-CLDP) to resolve the MLS, as well as to defend against the eavesdropping attack. Both SIC-LDP and SIC-CLDP algorithms are proven to have constant approximation ratios. Next, implementing SIC-LDP and SIC-CLDP repeatedly can minimize the number of time rounds scheduling n participating links, termed the shortest link scheduling (SLS) issue, with an approximation ratio of O(ln n). Lastly, theoretical proofs and simulation results demonstrate the correctness, covertness, and efficiency of our proposed algorithms under the eavesdropping attack.

title: Wireless Electromagnetic Tracking Using Ultra-Low Frequency Fields for Hardware-Constrained Medical Devices_supp2-3694425.mp4
authors: Chengzhi Hu
abstract: Quasi-static magnetic localization offers advantages in eliminating geomagnetic interference and compatibility with magnetic actuation. However, existing systems that rely on high-frequency magnetic fields (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\geq$</tex-math></inline-formula>1000 Hz) often require high sampling rates, which increase the communication bandwidth demands and power consumption in wireless applications. This article presents a wireless electromagnetic tracking system (WEMTS) that operates at ultra-low frequencies (210 Hz), specifically designed for hardware-constrained medical devices like capsule robots. The WEMTS hardware consists of two main components: three external coil groups and a wireless sensor module (WSM) that integrates a three-axis magnetic sensor and an RF transmitter. A multilateration-based sequential pose estimation method is implemented for the 6-DoF localization of the WSM, improving convergence stability and computational efficiency. Experimental results show that WEMTS achieves static localization accuracy of 3.09 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.99 mm in position and 2.21 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.62<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> in orientation, and dynamic tracking errors of 3.23 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 1.05 mm and 2.67 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.89<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> at 5 mm/s within a 400 mm × 200 mm × 300 mm workspace. To validate practical applicability, the WSM was integrated into a vibrating capsule and successfully tracked in real time inside an ex vivo porcine colon. The proposed WEMTS allows precise and robust tracking, effectively addressing the challenges posed by hardware-constrained medical devices.

title: CaUCD: Causal Unsupervised Change Detection
authors: Dong Ren, Yang Zhang, Wenbin Wang, Gui-Song Xia, Xiang Bai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Current unsupervised change detection (UCD) methods face two key limitations: inaccurate pseudo-label generation and failure to account for land cover influence, resulting in unreliable change area predictions. Specifically, existing methods neglect the influence of context during the generation of pseudo-labels and the probability of different land cover changes when training change detection networks. To address these issues, we designed a causal unsupervised change detection framework (CaUCD), consisting of two networks: casual unsupervised semantic segmentation network (CUSeg) for generating pseudo-labels and causal change detection network (C2D) for detecting change area. CUSeg removes contextual interference, thereby obtaining accurate pseudo-labels for training C2D. C2D eliminates different land cover influence, thus accurately detecting change areas. On four datasets (SECOND, SYSU, CNAM-CD and DSIFN), CaUCD surpasses state-of-the-art UCD methods and demonstrates considerable segmentation results. Our code will be made publicly available at: https://www.github.com/zhangshao249/CaUCD.

title: Cross-Modality Anatomy Knowledge Distillation for Retinal OCT Layer Segmentation with Limited Labels
authors: Xiang Li, Huihui Fang, Yuning Wang, Cen Chen, Qi Liu, Jinke Wang, Yi Shi, Lixin Duan, Weihua Yang, Yue Liu, Yanwu Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Automatic segmentation of retinal Optical Coher ence Tomography (OCT) images plays a vital role in the early diagnosis and treatment of ocular diseases. However, the scarcity of labeled OCT data poses a significant challenge for existing supervised learning methods. In contrast, Color Fundus Photography (CFP) provides clearer visualization of key anatomical regions, such as the optic disc and cup, making it easier to acquire high-quality annotations. To address the issue of insufficient OCT annotations, we propose a Cross-Modality Anatomy Knowledge Distillation (CMAKD) framework. This method leverages a large amount of annotated CFP data to learn cross-modality structural priors related to retinal layer organization and vessel-associated landmarks, which are then used to regularize the OCT model for retinal layer segmentation. In other words, CFP serves as a source of weak structural priors, providing spatial and contextual guidance for OCT feature learning rather than depth-resolved layer supervision or a direct supervisory signal. To achieve this goal, we design a Domain-invariant Contrastive Learning (DoCL) module to reduce the modality gap between CFP and OCT by aligning their feature distributions. Furthermore, we introduce a Layer-aware Self-ensembling Mean-Teacher (LA-MT) framework to fully exploit unlabeled OCT data and enhance the model’s ability to recognize layered boundary structures. Experimental results on three public datasets — Duke DME, MS, and GOALS — demonstrate that CMAKD significantly improves segmentation performance while reducing the reliance on OCT annotations. Under semi-supervised domain adaptation settings, CMAKD achieves relative performance gains of 17.8% and 8.2% on the Duke DME and MS datasets, respectively, and approaches the performance of fully supervised models on the GOALS dataset. Our code is available at: https://github.com/lixiangcog/CMAKD.

title: Exploring Physics Education: Andromeda-Adventure a Serious Immersive Game
authors: Sergio Ruiz-Loza, Linda M. Medina Herrera, Yoel Ledo Mezquita, A. K. Tomatani-Sánchez, Horacio A. Figueredo Rodríguez
abstract: This study evaluates the effectiveness of Andromeda, an augmented reality serious game designed to support learning of physics concepts. By integrating AR with game-based learning, Andromeda offers an immersive and interactive environment to explore energy and momentum conservation principles. Using pre- and post-tests with experimental and control groups (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N$</tex> = 118), results show that the experimental group achieved learning gains 18 points higher (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-100$</tex> scale) than the control group, a statistically significant difference (t-test, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}=\mathbf{0. 0 4}$</tex>). Student perception surveys also reflect positive attitudes toward the use of mobile AR learning tools. Overall, findings indicate that Andromeda significantly enhances conceptual understanding and problem-solving skills in undergraduate physics courses.

title: New Approaches to Analyzing Data from Smoke Topography Devices: A Pilot Analysis
authors: Vesna Zeljković, Junjia Zhu, Joshua E. Muscat
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: The United States paid more than $600 billion in 2018 because of cigarette smoking according to CDC. It has become increasingly recognized that the harm associated with tobacco products is not just a function of the frequency of use but how they are consumed, which reflect the physical and chemical characteristics of the tobacco product. This can be objectively measured through the use of smoking or vaping topography devices, which mechanically record inhalation information using a pressure transducer. The default topography measure typically used for research is summary measure such as puff volume. However, other individual measurements of smoke or vape flow through the device are potentially valuable for understanding exposure information, such as flow velocity which reflects the depth of inhalation into the lungs. In the current study, we illustrate the multidimensional aspect of these topography flow parameters, using data taken from a population-based study, that aim to better understanding the mechanics of factors that affect the dose of tobacco inhalation. The results show that component measures such as maximum inhalation flow have variability both within and between users. These data could serve as predictors of tobacco harm.

title: Remote Robot Operation Using Visual-Haptic Fusion : Influence of Object Size
authors: Yutaka Ishibashi, Pingguo Huang, Hitoshi Ohnishi, Konstantinos E. Psannis
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Information and Network Technologies (ICINT)
abstract: Remote robot operation systems with visual-haptic fusion enable cooperative manipulation by integrating visual information with force feedback. This paper investigates the influence of object size on cooperative manipulation using a simulation-based remote robot operation system with real haptic devices. The proposed system consists of two network-connected subsystems, each equipped with a haptic device, allowing cooperative manipulation of a virtual object using two robot hands. In the investigation, a single user operated two haptic devices simultaneously, while the object weight was kept constant and only its size was varied. The results show that object size significantly affects force characteristics and task performance, highlighting the importance of visual factors in visual-haptic remote robot operation.

title: Análise Automatizada de Registros de Áudio de Anamnese com Inteligência Artificial Aplicada a Reabilitação
authors: Marlete Maria da Silva, Gonçalo Carreiro de Farias, Emerson Fachin Martins, Claudia Ochoa-Diaz, Leticia Arisa Kobayashi Higa, Guillermo Alvarez Bestard
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence algorithms have broad applications in the healthcare field, including medical diagnostics, data analysis, disease detection, medication control, virtual assistants and electronic health records. In the field of physiotherapy, AI algorithms potential to optimize professionals' work, particularly in developing training plans for patients, significantly reducing the time required for this task. In the context of rehabitation, the anamnesis is a fundamental step, where initial information about the patient's medical history is collected, resembling a humanized interview that guides the beginning of therapy, defining the types of exercises and specific limitations. In Brazil, it is considered a mandatory protocol. This work proposes an AI-based tool to optimize the work of physiotherapists, integrating the Whisper (openai/whisper-large-v2) and Qwen 2.5-1.5B Instruct models. Furthermore, it aims to improve the personalization of training plans to enhance rehabilitation and quality of life for patients, making the anamnesis process safer, more effective, and personalized. The interview is recorded via audio, then the program transcribes it into text. This study utilized an interview between the professional and the patient, as well as a one-sided recording by the physiotherapist. Afterwards, the transcription is analyzed, and a anamnesis is created, incorporating alerts about critical psychosocial risk information.

title: Flight Toward Learning: Integrating the DRL Simulator as a Motivational and Educational Strategy in STEM Early Engineering Students
authors: Juan-Gabino Diaz-Martinez, Irandi Gutiérrez-Carmona, Hirohisa Kojima, Sajjad Keshtkar
abstract: This paper presents an educational innovation implemented at Tecnológico de Monterrey aimed at enhancing STEM motivation, engagement, and foundational engineering skills among high school and early engineering students. The intervention employed a progressive training sequence using the Drone Racing League (DRL) Simulator, transitioning from keyboard input to gamepad controllers and ultimately to professional-grade radio transmitters, culminating in real-flight practice with FPV drones. Two validated motivational instruments—the MUSIC® Model of Motivation Inventory and the MDI-EE (Motivational Diagnosis Instrument for Engineering Education)—were administered before and after the intervention. A pre-experimental one-group pre/post design was used due to institutional constraints that prevented the formation of a control group. Results showed significant increases across all motivational dimensions, including autonomy, perceived usefulness, interest, self-efficacy, and study predisposition. The revised version of this work also incorporates a structured review of related literature, clarification of the research gap, expanded sample and methodological justification, and theoretical grounding based on Self-Determination Theory and Flow Theory, along with explicit study limitations and directions for future research. Overall, the findings suggest that progressive simulator-based drone training is an effective, replicable, and scalable strategy for fostering STEM engagement and strengthening the connection between theoretical concepts and practical engineering experiences.

title: A Neural Network-Based Framework for Identifying Hazardous Road Segments Using Geometrical Road Attributes
authors: Igor Gavrilov, Pavel Hrubes, Emil Pelikan
abstract: Accurate identification of hazardous road segments is essential for improving road safety and prioritising preventive interventions. This study proposes a neural network-based framework for predicting hazardous road segments by leveraging detailed geometrical properties of roads, including lane number, lane width, and curvature. The proposed framework was applied to the D1 highway in the Czech Republic, using geospatial data and reported road accidents from 2024. The road geometry was divided into 10 m sectors, from which sequences of 300 m were generated to form input for a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network. The model was trained using a weighted binary cross-entropy loss function to address class imbalance, where hazardous segments represented only 29.2 % of samples. The trained model demonstrated strong predictive performance, achieving a weighted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score of 0.895 and a macro F1 score of 0.906, which effectively distinguished between hazardous and non-hazardous highway segments. The results demonstrate that integrating detailed road attributes within an LSTM-based framework can effectively capture spatial dependencies and non-linear relationships associated with accident occurrence. The framework provides a scalable approach for identifying high-risk segments across various road types, offering valuable support for data-driven decision-making in transportation safety management.

title: From Classroom to $1.4M Savings: Project-Based Operations Research Learning through Industry-Academia Collaboration in Corrugated Packaging
authors: Carlos Beltran-Perez, Alan Miralrio, Juan Carlos Cisneros Ortega, Angélica Hernández-Jaramillo, Magloria Alviso-Lara, Miguel Lorenzo, Jocelyn Herrera
abstract: This study presents a successful industry-academia collaboration between Tecnológico de Monterrey and a corrugated packaging manufacturer conducted during Fall 2022. The project addressed inefficiencies in manual paper selection, where historical data revealed production costs exceeded targets by 0.85% and quality compliance reached only 40.1%. A student team applied project-based learning principles with operations research methods, developing a Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model in LINGO that optimized paper combinations considering type, flute profile, grammage, and inventory availability. Real-world validation demonstrated significant improvements: combined monthly savings of MXN <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 6 4 M} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{1 6. 3 {\%}}$</tex> reduction) across validated families while improving quality compliance to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>90 {\%}$</tex> within specification. Beyond industrial impact, this project exemplified experiential learning, developing technical competencies (mathematical modeling, optimization software mastery) and professional skills (problem diagnosis, stakeholder communication, evidence-based decision-making). The LINGO license achieved ROI within 1-2 weeks, with annual savings potential exceeding MXN 31.7M. This work validates how industry-academia partnerships in operations research education simultaneously generate practical value and comprehensive competency development.

title: Comparison of Microbubble Tracking Algorithms for Ultrasound Localization Microscopy
authors: Uriel Ramilo, Gonzalo Garay, Juan Barolin, Victoria Sorriba, Mónica Bube, Juan Pablo Damián, Carlos Negreira, Alejandra Kun, Javier Brum
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Ultrasound Localization Microscopy (ULM) is a super-resolution imaging technique that allows the visualization of microvascular structures by detecting and tracking individual microbubbles. The performance of ULM strongly depends on the localization and tracking methods employed, affecting metrics such as image saturation, track count, and the geometric properties of reconstructed trajectories. In this work, in vivo transcranial ULM experiments were performed on mouse brains to experimentally compare two widely adopted microbubble tracking algorithms: simple-tracker and u-track. The comparison is based on ULM image reconstruction, saturation curves, track count, and trajectory tortuosity. While simple-tracker yields a higher number of reconstructed tracks and slightly higher image saturation, it also produces more tortuous trajectories. In contrast, u-track generates fewer but smoother trajectories, suggesting improved filtering of highly tortuous tracks that may arise from spurious associations. These results highlight a trade-off between the number of reconstructed trajectories and the generation of more tortuous paths, potentially arising from spurious associations in ULM.

title: Wearable Metabolic Sensing for Thermal Comfort Control in Smart and Energy-Efficient Buildings
authors: Pei Zhang, Huihe Qiu
abstract: At the building and city scale, conventional HVAC systems typically follow fixed temperature setpoints, leading to comfort complaints and energy waste as occupants' thermal states vary over time. Human-centric HVAC control offers a promising solution to improve indoor well-being while reducing building energy use, but it requires accurate, real-time estimation of individuals' metabolic rates for thermal comfort prediction. This study presents a wearable-friendly approach for estimating metabolic rate and, based on this method, develops a wristworn measurement system tailored for next-generation smart buildings. The proposed system replaces bulky calorimetry with a compact, physics-based sensing framework that integrates heart rate, whole-body unit heat loss, skin resistance, and body muscle percentage. Whole-body heat-loss estimation is improved by incorporating a segment-based convective heat loss coefficient, enabling more consistent mapping from wrist measurements across varying environmental conditions. Overall, this work provides scalable, occupant-specific inputs to comfort models and supports the development of closed-loop, human-centric HVAC strategies that simultaneously enhance comfort and reduce energy consumption.

title: A Markerless Hand Movement Assessment Tool for Upper Limb Rehabilitation post-stroke
authors: Reyez-Alvarez Amaury S, García-Villalobos Andrea, Blancas-Silva Sandra E, Vega-Martínez Gabriel, Toledo-Peral Cinthya L, Romano-Riquer Sonia P, Ramírez-Nava Ana G, Quinzaños-Fresnedo Jimena, Mercado-Gutierrez Jorge A
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Recently, markerless motion capture technologies, based on computer vision and artificial intelligence models, have been proposed as alternatives to wearable sensing technologies for human movement assessment, with advantages on non-invasiveness and accessibility. In this work, the development of a marker-less motion capture tool for hand movement assessment is presented, and a preliminary evaluation of its application during upper limb rehabilitation sessions assisted by Functional Electrical Stimulation for a post-stroke patient with left hemiparesis. The tool is based on the open-source library MediaPipe, which allows human body movement assessment with conventional RGB video cameras. Video and finger goniometry extracted from three rehabilitation sessions, corresponding to four different upper limb tasks assisted by Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES), were recorded and analyzed to evaluate the frame processing time (FPT) and the maximum joint angles (finger extension) achieved during different upper limb target movements (passive hand opening and active-passive ball grasp-and-release). FPT values were similar (0.1001 ± 0.0154 s vs 0.1000 ± 0.0119 s) in rehabilitation sessions recorded with two different computers (laptop vs workstation). In passive hand opening movements, angles were increased between sessions for most finger joints (up to 28.82°). In contrast, for passive-active ball grasp-and-release movements, the angle variability (standard deviation) was increased in time in multiple joints (up to ± 12.22°). These results support the feasibility of the proposed app as a robust, non-invasive markerless tool, for movement assessment during FES-assisted upper limb rehabilitation sessions of post-stroke patients, with advantages over systems with similar aims based on sensorized strategies and conventional PT/OT sessions.

title: Combining HLS and Computation Coding for Balanced Matrix-Vector Engines on FPGA
authors: Sayanti Pal, Alexander Lehnert, Marc Reichenbach
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: Limited number of DSP48 blocks versus abundant LUTs constrains the throughput of FPGA-based constant matrix–vector multiplications (MVM). We exploit Linear Computation-Coding (LCC) to build multiplier-less, deeply pipelined adder graphs on LUTs and combine it with a DSP-mapped HLS datapath via a pragmatic column-split partition. This hybrid mapping achieves balanced DSP and LUT utilization without sacrificing throughput, resulting in a scalable, high-performance MVM on FPGAs.

title: FedSiamese: Privacy-Preserving Face Verification using Federated Learning
authors: Arshia Akbari Aleagha, Athena Abdi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Face verification, as a key biometric technology, aims to confirm an individual’s identity from digital images. However, the widespread use of this technology has raised serious concerns regarding the privacy of sensitive user data. In response to this significant challenge, Federated Learning emerges as a novel approach that enables the training of intelligent models without the need to collect or share raw data. In this research, a face verification system based on Federated Learning is designed. This system leverages a Siamese deep network architecture to assess the similarity between two face images and determine if they belong to the same person. During the training process, the data remains completely confidential on the user’s local device, and only the learned model parameters are exchanged with a central server. The results indicate that the proposed method, while guaranteeing privacy, achieves a desirable level of accuracy and efficiency for implementation in security systems and access control applications.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Resource Aggregation and Low-Carbon Dispatch Paradigms for Carbon-Aware Power Systems
authors: Yu Zong, Guang Hu, Lile Jiang, Xiaoyu Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Driven by the dual-carbon strategic mandates, the transition of power dispatch paradigms—from traditional economic-centric balance to carbon-power synergistic optimization—has become a pivotal research frontier. This paper provides a comprehensive taxonomy of the current state-of-the-art in distributed energy resource (DER) aggregation and low-carbon dispatch within the context of deep electricity-carbon coupling. We categorize the theoretical landscape into three pillars: high-fidelity carbon flow tracing, carbon-feature-aware aggregation, and resilient low-carbon dispatch strategies. Key technological breakthroughs, including distributionally robust optimization (DRO) and multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), are critically analyzed regarding their efficacy in addressing DER stochasticity and carbon-flow non-linearity. Finally, we delineate future trajectories, such as physics-informed edge intelligence and blockchain-facilitated carbon certification, providing a visionary roadmap for constructing next-generation carbon-aware power systems.

title: A Load Forecasting Method Based on Trend Feedforward Correction and Deep Residual Learning
authors: Zerong Wang, Hao Dong, Hao Huang, Yitao Dong, Huaiting Hua
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasing proportion of new energy integration and the diversification of user consumption patterns in power systems, the volatility and uncertainty of electrical loads have increased significantly, affecting the stability of power grid operation. Accurate and reliable load forecasting is the key to addressing this issue. To this end, this paper proposes a load forecasting method based on Trend Feedforward Correction and Deep Residual Learning. The method first uses CEEMDAN to decompose the historical load sequence into multiple scales, extracting long-term trend components and short-term residual components to achieve a hierarchical representation of load data. Subsequently, LSTM and N-BEATS prediction models are constructed for the trend component and the residual component, respectively. The LSTM is used to predict trend information and generate a trend prior value. This prior value acts as a feedforward input to the N-BEATS model, providing constraints and guidance on the trend direction during its training process. Aided by the trend feedforward, the N-BEATS model can more accurately characterize the short-term fluctuation features of the load sequence, thereby obtaining high-precision residual prediction results. Finally, the trend prediction results and residual prediction results are superimposed to obtain the historical load prediction values. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method performs better than other models in load forecasting tasks.

title: Modeling and Transient Synchronization Stability Analysis of Fractional-Order Converters
authors: Jianping Wu, Xuyang Pan, Wei Luo, Shu Zhang, Yanpeng Xiao, Jijun Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To address the transient synchronization stability issue in fractional-order converters, this paper proposes an equal-area criterion based on fractional calculus. The virtual power angle characteristics of the fractional-order converter are qualitatively analyzed, along with the corresponding acceleration and deceleration areas. Theoretical analysis and simulation results demonstrate that, compared to a traditional converter, the fractional-order converter system exhibits a reduced acceleration area and an increased deceleration area. This verifies that the fractional-order converter possesses an enhanced stability margin, which can improve the system's transient synchronization stability.

title: A Planning Method for Site Selection and Capacity Determination of HVDC Cascaded Power Transmission from Long-distance High-Proportion Clean Energy Bases
authors: Xueying Guo, Yuwei Zou, Cheng Lei, Zhengchao Liu, Xudong Pan, Qin Zeng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper proposes a planning method for site selection and capacity determination of HVDC cascaded power transmission from long-distance high-proportion clean energy bases. First, alternative cascaded ends are determined based on the provinces along the HVDC corridor. Subsequently, monthly power balance analysis of cascaded ends and receiving ends is conducted to calculate the alternative installed capacity of supporting power sources, which is facilitated by capacity complementarity between the two ends. Capacities of converter stations at both cascaded and receiving ends are specified based on the correlation between the alternative installed capacity and HVDC transmission capacity. Further, carbon emissions and investment costs of each cascaded scheme are evaluated, thereby forming the alternative installed capacity matrix, carbon emission matrix, and investment matrix—all of which undergo normalization processing. Finally, an optimization objective function for cascaded schemes is constructed, and its maximum value is solved to ultimately derive the optimal HVDC cascaded power transmission scheme. This paper conducts a case study, using the ±800kV/8GW HVDC project for verification. The method of the paper can provide a reference for the planning and construction of HVDC cascaded power transmission and the selection of cascaded ends in power grid. It improves the economy of cross-regional HVDC power transmission and promotes the full utilization of cross-regional corridor resources.

title: Analysis and Design of Active Damping Strategy for LC-DAB System Based on Filter Inductor Current Single-Loop Control
authors: Xiao Huo, Xinchun Lin, Tianyu Fang, Chenyang Xie, Zijie Kang, Jing Zhou, Xiaoxiao Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Dual-Active-Bridge (DAB) converters have been extensively applied for power transmission owing to their advantages, such as bidirectional power flow and galvanic isolation. However, its input current contains significant high-frequency ripples, which degrade power quality and pose challenges to the stable operation of power systems. Although cascading an LC filter at the DAB input stage forming a LC-DAB converter system can effectively mitigate these high-frequency ripples, the interaction between the LC filter and the DAB converter often leads to resonance instability in the input current. Furthermore, when the DAB converter is deployed in energy storage unit (ESU) discharge scenarios, precise control of the discharge current is essential. To address these issues, this paper proposes an active damping strategy utilizing filter inductor current feedback within a single-loop inductor current control framework. A mathematical model incorporating control delay is established, revealing the specific impact of latency on the performance of the active damping scheme. Finally, experimental results are provided to verify the validity and effectiveness of the proposed theoretical approach.

title: FreMotion: A Frequency-Motion Synergy Framework for Imitation Learning in Robust Robotic Fine Manipulation
authors: Jie Mei, Xinkai Wu, Yue Zhang, Tao Song, Zhongxia Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Imitation learning has emerged as a promising paradigm for automating robotic fine manipulation, yet maintaining temporal coherence and execution precision in long-horizon tasks remains a formidable challenge. This requires addressing exponential error accumulation in long-horizon tasks, as well as the inherent trade-off between millimeter-level positioning precision and motion smoothness, which is critical for the stability and accuracy of robotic automation systems. To this end, we propose FreMotion, a task-driven asymmetric encoder-decoder framework designed to enhance the robustness and efficiency of robotic manipulation. Our approach introduces the Temporal Dynamic Encoder (TDE-Net), which employs linear attention with dynamic gating to capture long-range dependencies and mitigate error accumulation in continuous control. Simultaneously, the Frequency-Aware Decoder (FAD-Net) leverages a dual-path attention mechanism to explicitly disentangle and fuse high- and low-frequency information for precise visuo-motor coordination. Extensive experiments on bimanual robotic tasks demonstrate that FreMotion, with orders of magnitude fewer parameters (approx. 100M vs. Billions), not only outperforms state-of-the-art Vision-Language-Action (VLA) methods in success rate but also achieves faster inference time, thus ensuring high-frequency continuous control required for industrial automation.

title: A Frequency-Domain Vision Accelerator for High-Resolution Object Detection on RAW Data
authors: Haoyan Li, Kangjie Long, Wei Zhou, Xiangyu Zhang, Xin Lou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: While high-resolution inputs improve recognition performance, they impose significant hardware overhead due to the computational intensity and memory bandwidth demands of conventional image signal processing (ISP). This inefficiency primarily stems from the structural mismatch between pixel-level ISP operations and the semantic information required by back-end neural networks. To address this, we propose an end-to-end RAW-based frequency-domain vision pipeline designed for efficient high-resolution detection. Under normal imaging conditions, the proposed pipeline bypasses the full human-perception-oriented ISP and directly processes RAW sensor data through lightweight channel mapping and a hardware-friendly approximate discrete cosine transform (DCT). To minimize front-end data movement, we reformulate the color-space transformation within the frequency domain and retain only a compact set of task-relevant low-frequency coefficients. Furthermore, we employ a multiplier-less, shift-and-add-based arithmetic architecture to simplify control logic and enhance energy efficiency. The pipeline is fully compatible with standard convolutional neural networks, requiring no architectural changes to the back-end detector. We validated the design using a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)-based prototype on the Xilinx ZCU102 platform integrated with a YOLOv3-Tiny detector. The system achieves real-time processing of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6144\times 4096$</tex-math> </inline-formula> RAW inputs at 33.18 frames per second (FPS). Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed frequency-domain approach maintains detection accuracy comparable to RGB-based pipelines while significantly reducing hardware resource utilization and power consumption.

title: Advanced Full Spectrum Application for Endpoint Optimization in High Aspect Ratio Plasma Etching
authors: Yu-Cheng Chou, Canta Tsai, Rem Chang, Pascal Wang, Yu-Hsien Chou, Benjamin Huang
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: In high-aspect-ratio (HAR) plasma etching, insufficient sensitivity in endpoint detection (EPD) often leads to substantial variation in endpoint timing. Such fluctuations directly influence the critical dimension (CD) across wafers, ultimately compromising device performance and production yield stability. This study presents a machine-learning-assisted methodology that integrates full-spectrum optical emission analysis with Lam Research’s Equipment Intelligence® Data Analyzer (EI-DA). The approach identifies highly responsive spectral features for endpoint evaluation and significantly accelerating analysis efficiency. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed method achieves a 48% reduction in wafer-to-wafer EPD variation, thereby improving both process and yield stability across wafers.

title: ESD Breakdown Voltage and Leakage Improvement Through Lithography Process Optimization
authors: Vishal Singh Bartwal, PB Sahoo, Derrick Oon, Akshai Viswanathan, Tan Tze Hian, Karam Veer
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: An increase in leakage related wafer sort failures was observed in Bipolar-CMOS-DMOS (BCD) technology and was found to correlate with reduced breakdown voltage (BV) of a lateral PNP based electrostatic discharge (ESD) structure. When the BV shifts to a lower value, the device turns on earlier and may partially conduct under normal wafer sort bias conditions, resulting in elevated leakage and yield loss. This work investigates the sensitivity of ESD BV to lithography process variation, specifically critical dimension (CD) and overlay of the NWELL layer. Inline analysis revealed tool-to-tool variation in overlay performance, with higher overlay variation showing a correlation to reduced BV and yield loss. A targeted design of experiments (DOE), including focus exposure matrix (FEM) and overlay scaling, was performed to quantify the impact of CD and overlay variation. The results confirmed that increased CD and overlay deviation reduce BV because of the NWELL encroaching toward PWELL across the shallow trench isolation (STI) region and increasing local electric field concentration. To improve process robustness across lithography tools, CD retargeting and tighter overlay control were implemented as preventive action. In addition, retrofit mask modification was introduced to resize NWELL boundary and reduce sensitivity to overlay and CD variation, ensuring stable operation across all tools within normal process variation. These actions improved BV margins by 11% and eliminated leakage-related sort failures across production lots.
