title: A Unified Blockchain Framework for Smart City Resource Management with Convertible Tokens
authors: Amirhosein Mansouri, Mohammadhossein Ghorbi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel blockchain-based framework for smart city resource management, integrating energy, water, and gas consumption within a unified, tokenized ecosystem. Building upon previous research that explored blockchain applications for individual resource management, this work presents a holistic solution where resource efficiency is incentivized through a tokenized reward system. Consumers are rewarded with utility-specific tokens for conservation efforts and penalized for exceeding pre-defined consumption thresholds. The key innovation lies in the interoperability of these tokens. The proposed system allows for the dynamic conversion of tokens across different utilities. For instance, users can leverage water conservation tokens to offset penalties incurred from excessive gas consumption. This cross-resource exchange mechanism promotes resource optimization across multiple domains. In this method, the conversion rate of tokens to each other is determined based on the availability of each resource, its value, and environmental issues. This paper details the system's architecture, including smart contract implementation, and the dynamic conversion mechanism. Furthermore, it analyzes the potential impacts on resource sustainability and consumer behavior within the smart city context, ultimately paving the way for more efficient, equitable, and resilient urban environments.

title: Human-Centric Anomaly Detection in Surveillance Videos Using YOLO-World and Spatio-Temporal Deep Learning
authors: Mohammad Ali Etemadi Naeen, Hoda Mohammadzade, Saeed Bagheri Shouraki
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Detecting anomalous events in surveillance video streams poses substantial challenges, primarily due to the unpredictable nature of abnormal behaviors, severe data imbalance, and interference from complex, scene-dependent backgrounds. In this study, we present a deep learning–based anomaly detection framework that explicitly prioritizes human-related visual information while incorporating sequence-level learning for multi-category event classification. The framework begins by localizing human instances in video frames using YOLO-World, an open-vocabulary detector grounded in vision–language representations, followed by ByteTrack to preserve identity consistency over time. To mitigate the influence of irrelevant environmental context, non-human regions are deliberately de-emphasized via Gaussian blurring, enabling the model to focus on behaviorally salient motion and appearance cues. Spatial features are extracted using an InceptionV3 backbone initialized with ImageNet weights, while long-range temporal dependencies are captured through a bidirectional LSTM architecture for final classification. The proposed approach is assessed on a five-category benchmark derived from the UCF-Crime dataset, including Fighting, Burglary, Explosion, Arson, and Normal events. Experimental results yield a mean classification accuracy of 92.41% obtained from three distinct evaluations, with all categories achieving F1-scores above 0.85. Additional analyses based on ROC characteristics, confusion matrices, and macro- and weighted-level metrics further verify the robustness of the proposed method and its ability to generalize under class-imbalanced conditions. Overall, the findings indicate that emphasizing human-centric foreground information leads to more reliable detection of anomalous activities under complex visual conditions.

title: Trident: Efficient FPGA Acceleration of XMSS Tree in Post-Quantum Signature Scheme SLH-DSA
authors: Tianyou Bao, Joshua Ennis, Kirill Morozov, Jiafeng Xie
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: The emergence of quantum computing poses significant threats to conventional cryptographic systems, necessitating the efficient hardware acceleration of Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC), especially on the Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) platforms. SPHINCS<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, recently standardized by NIST (National Institute of Standards and Technology) as SLH-DSA (Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Algorithm), represents the only hash-based digital signature scheme. Its practical deployment, however, is restricted by computationally intense operations, particularly in the eXtended Merkle Signature Scheme (XMSS) tree, where WOTS+ (Winternitz One-Time Signature Plus) public key generation consumes the majority of signature generation cycles. With this background, this paper presents Trident, an innovative FPGA-based hardware accelerator that addresses critical performance and resource challenges in XMSS of SLH-DSA. First, we propose a triangle hash unit architecture that enables parallel execution of up to three hash operations simultaneously, directly addressing the computational bottleneck in XMSS tree construction and WOTS+ chain operations. Second, we develop an optimized memory caching scheme that reduces on-chip memory requirements via intermediate value management. Third, we implement the Trident on FPGAs and comprehensively evaluate it across all parameter sets at multiple security levels, i.e., up to 8.6× improvement in signature generation and up to 5.4× speed-up in verification operations. Extended Hypertree evaluation shows a 34.6× area-delay product (ADP) improvement on UltraScale+ FPGA for SLH-DSA-128s. This Trident represents a significant advancement toward practical SLH-DSA deployment in FPGA environments.

title: Secure Quantum Uplink MIMO System with Rydberg Atomic MIMO Receivers
authors: Jiaqi Liu, Jianxin Dai, Zhaohui Yang, Zhaoyang Zhang, Youguo Wang
abstract: Physical layer security (PLS) provides information-theoretic confidentiality for next-generation wireless systems, yet its practical deployment is fundamentally constrained by the sensitivity and noise characteristics of classical radio receivers. Recent advances in quantum sensing, particularly Rydberg atom-based receivers (RAQRs), present a promising approach to address these limitations. This paper introduces a Rydberg atomic quantum multiple-input multiple-output (RAQ-MIMO) architecture for secure multi-user uplink communications, where an array of Rydberg vapor cells functions as a high-sensitivity, reconfigurable receiving front-end. We develop a comprehensive end-to-end signal and channel model that incorporates quantum sensor response, wireless fading, and eavesdropper behavior under passive interception. To maximize the achievable secrecy rate, we propose a joint optimization framework that adaptively tunes key system degrees of freedom, including the Rabi frequency of the local oscillator, phase configuration, digital combining matrix, and user transmit power. Extensive simulations validate that RAQ-MIMO substantially outperforms classical MIMO in terms of secrecy rate across varying transmission distances, power budgets, and user loads—particularly under low-signal to noise ratio (SNR) and extended-range regimes. Our work establishes a quantum-sensing-assisted security foundation for future high-assurance wireless networks, bridging atomic physics with scalable communication system design with scalable communication system design under narrowband operation.

title: Back-to-Back Multiport Converter for Wind and Battery Integrated System With Reduced Power Conversion Stage
authors: Yu Huang, Dehong Zhou, Tiecheng Lan, Jianxiao Zou, Zewei Shen
abstract: The wind and battery integrated system is an attractive solution for improving power quality and stability in regions with high wind penetration. However, conventional configurations typically realize battery integration by employing high-voltage batteries or intermediate dc-dc converters, which present difficulties in balancing system cost, dc-link regulation capability, and power conversion stages. To this end, this paper proposes a back-to-back multiport converter (BTBMC) for battery integration, maintaining dc-link regulation while reducing costs and power conversion stages. In addition, a coordinated zero-sequence injection (CZSI)-based power flow control scheme is proposed by analyzing the decoupling characteristics of the BTBMC, ensuring machine/grid-side current control while facilitating the power flow among the wind turbine, dc link, battery, and grid. In the proposed scheme, a virtual two-level converter model is constructed to ensure the current control even under asymmetric dc-port voltages. Moreover, the zero-sequence component injection is coordinated on both the machine and grid sides to separately control the dc-link voltage and the battery power, thereby enabling flexible power flow in the wind and battery integrated system. Besides, the power flow control capability of the proposed scheme is analytically discussed. Experimental results on a downscaled prototype validate the effectiveness of the proposed topology and scheme.

title: Closed-Loop Sensorless Position Control of Dielectric Elastomer Soft Robots Via Actuator-Level Self-Sensing_supp1-3701524.mp4
authors: Paolo Roberto Massenio

title: Post-Quantum Security for 6G–Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Thomas Zasowski
abstract: Quantum computers have made huge progress in recent years. In this tutorial paper, we explain why they pose a threat to encryption methods that are used in today’s communication networks. As there is the unique opportunity of 6G becoming the first cellular standard where post-quantum security could be considered from the very beginning, we present approaches to achieve post-quantum secure communications. Benefits and drawbacks of quantum key distribution (QKD) and post-quantum cryptography (PQC) are compared. Suggestions are made on how these approaches can be considered resulting in a post-quantum-safe 6G standard.

title: Robust Rigid Body Pose Estimation for Sparse Point Sets Under Heteroscedastic Noise_supp1-3701525.mp4
authors: Taogang Hou
abstract: Rigid body pose estimation from sparse point correspondences is important in robotics but remains challenging when measurements are affected by heteroscedastic noise and occasional outliers. This issue is particularly evident in marker-based tracking of fast-moving platforms, where localization uncertainty may vary substantially across markers due to motion blur, occlusion, and sensing artifacts. To address this setting, we develop a robust registration framework tailored to sparse and structured point sets by modeling residuals with a heavy-tailed Student's t-distribution. Using its Gaussian scale mixture representation, the estimation problem is formulated as a maximum-likelihood objective with latent correspondence reliabilities, and solved via an iteratively reweighted least-squares procedure with a graduated non-convexity schedule. The resulting method adaptively down-weights unreliable measurements while retaining the efficiency of weighted rigid alignment. Simulation results across multiple noise models and outlier ratios show that the proposed approach achieves consistently low rotation and translation errors in sparse regimes. Real-world experiments on high-speed quadrotor tracking further demonstrate improved geometric consistency and reduced trajectory jerk relative to standard least-squares estimation.

title: DGPDT: Detection-Guided and Prompt-Driven Transformers for Automated and Generalizable Cobb Angle Estimation
authors: Chih-Yi Lu, Chen-Hung Tu, Chun-Yi Hsieh, Chi-Kuang Feng, I-Yun Lisa Hsieh
abstract: Accurate Cobb angle measurement is essential for scoliosis assessment but remains labor-intensive and observer-dependent. We introduce DGPDT (Detection-Guided Prompt-driven Transformer), a unified transformer-based framework that integrates vertebra detection and foundation-model segmentation for generalizable spinal analysis. A Roboflow Detection Transformer (RF-DETR) with a DINOv2 backbone localizes vertebrae, followed by post-processing to ensure anatomical continuity. The resulting bounding boxes serve as automatic prompts for a fine-tuned Segment Anything Model 2.1 (SAM 2.1), which generates high-resolution vertebral masks. Cobb angles are then computed from vertebral masks, enabling estimation of both main and compensatory curves. Evaluations on the in-house (TVGH-SpineXR) and external (SpineWeb-16) datasets demonstrate encouraging performance on both internal and external datasets, achieving mean Dice coefficients of 0.944 and 0.781, respectively, and mean absolute Cobb angle errors of approximately 2–3° in-domain and 4.93° under cross-domain evaluation. Despite being trained solely on TVGH-SpineXR, DGPDT maintains accuracy comparable to models trained directly on the benchmark dataset. By coupling detection-guided prompting with transformer-based segmentation, DGPDT achieves a clinically acceptable mean absolute error (<5°), suggesting good reproducibility and potential applicability beyond the training dataset.

title: Fast and Scalable Newton-Raphson Power Flow Analysis via Hierarchical Parallelism and Graph Node-based Hash Storage
authors: Tingdong Wang, Yinglong Ma, Yihu Liu, Siyan Liu, Yufei Zhang
abstract: The continuous expansion of smart grids has led to increasingly frequent power flow variations, thereby posing new demands for fast and scalable parallel implementation of Newton-Raphson power flow (N-R PF) calculations. However, existing parallel N-R PF methods often suffer from redundant floating-point operations and centralized data processing of matrix reconstruction for N-R PF calculation, which significantly increases the computational burden and impairs the parallel performance of N-R PF. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a fast and scalable hierarchical parallel strategy and graph node-based hash storage (HPGNH) method to accelerate N-R PF analysis. HPGNH integrates intra-node and inter-node parallel strategies for effectively eliminating redundant floating-point operations in Jacobian matrix updates and equation residual updates. Moreover, we present a graph node-based hash storage structure to efficiently construct and update the nonzero entries of sparse matrices in a fully parallel manner. Particularly for Q-limit N-R PF calculations, HPGNH only updates a subset of entries within the constructed storage structure instead of performing full-scale reconstruction, thereby significantly improving computational efficiency. Extensive experiments on twelve benchmark power systems demonstrate that HPGNH outperforms nine state-of-the-art methods, with computational performance improvements of 63.1% for N-R PF and 81.8% for Q-limit N-R PF.

title: GEM 2026 CFP
authors: N/A

title: A Bayesian Neural Network Approach for Probabilistic Breaking Force Prediction of ACSR Conductors with Uncertainty Decomposition
authors: Xiaoyu Luo, Ganyu Wang, Ming Nie, Yongchun Liang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel method for assessing the tensile strength of ACSR conductors by combining finite element analysis (FEA) with Bayesian neural networks (BNN). To address the limitations of traditional Monte Carlo methods that neglect the helical geometry and mechanical coupling between strands, a three-dimensional finite element model incorporating spiral contact was first established, and parametric analysis was conducted to generate training data. Subsequently, a Bayesian neural network was constructed to achieve high-precision prediction and uncertainty quantification of the breaking force probability distribution. The effectiveness of the proposed method was validated using typical ACSR conductors. Results indicate that compared with traditional methods, the proposed approach improves prediction accuracy by approximately 15% and distinguishes between epistemic and aleatoric uncertainties, providing a scientific basis for reliability design of transmission lines.

title: Research on Dynamic Equivalent Modeling of Wind Power Cluster Based on Transformer-BiGRU Network Data-driven
authors: Cong Peng, Ke Liu, Yi Yuan, Ming Nie, Pengju Sun
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of difficult parameter acquisition and high requirements for model completeness in the planning stage or complex terrain scenarios of traditional wind power cluster modeling methods, a data-driven equivalent modeling method based on Transformer-Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit(BiGRU) model is proposed, and the Grey Wolf Optimization(GWO) algorithm is used to optimize the hyper-parameters of the proposed model. Firstly, taking a wind power cluster as an example, input data (including wind speeds and voltage at the point of common coupling) and output power data of the wind power cluster under the voltage drop disturbance at the grid-connected point are collected to construct a historical dataset. Furthermore, the Transformer-BiGRU model is trained, and the validation set is used to evaluate the model performance and implement early stopping to obtain initial model parameters. Then, the GWO algorithm is used to optimize the hyper-parameters of the model, yielding the optimized model. Finally, the optimized model is used to predict and evaluate the test set, realizing accurate equivalent modeling of the dynamic response of the wind power cluster. This study provides a fast analysis method for power grid planning and operation personnel, achieving a good balance between accuracy and efficiency.

title: Research on Virtual Power Plant Trading Rules and Regional Practices
authors: Ran Lyu, Jing Yu, Mingxing Guo, Yanyuan Qian, Li Lan, Su Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: As a new operational model for aggregating distributed resources, the market-based trading mechanism of virtual power plants directly determines their capacity for sustainable development.This paper focuses on leading practices in Guangdong and Shandong provinces, deconstructing the core mechanisms through which virtual power plants participate in electricity markets. Employing comparative analysis, it delves into the differentiated regulatory designs adopted by Guangdong and Shandong during their marketisation processes, while examining how resource characteristics and regional development stages influence the suitability of regional mechanism designs. Subsequently, it further examines the underlying causes of regional mechanism differences by analysing three intrinsic characteristics of virtual power plants: the dual nature of aggregated resources spanning both generation and consumption sides, the absence of fixed grid connection points, and the emphasis on regulation capabilities. Finally, it presents prospects for the development of virtual power plants.

title: Support-Guessing Decoding Algorithms in the Sum-Rank Metric
authors: Thomas Jerkovits, Hannes Bartz, Antonia Wachter-Zeh
abstract: The sum-rank metric generalizes the Hamming and rank metric by partitioning vectors into blocks and defining the total weight as the sum of the rank weights of these blocks, based on their matrix representation. In this work, we explore support-guessing algorithms for decoding sum-rank-metric codes. Support-guessing involves randomly selecting candidate supports and attempting to decode the error under the assumption that it is confined to these supports. While previous works have focused on worst-case scenarios, we analyze the average case and derive an optimal support-guessing distribution in the asymptotic regime. We show that this distribution also performs well for finite code lengths. Our analysis provides exact complexity estimates for unique decoding scenarios and establishes tighter bounds beyond the unique decoding radius. Additionally, we introduce a randomized decoding algorithm for linearized Reed–Solomon codes. This algorithm extends decoding capabilities beyond the unique decoding radius by leveraging an efficient error-and-erasure decoder. Instead of requiring the entire error support to be confined to the guessed support, the algorithm succeeds as long as there is sufficient overlap between the guessed support and the actual error support. As a result, the proposed method improves the success probability and reduces computational complexity compared to generic decoding algorithms. Our contributions offer more accurate complexity estimates than previous works and clarify how support-guessing methods behave under different assumptions on the code class, rank profile, and decoding radius. These estimates provide a codingtheoretic basis for future work on decoding algorithms and applications of the sum-rank metric.

title: A Minimalist CMOS Memelement-Based Neuromorphic Architecture for Energy-Efficient Spike-Driven Pattern Classification
authors: Sourav Maji, Basit Shafat Makhdoomi, Vedant Upadhyay, Rajeev Kumar Ranjan, Sung-Mo Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Artificial Intelligence
abstract: This paper presents a memelement-driven neuromorphic architecture for energy-efficient spike-based pattern classification. The proposed framework integrates a proposed 7T MOS-only meminductor emulator (MIE) with a memristorbased synaptic unit to enable event-driven mismatch detection within a crossbar structure. The compact seven-transistor MIE, implemented in UMC 65 nm CMOS technology, operates up to 1 GHz while consuming only 1.23 nW static power and occupying 18.6 μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Leveraging its intrinsic memory dynamics, a meminductor-based neuron is realized, achieving 12.48 fJ/spike energy efficiency with 3.26 μW operating power and a layout area of 54.80 μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Integrated with the memristive synapse, the proposed neuromorphic block performs spike-driven pattern recognition with a total inference power of 11.65 μW. Robustness is validated through Monte Carlo, temperature, and processcorner analyses. Experimental verification using SN7404 and CD4007 ICs confirms the feasibility of the proposed memelementbased architecture for scalable low-power neuromorphic AI systems, with spike-based encoding enabling correct classification in otherwise unfeasible cases.

title: Computing Scheme for Space-Air-Ground Integrated Edge Computing Networks: A Reputation Blockchain-Enabled FRL Approach
authors: Sicheng Liu, Wenxiao Shi, Xue Wang, Fengye Hu
abstract: Space-air-ground integrated edge computing (SAGI-EC) networks have emerged as a novel architecture due to seamless connectivity, flexible deployment, and scalable resources. However, existing studies mainly focus on computing strategies, while challenges such as low node cooperation willingness and poor device reliability hinder the adoption of SAGI-EC. To address these challenges, we first propose a heterogeneous SAGI-EC framework that incorporates a reputation blockchain. Then, a multi-weight subjective logic model and an adaptive interaction weight updating method are employed to achieve accurate reputation evaluation and dynamic management. In addition, a problem is formulated to minimize the weighted sum of total time delay and energy consumption. By modeling the computing process as a Markov decision process and solving it through a federated reinforcement learning (FRL) approach, we propose a space-air-ground integrated edge computing algorithm based on a reputation incentive mechanism and FRL (RFSIEC). Simulation results indicate that our proposed scheme can effectively suppress and filter unreliable devices, significantly reducing the average total time delay and energy consumption while improving the task success rate.

title: An Integrated Structure of 5G and TSN Communication Network for Nuclear Power Plants
authors: Jiang Li, Long Zhao, ZiYue Jiao, HongZhi Sun, Tao Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: In the context of the accelerated digital and intelligent transformation of the nuclear power industry, nuclear power plants have put forward strict requirements for the delay accuracy, reliability and security of wireless communication networks. 5G private networks, with their high bandwidth, low latency and independent deployment features, have become critical communication infrastructure for nuclear power plant. TSN (Time-Sensitive Networking) compensates for the shortcomings of a single 5G technology in extremely real-time services with the advantages of high-precision Time synchronization, low latency jitter, and deterministic transmission. The combination of the two provides a dual guarantee of 'flexible access and deterministic transmission', perfectly meeting the demands of all scenarios such as nuclear power production control and intelligent operation and maintenance. This paper presents an integrated structure of two technologies in nuclear power plants communication network. It provides a reference for the digital transformation of nuclear power plants communication systems.

title: Enhanced meta-transfer learning for few-shot recognition of communication interference signals
authors: Shaogang Dai, Jingtong Wu, Yinzhi Wang, Huaji Zhou
abstract: The ongoing challenge of large amounts of labeled data requirements in existing communication jamming signal recognition methods motivates this work. We develop a few-shot recognition framework integrating Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML) and transfer learning, denoted MTL-RS+. A tri-phase progressive architecture is proposed, which includes pre-training, meta-training and meta-testing, pre-training initializes the network using known jamming classes; meta-training optimizes meta-parameters via N-way-K-shot episodic tasks, and meta-testing completes jamming signal recognition. An attention-enhanced residual module based on SENet is designed to use as the feature extractor, utilizing multi-scale convolutional kernels and channel attention mechanisms to enhance discriminative feature representation. An enhanced meta-transfer strategy is proposed that migrates the pre-trained feature extractor to the meta-training network, augmented by dynamic weight interpolation to bolster cross-domain generalization. Experimental results demonstrate that MTL-RS+ achieves average recognition accuracies of 90.89% (known classes) and 79.96% (unknown classes) with only 5 fine-tuning samples across 20 known and 10 unknown jamming types. This represents a > 6.4% accuracy gain over MAML-based baselines, significantly reducing annotation demands and enhancing anti-jamming robustness in dynamic electromagnetic environments.

title: Image-Quality-Guided Consistency-Alignment Network for Fault Diagnosis
authors: Zhuowei Li, Jianxu Mao, Wenbin He, Yaonan Wang, Junfei Yi, Caiping Liu, Hui Zhang
abstract: Signal-to-image transformation has been widely used in mechanical fault diagnosis to provide unified visual representations for downstream diagnostic models. However, the resulting diagnostic images can exhibit substantial quality variations caused by measurement noise, sensor degradation, and partial signal loss. Most existing methods either discard low-quality samples or implicitly assume equal reliability across samples, leading to information loss or degraded robustness. This paper proposes an image-quality-guided consistency-alignment network that explicitly estimates sample quality and leverages degraded data during training. First, vibration signals are decomposed into sub-bands, and energy-ratio criteria are used to select informative components for reconstruction. The reconstructed signals are subsequently fused into fixed-layout two-dimensional images via a sector-allocation strategy. Next, a diagnosis-aware quality assessment module assigns a quality score to each image to guide training. Low-quality samples are further regularized via cross-quality feature alignment using a quality-weighted supervised pairwise loss, encouraging them to align with high-quality counterparts from the same class. Finally, a Swin Transformer backbone performs classification. Experiments on the proprietary RGFD dataset and the public SEUB dataset demonstrate consistent gains under controlled mixed-quality settings, with accuracies of up to 99.4% and 98.7%, respectively, while additional evaluations under reproducible degradations further confirm the robustness of the proposed quality-aware learning mechanism.

title: Deduplication-while-Storage: A New Encrypted Deduplication Storage Paradigm
authors: Yufei Yao, Zhongyun Hua, Mingyang Song, Yifeng Zheng, Zhaoquan Gu, Qing Liao
abstract: Ensuring data confidentiality while achieving efficient deduplication is critical for cloud storage services. Existing works on encrypted data deduplication operate under a deduplication-before-storage (DbS) model, identifying duplicates only during the data uploading phase. They require the owners of duplicate data or additional key servers to assist in the data uploading phase for enabling encrypted deduplication. This makes it hard to balance data security and deduplication effectiveness. This paper introduces a new paradigm named deduplication-while-storage (DwS), which differs from the existing DbS model by enabling dynamic deduplication throughout the entire storage lifecycle, activated whenever owners of potential duplicate data are available. This paradigm aims to significantly boost deduplication ratio without compromising the security of outsourced data. To instantiate the DwS paradigm, we develop SGX-DwS, a shielded storage system that utilizes Intel SGX to support encrypted data deduplication. SGX-DwS builds on an ownership-based encryption mechanism that enables secure equality checks on ciphertexts with the cooperation of owners of duplicate data. It adopts a two-phase deduplication strategy. The first phase processes duplicates during uploading if a previous uploader of duplicate data is online and willing to assist (with incentives provided for participation), while the second phase addresses remaining duplicates during the storage phase when owners of the potential duplicates are simultaneously online and cooperative. Our experimental results demonstrate that SGX-DwS achieves high deduplication ratio with modest overhead, even in large-scale cloud storage environments. Specifically, the improvement in deduplication ratio achieved by SGX-DwS is up to 92.49% as compared to that in the DbS model.

title: LlymX: Multimodal LLM-Augmented XR for Context-Aware Information Access
authors: Luca Cordioli, Matteo Valoriani, Maristella Matera
abstract: Extended Reality (XR) and Large Language Models (LLMs) can support context-aware, hands-free information access in industrial environments. This paper presents LlymX, a prototype combining a Unity-based 3D environment with an LLM-driven backend leveraging retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and vector databases. LlymX introduces an interaction-oriented XR–LLM pipeline in which spatial user actions, such as gestures and object selection, are grounded in semantic object representations and linked to retrieval from a structured knowledge base. Through speech, gestures, and text, users can identify objects and access contextually relevant information grounded in domain documentation. A user study comparing LlymX against menu-based and infographic-based XR interfaces shows that the LLM-based modality is significantly more usable (SUS) than static infographics and comparable to traditional menus. Task completion time and cognitive workload showed no statistically significant differences, though participants appreciated the flexibility of conversational interaction.

title: NeuroShield-Diabetes: A Hybrid Nature-Inspired Neural Cryptography and Machine Learning framework for Privacy-Preserving Diabetes Data Sharing in Consumer Health
authors: Jing Yang, Jialin Dai, Muhammad Attique Khan, Weiyu Chen, Zaffar Ahmed Shaikh, Vijay Govindarajan, Bayan Alabdullah, Lip Yee Por, Wangkai Xie
abstract: Consumer health applications for diabetes management create critical privacy vulnerabilities when sharing sensitive patient data across healthcare ecosystems, demanding lightweight cryptographic solutions for resource-constrained mobile environments. This paper presents NeuroShield-Diabetes, a hybrid framework integrating neural cryptography with machine learning for privacy-preserving diabetes data sharing on smartphones and wearables. The framework employs tree parity machines for neural key exchange, achieving cryptographic strength comparable to 2048-bit RSA with 91% reduced computational complexity measured as CPU cycles per key generation operation. Adaptive differential privacy with calibrated noise injection maintains formal guarantees (ε ≤ 0.8 for high-risk queries) while preserving 94.7% data utility for glycemic prediction. Evaluation across 5,600 diabetes patients using MIMIC-III and UCI Diabetes datasets demonstrates 73% reduction in data sharing latency versus conventional cryptographic solutions. The neural intrusion detection system achieves 92.6% accuracy in identifying malicious access attempts with sub-120ms overhead. Field deployment confirms HIPAA compliance and GDPR conformance while maintaining interoperability with existing healthcare infrastructures, establishing NeuroShield-Diabetes as a practical privacy-preserving solution for consumer diabetes applications.

title: Reliable Power Management for DCMG Clusters Based on Adaptive Droop Curve and DESO Under Line Impedance and Cyber Attacks
authors: Dat Thanh Tran, Kyeong-Hwa Kim, Jih-Sheng Lai
abstract: The proposed scheme employs a multilayered approach to address the critical issue of ensuring the stability and reliability in DC microgrid (DCMG) clusters facing both physical constraints such as line impedance and cyber-security threats. First, a distributed tertiary control based on the partially decentralized secondary approach is employed with only a unidirectional communication structure to cut down the system cost and enhance the scalable capability of DCMG clusters. Second, to eliminate the effect of line impedance among clusters, the adaptive V*-P droop curves are utilized in the primary control to ensure the local power sharing in the intra-cluster and global power management in the inter-cluster under system uncertainty conditions. By using the adaptive V*-P droop curves, the utility grid (UG) agent automatically adjusts its droop curve to optimize the electricity expenditure under the electricity price change. Third, for the purpose of constituting a resilient control against false data injection (FDI) and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks both in the tertiary and secondary controllers, a dual-extended state observer (DESO) is presented to precisely estimate the attack signals. By using these estimated attack signals, a reliable power management scheme is constructed to achieve system stabilization without DC bus voltage (DCV) deviations as compared to existing schemes.

authors: Vincent Cohen-Addad, Tommaso d'Orsi, Anupam Gupta, Guru Guruganesh, Euiwoong Lee, Renato Paes Leme, Debmalya Panigrahi, Madhusudhan Reddy Pittu, Jon Schneider, David P. Woodruff

title: Smart InfraOps: Securing, Scaling and Monitoring GPU-Driven HPC in the Cloud-Native Era
authors: Ratan Raj Anandeshi, Devarsh Hemantbhai Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: High-Performance Computing (HPC) workloads in the cloud-native environment based on GPUs have advanced exponentially, which increased the need to deploy smart, secure, and scalable operational frameworks. The reference HPC infrastructures are not able to satisfy the new demands of scalability and dynamism, real-time observability, and automated compliance because of their hard, manual-based structures. This research proposes the Smart InfraOps, which combines the adaptive orchestration, AI-based observability, and zero-trust security into HPC systems based on GPUs. The system is a combination of Kubernetes native automation, GPU-aware scheduling, and intelligent use is achieved via telemetry to make the system maximally utilized, reduce costs and enhance resiliency. Experimental simulations were run with empirical results that proved Smart InfraOps to be better at utilizing GPS in 85 percent, lowering the scaling latency in 57 percent, decreasing the operations costs in 40 percent, and increasing the security compliance by 24 percent relative to the traditional GPU clusters. These findings validate Smart InfraOps as an efficient, cloud-native operational model that can effectively and safely manage, optimize and monitor the existent HPC workloads that are high in GPUs in the cloud-native era.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Analytics Framework Using Catboost for Risk Assessment in Critical Systems
authors: K. V. Sai Phani, G. Yashaswini, M. Sravan Kumar Reddy, Y. Madhu Sudhana Reddy, Anchula Sathish, B. Rama Subbaiah
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Stroke is a critical neurological disorder caused by an interruption in blood flow to the brain, often resulting in severe disability or death. Early prediction plays a vital role in improving patient outcomes and enabling timely medical intervention. This paper presents an advanced machine learning-based framework for stroke prediction using CatBoost integrated with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) techniques. The proposed system addresses key challenges in clinical data, including missing values, class imbalance, and irrelevant features, through a robust preprocessing pipeline incorporating imputation, Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE), and Chi-Square feature selection. Multiple machine learning models, including Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, K-Nearest Neighbors, Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, and XGBoost, are evaluated and compared. Among them, CatBoost demonstrates superior performance due to its efficient handling of categorical data and ordered boosting mechanism, achieving an accuracy of 95.79%. Model performance is validated using accuracy, precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score along with cross-validation to ensure robustness. Furthermore, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) is employed to enhance model interpretability by identifying key contributing factors such as age, body mass index, and smoking status. The proposed system provides a reliable and transparent decision-support tool, with potential applicability in other critical predictive analytics domains.

title: 3 - Total Super Sum Cordial Labeling Of Ladder Graph and Comb Graph
authors: Archana Rajpoot, Rinku Verma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: One variant of Cordial Labeling is Sum Cordial Labeling (SCL). The labeling 3-Total Super Sum Cordial Labeling (3- TSSCL) for Ladder Graph & Comb Graph is primary focus of this research. In this labeling scheme, each vertex is assigned an integer from a specified set. The edges of the graph are then labeled based on a specific rule corresponding to the defined labeling method. A graph that meets the conditions of 3-TSSCL is referred to as a 3-TSSCG.

title: Visual Detection of Corrosion Defects in In-service Oil Pipes by Pulsed Eddy Current
authors: Pan-Long Jia, Wen-Hong Liu, Rui Wang, Dong-Feng Li, Yong Li, Jun Han
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Under the circumstance that global oil and gas exploration and transportation have attracted much attention, the integrity of in-service oil pipes in the oil and gas well string is of vital importance to the safe and stable operation of the entire oil and gas production system. Due to being in a sulfur-containing corrosive environment for a long time, the in-service oil pipes are facing a severe problem of corrosions on their inner walls, which may lead to serious consequences such as the failure of the pipe string and the leakage of toxic gases. This research focuses on the pulsed eddy current detection of corrosions on the inner walls of in-service oil pipes. A pulsed eddy current dual-sensor differential probe is adopted to pick up the detection signals. The negative peak value of the normalized differential signal is utilized as the signal feature to establish the correlation law between the signal feature and the defect size and to achieve defect imaging. Finally, the Active Contour Models algorithm is used to effectively identify the edges of the defects.

title: Absorption-Scattering Characteristics in Underwater Optical Communication Links
authors: Jiade Zhang, Liwei Yang, Yue Zhang, Xuyang Zhao, Xindi Yu, Bowen Hou
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: This paper analyzes the effects of water absorption and scattering characteristics on optical signal transmission. Considering the bi-directional transmission requirements of underwater optical communication links, the forward link of underwater visible light is studied and modeled, and factors affecting optical transmission are analyzed to help realize efficient and fast transmission of underwater optical communication. The effects of absorption and scattering characteristics on the system are also investigated, and the absorption and scattering characteristics of seawater as well as their influencing factors are analyzed. The attenuation coefficient of seawater is calculated based on the absorption and scattering coefficients of seawater. Summarize the influence of absorption and scattering effects on signal transmission under different water qualities and wavelengths, and analyze the influence of seawater absorption and scattering characteristics on the system.

title: Sustainability Decommissioning of Radioactive Wastewater from Nuclear Power Plants by Means of Zeolite Materials
authors: Fabrizio Ambrosino
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: Sustainability entails the conscious and careful management of available resources, without affecting the future generations. Minimizing the impact of radioactive waste from nuclear power plants falls within this framework, with the aim of reusing as much as possible. In this study, zeolite materials have been characterized regarding their capacity to adsorb and remove artificial radionuclides from aqueous solutions simulating wastewater from nuclear power plants. Three zeolites, in powder state, have been tested. The high efficacy of synthetic zeolites rather than natural one has been demonstrated, providing almost 100% of removal of artificial radionuclides in half an hour. These results are the basis for the use of the zeolite in adsorbing radioactive elements in contaminated water solutions from nuclear facility.

title: SCAS-Det: A Lightweight Spatial–Channel Reconstruction and Dual-Attention-Enhanced Real-Time Object Detector for Autonomous Driving
authors: Tingye Cai
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Real-time object detection is a fundamental component of autonomous driving perception systems, where both detection accuracy and computational efficiency are critical for deployment on resource-constrained onboard platforms. Although recent YOLO-based detectors achieve promising performance, redundant spatial and channel information in intermediate feature representations still limits efficiency, particularly in complex driving environments containing large background regions and small-scale critical objects. To address this issue, this paper proposes SCAS-Det, a lightweight real-time object detection framework built upon YOLOv11. First, a Spatial–Channel Reconstruction Convolution (SCConv) is introduced into the backbone to suppress feature redundancy during convolution generation. By sequentially reconstructing spatial and channel information, SCConv improves feature information density while introducing negligible parameter and FLOPs overhead. Second, a Channel–Spatial Aggregated Boosting (CASAB) module is embedded before multi-scale feature fusion to enhance discriminative representations. The module integrates lightweight local feature enhancement with channel and spatial dual-attention mechanisms, enabling effective emphasis on critical regions such as small, distant, and occluded objects while maintaining deployment efficiency. Extensive experiments conducted on the KITTI and BDD100K datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior detection accuracy compared with the baseline YOLOv11 while preserving real-time inference speed and lightweight model complexity. The results validate that spatial–channel reconstruction and efficient dual-attention aggregation provide an effective solution for balancing accuracy and efficiency in autonomous driving perception.

title: A Secondary Ripple Suppression Method Based on Multilevel Active Power Decoupling
authors: Peiyu Xiong, Xiaojun Wang, Daqing Gao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: In high-power precision power supply applications such as particle accelerator magnet power supplies, stringent requirements are imposed on output voltage ripple coefficients better than 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup>. This paper focuses on suppressing the low-frequency output voltage ripple (below 300Hz). Conventional linear power supplies suffer from low efficiency, passive filtering demands excessively large capacitance, and single-stage active power decoupling (APD) is limited by the ripple current rating of the decoupling capacitor, making all of them inadequate for achieving the target level of ripple suppression. To address this, a multi-stage active power decoupling topology and its control method are proposed, designed to reduce the output voltage ripple coefficient from 1.5×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> to 5×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup>. The proposed method employs two independent APD circuits connected in cascade: the front stage provides preliminary attenuation of the majority of the low-frequency ripple power, while the rear stage achieves deep suppression of the residual ripple. A linear active disturbance rejection control (LADRC) strategy is incorporated to enhance system robustness. Experimental results on a 400W prototype demonstrate that the proposed method reduces the output voltage ripple coefficient from 1.5×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> (without APD) to 3.5×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup>, achieving a ripple suppression of −52dB (attenuation ratio of 7/3000). Under the same attenuation ratio, compared with a passive LC filter requiring 300mF of capacitance, the proposed method utilizes only 2mF of capacitance. Moreover, output stability comparable to that of a linear power supply is attained without the use of a linear pass transistor, thereby validating the effectiveness of the multi-stage APD configuration in surpassing the performance limitations of single-stage topologies.

title: Data Science for Climate Resilience and Smart Agriculture
authors: Sreekanth Yalavarthi, Monica Rohan Mali, Naim Shaikh, Suriya M, Kiran Kumar Reddy Penubaka, Karthikayen A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Climate change is one of the biggest challenges facing world agriculture, impacting crop yields, accessible resources, and food security. Traditional methods of agriculture are, however, less receptive to the adaptive demands required to deal with the changed environmental setting. The present research has, thus, attempted to address this issue by maximizing data science in support of climate resilience and intelligent agriculture through a multi-tiered approach for methodology. These range from the acquisition of satellite images, IoT-enabled sensor data in the field, and historical climate information, pre-processing, analytical modeling, system integration, and output validation. Machine learning techniques deployed include Random Forest and time-series forecasting to improve yield forecasting and estimation of climate risk. Results indicated that the enhancement in the accuracy of yield prediction attained 25 %, water consumption was reduced by 30 %, fertilizer usage dropped by 20 %, and the correct forecast for climate risk reached 85 %. A cloudintegrated platform featuring real-time analytics integrated with the decision support proved to be very flexible and easy to use in multiple agro-climatic zones. This research has shown the way data-driven models can influence the agricultural sector to an adaptive system, assuring a more efficient and sustainable system contributing good practices for climate-resilient farming and resource-informed management in the uncertain environmental scenario.

title: Low-Rate Turbo Code-Aided Burst Signal Code Block Synchronization
authors: Zejun Li, Shili Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: To address unknown code block boundaries in low-SNR burst communication system, this paper proposes a low-rate Turbo code–aided synchronization method without using attached synchronization markers. The method exploits distinct log-likelihood ratio (LLR) convergence behaviors during iterative decoding to identify and locate code block boundaries. To handle burst intermittency, a spectral entropy–based detector is used for signal detection. A hierarchical synchronization strategy is adopted in which coarse synchronization is first achieved with few iterations and precise boundary locking with full iterations effectively balancing performance and complexity. Simulation results show that the proposed method outperforms conventional ASM-based schemes in synchronization success rate and accuracy under low-SNR conditions, while remaining robust to frequency and phase offsets.

title: Ionizing Radiation Effects on Lithium-Ion Battery Electrolyte Diffusion Properties
authors: Jeff Stubblefield, Mohammad Qasem, Yazan Yassin, Bruce Gnade, Mahesh Krishnamurthy
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are increasingly used in high-radiation environments such as space, nuclear energy, medical, and waste management applications. Understanding the effects of total ionizing dose on LIB electrochemical performance is critical, as radiation-induced electrolyte decomposition is a primary failure mechanism. This study investigates the impact of X-ray irradiation (up to 6 Mrad, 320 keV) on 21700 NMC811 cells and isolated LiPF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf> based electrolyte (EC/DMC/DEC, 1:1:1). Impedance measurements show a 36 percent increase in SEI resistance, a 13 percent increase in subdiffusion time constant, significant HF formation, and near depletion of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\text{PF}_{6}}^{-}$</tex> at 1 Mrad. A modified equivalent circuit model (ECM) based on broadband EIS and HPPC parameter extraction is proposed to support radiation-aware battery management system design.

title: MSA-CongNet: Multi-Scale Attention with Noise-Aware Regularization for VLSI Congestion Prediction
authors: Ziyan Qu, Yuxiang Liu, Wenjun Zhu, Zhengkang Zhu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Accurate congestion prediction at the placement stage is critical for reducing design iterations and accelerating timing closure in modern electronic design automation (EDA) flows. Although learning-based methods have shown promise on EDA benchmarks such as CircuitNet, existing approaches suffer from three persistent limitations: (1) single-scale feature extraction fails to capture the spatial heterogeneity of congestion patterns ranging from local pin clusters to global routing demand; (2) convolutional architectures lack explicit modeling of long-range dependencies between distant hotspots sharing routing resources; and (3) models trained on early-stage placement features are brittle under the inherent noise of pre-routing estimations. To address these challenges, we propose MSA-CongNet, a unified framework comprising three synergistic modules: (1) a Multi-Scale Feature Pyramid (MSFP) that encodes hierarchical spatial context through top-down fusion across four resolution levels; (2) a Spatial Cross-Attention (SCA) mechanism with learnable geometric bias that captures non-local hotspot correlations; and (3) a Noise-Aware Regularization (NAR) strategy that enforces prediction consistency under controlled input perturbation. Experiments on CircuitNet demonstrate that MSA-CongNet reduces MAE by 36.8% over the strongest baseline while achieving 24% faster inference.

title: Applying Natural Language Processing to Digital Humanities: Exploring Literary Patterns with Machine Learning
authors: Subhashis Banerjee, Vishnu Vandana Devi V, Padma Venkat, Omar J. Alkhatib, Rohita Sharma, Anushyapraveen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: In this research, the importance of applying Natural Language Processing (NLP) to Digital Humanities is examined in order to identify literary patterns through advanced machine learning methods. Particularly, we use the state of the art, Transformer based fine-tuned GPT-4, to perform an analysis on literary complex texts. The model is optimized with a wide variety of literary corpora in order to improve its sentiment, emotion, and storyline segmentation capability, as well as to perceive more subtle nuances like the use of metaphorical language and themes. The GPT-4 performance is reflected against the traditional performances such as rulebased detection and machine learning models and proves to be more accurate, precise, and recalls. The results show that GPT-4 offers a deeper and more secure study of the literary trends, which is why it is an invaluable resource in the context of the future research of digital humanities. This article underscores the possibilities of machine learning as a breakthrough in literary studies, as it provides more insights on reading texts written in different languages, cultures, and historical times.

title: An AI-Based Framework for Decoherence Mitigation in Quantum Systems
authors: Rithik Dubey, Sakshi Takkar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The susceptibility to decoherence is significantly high in quantum computing systems, and the latter effect significantly compromises the computational reliability, particularly in Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) computers. Even though the existing mitigation strategies are currently focused on hardware-level error correction and pulselevel noise mitigation, system-level execution variables (by far, the most notable of them being waiting times caused by queues) can also contribute to the worsening of fidelity degradation, but this is hardly addressed. In its turn, this manuscript suggests an AI-based decoherence mitigation framework, in which the time spent in the queue is incorporated into fidelity prediction and scheduling. The framework elucidates the interrelations between quantum-state fidelity and waiting time through a physics-informed decoherence formulation, which is followed by the use of machine-learning to predict degradation of fidelity prior to circuits execution. Based on the estimated measures of fidelity, decoherence-aware scheduling algorithms are presented to either give priority or re-scheduling to quantum jobs that become highly disturbed by the coherence loss. The viability of integrating the AI-based predictive mechanisms into the system-level quantum computing processes is shown through simulation-based assessment, which indicates that the suggested methodology can improve the effective fidelity of the implemented quantum jobs as compared to the traditional scheduling approaches. The findings indicate that AI-supported software-level alleviation mechanisms can be used as an effective and efficient enhancement of the dependability of nearterm quantum computing systems due to their scalability and use of hardware as the basis of the error reduction approach.

title: FedTCP: Token-Centric Control Plane for Federated Learning under Data Drift and Client Churn
authors: Zhili Yang, Xiangyi Chen, Jiahui Wu, Zihao Wu, Xingyue Luo, Li Feng
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without sharing raw data, but practical deployments often suffer from data drift, client churn, and unstable network conditions. Existing methods typically rely on parameter-level synchronization, which incurs communication costs proportional to model size and becomes increasingly unstable under dynamic participation. To address these challenges, we propose a Token-Centric Control Plane for Federated Learning under Data Drift and Client Churn (FedTCP) that replaces model parameter synchronization with compact class-level token exchange. The server maintains global semantic tokens as coordination states, while clients update local models independently and communicate token-level statistics. This design reduces communication complexity from model-scale transmission to lightweight token exchange that is primarily determined by the number of classes and embedding dimensions. Furthermore, we introduce a reliability-adaptive gating mechanism that adjusts cross-client regularization strength according to participation ratio and token drift, improving robustness under dynamic conditions. Experimental results on multiple datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves up to 3.65% higher global test accuracy in dynamic environments than the strongest baseline, while significantly reducing communication overhead compared to conventional federated approaches.

title: AI-Based Evaluation of Survivorship Quality Metrics from Healthcare Data
authors: Gandepalli Varshita, Doddi Ashwan, Aiswarya S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Evaluation of cancer survivorship is one of the important steps towards improving the care of cancer survivors, and it can significantly improve the quality of life of cancer patients. In this paper, a framework for predicting cancer survivorship quality and recurrence risk using artificial intelligence is proposed, and the METABRIC dataset is used to support this. Age at diagnosis, tumour size, number of positive lymph nodes, and Nottingham Prognostic Index are among the features used to train different machine learning algorithms, namely, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Support Vector Machine. A systematic approach to preprocessing, cleaning, and normalisation of the dataset is also implemented to ensure the precise execution of the algorithms. The best-performing algorithm is then used to develop a web application based on the Flask framework to predict, estimate probabilities, and categorise cancer survivorship. In addition, another tool called analytics has been developed to visualize prediction history and survivorship trends. The performance of the proposed system shows promising results and could be advantageous for healthcare professionals.

title: Forecasting India’s Chemical Exports to Leading Trade Partners Using XGBoost
authors: Mohd Adil, Saba Ismail
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: This research examines the export performance of India’s chemicals and chemical products industry to its ten largest trading partners through the use of XGBoost, Random Forest, ARIMA, and LightGBM models. The models were trained and tested using annual export data from 1996 to 2024, with their performance gauged through MAE, RMSE, and SMAPE. The XGBoost demonstrated the highest forecasting accuracy, truly reflecting nonlinear patterns and temporal dependence. Export gap analysis for 2024 reveals the three leading untapped export potential markets such as Netherlands, Belgium and China, while also showcasing stronger-than-anticipated outcomes with key partners such as the US, UAE, and Japan. Projections for 2025 suggest steady growth in most markets, showcasing the strength of India’s chemical exports. The research emphasizes the utility of ensemble machine learning techniques in export forecasting and offers policy-relevant insights for policymakers and trade planners to maximize exports and discover high-growth markets.

title: Real-Time Credit-Card Approval with AID-Score: An AI-Driven Credit-Scoring and Policy-Fusion System
authors: Narendra Bisht, Zokir Mamadiyarov, Zafar Berdinazarov, Atabek Isaev, Ashraf Abdualimov, Inomjon Yusubov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Rapid digitalization and tighter regulation expose the limits of traditional scorecards and rule engines for credit-card approval, especially in calibration, explainability, and low-latency operation. This paper introduces AID-Score - an AI-Driven Credit Scoring system for real-time credit card approval in the banking system, that produces calibrated probabilistic scores, human-readable explanations, and auditable decisions. The approach fuses ensemble supervised learners (XGBoost, LightGBM, calibrated Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) with Platt scaling for probability calibration, SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) for local explanations, and a Technique for Order of Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution (TOPSIS)-inspired multi-criteria fusion layer to enforce policy constraints; thresholds are chosen by expected-loss minimization under stratified temporal evaluation. Key results on benchmark datasets: AUC-ROC = 0.994, AUPRC = 0.974, Brier = 0.050, Expected Calibration Error (ECE) = 0.135, p95 inference latency ≈ 164 ms. In business terms, at a representative operating point, AID-Score reduces portfolio expected loss (EL) to ≈ ₹53.75 crore versus ≈ ₹59.59 crore for a LightGBM baseline, a reduction of ≈ ₹5.84 crore (≈ 9.8%). Integrating AI scoring, Multi-Criteria Decision Making (MCDM) justification, and deployment optimizations yields an explainable, audit-friendly decision engine ready for real-time credit-card approval with measurable portfolio benefits.

title: Designing Web-Based Platform for Promoting Regional Tourism in Sumba Island
authors: Mahaning Indrawaty Wijaya, Inggrid Sudarto, Ali Gunawan, Richard Wiputra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Sustainable tourism development in regional areas faces complex challenges, including limited infrastructure, minimal promotion, and limited local community participation in the tourism ecosystem. In the era of digital transformation, web-based technology has emerged as a strategic solution to address these challenges. This research aims to implement web-based technology to support sustainable regional tourism development. Through design science research, this study explores important artifacts to design web-based platform to strengthen destination promotion to improve people awareness and interest in visiting the destination.

title: Research on Capacity Optimization of Distribution Transformers in Metro Stations
authors: Ji Luo, Yin Li
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The rational selection of power distribution transformer capacity is critical to the safe, reliable, and economical operation of subway station power distribution systems. Based on actual operational data from Xiamen Metro Line 3, this paper conducts a specialized study to address common issues in domestic subway stations, such as low load factors of distribution transformers and significant discrepancies between design parameters and actual operation. Through an in-depth analysis of the energy consumption characteristics of various loads, including ventilation and air conditioning, escalators and low-voltage systems, the study refines key parameters in the traditional demand factor method and verifies the power supply capacity under multiple operating conditions, including normal, fault, and fire scenarios. The results demonstrate that optimizing the capacity of station distribution transformers can significantly reduce equipment procurement costs and long-term operational energy consumption while ensuring power supply reliability. This provides a crucial basis for green and energy-efficient design in subsequent metro lines and stations across other cities in China.

title: Research on the Design of Children's Color Cognitive Intelligent Toys Based on Embodied Cognition Theory
authors: Jiwei Liu, Yuhao Jiao, Jiangsheng Wang, Ensheng Zhao, Yunpeng Jiang, Jie Gao
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Based on the embodied cognition theory, explore and construct a set of interactive color cognition intelligent toy methods and designs suitable for preschool children. Combining embodied cognition theory, multi-sensory interaction and color psychology, this paper explores the interactive relationship among body movements, sensory experiences and color cognition, and establishes a color cognition toy design hierarchy system constructed from the transformation layer, perception layer, interaction layer, emotion layer and feedback layer. Through market research, analysis of children's characteristics, questionnaires and KJ analysis method, the design requirements of color cognition toys were obtained. The Kano model and FBS model were introduced to categorize, refine and transform the design requirements, and clarify the logical relationship between the functions, behaviors and structures of the toys. The multisensory interaction cognitive model based on embodied cognition theory is more in line with children's learning methods. It can not only effectively improve children's efficiency in color cognition but also contribute to their intellectual growth.

title: Decentralized Task-Allocation in Bayesian Search: An Empirical Study Under Degraded Communications
authors: James Lott, Vahraz Honary
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Reliable multi-robot search is constrained by imperfect communications when deployed in field environments. This motivates empirical evaluation of lightweight decentralized alternatives that may degrade more gracefully as message reliability worsens. This paper presents a hardware-based empirical comparison of three decentralized task-allocation strategies for Bayesian search on a four-UGV testbed under progressive receiver-side Bernoulli message loss <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0 \%, 10 \%, 25 \%)$</tex>. The evaluated methods represent three coordination paradigms: belief-weighted Voronoi allocation, market-based assignment, and adaptive rectangular partitioning. Each was implemented in decentralized form and optimized for lightweight onboard computation and messaging. Performance is evaluated using post-clue efficiency (steps-to-find), workload balance, onboard computation fraction, and communication overhead density. Across all communication conditions, the market-based approach achieved the lowest post-clue steps-to-find and maintained nearly constant computation and messaging overhead as loss increased, while the Voronoi allocation and rectangular partitioning degraded more substantially. We also validate a hub-centric metric logging pipeline that enables accurate system-level evaluation even when message loss induces inconsistent local robot state.

title: Small-Signal Stability Analysis of Hybrid Grid-Forming and Grid-Following Converter Systems
authors: Ge Tang, Yanming Chen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the rapid development of novel power systems—characterized notably by high penetration of power electronics and renewable energy sources—grid-following (GFL) converters demonstrate robust stability under strong grid conditions but are prone to instability in weak grid scenarios. In contrast, grid-forming (GFM) converters boast excellent adaptability to weak grids, while their operating performance under strong grid conditions is unsatisfactory. Accordingly, the hybrid grid-connected operation of GFM and GFL converters has become a mainstream configuration in the new-type power system, making research on such hybrid systems a hot topic in the field. Concurrently, an in-depth investigation into the synchronization stability of these hybrid systems is of paramount importance. This paper first establishes a small-signal model for a grid-connected hybrid system comprising both GFL and GFM converters. Subsequently, utilizing eigenvalue analysis, the impact of variations in the grid short-circuit ratio (SCR) on the system's small-signal stability is analyzed. Finally, the validity of the theoretical analysis is verified through time-domain simulation results.

title: Exploring the Effectiveness of Artificial Intelligence in Transforming Future of Digital Marketing
authors: Shital Naikwade, Namita Chawla, Sunil Kumar Suvvari, Nilesh Anute, Rajendra Subhash Jarad, Mangesh Purushottam Dande
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) has an effect on digital marketing (DM) in today's globalized environment. If managed carefully, AI will change the DM landscape and businesses are starting to see the potential of AI associated with DM success. DM is one of many businesses that are radically changing from the rapid growth of AI and machine learning (ML) technology. This paper looks to explore how AI and ML is affecting digital marketing processes and what that means to businesses. Marketers use ML to find patterns of users on a mobile app or webpage. By allowing AI to learn these patterns undoubtedly evolves advertising offers by optimizing their campaign and predicting future behaviours of their users. This paper proposes a new method of employing ML for information systems, optimizing advertising services, engaging customers, generating sales, personalizing promotional offers based on user interests, and for just simply setting up a spam filter for email advertisements or Facebook services. This papers theory demonstrates that the methodology for enhancing advertising campaigns is in fact reasonable.

title: Optimized Task Distribution for Energy Conservation in Electric Vehicles via Edge Computing Networks
authors: Abhishek Baer
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: As electric cars become more well known, they have an issue of going out of nowhere and loosing power, the solution to many problems is an exponential increase in performance just by a little bit of improvments. The way that local vehicle swarms could be created is by running some computations in a vehicle and having a few sources run the rest as data needs to be shared or analyzed. A combination to increase vehicles routes efficiency is used to minimize the use of any vehicles resources throughout the journey to successfully reach their destination with consistent plans. Our way makes the big jobs go to where that particular node is best at to make using energy and power important efficient. Scientists have figured out a way to cut back on energy usage up to 30 percent in electric vehicles than before, it was said, which is a victory for the environment.

title: Generative AI-Driven Analytics Model for Predictive Institutional Performance Mapping
authors: Swastisudha Punyatoya, Brijendra Singh Yadav, Naveen Nandal, Prachi Bismaya, Sandeep Sampat Tadakhe, John T Abraham
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The suggestion is a collaborative contribution of the transformer-based neural networks such as GPT-4 or the variants of BERT algorithms with the conventional prediction algorithms (predictive analytics) to process the multidimensional data streams in the case of institutional organizations. The system can generate complete trajectories in performance by applying deep learning techniques like variational autoencoders, or VAEs, and generative adversarial networks or GANs through the application of historical performance metrics and stakeholder patterns of engagement and environmental variables. It relied on the python-based frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, Skitlearn, and the cloud-based environment to scale the implementation such as Azure ML and AWS SDK as SageMaker. The combination of Apache Kafka to process the data in real-time and Power BI to visualize the data in real-time makes it possible to have dynamic and real-time performance monitoring. It can be experimentally validated to yield much higher levels of accuracy in predicting 89% of various institutional situations compared to various traditional regression models. This strategy is transforming the manner of execution of strategic plans by offering practical knowledge concerning how to maximize on the resources and evade the hazards.

title: A Collision-Aware Mobile Robot Navigation in Grid Environment using Improved A*
authors: Zihao Chu, Jianheng Qiu, Jianing Xu, Zheyu Zhang, Haopeng Zhao, Hao Liu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: Effective path planning in cluttered environments remains a critical challenge for mobile robots. While traditional grid-based planners like A* excel at finding shortest paths, they often neglect overall path safety, leading to high-risk navigation. This paper proposes a Collision-Aware Improved A* (CA-A*) algorithm to address this limitation. Our approach enhances the classical A* framework by integrating a multi-layer environmental model that accounts for static obstacles and positional uncertainty. A novel cost function incorporates a collision risk assessment, penalizing paths that venture too close to hazards. Furthermore, an adaptive heuristic function guides the search towards safer regions. Comprehensive simulations in static benchmarks demonstrate that CA-A* generates significantly safer paths while maintaining competitive path lengths—substantially outperforming classical A*, Weighted A*, and Field D* in terms of safety metrics. Real-world experiments validate the algorithm’s practicality and robustness. The results confirm that the proposed method achieves a superior balance between path length and safety for mobile robot navigation, trading a minimal increase in distance for a substantial improvement in navigation safety.

title: Integrating AI and Deep Learning (MobileVNet) for Smart Agricultural Farming
authors: Allen Paul Esteban, Vijay Kumar Reddy Voddi, A. Sevuga Pandian, M.Nithya, R.Ramesh, N. Vini Antony Grace
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: While artificial intelligence (AI) and deep learning technologies are rapidly developing, traditional farming techniques are evolving to create the path for farming systems with more intelligence and efficiency. Still, some quite significant problems call for attention including environmental condition monitoring, early stage crop disease detection, and optimal use of resources. This work intends to investigate the possible applications of MobileVNet, a lightweight deep learning model, in the agricultural domain to address associated problems. Using real-time data acquired from sensors, drones, and mobile devices, MobileVNet is used for chores including crop health monitoring, pest detection, and precision irrigation. MobileVNet allows one to finish these tasks. Its design has concentrated on mobile and edge devices to ensure scalability and affordability for farmers with limited computing capacity. The proposed system links MobileVNet with Internet of Things (IoT) devices to improve processes of decision-making for sustainable agriculture. This gives one real-time insights. The method is to teach MobileVNet on a large collection of agricultural images including images of many kinds of crops, soil conditions, and pest infestations. The model has been tuned to provide accurate predictions on the possible yield and crop health categorization. Edge computing techniques also help to process data locally, so lowering latency and ensuring that farmers obtain correct transaction feedback. The findings help to support MobileVNet's ability to increase agricultural output and sustainability. The findings of the tests indicate that precision irrigation can find crop diseases with a degree of accuracy of ninety-two percent and reduce water consumption by thirty percent.

title: Early Detection of Diabetic Foot Ulcers Using DF-RESEFFICIENTNET-B0: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach
authors: Edara Divya Sai, Venigalla Swami Ayyappa, Datla Jaidev Venkata Ayyappa Varma, Repalle Kalyan Raghavendra, Addanki Sathyavani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Diabetic Foot Ulcers (DFUs) represent a serious complication of diabetes, often leading to infections, amputations, and increased mortality rates if not detected early. This study proposes DF-RESEFFICIENTNET-B0, a hybrid deep learning model that integrates ResNet50 and EfficientNetB0 for accurate DFU classification. The model leverages transfer learning, data preprocessing, and augmentation techniques to improve robustness and generalization. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model effectively distinguishes between healthy and ulcerated foot images with high accuracy.
