Additionally, GradCAM is employed to provide visual interpretability, enhancing clinical trust in the model. The proposed approach offers a reliable and efficient solution for early DFU detection, supporting improved patient care and decision- making.

title: Solar Energy Prediction Using Voting Ensemble Learning Regressor
authors: Mukund Kuthe, Parambrata Sanyal, Gopal Kumar Gupta, Ajit Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Accurate solar energy forecasting is crucial for efficient management and resource allocation in future sustainable cities because of the probable increase in renewable energy sources. Machine Learning (ML) is one of the most reliable options in solar energy prediction, capturing complex pattern in historical datasets. The primary objective of this research is to accurately predict solar energy using ML. Therefore, this article proposes a voting ensemble regressor that outperforms the traditional ML models in this predictive methodology, upon evaluation metrics chosen. The complete ML pipelining in this study involves acquisition of dataset from Kaggle repository, performing data preprocessing and feature engineering. The methodology employs principal component analysis (PCA) to reduce the dimension and retain originality in the dataset. The proposed voting ensemble regressor model combines three ML models, namely, Optimized XG Boost, Support Vector Regressor, and Random Forest, to enhance the performance in this predictive methodology. Furthermore, the authors explores all traditional ML models and compares the result in terms of MAE, RMSE, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value. The outcomes demonstrate how much better the suggested model is than the conventional ones. Therefore, this study provides a path for future research in projecting renewable energy.

title: Secure Cloud Data Mining with Blockchain-Driven Privacy Protection and Adversarial Attack Mitigation
authors: Thanu Priya N, V. Sheeja Kumari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This paper presents a secure cloud data mining framework that integrates blockchain-based privacy protection with adversarial attack mitigation to ensure reliable and trustworthy data analytics in distributed environments. The proposed system leverages blockchain technology for decentralized data verification, secure access control, and tamper-resistant transaction logging, while incorporating deep learning-based adversarial detection to enhance model robustness against malicious inputs. Privacy-preserving preprocessing techniques, including encryption and normalization, are applied to safeguard sensitive data before mining. The framework combines secure data sharing, intelligent mining, and attack resilience into a unified architecture. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves higher mining accuracy, improved attack detection rates, and enhanced privacy protection compared to conventional cloud mining and standalone security models. The results highlight the effectiveness of integrating blockchain and adversarial learning for secure, scalable, and efficient cloud data mining.

title: Quantum-Probability-Inspired Adaptive PSO for Carbon-Aware Cloud Scheduling
authors: G Preethi Kalyani, R Santhosh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Cloud data centers are energy-consuming and emit large amounts of carbon due to inefficient scheduling. The current schedulers tend to maximize latency or cost without considering concurrent SLA, energy and carbon goals. Multi-tenant workloads also increase the size of the runtime job-machine assignment space and make scheduling significantly complex. The presented paper suggests QP-Sched, a quantum-probability-based adaptive particle swarm optimizer of cloud scheduling. Every schedule is represented as a superposition-weighted probability mass-vector of discrete assignments. Adaptive inertia weights dynamically balance between exploration and exploitation over tri-objective scheduling demands. These estimates of carbon footprint are based on the calculation of PUE-scaled energy consumption and regional grid carbon-intensity coefficients. QP-Sched takes into consideration Kaggle cloud scheduling data when assessing SLA and energy benchmarking. Google 2019 cluster traces can be used to enable large-scale heterogeneous workload assessment of carbon-aware validation tasks. QP-Sched minimized the SLA violations by 22% in general in comparison to PSO and MILP. There was an 18% reduction of the cost of energy per task as compared to ordinary schedulers. It also converged 1.9 times faster than NSGA-II at similar constraints at all times. The framework can schedule multi-tenant cloud environments effectively and in a sustainable and SLA-compliant manner that does not depend on a specific hardware.

title: Machine Learning Based Approach For Malware Classification
authors: Saeed Abdulla Alhameli, Mohammed Sultan Alneyadi, Ahmed Mohammed Almheiri, Abeeralward Aljaberi, Sanaa Kaddoura
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: The increasing complexity of malware has reduced the effectiveness of traditional signature-based detection systems. While machine learning and deep learning techniques have emerged, many existing studies overlook the impact of class imbalance or lack a comprehensive comparison between different architectures under identical evaluation settings. These limitations affect the reliability of reported results for real-world malware detection. This paper presents a comparative study of Naïve Bayes, Support Vector Machine, feed forward Neural Network, and a one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN) for malware detection using the publicly available Malware Analysis Datasets: API Call Sequences. The models are evaluated under identical experimental settings, both with and without the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE), to assess the impact of class imbalance on detection performance. Experimental results show that SMOTE reduces bias toward the majority class and improves performance for the minority class. Deep learning models achieve the highest performance after applying SMOTE. The feed forward neural network achieved an F1 score of 0.8180. The 1D-CNN outperforms all other models, achieving balanced performance with an F1 score of 0.8249, precision of 0.8184, and recall of 0.8317. These results show that imbalance-aware training combined with deep learning models improves detection performance.

title: Detecting the Digital Footprint of Speculation: Machine Learning for Dubai’s Real Estate Sustainability
authors: Aaron Saji Abraham, Prajwal Kokatnur, Muath Alrammal, Milan Dordevic
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: This paper presents a data driven framework that uses machine learning to detect speculative behavior in Dubai’s real estate market at the transaction level. Using large scale records from the Dubai Land Department (DLD), the research shows features that capture the "digital footprint" of speculation, including holding period, temporal market positioning, price momentum, and localized net supply pressure across both off plan and existing properties. Moreover, the paper compares multiple classifiers: XGBoost, HistGradientBoosting, k-Nearest Neighbors, and Naive Bayes and integrates their predictive probabilities into a Speculation Index optimized for high recall. Furthermore, the research enables regulators to audit potentially destabilizing trades without relying on future information. Explainability of models is achieved using SHAP-based analyzes, which reveal a structural difference between developer driven off plan speculation and more diverse, momentum-driven behavior in the existing market. The paper provides actionable insights for policymakers by showing that existing market speculation is driven by localized price momentum rather than structural cycles as in the off-plan market. The proposed approach supports sustainable and transparent real estate dynamics in an emerging global city, focusing on how AI and advanced analytics can be used in inclusive, knowledge-driven urban governance.

title: Geometric Phases in NV Centers: Abelian and Non-Abelian Protocols Using Hyperfine-Engineered Dark Subspaces
authors: S. Biswas, V. A. Chirayath, J. Asaadi
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Geometric phases encode the holonomy acquired under cyclic control of a quantum Hamiltonian: for nondegenerate eigenstates the effect is Abelian (a scalar phase), while in degenerate manifolds the Wilczek-Zee generalization yields a matrix-valued holonomy that can mix states and depends on path ordering. We present an overview of measuring Abelian and non-Abelian geometric phases in nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond and propose a non-rotating measurement protocol enabled by the intrinsic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{14} ~\mathrm{N}$</tex> hyperfine manifold. As a first step, we propose to measure the Abelian Berry phase on a single hyperfine-resolved two-level transition using microwaveengineered closed loops with dynamical-phase cancellation by loop reversal. We then propose a non-Abelian protocol that engineers a two-dimensional degenerate dark subspace in an NV- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{14} ~\mathrm{N}$</tex> manifold via RF dressing and three phase-coherent microwave couplings (a tripod Hamiltonian). Two closed control loops implemented by winding independent microwave phases generate non-commuting SU(2) holonomies, which can be verified using final-only population readout and minimal tomography. We also present timing trade-off curves, full unitary Schrödinger evolution of the four-level tripod, and projected dark-subspace diagnostics that quantify both leakage and non-commuting holonomy.

title: A Cascading Deep Learning Framework for Large-Scale Microwave Circuit Design
authors: Changchun Li, Weidong Chen, Chang Chen, Xiang Zhang, Sensong An
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Electromagnetic (EM) simulation of large-scale, non-periodic microwave structures poses prohibitive computational challenges. Meanwhile, conventional domain decomposition methods (DDM) often struggle to resolve complex inter-subdomain connectivity. To address these limitations, we propose a novel AI-based computational framework leveraging the Multi-port Analysis Pixel EM Simulator (MAPES). This approach enables arbitrary partition connectivity and achieves rapid performance prediction with minimal training data requirements. Furthermore, to mitigate accuracy degradation arising from physical approximations, a neural network-based residual correction module is integrated to systematically refine the predictive responses. This hybrid physics-informed approach reduces computational complexity by orders of magnitude while maintaining high fidelity, providing a scalable and efficient solution for advanced EM design.

title: Role of AI in Fostering Professors’ Well-Being and Job Satisfaction
authors: N Janani, E Nafeza, Mithilesh, Venus Claver Dennen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The academic industry is changing as AI solves academics' biggest working issues. This research investigates how artificial intelligence improves academics' well-being and work satisfaction via technological interventions and supportive mechanisms. The study examines how AI can speed up administrative processes, decrease workplace stress, and improve learning environments. Artificial intelligence automates scheduling, grading, and student administration, freeing professors to focus on academics and research. The most noteworthy results show how artificial intelligence can adapt professional development, offer data-driven teaching tactics, and provide flexible learning environments. The technology's complete support networks reduce burnout, improve resource management, and diversify the workplace. Artificial intelligence's predictive analytics may potentially uncover issues and provide proactive assistance. The research found that strategic artificial intelligence may improve academic work cultures, improving educator job satisfaction, career development, and well-being.

title: Integrating Machine Learning and Data Analytics for Strategic Marketing Optimisation in the FMCG sector for consumer retention and loyalty
authors: Anshul Srivastava, Anamica Singh, Namita Sahay, Rita Mansukhlal Kotecha, Shambhavi Mishra, Vineeta Singh, Chirayu Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper will examine the revolution in marketing analytics, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of marketing campaigns and improve customer retention in the fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) industry. This paper integrates the machine learning algorithm and data analytics for customer retention and to optimise marketing decision-making. The capability to make data-driven marketing decisions has become a critical success factor in a market where customer loyalty is short-lived and consumer preferences keep changing. The purpose of the study is to close the gap between raw marketing data and actionable insights and, more specifically, how analytics can expand its personalisation, optimize the engagement touchpoints, and generate long-term loyalty. The primary data are collected using structured surveys and statistical models that include multiple regression, correlation using Python. The paper discusses the ways of brands relying on descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics to learn customer behaviour, support personalised marketing, and Customer Lifetime Value. It further points out how information-based approaches drive customer loyalty, brand loyalty, and competitive edge, providing a guide to the optimisation of campaigns and developing sustainable consumer relationships.

title: Deep Learning Empowers Internet of Things Device Energy Consumption Optimization: Design of Intelligent Gateway Resource Scheduling Based on CNN-LSTM Model
authors: Junxu Wei, Huqiang Liu, Xiaoyun Huang, Rui Zheng, Yongjun Qi
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: With the surge in IoT (Internet of Things) devices, energy efficiency management challenges for smart gateways are becoming increasingly prominent. Traditional static resource scheduling strategies struggle to adapt to dynamic loads. This paper proposes a hybrid intelligent scheduling model that integrates a convolutional neural network (CNN) with a long short-term memory (LSTM). First, the CNN extracts spatial correlation features from multi-dimensional monitoring data. Then, the LSTM learns the temporal evolution of the load. Finally, based on the predicted results, CPU (Central Processing Unit) frequency scheduling rules are dynamically generated to achieve on-demand resource allocation. Experimental results show that this strategy reduces energy consumption by approximately 30% under typical loads, significantly outperforms the delay violation rate of comparable methods, and improves resource utilization stability. This study validates the effectiveness and advantages of deep learning-driven proactive scheduling in energy optimization and service assurance for IoT gateways.

title: Unsupervised Learning for Health Insights: AI-Enabled K-Means Clustering in Chronic Disease Prediction
authors: Mukesh Joshi, Mamta Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Chronic diseases like diabetes, renal failure, high BP, and various cardiovascular disorders are the major causes of illness around the world, it requires regular monitoring and personalized healthcare system. The rapid increase in the volume of patient data generated from EHR i.e., electronic health records, various wearable devices, and from different clinical trials has created novel opportunities for designing data-driven disease management system. The present study explores the role of K-Means clustering technique i.e., an unsupervised machine learning technique, in evolving an active system for Chronic Disease Management (CDM). In this approach we do the clustering of patients that was based upon the various parameters like age, BMI, glucose level, blood pressure, and cholesterol. In addition, K-Means facilitates the identification of discrete group of patients with same risk (of health) profiles. These clusters could allow and guide various healthcare providers in tailoring various preventive strategies, optimization of different health care plans, and allocation of various resources more efficiently. The proposed research demonstrates how K-Means technique could boost early finding of diseases, improved patient stratification, and effective decision-making from healthcare analytics. Finally, the integration of cluster-based analysis for CDM system offers a capable method to achieve tailored, predictive, and preventive healthcare system.

title: Quantitative Evaluation of Amplitude and Phase Measurement Deviations Across Three Commercial VNAs for Common RF Components
authors: Liwen Liang, Kwok L. Chung, Jialiang Ni, Zhilie Tang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: As the core high-precision test instrument in RF and microwave engineering, the Vector Network Analyzer (VNA) provides full-vector S-parameter amplitude and phase measurements that directly determine the reliability of electromagnetic performance evaluation for RF devices such as antennas and microstrip lines. Most existing studies focus on measurement comparison between two VNAs using standard metrology devices, with insufficient research on multi-brand VNA measurement consistency for commonly used engineering RF devices. This paper selects three commercial VNAs (CEYEAR 3671D, KEYSIGHT E5063A, ROHDE & SCHWARZ ZNL20) to test the S-parameters of two patch antennas and one microstrip line under uniform SOLT calibration, and quantitatively evaluates measurement deviation of each instrument via the RMSE method. Results show that quantifiable amplitude and phase deviations exist across different VNAs, with phase deviation significantly larger than amplitude deviation; each VNA has unique performance strengths in different frequency bands. This study provides direct reference for instrument selection and measurement uncertainty analysis in RF testing.

title: Modified Lin-Kernighan-Helsgaun Based on Binary α
authors: Xianzhi Huang, Jiarui Zhang, Qunfeng Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: The Lin-Kernighan-Helsgaun algorithm is one of the most effective heuristics for the traveling salesman problem, and its performance largely depends on the candidate edge sets built from 1-tree based <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\bold symbol{\alpha}$</tex> values. However, computing these <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> values requires repeated 1 -tree construction and updates, which becomes a major preprocessing bottleneck on medium- and large-scale instances and complicates integration with other metaheuristics. This paper proposes a lightweight candidate-set construction method that replaces the classical 1tree <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> computation with Minimum Spanning Tree (MST)guided binary <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> strategy. A minimum spanning tree is first built as a global structural backbone, and multiple MST-guided initial tours are generated to identify frequently used edges. Each edge is then assigned a binary label indicating whether it appears in at least one initial tour, and candidate edges are selected by sorting incident edges using the binary label and edge cost. Experiments on TSPLIB instances show that the proposed method achieves solution quality comparable to classical The Lin-Kernighan-Helsgaun while reducing preprocessing time on many medium and large-scale instances, although the total runtime can occasionally be dominated by the downstream local search, demonstrating an effective trade-off between efficiency and performance for large-scale settings and hybrid frameworks.

title: Research on Digital Modeling of the Impact of Ocean Environment for Shipborne TT&C Antennas
authors: Ji Guo, Xing Li, Qian Sun, Tian Wang, Tianpeng Xu, Liang Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: This paper focuses on the multi-dimensional impacts of complex marine environment on the performance of shipborne Tracking Telemetry and Command (TT&C) antennas. Key factors are introduced, such as wind, waves, swells, and electromagnetic, with the action mechanisms of those factors presented. A novel simulation architecture of marine environment combined with TT&C antennas has been constructed, based on digital modelling. The research focuses on key technologies including the coupling of multiple factors in complex environments, and adaptive modeling and prediction. The effectiveness of the architecture is verified, through simulation and measured data. The degradation regularities of antenna performances, such as pointing accuracy, and gain attenuation, are analyzed, with providing the references for the anti-interference design, performance optimization, and operation and maintenance support of TT&C antennas.

title: Performance Driven Integration of Phase Change Materials in Indian Building Envelopes: A Climate-Sensitive Design Framework
authors: Mukesh S. Khedkar, A I. Deshmukh, Vaishnavi D. Kute, Pankaj D. Kute, Rahul B. Ingle, Amir Sohel F. Amdani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The research investigates building envelope performance assessment through climate-specific Phase Change Materials (PCMs) in tropical India. The research uses real-world data from eight actual deployments across five different climatic zones which extend beyond traditional simulation-based methods. The research evaluates PCMs through their different types and envelope integration methods and performance indicators which include Peak Indoor Temperature Reduction (PITR) and Cooling Load Reduction (CLR) and Thermal Lag (TL) and Comfort Hours Gained (CHG). The research demonstrates climate-specific advantages through its findings which show bio-PCMs and eutectics work well in composite and moderate climates yet paraffin-based PCMs achieve maximum PITR in hot-dry zones. The integration method between roof and wall and ceiling and hybrid systems determines thermal performance results with hybrid systems delivering the most consistent improvements. The research provides additional design trade-off information through a radar plot and material-property correlation analysis. The research provides strategic guidelines to enhance PCM implementation for passive thermal management in tropical buildings that aim to reduce energy consumption.

title: Cross-Domain Multi-Agent Digital Twin for Adaptive Traffic Signal Control and Intelligent Campus Mobility
authors: Alfredo Alan Flores-Saldivar, Jorge de Jesus Lozoya Santos, Juan Carlos Tudon Martinez, Edson Fernando Acosta Argueta, Yuxian Li
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion poses increasing challenges for intelligent mobility systems, particularly as robotic platforms require reliable infrastructure-aware information for decision-making. This paper presents a crossdomain digital twin framework that integrates empirically grounded traffic demand modeling, multi-agent simulation, adaptive signal control, and synchronized 3D visualization within a unified architecture. Signalized intersections are modeled as interacting vehicle and traffic light agents operating over a graph-based network. Infrastructure-level control is formulated as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), enabling adaptive and interpretable signal phase allocation based on observed queue states. Simulation outputs-including congestion dynamics, delay metrics, and signal evolution-are exported and synchronized with a Unity-based environment, forming a spatially and temporally consistent digital twin. Comparative evaluation against a fixed-time baseline demonstrates reductions in waiting time and queue length, together with increased throughput under realistic demand conditions. The framework further establishes a structured interface through which robotic agents, such as neighborhood electric vehicles (NEVs), can consume predictive infrastructure information to support navigation decisions. The results demonstrate that integrating adaptive multi-agent control with digital twin synchronization provides measurable traffic-level improvements while enabling scalable cross-domain coordination between infrastructure intelligence and robotic autonomy.

title: A lightweight deep learning and gradient boosting approach for Oral cancer classification
authors: S. Annie Kesviya, Manikandan J, R. Brindha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Oral cancer is a major health problem in the world and more so in less developed nations where access to early diagnostic facility is small or absent. Oral cancer presents itself in its early life stages with no symptoms and thus it is not diagnosed and survival is largely compromised. Conventional methods of diagnosis are based on manual histopathological examination that besides being time-consuming, is prone to errors and prone to biases on personal grounds. Recent growth in artificial intelligence, especially in deep learning and machine learning, are the opportunities that can enhance the accuracy and efficiency of medical image analysis. Most of the solutions in the literature are based on single-model architectures, and they can often not simultaneously extract features well and classify well. To overcome these constraints, we will present a new hybrid neural network structure called Oral Net-CB and this network is specially created to detect oral cancer at an earlier stage and precisely. The last processing pipeline is a complete preprocessing system based on methods like histogram equalization, contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE), and Gaussian filtering to boost the quality of the image and decrease noise. DenseNet121 is utilized to extract deep features because it is densely connected and it can extract dense spatial features efficiently. To classify, we apply Cat Boost, which is a high-performance gradient boosting algorithm that is most appropriate in case of class imbalance and in working with categorical data. Oral Net-CB with a publicly available data of oral cancer images is applied. The model had a classification accuracy of 94.3% that was relatively better and accurate than the current model.

title: Integrated energy-based assessment of settlement-induced crack patterns of Pietà dei Turchini church in Naples
authors: Andrea Montanino, Antonino Iannuzzo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The preservation of historical masonry buildings is a crucial challenge, particularly in light of modern monitoring and digital technologies, given the exceptional value of Italy’s and Europe’s architectural heritage. Masonry, however, is a highly peculiar material whose mechanical behaviour differs markedly from that of conventional structural materials. As a result, standard material models and finite element codes cannot reliably describe the stability of such constructions. Moreover, due to its unilateral response, cracking is common; therefore, monitoring systems must be suitably designed, utilising appropriate electronic devices and advanced sensing technologies.The aim of this work is to illustrate the combined use of two numerical methods developed within the framework of Heyman’s model for normal, rigid, no-tension materials: the Continuous Displacement for Fractures (CDF) and the Continuous Airy-based for Stress Singularities (CASS) methods. A concluding discussion highlights the complementarities between the two approaches through their application to a case study involving the lateral wall of the Church of Santa Maria Incoronatella della Pietà dei Turchini, offering insights into suitable monitoring system solutions that can be coupled with these models to effectively support the preservation of historic masonry heritage.

title: Federated Reinforcement Learning Model for Smart Grid Load Distribution
authors: Hare Krishna Mishra, Gautam Kumar, Ashish Kumar Suman, S.B.G. Tilak babu, D. Galingana, Randolph M. Balleras
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The current study will present a Federated ActorCritic Reinforcement Learning (FA2C) framework of smart grids to offer intelligent and privacy-responsive load allocation. The proposed solution enables different distributed energy nodes to obtain the best load management policies without necessarily sharing uncoded consumption data thus ensuring security and scalability. The implementation with the assistance of the popular simulation tool of smart grid modeling, the and gridLAB-D, to facilitate realistic experimentation is done through the framework. The grid state provided by gridLAB-D is high-resolution, and the FA2C agents can associate with the dynamic grid situations and optimize the effectiveness of distribution. The results of the experiment demonstrate that the federated design works effectively in terms of load balancing, reduction of peak demand pressure, and user privacy. Overall, the FA2C-GridLAB-D framework offers a good guideline to decentralized, intelligent and secure energy management of smart grids.

title: Optimizing food bank logistics: A mixed integer linear programming approach for efficient humanitarian aid distribution
authors: Jairo Enrique Parra Herrera, Diego Rossit, Daniel Rossit
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Food security stays a global challenge, as highlighted by the UN’s Sustainable Development Goal 2: Zero Hunger. Food banks reduce waste by addressing food surplus and shortages, but their operations require efficient planning. This study aims to develop a Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model to optimize food bank logistics, minimizing costs across the supply chain. The model considers transportation costs, supply and processing capacities, demand, and product types. Food is collected from suppliers, joined into packages at plants, and distributed via centers to recipients. Implemented in Python with Google OR-Tools and evaluated on data from Bogotá, Colombia, preliminary results show possible, cost-effective solutions. Decomposition of costs reveals that supplier-to-plant transportation dominates the overall costs $(97-98 \%$ of the total), while downstream stages (plants–distribution centers and plants–clients) together account for less than 3%. Fixed technology costs remain negligible ($0.2 \%$). This underlines that system-wide efficiency hinges almost entirely on upstream transportation, and interventions should target this stage. This research highlights MILP’s potential to improve logistics, reduce costs, and improve efficiency. Future work will incorporate dynamic demand, real-time data, and larger datasets.

title: Performance of Quantum Machine Learning Algorithm for Prediction of Dementia Disease
authors: R. Santhosh Kumar, S. Aisha Siddika, M. Ram Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Dementia is a brain disorder which affects the human memory, behavior and cognitive abilities. The person affected with dementia is unable to perform daily activities. So, there is a need of a model which identifies the Dementia. Many researchers have designed machine learning and deep learning models which uses MRI, clinical data, and medical imaging. The existing models take much time to provide the decision. The Quantum mechine learning algorithm will greatly reduce the execution time. A Quantum Dementia Classification (QDC) model is designed to predict the Dementia disease. The QDC model achieved the accuracy of 95 %. Early detection of Dementia disease can help the doctor to provide more appropriate treatment. The objective of this study is to develop an efficient Quantum Dementia Classification (QDC) model for accurate and early prediction of dementia using clinical and cognitive features. Index Terms-Dementia, Quantum Machine Learning, Quantum Dementia Classification, Prediction.

title: Optimization of Process Parameters in Milling Operation using VMC
authors: Ishan Jain, Aditiya Choudhary, Piyush Pant, Chitresh Nayak, Ajay Sharma, Narender Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Nowadays, cutting-edge modernization have led to the adoption of Computer controlled machines in several industries to boost output and quality. The computer controlled machines outrival conventional equipment in terms of accuracy and finishing. By adjusting the framework, CNC milling machining can be used to its greatest potential. Thus, this work reviews the process parameter optimization for CNC milling with carbide tools. The review shows how feed, speed, and DOC affect MRR, SR, and tool wear. Experimenters have been using Taguchi for upgradation. Surface and profile graphs are used to investigate the effects of milling on tool wear, MRR, and SR. It was determined that the 316 L stainless steel process parameters may be improved.

title: Website Heuristics and Their influence on Generation Z’s Purchasing Behaviour
authors: Mythili. R, Kiruthiga V
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the digital era, website design and usability significantly influence customer behavior, especially among the technologically adept Generation Z. This research examines the influence of website heuristics—namely navigation, aesthetics, interaction, trustworthiness, and personalization—on the purchase behavior of Generation Z in Chennai City. The study uses a mixed-method approach, integrating quantitative surveys and qualitative interviews to examine the preferences, expectations, and behaviors of this group. Research reveals that Generation Z prioritizes websites with intuitive navigation, aesthetically pleasing designs, engaging functionality, and extensive personalization. Trustworthiness, including safe payment methods and clear rules, is a pivotal component affecting purchasing choices. The research emphasizes the alignment of these heuristics with the psychological and cultural characteristics of Generation Z, including their inclination towards immediate satisfaction, social validation, and digital proficiency. Enterprises focusing on this demographic may use this information to optimize website design, elevate user experience, and increase online sales. This study offers practical advice for e-commerce platforms and digital marketers to enhance their strategies for Generation Z, hence fostering sustainable company success in Chennai's aggressive digital economy.

title: Simulated DPN-Like Gait Abnormality Recognition Using Wearable IMU and Plantar Pressure Sensing Framework: A Pilot Study
authors: Jiayuan Zhao, Yan Wang, Ruixiang Hu, Hui Wang, Xiangyu Zhan, Aihui Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Diabetic peripheral neuropathy (DPN) is associated with sensory-motor decline and altered gait, motivating objective, low-cost gait assessment tools. This paper presents a wearable multimodal sensing framework that combines an outsole-mounted plantar-pressure sensor array and an acquisition unit with on-board IMU to recognize DPNlike gait abnormalities in a pilot study. A dual-branch CNNBiLSTM network is developed to extract complementary spatial-temporal features from synchronized 14-channel sequences (8 pressure +6 -axis IMU) sampled at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 ~ H z}$</tex>. On a subject-wise leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) evaluation, the proposed approach achieves 75.13 % accuracy with AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=$</tex> 0.8403 in distinguishing normal from DPN-like gait. Under a window-level random split, it reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 0 0 \%}$</tex> accuracy (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=$</tex> 0.90) and outperforms single-modality baselines. These results suggest the potential of multimodal wearables for objective gaitabnormality recognition, while clinical validation on real DPN cohorts remains future work.

title: Predictive Analytics to Assess the Cost of Educational Exclusion in Rural India
authors: Sanjana Chauhan, Pankaj Sinha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This study estimates the socioeconomic costs of educational exclusion in rural India, focusing on how policy gaps affect access, attainment, and long-term economic outcomes. Using a mixed-methods approach with household surveys and Sarpanch interviews, the study finds that income disparities and gender play critical roles in school attendance. Girls face compounded barriers due to financial constraints, cultural norms, and safety concerns. Major obstacles include school costs, distance, inadequate infrastructure, lack of toilets, and mismatch between home and instructional languages. Delayed funds, shortage of trained teachers, and low parental awareness further exacerbate exclusion. Dropouts often end up in low-wage work or family enterprises, reinforcing intergenerational poverty. Despite challenges, strong community support for education exists. Policy recommendations include direct cash transfers, mother tongue instruction, improved infrastructure, gender-sensitive programs, and digital inclusion, alongside systemic reforms to reduce bureaucratic hurdles and ensure secure funding. A holistic approach is essential to foster equitable educational opportunities for rural children.

title: Research of PCB Rogowski Coils for On-line Monitoring Partial Discharge
authors: Chunming Wei, Bo Zhu, Chao Xu, Long Tan, Zhongzhi Qu, Jizheng Yang, Siming Gao, Ruihui Han
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Power cables are widely used in transmission and distribution networks, and their insulation condition is related to the safe operation of the entire power grid. In this paper, a new type of current sensor with a PCB-shaped Rogowski coil structure is utilized to detect the partial discharge signals inside the cable. The main components of the coil were elaborated in detail. By using MATLAB software, the influences of parameters such as the length of the unit board, cross-sectional diameter, number of coil turns, stray capacitance, and load resistance on the frequency characteristics and sensitivity of the coil were analyzed. The design principles of the coil were proposed, providing a theoretical basis for the design and manufacture of this sensor. The frequency characteristics of the coil were tested, and the results showed that the coil could truly reflect the waveform of the measured current without significant attenuation. Partial discharge tests were conducted on the cable, and the results indicated that the coil had a relatively high sensitivity. Moreover, the most prominent feature of this coil current sensor is that it can be installed on-site without power interruption and will not affect the original operating state of the equipment under test, laying a technical foundation for the promotion and application of PCB-type Rogowski coils in power systems.

title: Assessment of New Energy Carrying Capacity and Identification of Weak Links in Distribution Networks
authors: Dexiang Li, Huijie Sun, Zhijian Du, Kaihua Sun, Tianlu Luo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper investigates carrying capacity assessment and weak-link identification for new energy integration in distribution networks under the background of new power systems, with consideration of the complexity introduced by diverse energy injection characteristics. First, an optimal power flow model based on second-order cone programming (SOCP) is established, which incorporates operational constraints to evaluate the effective distribution capacity under different scenarios, thereby overcoming the limitations of traditional static analysis. Then, a weak node identification method based on hosting capacity and a multi-dimensional constraint factor analysis system are proposed. Finally, case verification is conducted using an improved IEEE 13-node distribution system. The results show that this method can accurately evaluate the spatial distribution characteristics of system carrying capacity, effectively identify system weak links and their constraint factors, providing important references for new energy planning decisions and system optimization.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Ridge-Meta Ensemble Model for Electrical Load Forecasting in Integrated Energy Systems
authors: Ruiting Shen, Changhao Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Reliable electrical load forecasting is the backbone for precise operation and intelligent scheduling of Integrated Energy Systems (IES). This study proposes a ridge-meta ensemble approach using ridge regression to merge three gradient boosting techniques: XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost, and predict electrical energy consumption. By fusing the three gradient boosting models with a ridge regression meta-learner, the framework balances high accuracy and computational efficiency. Validation uses 2021-2023 hourly electrical load data from Arizona State University's Tempe Campus, a real-world dataset with complex meteorological and temporal correlations. Test results show the ridge-meta ensemble model achieves an R-squared value of 0.8973, with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 1079.31 kilowatts and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 1490.39 kilowatts. Compared to traditional linear regression, this represents a 46.15% reduction in MAE and a 42.93% reduction in RMSE. Through SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis, we found that wet-bulb temperature and seasonal fluctuations are the primary factors influencing load predictions. With low computational overhead, the proposed model offers a feasible solution for real-time IES scheduling and management.

title: A Static Voltage Stability Index Evaluation Method Considering Distribution Network Resilience
authors: Yun Zhang, Zhen Zheng, Jingde Chen, Yaqing Li, Huamin Yan, Yilin Zhu, Feihang Lv, Haijun Xing
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the high penetration of renewable energy, distribution networks are evolving into multi-source interconnected networks, posing significant challenges to static voltage stability and resilience. To mitigate instability risks from inappropriate distributed generation (DG) placement and sizing, this paper presents a resilience-aware static voltage stability evaluation method using the Lu-index. Selected for its excellent linearity and real-time performance, this index overcomes the limitations of traditional indices in dynamic scenarios and high R/X ratio networks. Numerical simulations on the IEEE 33-node system quantify the impacts of DG placement, capacity, power factor, and load growth. Results reveal a non-monotonic "U-shaped" voltage stability trend with increasing DG capacity and demonstrate superior stability for mid-feeder DG integration compared to feeder head or feeder end placement. Reverse power flow under light-load conditions is identified as a key terminal integration instability risk. These findings provide theoretical and quantitative support for the scientific planning of DGs in modern distribution networks.

title: An Adaptive Voltage Control Approach Employing Distributional Soft Actor-Critic for Coordinated PV and BESS Systems
authors: Yue Wang, Zhijia Du, Xiaochun Xu, Dengcai Gong, Xiaowei Miao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The penetration of distributed photovoltaic generation and battery energy storage in distribution networks is increasing rapidly, which intensifies the difficulty of voltage regulation and loss management under stochastic power injections. This paper proposes an adaptive voltage control strategy based on distributional soft actor-critic for coordinated photovoltaic and storage operation. The voltage and var optimization task is formulated as a Markov decision process, where the agent observes key operating variables such as nodal injections, bus voltages, and the storage state of charge, then outputs continuous setpoints for photovoltaic active and reactive power as well as storage charging and discharging power. By learning a distributional value representation, the proposed method mitigates value overestimation and improves training stability under strong renewable fluctuations. Case studies on a modified IEEE 34-bus feeder indicate that the proposed controller suppresses voltage violations while reducing real power losses, and it achieves faster and more stable convergence than deep deterministic policy gradient and soft actor-critic baselines.

title: A Statistical Method for Cable Main Tray Materials Based on PDMS Database Research and Development
authors: Yulei Liu, Xuecui Ma, Weiming Zhang, Min Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The statistical analysis of main tray materials is a critical task in the design of main tray layouts. This paper, based on in-depth research into the PDMS 3D main tray component library and the statistical depth of the main tray material list, proposes a statistical method for materials based on the PDMS 3D model. It also uses PML language to write a statistical program. Finally, this method was experimentally verified in the design of main tray layouts for a certain nuclear power plant, with the experimental results showing that this program can effectively improve the efficiency and accuracy of material statistics for cable main trays.

title: Research on EMT Simulation of Power Systems Based on Coordination of ADPSS and MATLAB/Simulink
authors: Minli Gao, Haiyan Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the in-depth advancement of new-type power system construction, power systems are exhibiting the "dual-high" characteristics of high proportion of renewable energy and high proportion of power electronic equipment integration. This places higher demands on the accuracy, flexibility, and modeling efficiency of electromagnetic transient (EMT) simulations. A key limitation of traditional EMT simulation tools is the inherent difficulty in developing custom component models, a process characterized by significant complexity and time consumption. Consequently, these platforms create a bottleneck for the timely evaluation and validation of control algorithms for next-generation power electronic systems. To address this issue, this paper proposes an ADPSS-MATLAB/Simulink co-simulation method for power system EMT analysis, integrating the high-precision EMT simulation capability of ADPSS with the powerful numerical computation and modeling advantages of MATLAB/Simulink. Firstly, the core characteristics of ADPSS and MATLAB/Simulink and their interface interconnection principle are elaborated. Based on the ADPSS User-Defined Model (UDM) interface specification and combined with the MATLAB Embedded Coder code generation functionality, a complete technical workflow is established: "MATLAB/Simulink modeling - automatic code generation - ADPSS interface encapsulation - co-simulation validation". Key steps including model conversion, interface function customization, dynamic link library generation, and configuration file writing are designed in detail. Taking a Dual Active Bridge (DAB) DC-DC converter as the simulation object, employing a single phase-shift control strategy for validation, the control model is built in Simulink while the main circuit is constructed in ADPSS. EMT simulations are carried out in both a pure ADPSS environment and the ADPSS-MATLAB co-simulation environment. By comparing key simulation results, such as the transformer primary and secondary voltages, the engineering feasibility of the proposed ADPSS calling MATLAB co-simulation method is verified, providing a highly practical technical solution for the development and application of custom models in power system EMT simulations.

title: Power-Loop-Embedded Capacitor-Current Active Damping for Low-THD Islanded VSG–LCL Inverters
authors: Weide Guan, Peiling Jiang, Yang Zhou, Su Liu, Yingxi Zhu, Zhenbang Wang, Yang Chen, Zihao Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Islanded microgrids employing virtual synchronous generator (VSG) controlled inverters with LCL filters are prone to resonance-induced harmonic amplification due to the absence of grid stiffness and damping. This paper proposes a power-loop-embedded active damping strategy, where the filter capacitor current is fed back and injected at the interface between the VSG power-loop output and the voltage-control reference. The proposed injection emulates an equivalent virtual resistor in parallel with the LCL capacitor, thereby increasing the effective damping ratio and suppressing resonance peaks without sacrificing the desired VSG dynamics. A voltage–current dual-loop regulator is combined with the damping path to ensure stable voltage/frequency regulation and improved power quality in islanded operation. MATLAB/Simulink results on a 20-kW, 380-V prototype inverter with an LCL filter (L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>=200 μH, L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>=40 μH, C=4.4 μF) verify that the proposed method reduces the output-current THD from 44.14% to 11.18% under rated resistive loading and maintains robust transient behavior under 0-20 kW load steps in islanded mode.

title: Study on Broadband Power Fluctuations and Losses of DC-Link Capacitors in T-Type Three-Level Flexible Interconnection Equipment
authors: Lei Luo, Xueying Zhang, Huazhen Cao, Ran Cheng, Yao Duan, Minhua Ma
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Flexible interconnection equipment facilitates fault transfer and power smoothing while enhancing the integration of stochastic sources and loads. However, insufficient reliability, specifically DC link capacitor failure, restricts large scale application. This paper investigates T-type three-level flexible interconnection equipment, focusing on the degradation mechanism of DC-link capacitor reliability induced by harmonic currents and thermal stress. Under sine-wave pulse width modulation (SPWM), a double Fourier series expansion method is employed to develop frequency-domain models for switching functions and capacitor currents. The harmonic distribution characteristics of the current and their variation with the carrier phase time delay τ are revealed. Subsequently, a frequency-domain calculation method for capacitor losses based on an energy coefficient is proposed. Through linearization, the model is further simplified into an analytical expression directly correlated with system power. Compared to traditional methods, the proposed approach accurately quantifies the regulation effect of the carrier time delay τ on capacitor harmonic stress and establishes a simplified model linking capacitor losses to system power (P, Q). This addresses the issues of high computational burden and low accuracy inherent in conventional models, providing theoretical support for the reliability design and online operational optimization of capacitors in flexible interconnection equipment.

title: Equivalent Modeling Method for Virtual Synchronous Generator Converters Based on Hierarchical Clustering
authors: Kesheng Wang, Dongxing Zhao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources, a large number of power electronic devices are integrated into power systems, leading to a significant increase in system complexity. Fast and accurate electromagnetic transient simulation of power systems with inverter-based resources therefore becomes essential. By drawing on the coherency based equivalencing criteria developed for synchronous generators, this paper proposes a coherency identification method suitable for virtual synchronous generator converters. The proposed method adopts the similarity of converter power angle and voltage dynamic responses during fault conditions as the clustering criterion. Hierarchical clustering is applied for coherency identification, and parameters of the equivalent converters are subsequently aggregated. Finally, a multi converter system with virtual synchronous generator control is established in PowerFactory, and the dynamic responses of the equivalent model and the detailed model are compared under typical fault scenarios. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high equivalencing accuracy while significantly improving simulation efficiency.

title: A Comparative Study on Data Structures for Power Electronical Circuit Topology Derivation
authors: Botao Cao, Ke Jin, Xujun Jiang, Xingshuo Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The conventional topology derivation methods suffer from low efficiency and difficulties in exhaustively enumerating all possible topological structures. New approaches utilizing different graph-based storage structures still lack a comparative study on the applicability of various storage formats under different circuit conditions. Using the Buck converter as an example, this paper conducts a systematic comparative study of three graph-based storage structures, which include the adjacency matrix, adjacency list, and orthogonal list. Their performance is evaluated across three key dimensions: memory usage, runtime efficiency under different circuit scales, and capability to handle bidirectional components. It is indicated that each structure exhibits distinct advantages: the adjacency matrix is suitable for small-scale topology verification; the adjacency list offers balanced performance in medium-scale circuits; while the orthogonal list achieves optimal efficiency in large-scale circuits with multiple bidirectional components. This study provides a quantitative basis for selecting data structures in automated topology derivation. All findings are thoroughly validated through simulations.

title: Autoencoder Semi-Supervised Feature Selection Algorithm Based on Double Graph Discriminant Information
authors: Miao Luo, Ping Gong
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: High dimensional data are mostly characterized by high dimensions and few samples. There are a lot of irrelevant features and redundant features, which seriously affect the efficiency and generalization ability of the model. In this paper, an autoencoder semi-supervised feature selection algorithm based on double graph discriminant information is proposed. The algorithm first uses K-NS strategy to generate pseudo-labels of unlabeled data. Then the labeled data and unlabeled data are combined into a new data set to calculate the positive and negative structure information of the dataset, and the double graph discriminant information of the dataset is obtained. Finally, using the weighted Euclidean distance, the double graph discriminant information is constructed as a regularization term and added to the autoencoder feature selection process. The algorithm is tested on multiple datasets, and it is verified that the proposed algorithm can select the most representative feature subset, effectively remove irrelevant and redundant features, and improve the performance of subsequent learning tasks.

title: Quantum-Resistant Cryptography for Secure IoT Communication Systems
authors: Aakash S, Kalish S, Nivetha S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems has increased the need for communication mechanisms that can remain secure against emerging computational threats. Conventional asymmetric cryptographic schemes, including RSA and Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), are vulnerable to advances in quantum computing due to the ability of Shor's algorithm to undermine their underlying mathematical assumptions. To address these challenges, this paper presents PQ-IoT SecureCom, a quantum-resistant communication framework built using NIST-standardized post-quantum cryptographic primitives. The proposed framework employs ML-KEM (derived from Kyber) for secure key encapsulation and ML-DSA (derived from Dilithium) for digital authentication, combined with AESGCM to ensure data confidentiality and integrity. Additional security mechanisms, including PQC-based device certification, periodic session key updates, and nonce-based timestamp validation, are incorporated to mitigate replay and impersonation attacks. The framework also supports a hybrid operational mode that enables performance comparison between classical and postquantum cryptographic schemes. System behavior is monitored using a real-time dashboard that records cryptographic overhead, communication metrics, and detected security events. Experimental evaluation shows that ML-KEM and ML-DSA significantly enhance security while introducing computational overhead that remains acceptable for contemporary IoT devices. Through automated attack simulations and detailed event logging, PQ-IoT SecureCom demonstrates the practical feasibility of deploying scalable post-quantum security in IoT communication systems.

title: Sensitivity Enhancement of Quantum Radar Using Entangled Single Photon Source for Military Defense Security
authors: Simanchalo Panigrahi, Tejaswini Patro
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: Quantum radar represents a promising advancement over classical radar systems by exploiting quantum mechanical phenomena such as entanglement and quantum measurement to overcome fundamental detection limitations. This paper reviews the evolution of radar technology from conventional radar frequency systems to quantum radar architectures, with emphasis on sensitivity enhancement and detection of RF-stealth targets for military defense applications. Classical radar parameters such as radar range, transmitted power and cross section are revisited and extended to their quantum analogues. The role of entangled single photon sources in improving detection performance under high noise and jamming environments is discussed. Context of microwave quantum radar is reviewed along with photonic implementations. This paper further highlights the importance of quantum measurement theory, including system-apparatus entanglement, density matrix formalism and wave function collapse in extracting information from quantum radar systems. Although significant experimental challenges remain, recent advances indicate strong potential for future development of quantum radar in next generation defense and surveillance systems.

title: Emerging Trends of Gender Bias Within Students' Evaluation of Teaching (SET) in Higher Education: a Mini-Review and Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Efraín Abdiel López Velázquez, Asad Abbas, Bahareh Berenjforoush Azar, Soumaya Abdellatif
abstract: Reflecting the global commitment to equitable education articulated in the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals, this study investigates students' evaluation of teaching (SET) instruments widely used in higher education, which often contain many biased elements. This mini-review and bibliometric analysis aimed to identify major thematic breakthroughs in SET research, particularly regarding scientific production and collaboration, and to reveal trends in gender bias and its association with SET. To achieve these objectives, the mini-review and bibliometric analysis performed used the Scopus database, focusing on publications from 2020 to 2024. Data from seven papers were analyzed using PRISMA guidelines and Biblioshiny data visualization tools to synthesize the scholarly work. Key findings revealed a declining trend in recent scholarly publications about SET. United States of America notably leads the world in global scientific contributions in this area. Significantly, the analysis consistently highlights pervasive gender and intersectional biases that influence SET outcomes. These biases often disadvantage specific instructor demographics and tend to favor same-gender affinity among evaluators. This study provides a synthesized understanding of SET gender bias through its robust methodological framework. It offers valuable insights for promoting more equitable evaluation practices and fostering inclusive academic environments.

title: A System for Evaluating Compliance with Instructional Design Standards: A Formal Specification Approach Using Semantically-Rich Models
authors: Martin Gil-Oyarzun, Juan Felipe Calderon, Maria Elena Truyol, Monica Quezada-Espinoza
abstract: The growing demand for quality assurance in digital learning highlights limitations in traditional instructional design, especially in evaluation and adaptability. This paper presents a formal system that uses semantically-enriched JSON-LD representations to encode pedagogical elements (learning outcomes, assessments, and activities) and their alignment. The system enables automated validation against standards like Quality Matters, fostering consistency and transparency. A case study demonstrates its practical use in curriculum refinement. Key features include extensibility for complex models, integration with virtual learning environments, and compatibility with AI agents. This approach supports scalable, interoperable, and qualityassured digital ecosystems, aligned with educational innovation goals in Latin America.

title: Exploring Mentor Motivation in Participating in an International Congress on Engineering Education
authors: Alejandro Adorjan, Maite Korona, Cecilia Castro, Mariana Porta
abstract: Engineering mentoring programs promote student participation throughout their academic years, fostering the development and reinforcement of soft skills essential to their personal and professional growth. In this study, we posed the following research question (RQ): What are the primary motivations that lead engineering students, who serve as mentors, to voluntarily participate in the organisation of an International Conference on Education? The methodological approach was mixed, combining quantitative and qualitative methods. Regarding the findings, although the motivational aspects are diverse and complex, a primary initial insight concerns the role of certification and a sense of institutional belonging. Certification was one of the main factors that mentors cited as key to their participation. For future research, we plan to continue exploring extracurricular activities, further develop the survey, and identify mentoring program activities that strengthen the soft and interpersonal skills essential for future engineering professionals.

title: Study of Propellants for Muzzle Velocity Enhancement in Medium Caliber Gun Ammunition
authors: Durlav Swain, Deepak Govindraj, Nagendra Kumar, Rajagopal Chellamani Srikanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: The paper is focused on the enhancement of muzzle velocity for small and medium caliber gun ammunitions, typically those used by Army Air Defence, with an aim to cater for present day highly agile and small air targets such as Drones and Unmanned Aerial Systems. The paper covers the design aspects of lab feasible and institutionally permissible, gun barrel along with the requisite sub parts and accessories which could facilitate testing of gun propellant performance, in terms of chamber pressure generation and muzzle velocity achieved. The paper includes experimental test results on five types gun propellants with different composition primarily Nitrogen rich materials, which channelised the way forward for further research work.

title: Low-Cost Concrete Manipulatives for Multivariable Calculus: A Classroom Pre-Post Study on Selfefficacy and 3D Reasoning
authors: Juan Felipe Carmona, Yiby Morales, Jose M. Nieto-Jalil
abstract: This classroom-embedded study investigates whether low-cost, paper/cardboard manipulatives can strengthen students' perceived competence on core three-dimensional topics in Multivariable Calculus. We implemented a foldable, L-shaped modeling base with tangible surface pieces to support hands-on exploration of surfaces, accumulation for multiple integrals, and coordinate transformations, coupled with a standardized lesson sequence (conceptual activation, guided construction and exploration, representation bridge, exit ticket, and reuse). Four course sections (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> students) used the materials; inferential analyses focus on one representative section with anonymous pre (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{n}=29$</tex>) and post (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{n}=26$</tex>) surveys. Two self-efficacy items targeted translations of planar curves and the representation of 2 D equations as 3 D surfaces. The share selecting the top two confidence categories increased from 0.707 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.923(\Delta=0.216$</tex>; Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$h=0.58$</tex>; two-sided <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.0040$</tex>; 95 % CI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$[0.078,0.354]$</tex>; normalized gain <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{g}=0.74$</tex>). Item-level contrasts were 69.0 % to 88.5 % for translations <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(z=1.75,\ \mathrm{p}=0.0805$</tex>) and 72.4 % to 96.2 % for 2D to 3D representation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{z}=2.38, \mathrm{p}=0.0175)$</tex>. Results suggest that haptic-visual coupling, when explicitly bridged to sketches and symbolic expressions, is a scalable way to enhance students' readiness to engage surfaces, solids, and coordinate changes. Limitations include self-report outcomes, unpaired samples, and a single-site context.

title: Robot Teaching Assistant for Answering Student Questions in Higher Education: A Pilot Study
authors: Fuad Budagov, Mohammad Tariq Meeran, Tarmo Robal, Ali Atakan Basaran
abstract: With the increasing scarcity of teaching staff and their unavailability to provide personalized approaches, eyes are on technology. Could semi-autonomous robots, as teaching assistants, take over some of the burden from the shoulders of teachers? In this paper, we present a pilot study on the use of a robot as a teaching assistant in a higher education laboratory setting to support students' learning path by allowing them to accumulate knowledge through question-answering interaction with a robot teaching assistant. This robot teaching assistant is a companion of a teacher to provide support in teaching large groups of students, where a single lecturer may struggle to respond to all student inquiries within the allotted time. The robot teaching assistant was pre-trained with course-specific material, enabling it to answer topic-related questions directly. The findings from the pilot study highlight the potential of robot assistants to provide accessible, on-demand academic support and to complement teaching in practical learning environments. This study contributes to the growing body of research on robotassisted education and demonstrates the applicability of robots as teaching assistants in higher education.

title: Faster Estimation of Hilbert Transform for Photoacoustic and Ultrasound Biomicroscopy Applications
authors: Raymundo Cordero, João Carlos Machado, Krishynan Shanty Fernandes, Isabel Nunes Ramalho Da Rocha, Juliana Oliveira Da Silva Fernandes
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Nowadays, Ultrasound Biomicroscopy (UBM) and Photoacoustic (PA) are powerful approaches for the development of high-resolution, non-invasive biomedical imaging systems. Both UBM and PA produce radiofrequency (RF) signals whose envelopes are used to reconstruct the tissue images. These envelopes can be obtained through Hilbert transform. Usually, the imaginary part of the analytic signal related to the RF signal is used to efficiently get the Hilbert transform. However, as many tissue samples are analyzed in UBM and PA, faster digital processing methods, including a faster envelope detection method, are required to implement imaging systems. This paper proposes a simplified method to calculate the Hilbert transform based on data splitting and the properties of Fast Fourier transform (FFT). The splitting data strategy used in FFT is applied to reduce the number of arithmetic operations used to get the imaginary part of the analytic signal. Thus, different from other methods such as filtering or convolution, the proposed approach is mathematically equivalent to the method to get the Hilbert transform through the analytic signal. Tests prove that the proposed approach allows a faster estimation of the Hilbert transform for PA and UBM applications.

title: AI-Powered Predictive Analytics for Construction Delay Mitigation Using Real-Time IoT and BIM Integration
authors: Siddhartha Erelli, Saiprudhvi Donthula
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Construction assignments frequently face notable delays due to unforeseen risks, resource mismanagement, and wasteful scheduling. This study proposes an AI-powered predictive analytics framework that combines Building Information Modeling (BIM) and Internet of Things (IoT) data to enhance undertaking planning and execution. By leveraging machine learning algorithms, real-time sensor data, and historical undertaking records, the model aims to predict potential delays and suggest proactive reduction plans. The study examines the effectiveness of AI-driven decision-making in enhancing undertaking timelines, cost productivity, and risk management. A case study approach is utilized to confirm the framework, demonstrating how predictive analytics can optimize construction workflow and stakeholder collaboration. The proposed methodology provides a scalable solution for minimizing delays and maximizing productivity in large-scale construction assignments, marking a notable advancement in data-driven construction management.

title: Exploratory SVM Modeling of Facial Angular Asymmetry Using the Triglyceride-Glucose Index
authors: Jesús Fausto Córdova-Manzo, Gabriela Flores-Mondragón, Ángel Eduardo Llanas-Soto, Jana Liseth Tovilla-Trejo, Jairo Rahé López-Sánchez, Ángel Adrián Pascacio-Glaizar, Arturo Vera-Hernández, Josefina Gutiérrez-Martínez
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Peripheral facial palsy (PFP) exhibits heterogeneous motor impairment that may be influenced by systemic metabolic status. This study evaluates the joint contribution of total facial angular asymmetry (AsAnT) and the triglyceride–glucose (TyG) index to distinguish PFP patients from controls. A dataset of 120 subjects was analyzed using statistical testing, decision trees, and Support Vector Machines (SVM). PFP patients showed significantly higher AsAnT and TyG values (p < 0.001), with a weak but significant association between both variables. SVM classifiers achieved stable cross-validated accuracy (~72%) across feature representations. These findings indicate a weak but statistically significant association between facial angular asymmetry and metabolic status, supporting the feasibility of exploratory, hypothesis-generating modeling rather than causal inference.

title: BI-RADS Classification of Breast Tumors in Ultrasound Using Convolutional Autoencoder-Based Feature Extractor
authors: W. Gómez-Flores, W. C. A. Pereira
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: A convolutional autoencoder (CAE)-based feature extractor is presented for the classification of breast tumors in ultrasound (BUS) images into BI-RADS categories. The model uses a convolutional neural network (CNN) for BI-RADS classification, taking encoder feature maps at multiple abstraction levels as input. Both CAE and CNN parameters are learned simultaneously via a loss function that combines mean squared error (focused on minimizing image reconstruction error) and categorical cross-entropy (focused on minimizing BI-RADS classification error). The experiments use a dataset consisting of 1875 BUS images, with tumors annotated according to BI-RADS categories 2-5. The input BUS images are masked to enhance the shape of the tumoral region. The results indicate that this approach outperforms four pre-trained CNNs and two CAE-based methods in BI-RADS classification, achieving a global accuracy of 0.68. When BI-RADS predictions are dichotomized into benign (categories 2 and 3) and malignant (categories 4 and 5) classes, the approach reached a sensitivity of 0.90 and an AUC of 0.92. The improvement in classification performance is attributed to the model’s ability to incorporate features of varying complexity, from general features in the initial layers to more subtle features in the latent space. This characteristic differentiates this method from traditional CAE-based classification techniques, which typically rely solely on features from the latent space.

title: Challenges in Generalizing Automated Breast Density Classification Across Mammography Datasets
authors: Rodrigo Leite Prates, Wilfrido Gómez-Flores, Wagner C. A. Pereira
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Breast tissue density is associated with increased breast cancer risk and reduced lesion visibility in mammography, motivating the development of automated density classification methods. However, generalization across heterogeneous datasets remains a major challenge. This study investigates factors that limit the transferability of automated breast density classification models across public digitized mammography datasets. We systematically evaluate preprocessing strategies and four convolutional neural network architectures (VGG16, ResNet50, InceptionV3, and EfficientNetV2-L) under both within-dataset and cross-dataset settings, including contrast normalization, artifact handling, pectoral muscle removal, and view-specific training strategies. In particular, we analyze single-view training, in which models are trained on a single mammographic view and tested across all available views to assess cross-view generalization. Experiments conducted on two public digitized mammography datasets show that VGG16 and ResNet50 consistently achieve the most stable and competitive performance across datasets. In within-dataset evaluations, the best models achieve per-class F1-scores above 0.80, while cross-dataset performance degrades substantially, with F1-scores typically ranging from 0.50 to 0.70. Overall, the results highlight the impact of dataset shift on breast density classification and provide practical recommendations for building robust, reproducible systems suitable for deployment across heterogeneous clinical environments.
