title: Wavelet-Based Bayesian Random Forest Classifier for High-Voltage Insulator Pollution Severity Detection: A Hybrid Approach
authors: Zakaria Trachi, Abdelouahab Mekhaldi, Abderrahmane Beroual
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The reliability of high-voltage insulators in power transmission systems is often compromised by pollution-induced flashovers, especially in coastal and industrial regions. Traditional methods for assessing contamination levels are labor-intensive and prone to inaccuracies, requiring automated solutions. This study proposes a Wavelet-Based Bayesian-Optimized Random Forest Classification (WB-BRFC) method for classifying pollution severity on high-voltage insulators. The approach integrates wavelet decomposition for feature extraction with Bayesian-optimized random forest classifiers to enhance accuracy. Wavelet transforms enable multi-resolution analysis of leakage current signals, isolating transient and steady-state features indicative of contamination. Bayesian optimization systematically tunes hyper-parameters to maximize classification performance. Experimental validation using real-world data evidences that WB-BRFC achieves superior accuracy (0.9825), precision (0.9821), recall (0.9880), and F1-score (0.9812) compared to conventional methods including SVM (Support Vector Machines), Fuzzy Granular, and wavelet-based 1D-CNN. This hybrid approach leverages wavelet-based multi-resolution feature extraction, Bayesian hyper-parameter optimization, and ensemble classification, enabling unprecedented robustness for high-voltage insulator pollution assessment.

title: Semantic Region-Guided Transferable Attacks on Vision-Language Pretraining Models_supp1-3701225.pdf
authors: Chen Wan
abstract: Vision-language pre-trained (VLP) models have achieved strong performance on multimodal tasks, but they remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks. In black-box settings, transferability is often limited because perturbation generation relies on surrogate-specific saliency cues that generalize poorly across VLP architectures. In this letter, we propose Semantic Region-Guided Attack (SRGA), a transferable attack framework that replaces such cues with detector-derived semantic regions to provide more consistent cross-model guidance. Based on these regions, SRGA performs coordinated perturbation generation in both image and text modalities with lightweight regional transformations and spatially weighted optimization. Experiments on Flickr30 K and MSCOCO show that SRGA achieves competitive black-box transferability across diverse VLP architectures under multiple settings, with modest additional cost.

title: Okra-Stem Disease Classification in Spectral Image Using Ensemble Based Deep Learning Framework
authors: I. Vasudevan, G Mohan Vamsi, V Hema Sarayu, R. M. Dilip Charaan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Okra Stem Disease Classification is playing as major role in image processing on now days. The deep learning method is used to identify fungal diseases that attack okra crops in tropical environments. Antiquated methods of perceiving things are slow and may not be effective. In this system, ensemble based deep learning frame work that incorporates most varieties of neural networks has been used to detect diseases in okra stems. Received 2,847 labeled stem images of 15 farms in South India. The proposed model was more stable due to preprocessing such as noise removal, contrast enhancement, and data augmentation. The ensemble system was far superior to individual CNN models that scored only 98.8% on the days that they were correct. Findings indicate that precision farming can be applied in the real world where the diseases can be identified at an initial stage.

title: Precision Agriculture Using Deep Learning for Sugarcane Disease Identification
authors: P.T.Jamuna Devi, B. Jayanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Sugarcane is a globally significant crop that contributes substantially to sugar production and bioenergy industries. However, its productivity is frequently threatened by diseases such as red rot, mosaic, smut, and leaf scald. Traditional disease identification methods rely on manual inspection, which is labor-intensive, subjective, and difficult to scale across large agricultural fields. In recent years, deep learning techniques have emerged as powerful tools for automated plant disease detection. This paper presents a structured analytical review of deep learning-based approaches for sugarcane disease classification. Rather than proposing a new experimental framework, the study systematically synthesizes previously reported architectures, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), transfer learning models, and ensemble techniques. Architectural trends, performance behaviors, deployment suitability, and methodological limitations reported across studies are critically examined. The review identifies recurring challenges such as dataset imbalance, lack of benchmark standardization, limited cross-regional validation, and constraints in real-world deployment. By consolidating fragmented research findings into a coherent analytical framework, this study provides practical insights and future research directions for developing scalable, interpretable, and field-ready disease detection systems within precision agriculture ecosystems.

title: AI for Digital Forensics and Cyber Threat Intelligence
authors: Vijay Anil Patil, Diana George, Soumyakant Dash, Thamaraimanalan T, Moghal Yaseen Pasha, Yogapriya J
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Traditional detection methods cannot cope with the increased sophistication and volume of cyber threats. This calls for intelligent and adaptive cybersecurity solutions. To bridge this gap, this paper explores integrating an AI-based framework that can support malware detection, threat profiling, and forensic analysis by performing a mix of deep learning, anomaly detection, and natural language processing. The driving goal will be to increase involuntary threat identification accuracy, as well as reduction in false positives, while attracting a near real-time analysis on cyber incidents. In building this study proposed method, this work used CNNs and an RNN for malicious code detection, Isolation Forest for anomaly detection, and LDA and NER for the extraction of threat intelligence from unstructured data. The results presented pointed out that the CNN achieved a detection rate of 98.50%, while the Isolation Forest showed a detection rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 8 0 \%}$</tex> for unusual behaviors, but the classic models of SVM and Logistic Regression included in the experiment underperformed by a great margin. A systematic combination of these frameworks and techniques illustrates the improved performance in detection and depth of analysis. Finally, this study research proposes a scalable, intelligent cybersecurity solution that may have the potential to successfully enhance this study digital forensic processes and proactive threat intelligence.

title: Smart Waste: AI-Powered Community Waste Management System
authors: Kavitha Subramani, L Jabasheela, Somu CS, Cinthuja K, Akshayalakshmi Sv, Deepika M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Waste management has become a major issue in fast- growing urban areas. Increasing populations and changing consumption habits create large amounts of waste. Traditional disposal methods often lead to environmental pollution, health risks, and wasted resources. To tackle these issues, we need smart systems that combine precision with public involvement. To meet this demand, we have developed a Smart Waste Management System using Artificial Intelligence. This system uses a Convolutional Neural Network model to accurately classify waste images. This reduces sorting mistakes and allows for proper recycling and disposal categorization. In addition to classification, the platform features a user-friendly dashboard that aims to boost public knowledge and participation. Key features include daily ecofriendly tips, challenges to encourage sustainable habits, a tracker for monitoring waste separation, and a reporting tool for speedy resolution of waste- related problems. These elements create a complete system that not only improves waste classification efficiency but also fosters behavioral change and environmental responsibility. By cutting down on manual work, encouraging recycling, and enhancing community engagement, the system helps create cleaner urban areas, supports sustainable growth, and contributes to global efforts to cut waste and fight climate change.

title: Automotive Intelligence: AI for Safe, Secure, and Personalized Driving
authors: Chandni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The increasing integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the automotive industry is revolutionizing vehicle security, safety, and personalization. Driver safety and vehicle security are always the major concerns of the automotive industry. With the rapid advancement of science and technology, numerous methods have been developed to enhance vehicle security and safety, evolving from traditional measures such as key locks, seat belts, and alarms to modern solutions like fingerprint authentication and airbags. However, these advancements have yet to fully address the challenges posed by driver behaviour and misconduct, including distractions caused by eating, drinking, and mobile phone usage, which significantly increase the risk of accidents. Implementation of AI-driven driving solutions has potential to mitigate these issues. This paper investigates how AI techniques support driver safety and vehicle security by highlighting important approaches. The comparative analysis of various studies focused on driver's behaviour analysis, personalization and assistance, and vehicle security is presented. Further, the paper also explores various challenges faced, including data privacy and deployment limitations. Finally, it outlines the future scope of work, emphasizing the real-world implementation of automotive intelligence.

title: Decoding Hormonal Havoc: AI-Driven Lifestyle and Nutrition Guidance for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
authors: Ghousiya Begum K, Valarmathi R, Angelin Pricilla M, Harini R, Mayuri V S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is a complex endocrine metabolic hormonal disorder affecting women. The reproductive years of women often gets affected leading to infertility, insulin resistance, elevated cardio vascular risk, psychological disorders and etc., The AI-driven diagnostic framework can accurately predict PCOS and recommend a personalized dietary plan. A diverse set of ensemble models are implemented here, namely Random Forest (RF), AdaBoost (ADA), XGBoost (XGB), Gradient Boosting Machine (GBM), CatBoost (CB), Extra Trees (ET), along with Multi-Layer Percepton (MLP), Decision Trees (DT), Logistic Regression (LR), Support Vector Machines (SVM), Gaussian Naive Bayes (GNB), Bernoulli Naive Bayes (BNB), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Linear discriminant analysis (LDA), Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA), Voting Classifier (VC), and Stacking Classifier (SC). Data pre-processing strategies were employed to enhance the model's performance. The proposed Stacking Classifier (SC) achieved an accuracy of 91.74 %, precision of 93.75 %, recall of 80.56 %, F 1 -score of 86.6 %, and ROC-AUC score of 94.4 %, outperforming all other models. In the diagnostic model, a Graphical User Interface (GUI) was developed for real-time prediction that provides nutritional recommendations based on an individual's health parameters. By combining clinical records with AI, this study advances automated diagnosis of PCOS, potentially leading to earlier diagnosis, improved patient management, and tailored treatment. The proposed framework represents a meaningful step towards digital healthcare innovation and an accurate clinical decision-making tool for improving women's health.

title: Intelligent Terminal Test Report Automation and LLM-based Intelligent Root Cause Analysis Solution
authors: Zhurong Wu, Jiatai Wu, Hao Meng
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of low automation in report generation, reliance on manual experience for analyzing massive test results, and difficulties in product issue localization in intelligent terminal software testing, this paper proposes a test report auto-generation and PDF export framework based on LibreOffice UNO SDK, and introduces a large language model (LLM) to achieve intelligent root cause analysis of massive test data. Through UNO API, dynamic filling and formatting of test data and PDF document output are realized. LLM is utilized for cluster analysis and anomaly attribution of multi-dimensional test data, automatically identifying the root causes of product issues.

title: Influence of AI-Powered Chatbot on Digital Native's Consumer Purchase Intention
authors: Aditya Kumar Gupta, Vikrant Vikram Singh, Prashant Dev Yadav, Anushka Dhama, Sharad Khattar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The paper investigates the influence of AI-powered chatbots' factors, the visual interactivity, recommendation reliability, personalized interactions, and the response’s reliability on digital native consumers' purchase intentions on online platforms. PLS-SEM analysis used for data analysis from respondents had predicted that personalized interactions significantly influence the purchase intentions (β = 0.663, p = 0.001), which aligns with the anthropomorphism and the cognitive efficiency theory. In contrast to this, the visual interactivity (β = 0.209, p = 0.276), the recommendation reliability (β = 0.081, p = 0.515), and the response appearance (β = -0.049, p = 0.788) were not significant, which explains their role as more hygiene factors rather than the active drivers. The results of the study highlight the role of personalization in reducing fatigue in decision-making and fostering emotional engagement, particularly among digitally native consumers. This study goes into refining the implications of human-AI interactions in e-commerce and provides practical insights for optimization in chatbot designs.

title: Experimental Study on Negative Corona Characteristics in a Needle-to-Plate Gap under Unipolar Positive Voltage and Negative-to-Positive Polarity Reversal Conditions
authors: Yiyan Zhang, Li Zhang, Shiwei Du, Ikromjon Usmonovich Rakhmonov, Kurbonov Nurbek Nurullo Ugli
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To investigate the response of corona discharge at a needle tip under rapidly varying thunderstorm-like electric fields, a needle-to-plate test platform capable of polarity reversal was developed. Unipolar positive DC voltage and negative-to-positive polarity-reversal voltage were applied to the upper plate, and the induced current on the grounding line of the needle tip mounted on the lower plate was measured and analyzed. The results show that, under unipolar positive voltage, the negative corona current at the needle tip exhibits a clear stage-like evolution as the voltage increases. No identifiable corona pulse is detected at +10 kV. At +15 kV, a few isolated pulses begin to appear. At +20 kV, the discharge develops into a relatively stable pulse train. At +25 kV, the pulse frequency rises significantly and local pulse overlap becomes evident. At +30 kV, the continuous component is enhanced and the waveform gradually evolves toward a quasi-continuous negative glow state. Based on the absence of obvious pulses under the unipolar +10 kV condition, a polarity-reversal test from -10 kV to +10 kV was further carried out. The results indicate that distinct transient pulse peaks appear after reversal, demonstrating that polarity reversal can promote earlier onset of negative corona under low-voltage, near-threshold conditions. This phenomenon is mainly attributed to local electric-field distortion at the instant of reversal and the memory effect of residual space charge left from the preceding polarity stage. The results suggest that when the thunderstorm background electric field changes rapidly or undergoes polarity reversal, the discharge risk of sharp tips cannot be assessed solely on the basis of the steady-state corona inception threshold under unipolar excitation. The present work provides an experimental basis for understanding corona mechanisms at grounded sharp conductors and for evaluating discharge risk under thunderstorm conditions.

title: Evaluating the Impact of the Mid-Day Meal Scheme on School Enrollment Rates in Government Schools, Hazaribagh, Jharkhand Using Machine Learning
authors: Anshu Kumari, Abhishek Kumar, Ravi Kumar Burman, Anjana Mishra, Amanpreet Kaur, Satveer Kour
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: One of the objectives of the study was to apply machine learning techniques and block-level data between the years 2020 and 2025 (time at which this analysis was done) to determine the impact of the Mid-Day Meal Scheme on Hazaribag District government school enrolment. The research showed that free and healthy lunch made a positive impact on both attendance and enrolment of children in their respective families who were economically and socially disadvantaged. Exploratory data analysis results showed that although all areas of the study experienced larger increases in school enrolment as a result of the MDM programme, some areas showed large increases compared to others; whereas Bishnugarh, Chauparan, and Barkatha blocks registered large increases in school enrolment as a result of MDM programme, similar increases were not witnessed in Daru and Tatijhariya blocks. Machine learning prediction scores show the Random Forest Classifier with a high probability of predicting the growth of enrolment (92 %), whereas the Linear Regression predicts the percentage growth with a medium probability (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R} 2=0.27$</tex>). These are the most critical factors that affect enrollment as indicated by an analysis of feature importance, with a total attendance and retention rate being the essential factors, but the temporal elements such as the year have minimal impact. The findings indicate that there is a need to implement targeted interventions in order to ensure that access to education is equal in all blocks and emphasize that the most significant concerns when it comes to the success of the MDM scheme are not the meals, but the daily attendance and the reduction of the number of dropouts.

title: Text Classification for Cultural Heritage Corpora: A Review of Methods, Datasets, and Benchmarks
authors: Hasan Abdulla
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustaining Heritage: The Future of Conservation: Embracing Technology and Innovation for Sustainable Heritage (ICSH)
abstract: Text classification has become a central enabler for discovery, curation, and access within cultural-heritage institutions. Over the last decade (2015-2025), the field has seen a rapid transition from sparse linear models to transformer-based encoders and large language models, together with increased attention to multi-label and hierarchical taxonomies grounded in heritage ontologies such as the CIDOC Conceptual Reference Model and the Getty Art & Architecture Thesaurus. This paper presents a structured review of methods, datasets, and evaluation practices for text classification in cultural heritage corpora, with a focus on Arabic and multilingual settings, and provides practical guidance for deployment under noisy Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and Handwritten Text Recognition (HTR) conditions. Adhering to a PRISMA-2020-aligned protocol, the review synthesizes model families (classical, neural, transformer, layout-aware, and generative), training paradigms (fine-tuning, zero- or few-shot prompting, weak and active learning), and evaluation norms (including hierarchical and multi-label metrics). The analysis identifies consistent performance benefits from domain- and language-adapted encoders, ontology-aware decoding, calibration for multi-label decisions, and human-in-the-loop annotation. Gaps remain in shared Arabic-centric benchmarks, hierarchical metric standardization, and transparent reporting of energy and governance costs. A practical implementation playbook is offered for institutions with varying resource constraints, alongside a research agenda aligned with sustainable and inclusive heritage stewardship.

title: Ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) In Healthcare Applications Using Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Gulshan Dhasmana, Narender Chinthamu, Arti Badhoutiya, A. Kakoli Rao, P. William, Rajeev Sobti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper explores the pivotal role of Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) in enhancing Quality of Service (QoS) in healthcare applications, highlighting their transformative impact on patient monitoring and data-driven healthcare services. As healthcare environments increasingly rely on real-time data, ensuring robust QoS becomes essential. The integration of WSNs facilitates continuous health monitoring, supports elderly care through fall detection, and enables remote healthcare delivery via telemedicine. However, achieving reliable QoS in healthcare presents unique challenges due to the critical nature of medical data and the need for low-latency, high-accuracy, and energy-efficient data transmission.This study delves into various QoS mechanisms and protocols tailored for healthcare scenarios, assessing their effectiveness and limitations. Furthermore, it examines the potential of emerging technologies like edge computing and machine learning to advance QoS. By addressing these challenges, the paper aims to foster improved patient outcomes and streamline healthcare processes, marking a significant stride towards a responsive, efficient, and patient-centric healthcare system.

title: Global Patent Innovation Analysis of Wearable Exoskeleton Robot
authors: Qun Fang
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: This article shows the technical innovation trends and the market mapping of wearable exoskeleton robot by analyzing the patent data worldwide. This analysis includes industrial wearable exoskeleton robot, medical wearable exoskeleton robot, human-robot interaction technology includes EEG/EMG interface and EEG/EMG motion intention recognition. This study systematically elaborates on the specific subdivided patent technical fields of wearable exoskeleton technology, considering applicants in different time periods and geographical regions. Through this analysis, enterprises and R&D institutions in the related field can recognize technical cutting-edge trends, identify vacant niches in business development within different countries/regions and avoid infringement risk.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Approach for Real-Time Fault and Energy Theft Detection in Smart Energy Meters
authors: Vanjarapu Durgaprasad, Golagani Lavanya Devi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This study proposes hybrid solution that combines supervised learning classifiers, anomaly detection and signal processing feature extraction in order to determine both faults in electric meters and whether they are being tampered with. We are able to come to a conclusion using a generator-based approach, ensemble includes boosted (loss based) decision trees and non-supervised clustering techniques to decrease false-positives from a non-compliant device and obtain low latency performance appropriate for deployment at a site. The solution exploits streamed meter data and engineered features in both the time and frequency domains and uses adaptive threshold systems derived from previous load profiles. Testing against both simulated and actual datasets showed a very high degree of accuracy in detecting the fault event, low (how quickly) latency in finding when the meter fault occurred and resilient to adversarial attacks. Improvements compared to baseline detection methods demonstrate very large increases in both detection and fault isolation rates. The design was created so that there is room to grow, maintain privacy of the meters' users and comply with smart grid communication standards making it possible for utility firms to detect integrity issues and take action to minimize customer disruption and costs.

title: Digital Transformation in Healthcare: Integrating Clinical Research with Data Management Technologies
authors: Manda Ukey, Shashidar Reddy Abbidi, Teja Krishna Kota, Trupti Raikar, Mithun Mallepati, Pendyala John Adinarayana
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Clinical research and innovative data management technologies are converging, which is a paradigm shift in the contemporary delivery of healthcare services. This study uses artificial intelligence, machine learning and cloud computing to simplify the process of collecting patient data, improve the accuracy of research and speed up the process of making therapeutic discoveries. The collaboration between the researchers, clinicians, and data scientists can be facilitated through electronic health records, real-time analytics, and interoperable systems. This is possible by utilizing blockchain to guarantee the safety of data, natural language processing to extract unstructured data, and predictive analytics to anticipate patient outcomes in order to streamline the clinical trial process and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements in healthcare organizations. Natural Language Generation through the use of AI (Anthropic) and transformer-based GPT-4 architecture is used in this study to analyze and synthesize comprehensive patterns of digital healthcare transformation in detail, showing how advanced AI tools are used to provide evidence-based data on technology integration models.

title: IoT-Based Automated Road Defect Detection for Road Maintenance
authors: Shashwat Chouhan, Yash Rajpoot, Vaibhav Tripathi, Simran Dobal, Neha Garg, Amit Garg
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Due to the rapid growth of the transportation infrastructure, road safety and maintenance have become a critical concern. There are many conventional inspection methods that include vehicle-based survey, tape and measuring tools and visual inspections. These methods are labourintensive, time- consuming and sometimes unreliable too. To counter these challenges, a system is proposed in the paper that focuses on the road damage detection with the help of a camera and gyroscope-based inertial measurement unit. The proposed system captures a real time real image while it keeps recording the vibrations in the motion data. ESP 32 micro controller combines these signals including vibrations and visuals showing the potholes, cracks and the surface irregularities available in the road. Global Positioning System, (GPS) enables geo tagging of the damage locations that helps to efficiently map and reporting them. The proposed system offers a low cost, scalable and an automated method for better and reliable road condition monitoring. Experimental results shows that the prototype can detect the potholes in the road irregularities in real time. The combination of You Only Look Once (YOLO) based image detection and gyroscope based vibration analysis improve the detection reliability. Under optical conditions the proposed system has achieved an accuracy of about 97 %, indicating its potential for low-cost automated road monitoring.

authors: Mattheus França, Fabiane Benitti

title: Automatic Mapping Of Vegetated Areas In Benevento (Italy) From Sentinel-2 Images
authors: Francesca Guastaferro, Claudio Parente, Andrea Vallario, Vincenzo Verde
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: Mapping vegetated areas provides essential data for multiple purposes, from sustainable management of natural resources to precision agriculture, from urban planning to environmental protection. Indeed, the precise location, perimeter, and consistency of vegetated areas are crucial for monitoring plant health, optimizing water and fertilizer use, identifying at-risk areas, preventing health problems, and assessing the impact of climate change, contributing to biodiversity conservation and ecosystem resilience. Multispectral remote sensing images appropriately processed using algorithms capable of emphasizing the presence of living organisms where chlorophyll synthesis is occurring, and others capable of classifying "green areas" as such, provide valuable support for mapping vegetated areas. This process is typically assisted by an operator who provides the machine with statistical data to support the process of recognizing and classifying vegetated areas. This article aims to demonstrate that the process of mapping green areas can be completely automated and eliminate training phases. The study area is Benevento city and surrounding territory in the Campania Region (Italy), particularly rich in natural vegetation, cultivated fields, and urban parks. Sentinel-2 imagery at a resolution of 10 m is used for this purpose: the construction of the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) allows us to emphasize the presence of green areas, and the unsupervised K-means classifier provides automatic pixel recognition, distinguishing vegetation from non-vegetation. The use of test sites for the two classes involved, the construction of the confusion matrix, and the calculation of the accuracy parameters—producer accuracy (PA), user accuracy (UA), and overall accuracy (OA)—highlight the excellent level of the results achieved. For the entire study area, an OA value of 0.989 is recorded.

title: A Two-Stage U-Net-CNN Model for Ocean Sound Speed Profile Inversion
authors: Ziyue Li, Yuhong Liu, Wei Huang, Hao Zhang, Fang Ji, Qian Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: The uneven distribution of ocean sound speed is a key factor degrading the performance of underwater positioning, navigation, timing and communication (PNTC) systems, while traditional instrumental measurements of sound speed profiles (SSPs) fail to meet real-time application demands. Existing data-driven SSP inversion methods suffer from limitations such as high requirements for historical data continuity and low sensitivity to deep-sea sound speed changes, and acoustic field measurement errors further impair the inversion accuracy of artificial neural networks. To address these issues, we propose a two-stage U-net convolutional neural network (U-Net-CNN) for SSP inversion. We first use the compression reconstruction function of U-net to denoise the noisy measurement data of the signal propagation time, extracting hidden features with stronger anti-interference ability, and then we adopt a CNN to map the relationship from the hidden features to SSPs. Experimental results show that U-Net-CNN outperforms the standalone CNN, Compressed Sensing (CS), Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) and Empirical Orthogonal Function (EOF) methods by a significant margin.

title: Survey and Roadmap for Efficiency-Accuracy Cooptimization Towards Embodied AI Chip Design
authors: Jianwei Zhao, Hongnian Yu, Yun Li
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: The embodied intelligence chip is the “brain” of the embodied agent, which enables the agent to autonomously make decisions and perform actions in complex and dynamic real physical environments. Conventional chip design optimization methodologies are predominantly based on static solutions and manual strategies. These methods face the “curse of dimensionality” and time-varying challenges such as dynamic loads and thermodynamic constraints. This article reviews related research in three aspects: dynamic-aware chip architectures, intelligent optimization strategy design, and LLM-driven automated design. For prospects, the “AI codesign paradigm” is constructed, in which <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> is not only specialized in design, but also in requirement analysis, multiscale modeling, knowledge transfer, strategy enhancement, and verification, to realize autonomous design optimization. Jointly, outlook is drawn with respect to constructing MCU+NPU heterogeneous computing architectures, SSM world model collaboration mechanisms, and integrated sensing-computingcontrol systems.

title: Active Visual Alignment for Robust Digital Fingerprinting and Global Pairing of Texture-Rich Agricultural Collectibles
authors: Haotong Guo
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: The automated management and identity verification of high-value organic artifacts, specifically antique walnuts, face a critical challenge: ensuring identity persistence without reliable external tagging. Due to the unique, non-reproducible texturing of these biological objects, they possess the potential for "biometric" identification. However, morphological heterogeneity and sensitivity to lighting conditions often render traditional computer vision fingerprints unstable. This paper proposes a cyber-physical system designed to establish a stable "Physically-Locked Digital Anchor" for irregular objects. We introduce an Active Visual Alignment (AVA) framework that employs a closed-loop visual servoing strategy (MAE = 0.49°) to physically normalize the object’s pose. Unlike passive software augmentation, this physical standardization guarantees photometric consistency, ensuring that the captured shadow structures—the core of the digital identity—remain constant. Furthermore, to maintain database stability during batch recovery, we reformulate the pairing task as a global optimization problem solved via the Hungarian Algorithm. Experimental results on a hierarchical dataset containing 14,400 regression samples and 4,308 matching samples demonstrate that our system achieves 100% reconstruction accuracy in standard environments. Even under simulated extreme stress tests, the global optimization strategy maintains a 54.6% recovery rate, significantly outperforming single-view baselines (32.4%), thus validating its efficacy for high-precision inventory management.

title: Hybrid CNN-LSTM Architecture for Cybersecurity Threat Detection in Network Traffic
authors: Akshaj Saini, Tanmay Kumar Jaiswal, Parteek, Ayush Thakur, Aditya Rai, Shailja Saini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The increasing rate of growth of interconnected digital systems, cloud platforms, and Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructures has resulted in a significant rise in the complexity and rate of cyberattacks, and the conventional signature-based intrusion detection systems are no longer sufficient to counter these challenges. In order to overcome these challenges, this study presents a novel deep learning-based cybersecurity threat detection system with high accuracy and efficiency in detecting malicious network activities. In this regard, the proposed system is based on a hybrid convolutional neural network (CNN) and long short-term memory (LSTM) architecture for automatic feature extraction from network traffic data, where the CNN model is employed for detecting complex patterns in network traffic feature data and the LSTM model is utilized for detecting temporal dependencies associated with multi-stage cyberattacks. The proposed system is tested using a standard intrusion detection dataset, and the results show that the proposed model has achieved an accuracy of 98.72 %, precision of 98.45 %, recall of 98.81 %, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 98.63 % with a false alarm rate of 1.18 %.Additionally, the model maintains an average inference time of 2.3 ms per network flow, which is crucial in providing real-time threat detection in contemporary network environments. The above results demonstrate the effectiveness of deep learning methods in enhancing the reliability and responsiveness of cybersecurity defense mechanisms.

title: Dynamic Characteristics of a High-Frequency Vibration-Impact Rock-Breaking Device
authors: Weidong Wu, Lantao Zhu, Guojun Cao, Wentao Cui
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To meet the engineering demands for efficient excavation and stable structural operation in rock roadway driving and mining, this paper investigates the dynamic characteristics of a vibration-impact rock-breaking device through dynamic modeling, frequency-domain analysis, numerical simulation, and experimental validation. A three-degree-of-freedom dynamic model of the device is established, and the system transfer function in the frequency domain is derived using the Laplace transform to analyze the amplitude frequency response, phase-frequency response, and resonance characteristics. On this basis, a fully coupled frequency-domain sensitivity model is developed using matrix differential calculus and the adjoint variable method to reveal the effects of mass, stiffness, and damping parameters on the dynamic response of the system. RecurDyn multibody dynamic simulation and vibration response experiments are further conducted to validate the theoretical results. The results show that the first three natural frequencies of the system are 24.8 Hz, 91.0 Hz, and 120.2 Hz, respectively. The first-order mode dominates the low-frequency dynamic response, and the vibration box exhibits significant response amplification near 24.8 Hz. Experimental results indicate that the vibration response of the device is enhanced near 24 Hz, with a displacement amplitude of approximately 0.036 m. Both the frequency error and amplitude error between the theoretical and experimental results are less than 8%. Parameter analysis further shows that the mass of the vibration box has the most significant effect on the system output response, the bracket stiffness exerts a strong regulating effect on the first-order modal peak, and the damping of the vibration box plays a prominent role in high-frequency energy dissipation and amplitude control. The RecurDyn simulation results are in good agreement with the theoretical analysis, further confirming the rationality of the proposed model.

title: Single Platform, Dual Readout: Comparing EIS and EGFET Modes for Quercetin Sensing
authors: Giulia Elli, Ahmed Rasheed, Shamim Torkian, Sahira Vasquez, Paolo Lugli, Luisa Petti, Pietro Ibba
abstract: Quercetin, a polyphenol found in fruits and vegetables, is well known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and therapeutic properties, with potential roles in preventing cardiovascular, oncological, and neurodegenerative diseases. Its detection could be crucial in novel foods validation. However, its effective detection in complex food matrices remains a challenge due to limitations of conventional analytical methods, which are often labor-intensive and lack portability. In this work, we present a novel dual-mode biosensing approach integrating electrolyte-gated carbon nanotube field-effect transistors (EG-CNTFETs) with electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) for the label-free detection of quercetin, employing Horseradish peroxidase (Hrp) as biorecognition element to enhance specificity. EG-CNTFETs enabled real-time monitoring of the sensor response, showing a decrease in drain-source current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</inf>) upon quercetin addition, with a sensitivity of −13.6%/mM in the 0.1–2mM range. Complementary EIS measurements confirmed the detection of quercetin via a frequency-specific impedance drop at 1 kHz, yielding a sensitivity of −15.7Ω/mM. This synergistic approach not only improves detection accuracy but also offers a deeper understanding of the interfacial processes during analyte recognition, paving the way for robust, portable sensing systems for phenolic compounds in food and biomedical applications.

title: Energy-Efficient Complexity-Aware Scheduling in Cloud-Edge Computing
authors: Thilagavathy Sakthivel, Rajasekar Murugesan, Anitha Gopalan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Cloud-edge computing has become a revolutionary paradigm to assist the design of latency-dense and computationintensive applications like Internet of Things (IoT), smart healthcare, autonomous systems, and real-time analytics. Nevertheless, methods of managing workloads, energy efficiency, and computational complexity, as well as the heterogeneity of both cloud and edge resources, create a considerable problem in the task scheduling. The current paper suggests an Energy-Efficient Complexity-Aware Scheduling (EECAS) system in cloud-edge scenarios, which is used to combine forces to minimize energy consumption/ usage, execution time and computational overhead. The suggested model describes the tasks according to the complexity of calculation, time constraints, and the resources used and allocates dynamically to the edge or cloud node with the help of a hybrid optimization strategy. Complexity-sensitive decision engine reduces the redundant offloading and optimizes the distributed resources. The structure is a combination of adaptive workload balancing, energy-aware resource scheduling, and latency-sensitive scheduling to increase overall system performance. The simulation outcomes have shown that the suggested EECAS method contributes to a substantial decrease in total energy consumption and scheduling overhead with the enhancement of the rate of task completion and system throughput in comparison with the traditional methods of scheduling. The model offers a next-generation sustainable and scalable approach to cloud-edge infrastructures to support the emerging real-time and AI-driven workloads.

title: Green accounting and firm performance: PRISMA review
authors: Huu Anh Nguyen, Thi Kim Ngoc Nguyen, Thi Cam Van Nguyen, Phan Minh Hoa Nguyen
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: The growing global emphasis on sustainability and corporate accountability has positioned green accounting as a crucial mechanism for integrating environmental considerations into business operations. This study aims to systematically synthesize empirical evidence on the relationship between green accounting and firm performance using the PRISMA 2020 framework combined with bibliometric analysis. Data were collected from two major academic databases, Scopus and Web of Science, covering publications from 1999 to 2025. A total of 407 peer-reviewed studies were selected through a multistage screening process following the PRISMA protocol. The bibliometric analysis, conducted using the Bibliometrix R-package and VOSviewer, reveals a strong global interest in this research area, with Asia - particularly China - emerging as the leading contributor. Core publication sources include Journal of Cleaner Production, Business Strategy and the Environment, and Sustainability. The findings indicate that green accounting plays a significant role in linking financial accountability with environmental stewardship, helping firms achieve operational efficiency, cost savings, and improved stakeholder trust. While most studies confirm a positive association between green accounting and firm performance, results varied across industries, regions, and regulatory contexts, suggesting the influence of mediating factors such as governance quality, environmental regulation, and stakeholder engagement. This review contributes to the academic discourse by mapping the intellectual structure of the field and identifying emerging themes such as ESG integration, carbon disclosure, and sustainable finance. The study underscores that green accounting should be understood not merely as a reporting instrument but as a strategic management framework for achieving sustainable competitiveness. The findings provide valuable implications for scholars, policymakers, and practitioners seeking to strengthen the link between environmental responsibility and long-term corporate performance.

title: A novel heterogeneous limit analysis framework for the collapse assessment of curved masonry constructions under different loading scenarios
authors: Mohammad Pourfouladi, Natalia Pingaro, Gabriele Milani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative computational framework for the collapse assessment of masonry constructions, which seamlessly integrates advanced parametric modeling with Upper Bound Limit Analysis (UBLA). The methodology is built upon the PoliBrick plugin, implemented within the Rhinoceros/Grasshopper environment, which enables the automatic generation of three-dimensional brick assemblages that accurately represent the stereotomy of complex geometries, including arches, vaults, and walls. This geometric output is directly interfaced with a home-made heterogeneous Upper Bound Limit Analysis (UBLA) code implemented in MATLAB. The code models masonry blocks as infinitely resistance units. All nonlinearities are concentrated at the interfaces. These contact surfaces are governed by a rigid-perfectly plastic Mohr-Coulomb criterion. The versatility and accuracy of the proposed framework are demonstrated through its application to two distinct loading scenarios and structural typologies. First, it is validated against an in-situ tested masonry arch subjected to a vertical concentrated load, showing excellent agreement with experimental collapse loads. Second, its efficacy for seismic assessment is proven by analyzing a cross-vault, where the critical influence of stereotomy is systematically investigated across four different brickwork patterns. The results quantify the profound impact of brick disposition on the structural capacity, revealing performance variations of up to 84% in the collapse multiplier. The proposed framework thus provides practitioners with a practical, efficient, and accurate tool for the rapid prediction of collapse mechanisms in a wide range of masonry constructions under diverse loading conditions.

title: VulnCloud Scanner: Lightweight Multiengine Scanning for Container Security
authors: Divas A S, Barath S, Geethanjali D, Umasankari N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Container security has become a major issue for organizations adopting cloud-native architectures. Existing scanner reports lack exploit context, deployment-specific applicability and knowledge of whether the vulnerable function is ever executed. This leaves security teams overwhelmed by alerts that are theoretically flagged but practically irrelevant. This paper presents VulnCloud, an open-source Kubernetesnative framework that turns inconsistent scanner results into actionable and deployment-aware risk signals. The VulnCloud orchestrates many scanning engines through a confidenceweighted consensus model. Enriches findings with exploitlikelihood and threat intel, context-aware applicability filtering, and validates exploitability through hybrid static call-graph and runtime reachability analysis. An admission controller enforces the composite risk score through policy-as-code to block images. On evaluation over 100 diverse container images, VulnCloud achieves a decision accuracy of 96.8 %, a precision of 99.7 %, and a false-positive rate of 0.5 %. This offers security teams a fully auditable, CI/CD native pipeline that is low-noise and highassurance.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of Multiband Fractal Antenna Based on Koch Curve Structure
authors: Sakthisudhan Karuppanan, Nandhini N, Prakash N, Vidusha S P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: A triangle fractal antenna was proposed in this research article. It was designed, simulated and fabricated for application in wireless communication. The design of antenna concept is based on a fractal geometry, specifically the subsequent iteration Koch fractal patch implemented on an FR-4 substrate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{\mathrm{r}}=4.4$</tex>, thickness = 1.6 mm) with substrate dimension of 70 × 60 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, partial ground plane dimension of 25 × 60 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. When the geometric coordinates of an electrical length of patch surface increases (iterations) without affecting the overall dimensions, this design enables compatibility. The proposed fractal coordinates were simulated in CST Microwave Studio 2025. Based on the simulation results, there was a resonance frequency of 2.031 GHz with a fractional bandwidth of 44.79%, which indicates wideband characteristics. The fabricated prototype shows multiband performance at 1.03 GHz, 1.55 GHz, 1.84 GHz and 2.47 GHz respectively. Among the resonances at 1.84 GHz and 2.47 GHz the demonstration satisfied impedance matching performance with return losses of -41.9 dB and -41.2 dB. Moreover, these VSWR has satisfied the transmission line characteristics. Simulated versus measured results are in reasonable agreement indicating the proposed fractal antenna design is effective. The results indicate that the achieved antenna has good wideband and narrowband Characteristics, which is useful for modern-day wireless applications.

title: Multimodal Model Based on Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining for Micro-Expression Recognition
authors: Peng Yang, Yanyang Zhou, Qilin Wei, Zhifeng Zeng
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Micro-expressions, fleeting facial expressions lasting 1/25 to 1/5 s, are characterized by low intensity and involuntary occurrence, making them critical in applications such as psychological evaluation and forensic lie detection. This study proposes MECLIP, a dual-modal micro-expression recognition framework built on Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining (CLIP). The MECLIP introduces three key innovations. (1) Visual stream: The original CLIP image encoder was reformulated as a temporal transformer equipped with a hierarchical spatiotemporal feature extractor that employs multi-scale spatiotemporal attention to jointly capture local muscle movements and global expression dynamics. (2) Text stream: Large language models (e.g., ChatGPT) generate fine-grained physiological behavior descriptions to replace traditional categorical labels. Learnable, context-aware prompt tokens further tighten visual-text semantic alignments through contrastive learning. (3) Multimodal fusion: A learnable cross-modal fusion module combines visual and textual representations, enabling the model to jointly reason about facial muscle dynamics and their semantic interpretations throughout micro-expression evolution. On the CAS(ME)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> dataset, MECLIP attained an unweighted average recall of 39.6% and an unweighted F1 of 40.0%, outperforming the previous best baseline by 3.8% and 5.9% in relative terms. Given that micro-expression recognition is an intrinsically difficult task where accuracy remains limited even under controlled conditions, the consistent gains of the proposed multimodal approach highlight the potential of language-augmented contrastive learning for micro-expression analysis.

title: AI Powered: Gamification Strategy for Dyslexic Children
authors: Harshith Leburu, Kishore Kumar, Senduru Srinivasulu, Suganthi. L, Jeberson Ratna raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Online Resource for Child Education Games that Help Children with Dyslexia Learn. Developed and delivered by a series of tests that evaluate the mental capabilities of children with dyslexia; these tests identify both strengths and weaknesses; the results of these tests enable us to create customized games for children that improve their reading skills, memory and attention span. All games are designed to maximize the participants' involvement in the process through an entertaining, engaging and cognitive approach; our continuous support through guiding children allows us to help them develop their strengths to the fullest potential. The reinforcement and progress tracking are interactive and provide insights which are helpful for both children and parents, showing the areas of growth and the ones that need assistance. Furthermore, its online accessibility makes it easy to reach from any device, thus allowing flexible and convenient learning. The project which integrates adaptive assessments, customized games, and real-time monitoring develops an engaging and supportive environment that not only enhances skill acquisition but also boosts self-esteem, thereby providing a fun and effective alternative to the old-fashioned methods of learning for dyslexic students.

title: Improving Skin Cancer Detection Using Deep Learning Methods
authors: Aisha Singh, Moresh M. Mukhedkar, Vivek P. Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Skin cancer is a worldwide health issue because of its relatively common and onerous histopathology unless diagnosed early. Recognizing malignant skin lesions in a timely manner is essential for increasing survival rates and minimizing the complexity of treatment. In this paper, a deep learning-enabled system is proposed for automatic identification of several types of skin cancer from dermatoscopic images. The proposed framework aims to recognize seven types of lesions: Actinic Keratoses and Intraepithelial Carcinoma (akiec), basal cell carcinoma (bcc), benign keratosis-like lesions (bkl), dermatofibroma (df), melanoma (mel), melanocytic nevi (nv) and vascular lesions(vasc). CNNs are chosen, as they have demonstrated the ability to learn discriminative features from image itself, without employing any hand-crafted feature designs. The model is trained on the publicly available HAM10000 dataset. Additionally, a comparative perspective between classical machine learning approaches and deep learning models is considered. The proposed system demonstrates strong potential for real-world clinical deployment and scalable diagnostic solutions. The experimental results show that the proposed method achieves satisfactory performance with a training accuracy of 96.00% and validation accuracy of 97.00%. These results demonstrated that the designed deep learning model has a potential to be used as an assistive tool for early detection of skin cancer and also decision making by dermatologists. In addition, this study also demonstrates the importance of deep learning methods in medical image analysis and helps to promote the application of public datasets like HAM10000 in facilitating future research on automatic dermatological diagnosis.

title: Enhancing Design Control Compliance in Medical Devices Leveraging AI, Blockchain and Real-Time Monitoring
authors: Ranga Swamy Sirisati, Kotari Sridevi, Kanusu Srinivasa Rao, Ratna Kumari Challa, Pradeep Venuthurumilli, Swapna Kasarla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Design controls are a requirement mandated to optimize the safety, performance, and regulatory compliance of medical devices, especially part 820.30 of the FDA. However, despite the critical nature of these controls, repeated FDA 483 observations can be traced back to substantial margin of discrepancy in implementing these requirements, which result in recalls, compliance exposure and delay in time-to-market. Recent works of their functions and FDA 483 observations compare any gaps in the early stages (before and during the initiative implementation and late-stage control and support design elements; and finally. To demonstrate how the method/analysis can be used to improve medical device development practices. Through an examination of historical data trends and common design non-compliances, this survey presents implementable best practices and information to manufacturers to help them reduce design control inadequacies. The rationale behind the findings is to strengthen product quality, increase the respect of regulation, and achieve better results for the manufacturer and the patient. This paper will also cover how to leverage new approaches and technologies that can be implemented to overcome these challenges, specifically related to empowering design control systems with the use of automation, data analytics, and real-time compliance monitoring.

title: Biologically-Inspired Networking: Exploring Nature-Inspired Algorithms in Wireless Sensor Network Design
authors: Narender Chinthamu, Vipashi Kansal, Krishna Kant Dixit, Anurag Shrivastava, Arun Pratap Srivastava, Akhil Sankhyan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper explores the application of biologically inspired algorithms in the design and optimization of Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). By drawing parallels between biological systems and technical challenges, this study delves into how principles of swarm intelligence, genetic algorithms, and other nature-inspired approaches can enhance WSN performance. These algorithms are particularly suited to address issues such as energy efficiency, robust network architecture, and adaptive communication protocols in dynamic environments. The paper provides a thorough examination of various biologically inspired algorithms, their implementation in WSNs, and evaluates their effectiveness through simulation and real-world testing. The findings suggest significant improvements in WSNs’ adaptability, efficiency, and scalability, promoting a shift towards more resilient and autonomous network infrastructures.

title: Forecasting Analytics for Lean Supply Chain Management: A Scoping Review
authors: Rostyslav Pietukhov, Mujthaba Ahtamad
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Lean manufacturing focuses on waste reduction and operational flow but increasingly requires predictive analytics to navigate volatility and uncertainty in data-driven industries. Integrating forecasting models into lean frameworks offers businesses a pathway to improve decision-making, enhance responsiveness, and sustain competitive advantage. This scoping review examines how forecasting integrates within lean supply chain systems, with emphasis on models used and delivered value. Using PRISMA-ScR guidelines, 10 studies (2009–2024) were analysed across diverse industries and regions, indicating narrow but focused scope. Three key themes emerged: (1) forecasting acts as a strategic enabler of lean, supporting proactive planning, waste minimisation, and Just-in-Time operations; (2) classical statistical models (e.g., ARIMA, Holt-Winters) remain prevalent despite advances in AI and Machine Learning, reflecting a pragmatic preference for simplicity; and (3) integration challenges, such as data quality issues and proprietary software reliance, highlight the need for scalable, context-aware analytics solutions. This study clarifies forecasting’s role as a lean enabler and provides practical value by guiding practitioners in aligning forecasting approaches with lean frameworks to improve operational performance. Future research should focus on developing data-driven integration frameworks, assessing forecasting model trade-offs and success factors, and exploring how IoT and agentic AI could enable real-time, autonomous lean supply chains in data-rich environments.

title: An Overview of Analyst Forecast Research: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Phan Minh Hoa Nguyen, Thi Kim Ngoc Nguyen, Hoang Tu Nhi Truong
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: This paper aims to provide a comprehensive overview of research on analyst forecasts by applying bibliometric techniques to identify influential contributors, collaboration patterns, and key research themes. A dataset of 1,995 documents published between 1975 and July 2025 was extracted from Scopus using the keywords "analyst forecast" and "analyst prediction". Bibliometric analysis was performed with VOSviewer and Biblioshiny to examine publication trends, influential authors, journals, and countries, as well as co-authorship networks, keyword co-occurrence, and citation structures. The results show a steady growth of publications, with Accounting Review and Review of Accounting Studies being the most influential journals. Gerald J. Lobo and Lawrence D. Brown emerge as leading authors, while the United States plays a dominant role in international collaboration. Keyword analysis highlights themes of forecast accuracy, market efficiency, behavioral biases, and corporate governance, with recent attention to uncertainty and asset pricing. Highly cited works form the intellectual foundation of this field. The study provides valuable insights for academics by highlighting key contributors, thematic clusters, and emerging trends, thereby guiding future research directions.

title: Research on Energy Optimal Path Planning Algorithm for UUV Considering Dynamic Constraints
authors: Meng You, Zhanfeng Ying, Hao Ge, Dingcheng Peng
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (UUVs) play an increasingly important role in marine resource exploration, underwater detection, and other marine applications. However, existing path planning algorithms generally lack systematic consideration of the coupling effects between ocean currents and UUV dynamics. To address this issue, this paper proposes an energy-optimal path planning method for UUVs in three-dimensional (3D) ocean current environments, which integrates dynamic constraints and realistic thruster characteristics. First, a UUV dynamic model is established with the influence of relative current velocity taken into account, enabling the dynamic evaluation of path feasibility. Second, a power characteristic model is constructed based on the actual performance curve of the propulsion motor, which converts dynamic thrust requirements into an accurate energy consumption calculation model. On this basis, an improved path planning algorithm suitable for 3D complex flow fields is developed. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm increases the ocean current utilization rate by 56.74% and reduces total energy consumption by 25.56% while ensuring path dynamic feasibility.

title: IoT Based Smart Cart for Automated Billing in Supermarkets
authors: Sumit Srivastava, Sushanta Mahanty, Krishna Kant Singh, Amit Kumar Das, Shraddha Shreyanshu, Akash Sharma
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The challenges that modern retail stores are continually experiencing include queues in the billing counters, heavy dependence on manpower, and increasing cases of shoplifting and billing fraud. To address these constraints, this study presents a prototype of the IoT-based smart cart that would be used to automatize billing, improve the efficiency of shopping, and decrease labour expenses. The smart cart includes a unique QR code. When scanned shopping session is initiated that links the cart to the user's profile. Products are added to the cart by scanning their barcodes through the application. To validate and provide security, weight and ultrasonic sensors, as well as ESP32-CAM module, are offered to the cart to scan the cart with an integrated camera to run a YOLO-based object detection algorithm to detect unscanned or fraudulent entries. Products added by the customer is instantly reflected on the web app and if any mismatch is found then store manager is alerted. The customer then goes to a special checkout point where he or she pays and leaves the store. The system ensures secure and customer friendly shopping with instant billing updates, fraud detection, and reduces labour needs.

title: Improving Brand Interaction by Integrating AI into Marketing Communication
authors: Vasudha Jaggula, Gitanjali Shrivastava, Naim Shaikh, Vivek Veeraiah, Ankur Gupta, Arpit Namdev
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the present digital age, AI is changing way research helps customers. These are some of biggest changes in how people interact with brands. Research examines utilization of AI in marketing communication, focusing on chatbots, and recommendation systems, with delivering data-driven, customized, and efficient consumer experiences. Research performs literature analysis to analyze and compare traditional advertising with AI-driven alternatives. Researach shows that AI helps organizations build stronger relationships with their customers, that lead to more engagement, and trust. AI can be helpful in more ways than merely providing customer support, according to the research. As a result of making it simpler for people to communicate with one another in real time and by targeting clients based on the products they are most likely to purchase, it also assists individuals in getting to know one another better. The essay teaches us that it is important to establish a strategy for dealing with any potential issues that may arise during the installation of artificial intelligence. Because it provides the marketing industry with new communication methods that are scalable and centered on the client, this initiative provides the marketing sector with both theoretical and practical benefits.

title: Learning with Machines: What Shapes the Use of ChatGPT in Universities?
authors: Delenvya Savinka Frensya, Razib Chandra Chanda, Miao Ling Zhang, Priscilia Calista Kowirya, Anderes Gui, Suryanto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The rapid advancement of technology has empowered classroom instructors to evolve into tech-savvy educators. Among recent innovations, Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) has emerged as a transformative force with the potential to reshape teaching and learning. However, despite its promising capabilities, students’ intention to use GenAI in higher education remains relatively low. This study aims to examine the key factors influencing students’ intention to adopt GenAI in academic settings. Focusing on five core variables, Perceived Usefulness, Perceived Trust, Perceived Risk, Moral Obligation, and Perceived Intelligence, the research investigates how these elements shape students’ behavioral intentions. A quantitative approach was employed, with data collected from 224 students via an online survey. Importantly, participants who did not previously use GenAI for educational purposes. The data were analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) to test the hypothesized relationships. The findings reveal that Moral Obligation, and Perceived Usefulness positively influence students’ intention to use GenAI, while Perceived Risk has a significant negative effect. In contrast, Perceived Trust and Social Influence were not found to have a significant impact on students’ intentions. These results suggest that students are more inclined to adopt GenAI when they perceive it as morally relevant, and useful, and when associated risks are minimal, rather than being influenced by trust or social endorsement. The study offers both theoretical and practical implications for stakeholders involved in the development and integration of AI-driven tools, such as chatbots, within higher education.

title: Real-Time UAV Swarm Autonomy Using Edge-Deployed Small Language Models
authors: Kruthik Narasimhamurthy, Prajwal Mahesha Sethurani, Aryan Anand, Prabha Sundaravadivel, H. Allen Torbert, Lakshman Tamil
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: This work demonstrates an edge-enabled autonomous UAV swarm framework in which onboard intelligence enables real-time decision-making without reliance on cloud connectivity. The video demonstration showcases autonomous drone operation driven by a small language model (SLM) optimized for deployment across heterogeneous hardware platforms, including NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, edge boards, and companion laptops. The system highlights how lightweight language-based reasoning can be integrated directly into drone autonomy pipelines to support mission execution, coordination, and adaptive behavior. In the demonstrated setup, each UAV performs onboard inference to interpret mission context and execute autonomous actions while maintaining low latency and energy efficiency. Hardware-aware optimization allows the same SLM architecture to run consistently across embedded and non-embedded devices, enabling seamless transitions between onboard autonomy and ground-based supervision. The demonstration illustrates realtime responsiveness, stable execution, and decentralized intelligence, which are critical for scalable UAV swarm operations. By validating autonomous behavior through an end-to-end video demonstration, this work emphasizes the practicality of deploying language-driven reasoning models directly at the edge. The results highlight a viable pathway toward robust, scalable UAV swarm autonomy using embedded AI, supporting applications that require rapid decision-making in communication-limited environments.

title: Medicinal Plant Disease Analysis by Using CNN-based Approaches
authors: Yamini Singh, Sanjay Kumar Dubey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The fast evolution of artificial intelligence has greatly revolutionized the concept of identifying plant diseases especially by the use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). In recent years, the change in research in this field has shifted to simple image-level classification of diseases into more complex hybrid learning models, real-time object detector models, architectures founded on attention and solutions at the system level. The current paper offers a systematic review of 30 peerreviewed articles published in 2022-2026 and it is dedicated to the gradual development of methods applied to the analysis of plant diseases and medicinal plants. The recent methodological advances studied in this paper comprise CNN-only models, transfer learning, hybrid models, object detection systems, attention mechanisms, and realizations at a scale of a system. Technique-wise distribution (pie chart), year-wise growth of publications (stacked bar graph) and task-evolution flow diagrams are used to perform analysis of a trend. The research review draws attention to the transition in the research priorities to deployable, real-time, and large-scale solutions of monitoring. The results can be seen as the synthesized view of the research tendencies and can be utilized as a framework of reference in further studies of AI-driven plant and medicinal disease analysis.

title: Earliest Failure Localization in Pipelines Via Quantum Search
authors: Alexander Fisher, Christophe Bobda
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Debugging pipelined RTL designs is often delayed by weak localization: a failure may be observed late in a trace even when the first illegal condition occurs earlier and is later masked or overwritten. We formulate earliest-stage localization as a predicate search problem over a fixed environment model. Stage-scoped Boolean (and bounded-temporal) rules induce perstage violation predicates and an earliest-stage predicate that accepts an assignment only when a stage violates and no earlier stage violates, yielding disjoint attribution by construction. We show how to realize this predicate as a reversible compute-phase-uncompute oracle, and we cast oracle synthesis as a time-space optimization problem on the predicate's Boolean directed acyclic graph under a hard workspace budget. Our main contribution is a budgeted active-volume compilation pass that emits legal compute/uncompute schedules with peak live intermediates bounded by an ancilla budget, while minimizing a recomputation-aware unit-cost proxy. A grounded case study on an exported 41-node predicate graph demonstrates the induced workspace-recomputation tradeoff and separates exact versus heuristic scheduling behavior in the tight-budget regime.

title: Energy-Efficient Trajectory Prediction Routing Protocols for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Shams Tabrez Siddiqui, Nadim Rana, Adeel Ahmad, Mohd Sarfaraz, Abu Salim, Haneef Khan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The use of Unmanned aerial vehicles is becoming vital in many activities, i.e., surveillance and data collection. The efficiency of the energy is paramount to the maximum functionality of the systems, as it affects the time that a UAV could exist in the air and its performance efficiency. In addition to energy issues, the policies that will govern unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), internet connectivity and information exchange are also dynamic since they determine the effectiveness of drone cooperation. This paper combines two areas of critical importance, i.e., predicting energy-saving routes and routing communication. To do this, we use historical flight data and use advanced machine learning algorithms to make predictions on the movements of UAVs more accurate. The main aim of our study is to examine the possibility of using improved trajectory forecasts to aid people in making better routing choices therefore less energy is used in UAV network.

title: Quantum Enhanced Secure Communication Integrating Quantum Entanglement Based Secure Channels with XOR Based Encryption for Resilient Data Exchange
authors: M. Sivaranjini, N. Raviragul, V. Joel Kirubakaran, A. Raguraman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Digital interaction's expansion has created a necessity for safe and efficient data transfer. Standard encryption procedures can be cracked by sophisticated hacking techniques, and moreover, they will become obsolete in the future with the emergence of quantum computers. This article introduces a hybrid cryptography that consists of a secure channel based on quantum entanglement and a lightweight encryption scheme using XOR operations. Quantum key distribution allows for a tamper-proof key exchange, and at the same time, XOR encryption provides a very simple and quick encryption and decryption process. Thus, this double-layer security system not only ensures data privacy and data integrity but also makes it difficult for the attackers to use brute force or become one of the middle parties during the transaction. Moreover, the XOR algorithm can lower the number of calculations needed for the process, so the method can be utilized in real-time secure communication without any computational overhead. Simulation experiments are used for the evaluation of security, the time for the key generation, and the efficiency of encryption. Experiments of results underline the excellent protection of information interception and low technical overhead signalled in comparison with traditional methods of cryptography. This paper exemplifies one possible way to mix quantum and classical encryption techniques to build a secure communication system that is reliable, upgradable, and less vulnerable to attacks, which could eventually lead to a post-quantum era.

title: An Energy- and Area-Efficient Two-Stage Latch Comparator Using Common-Mode Reset in 65nm CMOS
authors: Larry Yu, Ping Gui
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: This paper presents an energy- and area-efficient two-stage comparator implemented in TSMC 65 nm CMOS that incorporates a Floating Inverter Amplifier (FIA) followed by a latch using a common-mode reset scheme. The FIA improves input sensitivity and reduces kickback noise by isolating the input nodes from large latch voltage swings. This paper proposes a new latch design that has an initial state at a common-mode voltage, thereby reducing switching energy during the reset phase. The comparator can resolve differential input voltages as small as 2 mV with a clock period of 500 ps, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$74.33 \mu ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> inputreferred noise while consuming 81.69 fJ per comparison.

title: Intelligent Modelling for Legislative Reforms in Enhancing Women’s Empowerment
authors: Kavita Saini, Lehar Isarani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of legislative reforms, particularly the 73rd Constitutional Amendment (1992) and subsequent state-level policies increasing women’s reservation, on women’s political empowerment within India’s Panchayati Raj institutions. Utilizing a mixed-methods approach, the research integrates quantitative analysis of election data and participation trends with qualitative insights from interviews with women leaders in Kerala and Uttar Pradesh.
